English: These are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt with Jacob, each with his household:
Kokborok: Omorokno Jakobni bwsarok; bórokno bo bai baksa ha Ijibo thángnai núkhungrok;

English: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah,
Kokborok: Ruben, Simion, Lebi tei Juda,

English: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin,
Kokborok: Isakar, Jebulun tei Benjamin,

English: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.
Kokborok: Dan, Naptali, Gad tei Aser.

English: All the offspring of Jacob were seventy persons; Joseph was already in Egypt.
Kokborok: Jakobni bwsarok bebagwi khoroksnichi ká-o. Bwsla Josep swkang’-no Ijibo sógwijak.

English: Then Joseph died, and all his brothers, and all that generation.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep tei bini bebak takhukrokbo thwi bai kha tei bosongrokbo thwi bai kha.

English: But the descendants of Israel were fruitful and increased greatly; they multiplied and grew exceedingly strong; so that the land was filled with them.
Kokborok: Israilni bosongrok doui er lai kha tei phan gwnang wngwi hamchaui ragwi bacha kha; hakhe a ha-o bórok máng kupulung wng kha.

English: Now there arose a new king over Egypt, who did not know Joseph.
Kokborok: Tabuk Josepno sini ya borok khoroksa ha Ijipni bugra chá kha.

English: And he said to his people, “Behold, the people of Israel are too many and too mighty for us.
Kokborok: Tei bini bororokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, Israilsarok chini slái belaino kwbáng, nang lai khe chini kebengnai arirok bai kwtha laiwi chwng bai choba khlainai tei chini hani yógwi thángnai.

English: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, lest they multiply, and, if war befall us, they join our enemies and fight against us and escape from the land.”
Kokborok: Chwng bórokno siyan khe ma tẃi lainai, bórokno er ya khlai tonna nangnai, erwi teibo báng khe chobarok nang lai khe chini arirok bai kwtha laiwi chwng bai choba khlainai tei chini hani yógwi thángnai.”

English: Therefore they set taskmasters over them to afflict them with heavy burdens; and they built for Pharaoh store-cities, Pithom and Ra-amses.
Kokborok: Abo bai’-no samung tang rwnairok bórokno samung kwrak kwrak khlai rwui sátok kha tei bórokni yak tẃi khe Pharaoni bagwi manwi rúngjaknai kamiyung kainwi Pithon bai Ramaseno tisa kha.

English: But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and the more they spread abroad. And the Egyptians were in dread of the people of Israel.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok, bórokno sukhrup rw rwk wng kha, yang khe bórok teibo se er rw rwgo tei yang yang pírjak rw rwgo.

English: So they made the people of Israel serve with rigor,
Kokborok: Ijipni bororok bórokno kirijak lai kha. Hakhe bórok teibo khairok yaui Israilsarokno samung tang rw kha.

English: and made their lives bitter with hard service, in mortar and brick, and in all kinds of work in the field; in all their work they made them serve with rigor.
Kokborok: Samung kwrak kwrak tang rwui bórokno tong kesebo khe ton kha, holong, ha kwrak bokhok rw kha bórokni haba-o mula tang rw kha. Bebak samungo’-no kisabo khairok yaui tang rw kha.

English: Then the king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives, one of whom was named Shiphrah and the other Puah,
Kokborok: Ijipni habugra Hibru lomojwk-kumajwk khoroknwi Sipra tei Puano hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “When you serve as midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them upon the birthstool, if it is a son, you shall kill him; but if it is a daughter, she shall live.”
Kokborok: “Norok Hibru bwrwirokno bá rw khe nai di tei abo bwsa chwla hwn khe achai kheno báma nogo’-no butharwi pái di tei bwrwi achai khe le narwk di.”

English: But the midwives feared God, and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but let the male children live.
Kokborok: Tamo khe lomojwk-kumajwkrok Kaitorno kirijagwi Ijipni habugra dagima hai khlai li ya, chwla bwsarokno kwthang narwk kha.

English: So the king of Egypt called the midwives, and said to them, “Why have you done this, and let the male children live?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Ijipni habugra a lomojwk-kumajwkrokno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tamo norok omo hai ma khlai lang tamo chwla bwsarokno kwthang narwk lai lang?”

English: The midwives said to Pharaoh, “Because the Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; for they are vigorous and are delivered before the midwife comes to them.”
Kokborok: A lomojwk-kumajwkrok Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Hibru bwrwirok Ijipni bwrwirok hai ya bórok kwrak gwja, lomojwk-kumajwkrok sógwi ya sini’-no bórok báui pái-o.”

English: So God dealt well with the midwives; and the people multiplied and grew very strong.
Kokborok: Kaitor a lomojwk-kumajwkrokni saka yak tonjak. Hakhe bororok er kha tei teibo kwrak khe bacha kha.

English: And because the midwives feared God he gave them families.
Kokborok: A lomojwk-kumajwkrok Kaitorno kirimani bai Kaitor bórokno sakni núkhung rw kha.

English: Then Pharaoh commanded all his people, “Every son that is born to the Hebrews you shall cast into the Nile, but you shall let every daughter live.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao bini bebak bororokno hai hwnwi dagi kha, “Hibrurokni núkhung mathenino chwla bwsa achai kheno norok Nil twima-o khiklaiwi rw di, tamo khe bwrwi bwsa achai khe le kwthang narwgwi ton di.”

English: Now a man from the house of Levi went and took to wife a daughter of Levi.
Kokborok: Levi bosongni chwla khoroksa, Lebi bosongni’-no bwrwi khoroksano káijak kha.

English: The woman conceived and bore a son; and when she saw that he was a goodly child, she hid him three months.
Kokborok: A bwrwi sakderiya wngwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá kha; tei bwrwima a chwraino naithok nukma bai taltham huiwi ton kha.

English: And when she could hide him no longer she took for him a basket made of bulrushes, and daubed it with bitumen and pitch; and she put the child in it and placed it among the reeds at the river's brink.
Kokborok: A chwraino tei huiwi tonwi man li ya wngwi bo papirani japa kaisa naui Nil tei alkatra phul kha hakhe a chwraino aro daui Nil twimani twi rukungo tonma papira bwlwngo khe tonwi rwi kha.

English: And his sister stood at a distance, to know what would be done to him.
Kokborok: Hakhe bibijwk hakchalo bachaui tongwi a chwraino tamo khlai abono naichomwi tong kha.

English: Now the daughter of Pharaoh came down to bathe at the river, and her maidens walked beside the river; she saw the basket among the reeds and sent her maid to fetch it.
Kokborok: Aphuru Pharaoni bwsajwk twima-o tukuna bagwi phai kha tei bini selengjwk a twi rukung tẃi himwi tháng khe bo a japano papira bwlwngo nuk kheno bini selengjwkno a japano khochaui tubu di hwnwi dagi kha.

English: When she opened it she saw the child; and lo, the babe was crying. She took pity on him and said, “This is one of the Hebrews' children.”
Kokborok: Bo a japano phiyok khe aro chwrai bwsa chwla nuk kha: A chwrai kabwi tongo. Bo bono khairokjagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo khoroksa swk Hibruni bwsano wngnai.”

English: Then his sister said to Pharaoh's daughter, “Shall I go and call you a nurse from the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?”
Kokborok: Hakhe bini bukhuk Pharaoni bwsajwkno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Ang chwraino chá rwna khan rwna bagwi Hibru bwrwima khoroksa swkno nini thani ringwi rw phaina da?”

English: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, “Go.” So the girl went and called the child's mother.
Kokborok: Pharaoni bwsajwk bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di.” Hakhe a bwrwi thángwi a chwraini bumano ringwi tubu-ui phai kha.

English: And Pharaoh's daughter said to her, “Take this child away, and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages.” So the woman took the child and nursed him.
Kokborok: Tei Pharaoni bwsajwk bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng thángwi o chwraino chá rwui di, khan rwui di, ang aboni bagwi nono rang rw anw.” Hakhe a bwrwima a chwraino tlangwi thángwi chá rwui kha, khan rwui kha.

English: And the child grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son; and she named him Moses, for she said, “Because I drew him out of the water.”
Kokborok: Hakhe a chwrai tor rwk lok rwk wng kha, hakhe bo ulo Pharaoni bwsajwkni thani tubu-ui phai kha, hakhe bini sajla snam kha, bo a chwraino “Ang twini se tisaui tubu-o tei bono Musi hwnwi mung phar kha.”

English: One day, when Moses had grown up, he went out to his people and looked on their burdens; and he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his people.
Kokborok: Musi torwi salsa bini bororokno thángwi naiwi bórok bwswk khe samung kwrak kwrak tang rwjagwi tong abono nuk kha tei khoroksa Ijipsa bini Hibru khoroksano bumani nuk kha.

English: He looked this way and that, and seeing no one he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
Kokborok: Bo abono nuk kheno aro borok khoroksa phano kwrwi nugwi a Ijipsano butharwi hasingkhongo phobwi rw kha.

English: When he went out the next day, behold, two Hebrews were struggling together; and he said to the man that did the wrong, “Why do you strike your fellow?”
Kokborok: Teini salo bo tháng phi kha, hakhe Hibrusa khoroknwiokno bú laiwi tong laimani nuk kha; hakhe bo cha ya khlainaino hwnwi sa kha “Tamni nwng sakni boroknoba ma bu?”

English: He answered, “Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid, and thought, “Surely the thing is known.”
Kokborok: Bo uphilwi Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sabo nono chini bagwi bugra tei nokphang buj thumnai snam kha ba? Nwng ba tamo a Ijipsano butharma hai da ano bo butharna nai?” Hakhe Musi kirijak kha tei uansuk kha, “Sói omo saui man bai khna’-no,” hwnwi chong kha.

English:
When Pharaoh heard of it, he sought to kill Moses.
But Moses fled from Pharaoh, and stayed in the land of Midian; and he sat down by a well.
Kokborok:
Pharao a kókno khna kheno, bo Musino butharna chong kha.
Tamo khe Musi Pharaoni nokni khár kha tei Midian ha-o tongwi kha. Twikhor samo khe thángwi achugwi kha.

English: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters; and they came and drew water, and filled the troughs to water their father's flock.
Kokborok: Midianni ochaini bwsajwksong khoroksni tongo. Bórok phaiwi twi khók phai kha tei buphani merarokno twi khan phai kha.

English: The shepherds came and drove them away; but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.
Kokborok: Mera mwrwknairok phaiwi a bwrwirokno riphrigwi rohor kha.

English: When they came to their father Reuel, he said, “How is it that you have come so soon today?”
Kokborok: Bórok bupha Reulni thani phai khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok bahai aswk dakti phai laiwi man kha?”

English: They said, “An Egyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds, and even drew water for us and watered the flock.”
Kokborok: Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijipsa khoroksa chwngno mera mwrwknairokni yakni phiyók kha, bo chini bagwi twi khógwi merarokno twi khansru kha.”

English: He said to his daughters, “And where is he? Why have you left the man? Call him, that he may eat bread.”
Kokborok: Bo bwsajwksongno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Buko a borok buro? Bono ringhor di, bo oro mai chá phai thung.”

English: And Moses was content to dwell with the man, and he gave Moses his daugher Zipporah.
Kokborok: Musi a borok bai tongnani lon kha, hakhe bo Musino bwsajwk Jipora bai káiwi rw kha.

English: She bore a son, and he called his name Gershom; for he said, “I have been a sojourner in a foreign land.”
Kokborok: A bwrwi bwsa chwla khoroksa bá kha tei Musi bono Gersom hwnwi mung phar kha, tamni hwn khe buini ha-o haino buini borok wngwi tongmani se.

English: In the course of those many days the king of Egypt died. And the people of Israel groaned under their bondage, and cried out for help, and their cry under bondage came up to God.
Kokborok: Sal kwbángma lái thángma ulo khe Ijipni habugra thwi kha. Tei Israilni bororok bórokni yago klaiwi kengai lai kha tei chubachuni bagwi kap lai kha tei seleng wngwi kap laimano Kaitor khna kha.

English: And God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.
Kokborok: Kaitor bórokni kengaimano khna kha tei bo Abraham, Isak tei Jakob bai swmai tangmano muitu wng kha.

English: And God saw the people of Israel, and God knew their condition.
Kokborok: Kaitor Israilni bororokno nuk kha tei bórok bahai wngwi tong lai kha abono Kaitor nuk kha.

English: Now Moses was keeping the flock of his father-in-law, Jethro, the priest of Midian; and he led his flock to the west side of the wilderness, and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.
Kokborok: Musi, bwkra Jethroni merarokno mwrwgwi tongo. Jethro wng kha Midian hani ochai. Bo merarokno salsibaini haphar bukcha tẃi khe tubu-ui Horebo sók phai kha, abo Kaitorni hathái.

English: And the angel of the Lord appeared to him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush; and he looked, and lo, the bush was burning, yet it was not consumed.
Kokborok: Subraiyungni suari hórni bising tẃi khe satasuta-o bo bai nukjak kha, bo a satasuta-o hórjlai le nugo phiya abo kha se kham ya.

English: And Moses said, “I will turn aside and see this great sight, why the bush is not burnt.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang samo thángwi kwmamsingchano nai anw tamo a satasutarok kham ya wng.”

English: When the Lord saw that he turned aside to see, God called to him out of the bush, “Moses, Moses!” And he said, “Here am I.”
Kokborok:
Subraiyung Musino nainani bagwi samo sederwi phainani nai tongmano nuk kheno Kaitor a satasuta-o tongwi “Musi, Musi!” hwnwi ring kha.
Hakhe Musi, “Tamo, ang oro se,” hwnwi sa kha.

English: Then he said, “Do not come near; put off your shoes from your feet, for the place on which you are standing is holy ground.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha “Samo ta phai di; nini yakungni yaklap sokok di, nwng bachaui tongma a ha wng kha kwthar.”

English: And he said, “I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.” And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God.
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwphani Kaitor, Abrahamni Kaitor, Isakni Kaitor tei Jakobni Kaitor.” Hakhe Musi Kaitorno naisikna kiriui mwkhang kholop kha.

English: Then the Lord said, “I have seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters; I know their sufferings,
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang ha Ijibo tongnai ani bororok sátokjakmano nuk kha, bórokni samung tang rwnairok bai sátokjagwi kap laimano khna kha. Ang bórokni tektono si-o.

English: and I have come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land to a good and broad land, a land flowing with milk and honey, to the place of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
Kokborok: Ang bórokno ha Ijipni bororokni yakni phiyókna bagwi ongkhorwi phai kha. Tei a hani tikhlaiwi kaham tei kuaro, dut tei piya bwtwi kochokjak ha-o tlangnai. Kanansarok, Hitisarok, Amorsarok Peressarok, Hibisarok tei Jebussarokni ha-o tlangnai.

English: And now, behold, the cry of the people of Israel has come to me, and I have seen the oppression with which the Egyptians oppress them.
Kokborok: Nai di, tabuk Israil bororok kap laimani ani khunju-o sók phai kha tei Ijipsarok bórokno bahai khe sátok-pitok khlai ang nuk kha.

English: Come, I will send you to Pharaoh that you may bring forth my people, the sons of Israel, out of Egypt.”
Kokborok: Phai di, ang nono Pharaoni thani rwi rohornai, nwng ani borok, Israilrokno ha Ijipni tikhlai ma tubunai.”

English: But Moses said to God, “Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh, and bring the sons of Israel out of Egypt?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi Kaitorno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang le sabo ba, Pharaoni thani thángwi Israil bororokno Ijipni tikhlai tubunai?”

English: He said, “But I will be with you; and this shall be the sign for you, that I have sent you: when you have brought forth the people out of Egypt, you shall serve God upon this mountain.”
Kokborok: Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng bororokno Ijipni tikhlai tubu phuru khe ang nwng bai tongnai; tei nwng ano o hathái-o ma surinai, omono ang nono rohormani mari wngnai.”

English: Then Moses said to God, “If I come to the people of Israel and say to them, ‘The God of your fathers has sent me to you,’ and they ask me, ‘What is his name?’ what shall I say to them?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Kaitorno sẃng kha, Israilrokni aro sógwiwi sa phuru khe, norokni chuchusongni Kaitor se ano rohoro hwnwi sa khe tei bórok ano bini bumung tamo hwnwi sẃng khe, bórokno ang tamo hwnwi sanai?

English: God said to Moses, “I AM WHO I AM.” And he said, “Say this to the people of Israel, ‘I AM has sent me to you.’ ”
Kokborok: Kaitor Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang ang’-no” bo Musino sa kha, “Ang ang’-no nono rohoro hwnwi Israilrokno saui di.”

English: God also said to Moses, “Say this to the people of Israel, ‘The Lord, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you’: this is my name for ever, and thus I am to be remembered throughout all generations.
Kokborok: Kaitor Musino hai hwnwibo sa kha, “Israilrokno hai hwnwi saui di, ‘Nini chuchusongni Subraiyung Kaitor, Abraham tei Isakni Kaitor, Jakobni Kaitor se norokni thani rwui rohoro, mol brúm brúm ani mung omo hai’-no. Tei omo hai khe bosong mathe bai muitu khlaijak máng tongnai.’ ”

English: Go and gather the elders of Israel together, and say to them, ‘The Lord, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, has appeared to me, saying, “I have observed you and what has been done to you in Egypt;
Kokborok: Israilni tẃiphangrokno thángwi hai hwnwi saui di. “Norokni chuchusongni Subraiyung Kaitor, Abraham, Isak tei Jakobni Kaitor ang bai nukjagwi sa kha tei ang naikhlai nuk kha, Ijiprok norokno tamo khlaiwi tong kha;

English: and I promise that I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt, to the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, a land flowing with milk and honey.” ’
Kokborok: tei ang kók rw kha, ‘Ang norokno Ijiprokni aro sátokjakmani yakni phiyógwi Kanansarokni, Hitisarokni, Amorsarokni, Peressarokni, Hibisarokni tei Jebussarokni ha-o tlangwi thángnai, a ha dut tei piya bwtwi bai kupulung.’ ”

English: And they will hearken to your voice; and you and the elders of Israel shall go to the king of Egypt and say to him, ‘The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us; and now, we pray you, let us go a three days' journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.’
Kokborok: “Ani bororok nini samarokno khnanai, hakhe nwng Israilni tẃiphangrok bai Ijipni habugrani thani thángwi hai hwnwi saui di, ‘Hibrurokni Subraiyung Kaitor baithang chwng bai nukjak kha. Tabuk salthamni lama thángwi hasing panthoro chini Subraiyung Kaitorno ratharwi yapharna bagwi chwngno rohor di.’

English: I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless compelled by a mighty hand.
Kokborok: Ang saui mano, Ijipni habugra yak kwrak bai khlai rwjak ya khe rohor glak.

English: So I will stretch out my hand and smite Egypt with all the wonders which I will do in it; after that he will let you go.
Kokborok: Hakhe ang ani yak phologwi Ijipno uanamasingcha bai khe swpraknai. Abo hai ang aro khlainai. Aboni ulo bo norokno yakarwi rohornai.

English: And I will give this people favor in the sight of the Egyptians; and when you go, you shall not go empty,
Kokborok: Ang o borokno Ijiprokni thani khá phur rwnai, norok tháng phuru khe bukcha tháng glak.

English: but each woman shall ask of her neighbor, and of her who sojourns in her house, jewelry of silver and of gold, and clothing, and you shall put them on your sons and on your daughters; thus you shall despoil the Egyptians.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bebak bwrwirokno bórokni nok arini bwrwirokni thani rangchak, rukphai, uakhum, toyarok tei rí-chumrok sanwi na di tei norok sajwk-sajlasongrokno abono kan rwui tẃi di, abo hai khe norok Ijipni borokni rang rírokno tlangwi tháng di.”

English: Then Moses answered, “But behold, they will not believe me or listen to my voice, for they will say, ‘The Lord did not appear to you.’ ”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Subraiyungno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bórok ano poito khlai ya hwn khe eba ani kók khna ya wngwi ‘Subraiyung nwng bai nukjak ya hwnwi sa khe ang tamo khlainai?’ ”

English: The Lord said to him, “What is that in your hand?” He said, “A rod.”
Kokborok:
Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nini yago tongma abo tamo?”
“Omo raidang se” hwnwi bo sa kha.

English: And he said, “Cast it on the ground.” So he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses fled from it.
Kokborok: Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Abono ha-o khiklai rw di” hakhe abo chibuk khaui tháng kha; bo aroni khár kha.

English: But the Lord said to Moses, “Put out your hand, and take it by the tail”—so he put out his hand and caught it, and it became a rod in his hand—
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Yak phologwi a chibukni khitung rom di” hakhe bo yak phologwi a chibukno rom kha tei a chibuk raidang wngwi tháng phi kha,

English: “that they may believe that the Lord, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you.”
Kokborok: “Bórokni chuchusongni Kaitor, Abrahamni Kaitor, Isakni Kaitor, Jakobni Kaitor nwng bai nukjakmano poito khlaina bagwi omono khlai di.”

English: Again, the Lord said to him, “Put your hand into your bosom.” And he put his hand into his bosom; and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous, as white as snow.
Kokborok: Subraiyung bono hai hwnwibo sa kha, “Nini yakno khaklabo ton di.” Hakhe bo bini yakno khaklabo ton kha; bo khaklapni yak nor kheno nuk kha yak biyadi chájak wngwi kothor kwsap phur tẃi khe phur kha.

English: Then God said, “Put your hand back into your bosom.” So he put his hand back into his bosom; and when he took it out, behold, it was restored like the rest of his flesh.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa phi kha, “Nini yakno khaklabo ton phi di.” Bo haino hai khaklabo yak ton phi khua, yak khaklapni nor kheno nuk kha, a yak haino hai hamwi tei kubun bahanrok haino wng phi kha.

English: “If they will not believe you,” God said, “or heed the first sign, they may believe the latter sign.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha “Bórok nono poito khlai ya hwn khe, swkangni marino sak nang ya hwn khe tei kaisano le poito phano wngwi mano.

English: If they will not believe even these two signs or heed your voice, you shall take some water from the Nile and pour it upon the dry ground; and the water which you shall take from the Nile will become blood upon the dry ground.”
Kokborok: Bórok a samung kwnwino poito khlai ya hwno eba nini kókno sak nang ya hwn khe, nwng Nil twimani twi kisa naui hakungo khiklai rw di; nwng a Nil twimani twi naui hakungo khiklaima a twi thẃi khaui thángnai.”

English: But Moses said to the Lord, “Oh, my Lord, I am not eloquent, either heretofore or since thou hast spoken to thy servant; but I am slow of speech and of tongue.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi Subraiyungno hai hwnwi sa kha “O ani Subraiyung ang man glak. Ang yaphangni simino kók kwrwngbo wng ya, nwng ano kók sa phuruni simi tabuk jorabo ang kókbo rwngsa ya kho, ang kók rwng ja ya. Douibo saui man ya, kókbo kwcha-o.”

English: Then the Lord said to him, “Who has made man's mouth? Who makes him dumb, or deaf, or seeing, or blind? Is it not I, the Lord?
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sabo borokni búkhúkno snam? Sabo bono kók man ya khlai, nathong eba mokol nuknai eba mokol phula khlai? Abo ang ya diba?

English: Now therefore go, and I will be with your mouth and teach you what you shall speak.”
Kokborok: Tabuk tháng di, ang nini khugo tongnai tei tamo sana nang phrwngnai.”

English: But he said, “Oh, my Lord, send, I pray, some other person.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “O Subraiyung, kubun tei khoroksa swkno phano rohor ja di.”

English: Then the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses and he said, “Is there not Aaron, your brother, the Levite? I know that he can speak well; and behold, he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you he will be glad in his heart.
Kokborok: Abo bai’-no Subraiyung Musino jolijagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Lebisa nini takhuk Aron ba nwng bai baksa kwrwi diba? Ang si-o bo kaham khe kók saui mano, ‘Nai di, bo nwng bai malaina bagwi ongkhorwi phai kha, bo nono nuk kheno khá srangjaknai.’

English: And you shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth; and I will be with your mouth and with his mouth, and will teach you what you shall do.
Kokborok: Nwng bono saui khna rw di, bono kók phrwngwi rw di. Ang nini khugo tei bini khugo khe tongnai tei tamo khlaina nangnai ang norokno phrwngnai.

English: He shall speak for you to the people; and he shall be a mouth for you, and you shall be to him as God.
Kokborok: Bo bororokno nini wngwi kók sanai. Bo nini bagwi khukphang wngnai, nwng bini thani Kaitor hai wngnai.

English: And you shall take in your hand this rod, with which you shall do the signs.”
Kokborok:
Phiya nwng nini yago o raidangno na di, omo bai khe nwng uanamasingcha samung tangnai.”
Musi ha Ijibo kiphilwi phaimani

English: Moses went back to Jethro his father-in-law and said to him, “Let me go back, I pray, to my kinsmen in Egypt and see whether they are still alive.” And Jethro said to Moses, “Go in peace.”
Kokborok: Musi bini bwkra Jethroni thani kiphilwi thángwi hai hwnwi saui kha, “Ano ani jaiti masarok tongma Ijibo kisa thángna bagwi rohor gra di, bórok kwthang da tong bai kho kisa naiwi phai grana.” Hakhe Jethro Musino “Kwchang kwchang khe tháng di hwnwi sa kha.”

English: And the Lord said to Moses in Midian, “Go back to Egypt; for all the men who were seeking your life are dead.”
Kokborok: Midiano tháng phuru-o, Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijibo kiphilwi tháng di. Nono butharna nainai bororok bebakno thwi bai kha.”

English: So Moses took his wife and his sons and set them on an ass, and went back to the land of Egypt; and in his hand Moses took the rod of God.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi, bihik tei bwsajlasongno korai chirka saka-o kasa rwui lam rw kha tei Ijibo kiphilwi tháng kha. Musi bini yago a Kaitorni raidangno tẃi kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “When you go back to Egypt, see that you do before Pharaoh all the miracles which I have put in your power; but I will harden his heart, so that he will not let the people go.
Kokborok: Haino hai Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng Ijibo thángwi sógwi khe, ang nini thani rwmani a suarrok bai Pharaoni bwskango bebak a uanamasingcha samungrokno khlai di. Tamo khe bororokno yakarwi rohor ya khlaina bagwi ang Pharaoni bwkhano kwrak khlainai.

English: And you shall say to Pharaoh, ‘Thus says the Lord, Israel is my first-born son,
Kokborok: Hakhe nwng Pharaono saui di, Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o, ‘Israil wng kha ani swkang sakra,

English: and I say to you, “Let my son go that he may serve me”; if you refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay your first-born son.’ ”
Kokborok: ang nono sa-o, “Ano surina bagwi ani sajlano rwi rohor di;” Nwng bono rwi rohor ya khe, Ang nini sajla okrano butharnai.’ ”

English: At a lodging place on the way the Lord met him and sought to kill him.
Kokborok: Ijibo thángma lama-o kwrẃngsa-o bathak thani Musi Subraiyung bai malai khe Subraiyung bono butharna nai kha.

English: Then Zipporah took a flint and cut off her son's foreskin, and touched Moses' feet with it, and said, “Surely you are a bridegroom of blood to me!”
Kokborok: Hakhe Jipora holong kubuk kaisa naui bwsajlani sakur raui rw kha tei Musini yakungo tangwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng thẃini se ani bwsai!”

English: So he let him alone. Then it was that she said, “You are a bridegroom of blood,” because of the circumcision.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bono saichung khe yakarwi klang kha: Hakhe bihik hai hwnwi sa kha, sakur ramani tẃi khe “Nwng thẃini se ani bwsai.”

English: The Lord said to Aaron, “Go into the wilderness to meet Moses.” So he went, and met him at the mountain of God and kissed him.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Aronno hai hwnwi dagi kha, “Musi bai malaina bagwi haphar bukcha-o tháng di. ‘Hakhe bo thángwi Kaitorni hathái-o bo bai malaiwi bono motom su kha.’ ”

English: And Moses told Aaron all the words of the Lord with which he had sent him, and all the signs which he had charged him to do.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi, Subraiyung tamni bagwi bono rohor kha bebakno sa kha tei bebak uanamasingcha samung khlai di hwnwi sajakmaroknobo sa kha.

English: Then Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the people of Israel.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bai Aron Israilni bebak chakrarokno kwrẃngsa khe thum kha.

English: And Aaron spoke all the words which the Lord had spoken to Moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.
Kokborok: Musi Subraiyung bai sajakma bebak kókrokno Aronno saui khna rw kha tei bororokni bwskango uanamasingcha samungrokno tangwi phunuk kha.

English: And the people believed; and when they heard that the Lord had visited the people of Israel and that he had seen their affliction, they bowed their heads and worshiped.
Kokborok: Hakhe bororok poito khlai lai kha tei Israilrokno Subraiyung naikhlai phi kha tei sátokjakmarok nuk lang kha hwnwi khna kheno bórok khorok bomson lai khulum lai kha.

English: Afterward Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘Let my people go, that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.’ ”
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Musi bai Aron Pharaoni thani thángwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Israilni Subraiyung Kaitor hai hwnwi sa-o, ‘Ani bororokno rwi rohor di, bórok haphar bukcha-o thángwi ano bajuna bagwi chámung panda khlainai.’ ”

English: But Pharaoh said, “Who is the Lord, that I should heed his voice and let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and moreover I will not let Israel go.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung sabo, ang bini kók khnana nangnai tei Israilrokno bo rwi rohorna nangnai? Ang Subraiyungno sini ya tei Israilrokno thángnani rw ya.”

English: Then they said, “The God of the Hebrews has met with us; let us go, we pray, a three days' journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to the Lord our God, lest he fall upon us with pestilence or with the sword.”
Kokborok: Musi bai Aron hai hwnwi sa kha, “Hibrurokni Kaitor chwng bai malai kha, chwng nono koi ja-o salthamni lama-o thángwi haphar bukcha-o khe chini Subraiyung Kaitorno ratharwi barokna bagwi tháng rw ja di, wng ya khe bo chwngno bemuk hamya bai eba choba bai khe butharnai.”

English: But the king of Egypt said to them, “Moses and Aaron, why do you take the people away from their work? Get to your burdens.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Ijipni habugra bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Musi bai Aron tamo norok bororokno bórokni samungni tlangna nai lai? Selengrokno samungo rwi bai di.”

English: And Pharaoh said, “Behold, the people of the land are now many and you make them rest from their burdens!”
Kokborok: Pharao sa kha, nai di, norok tabuk báng lai kha, hakhe samungni yakarwi thángna nai lai-o.

English: The same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people and their foremen,
Kokborok: A salo Pharao bororokno mula tang rwnai bokhorokrokno tei haba sonairokno dagiui hai hwnwi sa kha:

English: “You shall no longer give the people straw to make bricks, as heretofore; let them go and gather straw for themselves.
Kokborok: “Tabukni simi norok bórokno it takna bagwi chwkap ta rw si di; bórokno sak sak thángwi bórokni bagwi chwkap thum lai thung.

English: But the number of bricks which they made heretofore you shall lay upon them, you shall by no means lessen it; for they are idle; therefore they cry, ‘Let us go and offer sacrifice to our God.’
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok swkango takma swkno tabukbo ma taknai hwnwi saui rw di, nwng kisabo khái klaiwi man glak. Bórok seler gwja; aboni bagwino bórok ‘Chwngno chini Kaitorno ratharwi barokna thángna rw di’ hwnwi chirik lai-o.

English: Let heavier work be laid upon the men that they may labor at it and pay no regard to lying words.”
Kokborok: Bórokno teibo tang rwna bagwi samung kwrak boksinwi rw di tei bórok tatal samarokno khunju ta kái di.”

English: So the taskmasters and the foremen of the people went out and said to the people, “Thus says Pharaoh, ‘I will not give you straw.
Kokborok: Hakhe samung tang rwnai bokhorokrok tei haba sonairok thángwi bórokno hai hwnwi saui kha, “Pharao hai hwnwi sa-o, ‘Ang norokno chwkap rw glak.

English: Go yourselves, get your straw wherever you can find it; but your work will not be lessened in the least.’ ”
Kokborok: Norokno buro mannai norokni nangma chwkaprok naituk lai tubu di, hai phano norokni samungrok le khái klaijak glak.’ ”

English: So the people were scattered abroad throughout all the land of Egypt, to gather stubble for straw.
Kokborok: Hakhe bororok chwkap thumna bagwi Ijip ha gwnangwi pírjagwi tháng lai kha.

English: The taskmasters were urgent, saying, “Complete your work, your daily task, as when there was straw.”
Kokborok: Samung tang rwnai bokhorokrok belai khe nangjagwi hai hwnwi dagiui sa kha, “Norok doui’-no samung ma páinai, salsani samung salsa’-ono; swkango chwkap tong phuru pái tẃi kheno ma páinai.”

English: And the foremen of the people of Israel, whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, and were asked, “Why have you not done all your task of making bricks today, as hitherto?”
Kokborok: Tei Israilni haba sonai barja berjakrokno, Ijipni samungo samung tang rwnai bokhorokrok bórokno bú lai kha. “Tamo swkango it tak laima swk tabuk tak lai ya?” hwnwi kul lai kha.

English: Then the foremen of the people of Israel came and cried to Pharaoh, “Why do you deal thus with your servants?
Kokborok: Hakhe Israilni haba sonairok Pharaoni thani phaiwi hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Nwng tamo nini selengrok bai ahai samung khlai lang?

English: No straw is given to your servants, yet they say to us, ‘Make bricks!’ And behold, your servants are beaten; but the fault is in your own people.”
Kokborok: Nini selengrokno chwkap se rwjak ya, hai phano bórok chwngno it tak lai di hwnwi sa-o. ‘Nai di, nini selengrok bujak bai kha; tamo khe cha ya le nini sakni bororok se cha ya.’ ”

English: But he said, “You are idle, you are idle; therefore you say, ‘Let us go and sacrifice to the Lord.’
Kokborok: Tamo khe habugra hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok seler gwja samung tangna nai lai ya; abo bai’-no norok, ‘Tho him di,’ Subraiyungni thani ratharwi barok lainai hwnwi sa lai-o.

English: Go now, and work; for no straw shall be given you, yet you shall deliver the same number of bricks.”
Kokborok: Tháng di samung tang lai di, norokno chwkap rwjak glak, hai phano swkango takma swkno it ma tak lainai.”

English: The foremen of the people of Israel saw that they were in evil plight, when they said, “You shall by no means lessen your daily number of bricks.”
Kokborok: Israilni haba daginairok nuk kha, bórok bejua-o klai laijak, tamni hwn khe, “Norok sal brúm it takmani slái kisabo khái klai man glak,” hwnwi sajak kha.

English: They met Moses and Aaron, who were waiting for them, as they came forth from Pharaoh;
Kokborok: Bórok Pharaoni aroni ongkhorwi phai khe naising lai tongnai Musi bai Aronno hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Subraiyung norok tamo samung khlai kha nuk kha.

English: and they said to them, “The Lord look upon you and judge, because you have made us offensive in the sight of Pharaoh and his servants, and have put a sword in their hand to kill us.”
Kokborok: Habugra bai bini tẃiphangrokni thani chwngno naisler rwna bagwi Subraiyung nono majra rw thung. Chwngno butharna bagwi bórokni yago seng se yapharwi rw si-o.”

English: Then Moses turned again to the Lord and said, “O Lord, why hast thou done evil to this people? Why didst thou ever send me?
Kokborok: Musi Subraiyungni thani uphilwi hai hwnwi sa phi kha, “O Subraiyung nwng tamo bororokni hamya khlai lang? Nwng tamo wngwi ano rwi rohor?

English: For since I came to Pharaoh to speak in thy name, he has done evil to this people, and thou hast not delivered thy people at all.”
Kokborok: Nini mungwi ang Pharaoni thani sana phai phuruni simi bo o bororokni hamya khlai kha tei nwng nini bororokno phi se phiyók ya.”

English: But the Lord said to Moses, “Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh; for with a strong hand he will send them out, yea, with a strong hand he will drive them out of his land.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tabuk ang Pharaono tamo ma khlai nwng nuknai; bo bini phankrak yak bai khe bórokno bini hani tikhlaiwi rohornai.”

English: And God said to Moses, “I am the Lord.
Kokborok: Kaitor Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang se Subraiyung.

English: I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, as God Almighty, but by my name the Lord I did not make myself known to them.
Kokborok: Ang Abraham bai nukjak kha, Isak tei Jakobni thani Kwplaisa Kaitor wngwi nukjak kha, tamo khe ang bórokni thani Subraiyung kwtharno bórokno saui man rw li ya.

English: I also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land in which they dwelt as sojourners.
Kokborok: Ang Kanan hano bórokno rwna hwnwi swmai tangjak. Bórok a ha-o buini borok wngwi se tong lai-o.

English: Moreover I have heard the groaning of the people of Israel whom the Egyptians hold in bondage and I have remembered my covenant.
Kokborok: Aboni bere ang Israil bororokni kengai laimano khna kha, Ijipsong bórokno seleng snamwi khaui ton kha tei ang ani swmainobo muitu wng kha.

English: Say therefore to the people of Israel, ‘I am the Lord, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will deliver you from their bondage, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with great acts of judgment,
Kokborok: Abo bai’-no Israilrokno hai hwnwi sa di, ‘Ang Subraiyung, norokno Ijiprokni yago seleng wngwi tongmani yakni phiyóknai tei yak kwrak bai khe norokno khengwi rwnai tei bórokno kwrak khe yagul sunai.

English: and I will take you for my people, and I will be your God; and you shall know that I am the Lord your God, who has brought you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.
Kokborok: Ang norokno ani borok snamnai tei ang norokni Kaitor wngwi Ijipni yago seleng wngwi tongmani yakni phiyóknai, ang norokni Subraiyung Kaitor hwnwi norok sininai.

English: And I will bring you into the land which I swore to give to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; I will give it to you for a possession. I am the Lord.’ ”
Kokborok: Ang Abrahamno, Isakno tei Jakobno rwna hwnmani ha-o tubunai. Ang a hano norokni’-no khlainai. Ang’-no Subraiyung.’ ”

English: Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel; but they did not listen to Moses, because of their broken spirit and their cruel bondage.
Kokborok: Musi o kókrokno Israil bororokno sa kha; hai phano bórok Musini kókno khunju kan li ya. Bórokni bwkha báimani bai tei belai khe khairokmasingcha khe sekhrupjakma bai se abo hai wngo.

English: And the Lord said to Moses,
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijipni bugra Pharaono thángwi saui di,

English: “Go in, tell Pharaoh king of Egypt to let the people of Israel go out of his land.”
Kokborok: Israilni bororokno nini hani rwi rohor di.”

English: But Moses said to the Lord, “Behold, the people of Israel have not listened to me; how then shall Pharaoh listen to me, who am a man of uncircumcised lips?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi Subraiyungno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, Israil bororok se ani kókno khna gra ya, bahai khe Pharao ang tẃi khoteni kókno khnanai?”

English: But the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, and gave them a charge to the people of Israel and to Pharaoh king of Egypt to bring the people of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
Kokborok:
Subraiyung Musi bai Aronno hai hwnwi dagiui sa kha, “Israil bai Ijipni habugrano sa di, Israilrokno ha Ijipni tikhlai tubu di hwnwi ang sa-o.”
Musi bai Aronni núkhungni bororok

English: These are the heads of their fathers' houses: the sons of Reuben, the first-born of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi; these are the families of Reuben.
Kokborok: Omorokno wng kha bórokni bokhorokrokni mungrok; Israilni bwsa Rubenni bwsarok wng kha, Hanok, Palu, Hesron tei Karmi. Omorokno Rubenni nokni bororok.

English: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman; these are the families of Simeon.
Kokborok: Simionni bwsarok khe Jemuel, Jakimni, Ohad, Jakhin, Johar tei Sahul, Kananjwkni bwsarok; Omorokno Simionni bororok.

English: These are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari, the years of the life of Levi being a hundred and thirty-seven years.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha, bosong mareui Lebini bwsarokni mungrok; Gersom, Kohat tei Merari, Lebi kwthang tong langma umor wng kha, bisi sarathamchisni;

English: The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shime-i, by their families.
Kokborok: Gersomni bwsarok bórokni núkhungno rwgwi Libni tei Simei.

English: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, the years of the life of Kohath being a hundred and thirty-three years.
Kokborok: Kohatni bwsarok; Amram, Ijar, Hebron tei Ujiel, Kohat thangwi tong langmani umor bisik sarathamchitham.

English: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their generations.
Kokborok: Merarini bwsarok; Mahali tei Musi. Bosong rwgwi omorokno Lebini núkhungni bororok.

English: Amram took to wife Jochebed his father's sister and she bore him Aaron and Moses, the years of the life of Amram being one hundred and thirty-seven years.
Kokborok: Amram buphani bukhuk Jokebedno káijak kha hakhe bo Musi bai Aronno bá kha, Amram nokha bisik sara tei thamchisni thangwi tong lang kha.

English: The sons of Izhar: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
Kokborok: Ijarni bwsarok khe, Kora, Nepheg tei Jikhri.

English: And the sons of Uzziel: Misha-el, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
Kokborok: Ujielni bwsarok khe; Misael, Eljaphan tei Sithri.

English: Aaron took to wife Elisheba, the daughter of Amminadab and the sister of Nahshon; and she bore him Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
Kokborok: Aron Eliseba bai káijago, Eliseba wng kha Aminadabni bwsajwk tei Nasonni bukhuk. Bo Nadab Abihu, Eliasar tei Ithamarno bá kha.

English: The sons of Korah: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph; these are the families of the Korahites.
Kokborok: Korani bwsarok khe Asir, Elkana tei Abiasap; Omorokno Korani núkhungni bororok.

English: Eleazar, Aaron's son, took to wife one of the daughters of Puti-el; and she bore him Phinehas. These are the heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites by their families.
Kokborok: Aronni bwsla Eliasar Putielni bwsajwkno káijak kha. Bo Phinehasno bá kha. Omorokno Lebini bosong núkhungni

English: These are the Aaron and Moses to whom the Lord said: “Bring out the people of Israel from the land of Egypt by their hosts.”
Kokborok: Aron bai Musino’-no Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o; “Israil bororokno ha Ijipni tikhlai tubu-ui phai di.”

English: It was they who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt about bringing out the people of Israel from Egypt, this Moses and this Aaron.
Kokborok: Bórokno Ijipni habugra Pharaono Israilni bororokno Ijip hani tikhlaina kókno sa-o, o Musi bai Aron’-no a sanairok.

English: On the day when the Lord spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,
Kokborok: Subraiyung Ijip ha-o Musino kók sa phuru

English: the Lord said to Moses, “I am the Lord; tell Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I say to you.”
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang se Subraiyung; Ijipni habugra Pharaono ang nono samarokno saui di.”

English: But Moses said to the Lord, “Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips; how then shall Pharaoh listen to me?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi Subraiyungno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng saui mano ang kók rwng tha ya bahai khe habugra ani kókrokno khunju kainai?”

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “See, I make you as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang nono Pharaoni thani Kaitor hai snamnai. Nini takhuk Aron khe nini diyari wngnai.

English: You shall speak all that I command you; and Aaron your brother shall tell Pharaoh to let the people of Israel go out of his land.
Kokborok: Ang dagima hai khe nwng kók sa di. Nini takhuk Aron khe Israil bororokno yakarwi rohor di hwnwi Pharaono sanai.

English: But I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and though I multiply my signs and wonders in the land of Egypt,
Kokborok: Ang Pharaoni bwkhano rak rwnai, ha Ijibo kwbángma marirok kwmamsingcha samungrok phunuk phano Pharao nini kókno khna glak.

English: Pharaoh will not listen to you; then I will lay my hand upon Egypt and bring forth my hosts, my people the sons of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great acts of judgment.
Kokborok: Hakhe ang Ijipni saka-o ani yak beranai tei ani bororokno Ijipni tikhlai tubunai.

English: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I stretch forth my hand upon Egypt and bring out the people of Israel from among them.”
Kokborok: Ang Ijipsarokni saka-o yak rw phuru tei bórokni bisingni Israilrokno tikhlai tubu phuru khe, ang’-no Subraiyung hwnwi Ijipsarok saui man anw.”

English: And Moses and Aaron did so; they did as the Lord commanded them.
Kokborok: Musi bai Aronbo abo haino khlai kha; bórok Subraiyung bó rokno dagima tẃi khlai lai kha.

English: Now Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three years old, when they spoke to Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Pharaoni thani kók sa phuru Musini umor bisi charchi wng kha tei Aron khe bisi charchitham wng kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musi bai Aronno hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “When Pharaoh says to you, ‘Prove yourselves by working a miracle,’ then you shall say to Aaron, ‘Take your rod and cast it down before Pharaoh, that it may become a serpent.’ ”
Kokborok: “Norok kwmamsingcha samung tangwi sóino hwnwi phunuk di, hwnwi Pharao norokno sa phuru khe, hakhe nwng Aronno, ‘Nini raidang naui chibuk kha rwna bagwi ha-o khiklai rw di’ hwnwi sa di.’ ”

English: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and did as the Lord commanded; Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh and his servants, and it became a serpent.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bai Aron Pharaoni thani thángwi Subraiyung sama tẃi’-no khlai kha; Aron bini raidangno Pharao bai bini bororokni bwskango ha-o khiklai rw kha tei abo chibuk khaui tháng kha.

English: Then Pharaoh summoned the wise men and the sorcerers; and they also, the magicians of Egypt, did the same by their secret arts.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Pharao Ijipni bini siyan gwnangrokno tei swkal kwrwngrokno ring kha; bórokbo bórokni rwngmung bai abo hai’-no khlai kha.

English: For every man cast down his rod, and they became serpents. But Aaron's rod swallowed up their rods.
Kokborok: Bebak bororokno bórokni raidangno ha-o chibuk snam lai kha tei abórok chibuk khaui tháng kha. Tamo khe Aronni raidang khe bórokni raidangno monogwi khibi kha.

English: Still Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them; as the Lord had said.
Kokborok: Hai phano Pharaoni bwkha kwrak wngwi tong kha tei bo Musi bai Aronni kók khna li ya; abo Subraiyung sama haino wng kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Pharaoh's heart is hardened, he refuses to let the people go.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Pharaoni bwkha kwrak khlai kha, bo bororokno thángna rw li ya.

English: Go to Pharaoh in the morning, as he is going out to the water; wait for him by the river's brink, and take in your hand the rod which was turned into a serpent.
Kokborok: Pharao phung aidrobo twima-o tháng phuru bo bai thángwi malai di; twi rukungo khe bono naisingwi tong di tei chibuk khanai a raidangno yago tẃiwi tháng di.”

English: And you shall say to him, ‘The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, sent me to you, saying, “Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness; and behold, you have not yet obeyed.”
Kokborok: Hakhe nwng bono hai hwnwi sa di, ‘Subraiyung, Hibrurokni Kaitor, ano hai hwnwi saui nini thani rohoro, ani bororok haphar bukcha-o thángwi ano suriui manna tẃi khe ani bororokno rwi rohor di; nai di nwng tabukbo khnana nai ya wngwi tongo.’ ”

English: Thus says the Lord, “By this you shall know that I am the Lord: behold, I will strike the water that is in the Nile with the rod that is in my hand, and it shall be turned to blood,
Kokborok: Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o, “Omo hai khe nwng ano Subraiyung hwnwi sinai; ‘Nai di, ang ani yago tongmani raidang bai Nil twima-o swpraknai, a twi thẃi khaui thángnai,

English: and the fish in the Nile shall die, and the Nile shall become foul, and the Egyptians will loathe to drink water from the Nile.” ’ ”
Kokborok: árok thwinai, Nil twimani twi mwnamwi thángnai, Ijipni bororok oroni twino nungwi man glak.’ ”

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Say to Aaron, ‘Take your rod and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, their canals, and their ponds, and all their pools of water, that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.’ ”
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aronno dagi di, ‘Nwng nini raidangno yago na di tei nwng nini yakno Ijipni twi phaising pholok di, twimarokni bwkhak, twisarokni tei pukhirirokni, twidurokni bwkhak thẃi kha rwna bagwi pholok di;’ Ijipni ha gwnangwi’-no thẃi máng wngnai, buphangni eba holongni twi tonma manwirokni twirokbo thẃi kha bainai.”

English: Moses and Aaron did as the Lord commanded; in the sight of Pharaoh and in the sight of his servants, he lifted up the rod and struck the water that was in the Nile, and all the water that was in the Nile turned to blood.
Kokborok: Musi bai Aron Subraiyung dagima haino khlai kha; Pharaoni mokol bwskango tei bini selengrokni mokol bwskango bo bini raidangno tisa kha tei Nil twima-o swprak kha tei Nil twimani bebak twirok thẃi khaui tháng kha.

English: And the fish in the Nile died; and the Nile became foul, so that the Egyptians could not drink water from the Nile; and there was blood throughout all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Nil twimani árok thwi kha, Nil twima mwnam kha, hakhe Ijipsarok Nil twimani twi nungwi man li ya tei Ijipni ha gwnangwi’-no thẃi máng wng kha.

English: But the magicians of Egypt did the same by their secret arts; so Pharaoh's heart remained hardened, and he would not listen to them; as the Lord had said.
Kokborok: Ijipni danda-baji kwrwngrokbo bórokni kókmung bai abo haino khlai lai kha; hakhe Pharaoni bwkha ragwino tong kha, bo bórokni kókrokno khna li ya; Subraiyung sama haino Musi bai Aronni kókrok khna li ya.

English: Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he did not lay even this to heart.
Kokborok: Pharao kiphilwi bini nogo thángwi pái kha. Bo omorokno kisa phano bwkha-o nangjak ya.

English: And all the Egyptians dug round about the Nile for water to drink, for they could not drink the water of the Nile.
Kokborok: Nil twimani twi nungwi man ya wngwi Ijipsarok Nil twimani yang yang hakhor khur lai kha.

English: Seven days passed after the Lord had struck the Nile.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Nil twimano swprakma yagulo salsni lái kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go in to Pharaoh and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord, “Let my people go, that they may serve me.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Pharaono thángwi saui di, Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o, ‘Ano monna bagwi ani bororokno yakarwi rohor bai di.’

English: But if you refuse to let them go, behold, I will plague all your country with frogs;
Kokborok: Phiya nwng bórokno yakarwi rohor ya khe, nai di ang nini ha gwnangwi’-no yongla máng kupulung khe rwnai,

English: the Nile shall swarm with frogs which shall come up into your house, and into your bedchamber and on your bed, and into the houses of your servants and of your people, and into your ovens and your kneading bowls;
Kokborok: Nil twima yongla bai kupulung wngnai, abórok nini nogo thángnai nini thuma nogo thángnai, thuyamo kasauinai, nini selengrokni nokrogo tei nini bororokni thanibo thángnai thaparogo hapnai, kárairogo kasauinai;

English: the frogs shall come up on you and on your people and on all your servants.” ’ ”
Kokborok: yonglarok nini thani nini bororokni tei nini selengrokni thani phainai.’ ”

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Say to Aaron, ‘Stretch out your hand with your rod over the rivers, over the canals, and over the pools, and cause frogs to come upon the land of Egypt!’ ”
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha. “Aronno sa di, bo bini raidangno twimarok, twisarok tei twidurok phaising phologwi yonglarokno ha Ijibo phai rw si thung.”

English: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe Aron bini raidangno Ijipni bebak twi phaisingo tisaui pholok hor kha tei yonglarok phaiwi bebak ha Ijibo kupulung wng phai kha.

English: But the magicians did the same by their secret arts, and brought frogs upon the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Tei danda-baji kwrwngrokbo bórokni kókmung bai khe yongla snamwi ha-o yonglarok tubu-ui phai kha.

English: Then Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron, and said, “Entreat the Lord to take away the frogs from me and from my people; and I will let the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Musi bai Aronno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Yonglarokno ani oroni bororokni aroni tlang di hwnwi Subraiyungno suri si di; ang bororokno Subraiyungni thani ratharwi yapharna bagwi yakarwi rohor sinai.”

English: Moses said to Pharaoh, “Be pleased to command me when I am to entreat, for you and for your servants and for your people, that the frogs be destroyed from you and your houses and be left only in the Nile.”
Kokborok: Musi Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang surina hwn khele chajago tamo khe chong si di, buphuru ang nini nokni, nini nokni bororokni tei nini selengrokni aroni yonglarok komor tháng si thung hwnwi surinai, phiya Nil twima-o simi se tong rwknai.”

English: And he said, “Tomorrow.” Moses said, “Be it as you say, that you may know that there is no one like the Lord our God.
Kokborok:
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Khwna’-no suri si di;”
Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chini Subraiyung Kaitor hai tei khoroksabo kwrwi hwnwi sina bagwi nwng sama tẃi’-no wng si thung.

English: The frogs shall depart from you and your houses and your servants and your people; they shall be left only in the Nile.”
Kokborok: Nwng nini bororok tei nini selengrok yonglani yakni yók bainai. Nil twima-o karwi tei kwrẃngsa-obo tong rwk glak.”

English: So Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh; and Moses cried to the Lord concerning the frogs, as he had agreed with Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bai Aron Pharaoni aroni tháng lai kha, Pharaoni thani lonwi Phaima hai kheno Musi Subraiyungni thani yonglarokno tlang phi si di hwnwi suri kha.

English: And the Lord did according to the word of Moses; the frogs died out of the houses and courtyards and out of the fields.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musi sama haino khlai kha, nogo yongla tongmarok, nokhla tongmarok, hapharo tongmarok thwi bai kha.

English: And they gathered them together in heaps, and the land stank.
Kokborok: Bórok bebakno kwrẃngsa-o khe rúng lai kha tei ha gwnangwi mwnam kha.

English: But when Pharaoh saw that there was a respite, he hardened his heart, and would not listen to them; as the Lord had said.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Pharao a yonglarokno thwima nuk khe bo bini bwkha rak rw phi khua, bo bórokni kókno khna li ya, abo Subraiyung sama tẃi’-no wng kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Say to Aaron, ‘Stretch out your rod and strike the dust of the earth, that it may become gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.’ ”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aronno sa di, ‘Nini a raidangno tikhlaiwi pholok di tei ha Ijip gwnangwi’-no yóngsa kwsaprok wngna bagwi hadlwi-o swprak di.’ ”

English: And they did so; Aaron stretched out his hand with his rod, and struck the dust of the earth, and there came gnats on man and beast; all the dust of the earth became gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Bórok haino khlai kha; Aron bini yak phologwi raidangno tisaui hadlwi-o khe swprak kha tei aro ha gwnangwi bórokni thani tei mal-matarokni saka-o sók phai kha; ha gwnangwini hadlwi yóngkha kwsap khaui ha Ijipno kupulung khlai kha.

English: The magicians tried by their secret arts to bring forth gnats, but they could not. So there were gnats on man and beast.
Kokborok: Danda-baji phunuknairok bórokni kókthái bai abo hai yóngkha kwsap snamna nai lai kha, tamo khe bórok man lai li ya. Hakhe bororokni saka-o tei mal-matarokni saka-o yóngkha máng wng kha.

English: And the magicians said to Pharaoh, “This is the finger of God.” But Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them; as the Lord had said.
Kokborok: Hakhe a danda-baji kwrwngrok, Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo Kaitorni yasi se.” Tamo khe Pharaoni bwkha kwrak wng kha, Subraiyung sama haino bo bórokni kókno khna li ya.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Rise up early in the morning and wait for Pharaoh, as he goes out to the water, and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord, “Let my people go, that they may serve me.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Phung aidrop bachaui Pharaono naisingwi tong di, bo twi bwkhak tháng phuru khe bono sa di, Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o, ano surina bagwi ani bororokno yakarwi rohor di.

English: Else, if you will not let my people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies on you and your servants and your people, and into your houses; and the houses of the Egyptians shall be filled with swarms of flies, and also the ground on which they stand.
Kokborok: Eba nwng ani bororokno yakarwi rohor ya hwn khe, nai di, ang nini thani nini selengrokni thani nini bororokni thani tei nini nokrogo thampui bodol rwi rohornai. Hakhe Ijipsarokni nok brúmno thampui kupulung wngnai tei bórok bachama-obo haino kupulung wngnai.

English: But on that day I will set apart the land of Goshen, where my people dwell, so that no swarms of flies shall be there; that you may know that I am the Lord in the midst of the earth.
Kokborok: ‘Tamo khe a salo ang ani bororok tongma ha Gusenno khagwi tonnai, hakhe aro thampuini bodol tong glak; ha saka-o ang’-no Kaitor hwnwi omo hai khe nwng saui mannai.

English: Thus I will put a division between my people and your people. By tomorrow shall this sign be.” ’ ”
Kokborok: Omo hai khe ang ani bororok bai nini bororokno swkaknai khwnani bisingo’-no omo hai mari nuknai.’ ”

English: And the Lord did so; there came great swarms of flies into the house of Pharaoh and into his servants' houses, and in all the land of Egypt the land was ruined by reason of the flies.
Kokborok: Subraiyung omo hai’-no khlai kha, Pharaoni nogo tei bini selengrokni nogo tei bebak Ijip ha-o bodol kotor khe thampui hap rw kha. A thampuirokni tekto bai lengpro bai kha.

English: Then Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron, and said, “Go, sacrifice to your God within the land.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Musi bai Aronno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di, o ani ha-ono norokni Kaitorni thani ratharwi barogwi di.”

English: But Moses said, “It would not be right to do so; for we shall sacrifice to the Lord our God offerings abominable to the Egyptians. If we sacrifice offerings abominable to the Egyptians before their eyes, will they not stone us?
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Abo hai khlaima le cha glak, tamni hwn khe chwng ratharwi Subraiyung Kaitorno yaphar khe Ijipsarok abono selengjaknai, chwng Ijipsarokni mokol bwskango bórok chajak yano ratharwi barok khe bórok chwngno holong bai khitarwi buthar glak diba?

English: We must go three days' journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the Lord our God as he will command us.”
Kokborok: Chwng bo bai chajakma hai kheno salthamni lama thángwi haphar bukcha-o ratharwi ma yapharwinai.”

English: So Pharaoh said, “I will let you go, to sacrifice to the Lord your God in the wilderness; only you shall not go very far away. Make entreaty for me.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang norokno haphar bukcha-o thángwi ratharwi yapharna bagwi rwi rohornai, tamo khe norok belai hakchalo le ta tháng di. Ani bagwi suri di.”

English: Then Moses said, “Behold, I am going out from you and I will pray to the Lord that the swarms of flies may depart from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, tomorrow; only let not Pharaoh deal falsely again by not letting the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.”
Kokborok: Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ang nini oroni ongkhorwi thángo tei ang Subraiyungni thani surinai, Pharaoni aroni bini selengrokni aroni tei bororokni aroni khwna’-ono thampui bodolrok tháng thung hwnwi surinai. Tamo khe Pharao haino hai tatal saui bororokni ratharwi yapharna bagwi rwi rohor yaui ta tong thung.”

English: So Moses went out from Pharaoh and prayed to the Lord.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Pharaoni aroni thángwi Subraiyungni thani suri san kha.

English: And the Lord did as Moses asked, and removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people; not one remained.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musi sama tẃi’-no khlai kha tei thampui bodolrokno Pharaoni aroni bini selengrokni aroni, bini bororokni aroni nor kha. Masa phano kwrwi kha.

English: But Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also, and did not let the people go.
Kokborok: Tamo khe tabukbo Pharao bini bwkhano kwrak khlai tong kha tei bororokno thángnani rw li ya.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go in to Pharaoh, and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord, the God of the Hebrews, “Let my people go, that they may serve me.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Pharaoni thani thángwi saui di, ‘Hibrurokni Kaitor, Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o, “Ani bororok Ano monwi manna tẃi khe bórokno yakarwi rohor di.

English: For if you refuse to let them go and still hold them,
Kokborok: Tumungno nwng tabukbo rwi rohorna soi ya wngwi, romwi narwk khe,

English: behold, the hand of the Lord will fall with a very severe plague upon your cattle which are in the field, the horses, the asses, the camels, the herds, and the flocks.
Kokborok: nai di, Subraiyungni yak harungo tongma nini mal-matarokni saka-o kutung khiklainai, korairok, korai chirkarok, mangingangrok, merarok, punrokni saka-o klainai.

English: But the Lord will make a distinction between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt, so that nothing shall die of all that belongs to the people of Israel.” ’ ”
Kokborok: Tamo khe, Subraiyung Israilrokni mal-mata bai Ijipsarokni mal-matano khagwi tonnai, hakhe Israilni mungsa mal-matarok thwi glak.” ’ ”

English: And the Lord set a time, saying, “Tomorrow the Lord will do this thing in the land.”
Kokborok: Subraiyung a jorano chongwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung khwna-o o ha-o omo hai kheno khlainai.”

English: And on the morrow the Lord did this thing; all the cattle of the Egyptians died, but of the cattle of the people of Israel not one died.
Kokborok: Hakhe teini salo Subraiyung sama tẃi’-no khlai kha, Ijipsarokni bebak mal-matarok thwi kha, tamo khe Israilni khe masa phano thwi li ya.

English: And Pharaoh sent, and behold, not one of the cattle of the Israelites was dead. But the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people go.
Kokborok: Habugra bahai tamo wng kha hwnwi sẃng khe Israilni mal-matarok masa phano thwi ya hwnwi sajak kha. Tamo khe bo bwkha kwrak wng kha tei bo bororokno thángna rw li ya.

English: And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron, “Take handfuls of ashes from the kiln, and let Moses throw them toward heaven in the sight of Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Phiya Subraiyung Musi bai Aronno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Thapani mochomsa thapla na di tei abono Musi Pharaoni mokol bwskango a thaplano nokha bwkhak sarwi rw di.

English: And it shall become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and become boils breaking out in sores on man and beast throughout all the land of Egypt.”
Kokborok: Abo hadlwi kwsap hai khe Ijipni ha gwnangwi’-no pírjaknai tei abo ha Ijipni gwnangwi’-no bororokni sago tei mal-matarokni sago kula kotor tháiwi pekhogwi kwsa wngnai.”

English: So they took ashes from the kiln, and stood before Pharaoh, and Moses threw them toward heaven, and it became boils breaking out in sores on man and beast.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok thapla kisa na kha tei Pharaoni bwskango bachaui kha, hakhe Musi nokha bwkhak sarwi rw kha, abo bororok bai mal-matarokni sago kula kotor thái rwui koproi rw kha.

English: And the magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils, for the boils were upon the magicians and upon all the Egyptians.
Kokborok: Danda-baji kwrwngrok kulani bagwi Musini bwskango bachaui man li ya, Danda-baji kwrwngrok tei bebak Ijipsarokni sago kula thái kha.

English: But the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he did not listen to them; as the Lord had spoken to Moses.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyung Pharaoni bwkhano rak rw kha tei bo bórokni kókrokno khna li ya; Subraiyung Musino sama haino wng kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Rise up early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh, and say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord, the God of the Hebrews, “Let my people go, that they may serve me.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Phung aidrop bachaui Pharaoni thani thángwi hai hwnwi saui di, ‘Ano Surina bagwi ani bororokno rwi rohor di,’ hwnwi Hibrurokni Subraiyung sa-o.”

English: For this time I will send all my plagues upon your heart, and upon your servants and your people, that you may know that there is none like me in all the earth.
Kokborok: Tabuk le ang nini bwkha-o tei nini selengrokni thani nini bororokni thani bebak kutungrokno rwi rohor sinai, hakhe o ha saka-o ang hai khoroksabo kwrwi hwnwi bebakno saui mannai.

English: For by now I could have put forth my hand and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would have been cut off from the earth;
Kokborok: ‘Tumungno ang yak tisaui nono tei nini bororokno kutung rohorwi swprak khe, norok komor súgwi tháng kha mun.’

English: but for this purpose have I let you live, to show you my power, so that my name may be declared throughout all the earth.
Kokborok: ‘Ang nono ani phan phunuknai tei ha gwnangwi ani mung sajakna bagwi se ang nono kwthang tono.

English: You are still exalting yourself against my people, and will not let them go.
Kokborok: Nwng tabukbo ani bororokni thani kung chugui bórokno yakarwi rwi rohor yaui tong kho.’

English: Behold, tomorrow about this time I will cause very heavy hail to fall, such as never has been in Egypt from the day it was founded until now.
Kokborok: Nai di khwna-o omo hai jora-o ang belai khe kothor klai rwnai, Ijip ha-o yaphangni simi abo hai uáisa phano wng ya kho abo tẃi se wngnai.

English: Now therefore send, get your cattle and all that you have in the field into safe shelter; for the hail shall come down upon every man and beast that is in the field and is not brought home, and they shall die.” ’ ”
Kokborok: Abo bai’-no tabuk norokni bebak mal-matarokno na rw horwi nok bisingo chop bai di. Kothor bororokni tei mal-matarokni tei tokrokni saka-o klai phainai, nogo chopjakyarok khe bebakno thwinai.”

English: Then he who feared the word of the Lord among the servants of Pharaoh made his slaves and his cattle flee into the houses;
Kokborok: Aphuru Pharaoni bororokni bere Subraiyungni kókno kirinairok selengrokno tei mal-matarokno nogo tubu-ui ton phai lai kha.

English: but he who did not regard the word of the Lord left his slaves and his cattle in the field.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyungni kókno sak nang yarok khe selengrokno tei mal-matarokno hapharo’-no ton kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Stretch forth your hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt, upon man and beast and every plant of the field, throughout the land of Egypt.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijip ha-o bororokni saka-o, mal-mata, buphang uáphang tei bebak hapharni jabra mai khulrokni saka-o Ijip ha gwnangwi kothor klai rw na bagwi nini yakno nokha saka bwkhak pholok di.”

English: Then Moses stretched forth his rod toward heaven; and the Lord sent thunder and hail, and fire ran down to the earth. And the Lord rained hail upon the land of Egypt;
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bini raidangno nokha bwkhak pholok kha; Subraiyung phirang bai kothor klai rw kha, ha saka-o hór klai phai kha. Tei Subraiyung Ijipni ha-o kothor klai rw kha.

English: there was hail, and fire flashing continually in the midst of the hail, very heavy hail, such as had never been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.
Kokborok: Kothor klai kha, kothor bising bising hór hai khe nokhabo philigo. Ijip dopha wng phuruni simi ha Ijibo abo hai belai khe kothor kw se klai ya kho.

English: The hail struck down everything that was in the field throughout all the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and the hail struck down every plant of the field, and shattered every tree of the field.
Kokborok: Ijipni bebak ha gwnangwi’-no bororokni saka-o, mal-matarokni saka-o kothor swprak kha. Hapharni bebak mai khulni saka-o tei bebak buphangrokni saka-o swprak kha, buphangrok bai kha.

English: Only in the land of Goshen, where the people of Israel were, there was no hail.
Kokborok: Israil bororok tongma ha Goseno simi kothor klai li ya.

English: Then Pharaoh sent, and called Moses and Aaron, and said to them, “I have sinned this time; the Lord is in the right, and I and my people are in the wrong.
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Musino ring rw horwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang o jora-o sumsokno khlai laha, Subraiyung le chama-o se tongo. Ang bai ani bororok se cha li ya.

English: Entreat the Lord; for there has been enough of this thunder and hail; I will let you go, and you shall stay no longer.”
Kokborok: Subraiyungno suri si di. Tabuk o kothor bai phirang kokma aswk bai’-no belai se wng kha. Ang norokno yakarwi rohor sinai tei norok kwdwk tong laina nang lai glak kha.”

English: Moses said to him, “As soon as I have gone out of the city, I will stretch out my hands to the Lord; the thunder will cease, and there will be no more hail, that you may know that the earth is the Lord's.
Kokborok: Musi bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang o kamiyungni tháng khe suriuinai. Phirang thaknai tei kothorbo klai glak, hakhe o ha Subraiyungni se hwnwi saui mannai.

English: But as for you and your servants, I know that you do not yet fear the Lord God.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe ang nono tei nini selengrokno si-o, hai phano nwng Subraiyung Kaitorno kiri glak.”

English: (The flax and the barley were ruined, for the barley was in the ear and the flax was in bud.
Kokborok: Khulrok, barlirok, komor bai kha, barli aphuru se bar kho tei khul khe nongkhordrop.

English: But the wheat and the spelt were not ruined, for they are late in coming up.)
Kokborok: Tamo khe gom bai maiswi khe ulo se munma ba abo mungsabo hamya wng li ya.

English: So Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh, and stretched out his hands to the Lord; and the thunder and the hail ceased, and the rain no longer poured upon the earth.
Kokborok: Musi Pharaoni aroni thángwi yak tisaui Subraiyungni thani suriui kha, phirang, kothor uatwi bebakno thak kha.

English: But when Pharaoh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunder had ceased, he sinned yet again, and hardened his heart, he and his servants.
Kokborok: Pharao a wngmarokno nuk kha tei haino hai sumsok khlai phi kha. Bo tei bini bororok haino hai kwrak wng lai phirwi tong kha.

English: So the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people of Israel go; as the Lord had spoken through Moses.
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung Musini khúk tẃi sama haino bo Israilrokno thángna rw li ya.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Go in to Pharaoh; for I have hardened his heart and the heart of his servants, that I may show these signs of mine among them,
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Pharaoni thani tháng di, ‘Ang bórokni thani kwmamsingcha samung khlai phunukna bagwi se bini bwkhano tei bini bororokni bwkhano rak rw kha.’

English: and that you may tell in the hearing of your son and of your son's son how I have made sport of the Egyptians and what signs I have done among them; that you may know that I am the Lord.”
Kokborok: Ano Subraiyung hwnwi norok saui manna bagwi norokni nwsarokno tei nusuksongno ang bahai khe Ijipsarokno thung kha tei kwmamsingcha samungrok khlai kha, saui khna rw thung hwnwi se omorok khlai-o.”

English: So Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and said to him, “Thus says the Lord, the God of the Hebrews, ‘How long will you refuse to humble yourself before me? Let my people go, that they may serve me.
Kokborok: Musi bai Aron Pharaoni thani thángwi hai hwnwi saui lai kha, “Hibrurokni Subraiyung Kaitor hai hwnwi sa-o, ‘Nwng tei bwswk ani thani bom yaui tong sinai? Ano surina bagwi ani bororokno yakarwi rwi rohor si di.

English: For if you refuse to let my people go, behold, tomorrow I will bring locusts into your country,
Kokborok: Tumungno nwng ani bororokno thángnani rw ya hwn khe nai di, ang khwnano nini ha saka-o kúkthorano tubunai.

English: and they shall cover the face of the land, so that no one can see the land; and they shall eat what is left to you after the hail, and they shall eat every tree of yours which grows in the field,
Kokborok: Abórok ha nukjak ya khe, ha gwnangwi’-no kobolnai kothorni yagulo tong rwkmarokno bórok cháui páinai tei hapharo wngma bebak norokni buphangrokno cháui páinai.

English: and they shall fill your houses, and the houses of all your servants and of all the Egyptians; as neither your fathers nor your grandfathers have seen, from the day they came on earth to this day.’ ” Then he turned and went out from Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Tei norokni noksing kupulung khe hap phainai norokni selengrokni nogo kupulung wngnai tei Ijipni bebak bororokni nogo kupulung wngnai; nwphasong eba nuchusong abo hai uáisabo achai phuruni simi tabukbo nuk ya kho.’ ” Hakhe bo uphilwi Pharaoni aroni tháng kha.

English: And Pharaoh's servants said to him, “How long shall this man be a snare to us? Let the men go, that they may serve the Lord their God; do you not yet understand that Egypt is ruined?”
Kokborok: Pharaoni selengrok bono hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “O borok tei bwswk chini bagwi busu hai wngwi tongnai? Bororokno bórokni Subraiyung Kaitorno surina bagwi rwi rohor si di; ha Ijip Komorwi tongmani nwng tamo saui man ya da?”

English: So Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh; and he said to them, “Go, serve the Lord your God; but who are to go?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bai Aronno Pharaoni thani tubu lai kha; Pharao bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di norokni Subraiyung Kaitorno suriui di; tamo khe sabo sabo tháng lainai?”

English: And Moses said, “We will go with our young and our old; we will go with our sons and daughters and with our flocks and herds, for we must hold a feast to the Lord.”
Kokborok: Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng chini okra chwrai bebakno tháng lainai tei mal matarok bebak tlangwi thángnai. Chwng Subraiyungni panda manjakna nangnai.”

English: And he said to them, “The Lord be with you, if ever I let you and your little ones go! Look, you have some evil purpose in mind.
Kokborok: Tei bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyungni mungwi sa-o, ang norokni bwrwirok bai chwrairokno le norok bai baksa rwi rohor ya. Omo hai khe ba norok mungsa swk ani bagwi hamya se chapma klai bla?

English: No! Go, the men among you, and serve the Lord, for that is what you desire.” And they were driven out from Pharaoh's presence.
Kokborok: Tei norok chwlarok simi thángwi Subraiyungno suri lai di, norokbo haino nai lai di ta.” Ulo khe bórok Pharaoni aroni rwi rohorjak kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come upon the land of Egypt, and eat every plant in the land, all that the hail has left.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijip ha gwnangwi, kúkthora phaiwi, kothor klai rwi klangma bebak mai khul jabrarok buphangrok chá phaina bagwi nwng nini yakno pholok di.”

English: So Moses stretched forth his rod over the land of Egypt, and the Lord brought an east wind upon the land all that day and all that night; and when it was morning the east wind had brought the locusts.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi ha Ijip phaising bini raidangno pholok kha, hakhe Subraiyung hor sal a ha saka-o salkabaini nokbar sipcha rw kha; hai khe salkabaini nokbar kúkthora tubu-ui phai kha.

English: And the locusts came up over all the land of Egypt, and settled on the whole country of Egypt, such a dense swarm of locusts as had never been before, nor ever shall be again.
Kokborok: Ijip ha gwnangwi kúkthora phaiwi sók phai kha tei Ijipni ha gwnangwi’-no tong phai kha, abo hai kúkthora bodol uáisabo aro sók phai ya kho, sobo sók phai glak.

English: For they covered the face of the whole land, so that the land was darkened, and they ate all the plants in the land and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left; not a green thing remained, neither tree nor plant of the field, through all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Bórok ha gwnangwi’-no kobol kha. Ha se ma mwnak si-o tei ha gwnangwini jabra-bobra, buphang uáphang tei kothor bwtháirok karwi klangmano bebakno cháui pái kha, kwkhrang hwn khe mungsa se kwrwi kha, Ijip hani tamo sam tamo hapharni buphangrok mungsabo kwrwi kha.

English: Then Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron in haste, and said, “I have sinned against the Lord your God, and against you.
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Musi bai Aronno doui ring rw horwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang norokni Subraiyung Kaitorni thani tei nini thani sumsok khlai kha.

English: Now therefore, forgive my sin, I pray you, only this once, and entreat the Lord your God only to remove this death from me.”
Kokborok: Abo bai’-no ano tabuk uáisa ani sumsok nakar ja di, ang nono koi ja-o tei nini Subraiyung Kaitorno, ani oroni o butharnaino rosaui tlang ja di hwnwi koi ja di.”

English: So he went out from Pharaoh, and entreated the Lord.
Kokborok: Musi Pharaoni aroni nongkhorwi thángwi Subraiyungni thani koiwi suri kha.

English: And the Lord turned a very strong west wind, which lifted the locusts and drove them into the Red Sea; not a single locust was left in all the country of Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung salthángbaini belai khe nokbar sipcha rw kha, abo kúkthorarokno rosaui twijlang kwchago tlangwi tháng kha; kúkthora masa phano ha Ijibo tong rwk li ya.

English: But the Lord hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Pharaoni bwkhano rak rw kha tei Israil bororokno thángna rw li ya.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward heaven that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, a darkness to be felt.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijip ha gwnangwi mwnak rwna bagwi nini yakno nokha phaising pholok di.”

English: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and there was thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days;
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bini yakno nokha phaising pholok kha, saltham jora ha Ijip gwnangwi belai khe mwnagwi tong kha.

English: they did not see one another, nor did any rise from his place for three days; but all the people of Israel had light where they dwelt.
Kokborok: Bórok khoroksa bai tei khoroksa nuk lai li ya, khoroksa phano sak sakni tongthaini saltham jora kutul lai li ya, tamo khe Israil ha gwnangwini bororok bórokni tongthai-o chati mwchẃng laiwi tong lai kha.

English: Then Pharaoh called Moses, and said, “Go, serve the Lord; your children also may go with you; only let your flocks and your herds remain behind.”
