Kokborok: Aphuru khe Pharao Musino ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di Subraiyungno suriui di; norokni bwrwi bwsa tei chwrairokbo norok bai baksa tháng si thung; tamo khe norokni mal-matarok, pun tokrok simi klangwi tháng si di.”

English: But Moses said, “You must also let us have sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng chwngno ratharwi chini Subraiyung Kaitorno yapharma tei sógwi yapharmanobo ma khlai rwnai.

English: Our cattle also must go with us; not a hoof shall be left behind, for we must take of them to serve the Lord our God, and we do not know with what we must serve the Lord until we arrive there.”
Kokborok: Chini mal-mata, pun tokrokbo chwng bai baksano ma tlangwi thángnai; mal-mata masa phano chwng karwi klangwi man ya, tamni hwn ba chwng abórokno tlangwi’-no chini Subraiyung Kaitorno ma khulumuinai, tamo bai khe Subraiyungno ma surinai aro sógwi ya sak saui man ya kho.”

English: But the Lord hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he would not let them go.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyung Pharaoni bwkhano rak rw kha tei bórokno thángna rw li ya.

English: Then Pharaoh said to him, “Get away from me; take heed to yourself; never see my face again; for in the day you see my face you shall die.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani oroni tháng di, nwng hus wng di tei ang bai tei ta malai phai di wng ya khe nwng ma thwinai.”

English: Moses said, “As you say! I will not see your face again.”
Kokborok: Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng samani tẃi’-no wng si thung, ang nwng bai tei malai li ya.”

English: The Lord said to Moses, “Yet one plague more I will bring upon Pharaoh and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence; when he lets you go, he will drive you away completely.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang tei Mungsa swprakmung Pharaoni saka-o tei Ijiprokni saka-o tubunai; aboni ulo khe bo norokno thángnani rw anw bo norokno rwi rohor phuru khe, norokno bebakno rwkhlaiwi se rohornai.

English: Speak now in the hearing of the people, that they ask, every man of his neighbor and every woman of her neighbor, jewelry of silver and of gold.”
Kokborok: Tabuk bororokno sa di, chwlarok tei bwrwirokbo bórokni nok arini rangchak tei rukphaini manwirok sanwi na thung.”

English: And the Lord gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover, the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh's servants and in the sight of the people.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Ijipsarokno Israilrokni kókno khna rw kha. Aboni bere Musino Ijip hani Pharaoni selengrok tei bororok kotor khe nugo.

English: And Moses said, “Thus says the Lord: About midnight I will go forth in the midst of Egypt;
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa-o, ang hor kuthugo khe ha Ijip kwchar tẃi laiwi thángnai.

English: and all the first-born in the land of Egypt shall die, from the first-born of Pharaoh who sits upon his throne, even to the first-born of the maidservant who is behind the mill; and all the first-born of the cattle.
Kokborok: Ha Ijipni bebak swkang sakrarok thwi bainai, achukthayungo achuknai Pharaoni bwsa okrani simi mai súknaijwk selengjwkrokni nokni swkang sakrarok thwinai.

English: And there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as there has never been, nor ever shall be again.
Kokborok: Ha Ijip gwnangwi’-no kap lai-kok lai wng lainai, abo hai uáisabo wng ya kho, phiya tei uáisa phanobo wng glak.

English: But against any of the people of Israel, either man or beast, not a dog shall growl; that you may know that the Lord makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.
Kokborok: Tei Israilrokni khe borok tei mal-matarokni bere sẃirok suda sẃng glak, omo hai khe ang Israil bai Ijipsarokni bisingo khaknai, Israilrok khe mungsa wng glak.

English: And all these your servants shall come down to me, and bow down to me, saying, ‘Get you out, and all the people who follow you.’ And after that I will go out.” And he went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.
Kokborok: Bebak nini selengrok ani thani phaiwi ano khulumui hai hwnwi ma sa phainai, ‘Nwng tei nini kók khnanairok nongkhor bai si di.’ Aboni ulo khe ang ongkhorwi thángnai.” Hakhe bo jolijagwi Pharaoni aroni ongkhorwi tháng kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Pharaoh will not listen to you; that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.”
Kokborok: Ulo khe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Pharao nini kókno khna glak; hakhe ha Ijibo teibo kwbángma uanamasingcha samungrok tangjaknai.”

English: Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; and the Lord hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the people of Israel go out of his land.
Kokborok: Musi bai Aron a bebak uanamasingcha samungrok Pharaoni bwskango khlai kha; tei Subraiyung Pharaoni bwkhano rak rw kha, bo Israil bororokno bini hani thángna rw li ya.

English: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt,
Kokborok: Ha Ijibo Subraiyung Musi bai Aronno hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “This month shall be for you the beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year for you.
Kokborok: “O talrok nini bagwi chengmani tal wngnai, o tal nini bagwi bisini tal yaphang wngnai.

English: Tell all the congregation of Israel that on the tenth day of this month they shall take every man a lamb according to their fathers' houses, a lamb for a household;
Kokborok: Bebak Israilrokno sa di, o talni salchini salo khe bebak núkhung thwgwino bórokni núkhungni borok bángmano naiwi mera bwsa masa na thung;

English: and if the household is too small for a lamb, then a man and his neighbor next to his house shall take according to the number of persons; according to what each can eat you shall make your count for the lamb.
Kokborok: tumungno kaisa núkhung belai kisa wngma bai mera bwsa masa cháui pái man ya wng khe a borok bini nok samni tei kaisa núkhungni bororok bai borok thwgwi banta khe naui cháui pái manna tẃi khe na di. Khoroksa khoroksa cháui manma tẃi’-no mera bwsano thwgwi ma nanai.

English: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a male a year old; you shall take it from the sheep or from the goats;
Kokborok: Nini mera mungsa khait kwrwi wngna nangnai tei bisi kaisa umorni wngna nangnai; nwng abo mera bodolni eba pun bodolni naui mano;

English: and you shall keep it until the fourteenth day of this month, when the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill their lambs in the evening.
Kokborok: norok abono o talni sal chibrwi sal jora ma tonnai, aphuru khe Israilni bodolni bebak bororok sanja-o khe mera bwsano ma rathar lainai.

English: Then they shall take some of the blood, and put it on the two doorposts and the lintel of the houses in which they eat them.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok kisa thẃi naui duglamni babainwi thongla-o tei duglamni bokhorogo phulwi ma rw lainai tei a nogo’-no muihanrok ma chá lainai.

English: They shall eat the flesh that night, roasted; with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
Kokborok: A horono a muihanrokno ma hang lainai tei sam kwkha bai khe bului auang bángla bai khe ma chá lainai.

English: Do not eat any of it raw or boiled with water, but roasted, its head with its legs and its inner parts.
Kokborok: Abórokno kwthwng khe eba rugwibo ta chá di, tamo khe abono hóro hang di, bokhorok tei yathekrokno tei bubuk-bwkharokno hóro hang di.

English: And you shall let none of it remain until the morning, anything that remains until the morning you shall burn.
Kokborok: Norok phung aimani jora abono mungsabo ta narwk di; mungsa swk karjagwi tong khe sógwi khibi di.

English: In this manner you shall eat it: your loins girded, your sandals on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and you shall eat it in haste. It is the Lord's passover.
Kokborok: Norok abono omo tẃi kheno chá di: Norok kamchwlwi lolang chumwi chango rísa khaui, yakungo yaklap ma kánai, yago raidang tẃijak khe; norok abono doui ma chánai. Omo Subraiyungni phiyókma salni panda.

English: For I will pass through the land of Egypt that night, and I will smite all the first-born in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and on all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments: I am the Lord.
Kokborok: Tamni hwn khe ang a horo ha Ijip saka tẃi lái thángnai tei ang ha Ijipni bebak borok tei mal-matarokni sakrarokno butharnai tei ha Ijipni bebak mwtairokno buj thumnai tei majra rwnai; ang’-no Subraiyung.

English: The blood shall be a sign for you, upon the houses where you are; and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and no plague shall fall upon you to destroy you, when I smite the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Ha Ijipni borokno buthar phuru norok tongmani nokni a thẃirok khe norokni bagwi mari wngnai; ang thẃino nuk khe norokno láiwi thángnai tei ang ha Ijipno swprak phuru mungsa deramungrok norokni thani phai glak.

English: “This day shall be for you a memorial day, and you shall keep it as a feast to the Lord; throughout your generations you shall observe it as an ordinance for ever.
Kokborok: “O sal norokni bagwi muituni sal wngnai tei norok o salno Subraiyungni mungwi ter khlai di; norokni bosong mareui kusung kusungno omo norokni bagwi kerong wngnai.”

English: Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; on the first day you shall put away leaven out of your houses, for if any one eats what is leavened, from the first day until the seventh day, that person shall be cut off from Israel.
Kokborok: “Norok salsni auang bángla ma chánai; puilani salo norokni nokni chuanrok ma khibinai; tamni hwn ba khoroksa swk chuan buljak auangno puilani salni simi salsnini sal jora chá khe bo Israil bodolni khagwi rwjaknai.

English: On the first day you shall hold a holy assembly, and on the seventh day a holy assembly; no work shall be done on those days; but what every one must eat, that only may be prepared by you.
Kokborok: Puilani salo norok kwthar kuthum laimung kuthum lai di; tei salsni salo khe kwthar kuthum laimungni sal; a salrogo samungrok tangwi man glak; nwng chána bagwi sóngmarokno simi se khlai mannai.

English: And you shall observe the feast of unleavened bread, for on this very day I brought your hosts out of the land of Egypt: therefore you shall observe this day, throughout your generations, as an ordinance for ever.
Kokborok: Norok auang bángla chámung ter ma khlainai, tamni hwn ba ang a salo’-no Israil bodolno ha Ijipni phiyógwi tubu-o: Aboni bagwi mol brúm brúmno norokni bosong mareui a salno muitu ma khlainai.

English: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread, and so until the twenty-first day of the month at evening.
Kokborok: Puilani talni sal chibrwini salni sanja-o khe norok auang bángla ma chá lainai, a talni sal nwichisani sanja jora ma chá lainai.

English: For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses; for if any one eats what is leavened, that person shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he is a sojourner or a native of the land.
Kokborok: Salsni jora norokni nokrogo auano buljaknai tonwi man glak; khoroksa swk phano auano buljaknai bai buljak auang chá khe a borok Israil bodolni khagwi rwjaknai. Bo hani borok phano eba phatarni borok phano wng thung yók glak.

English: You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.”
Kokborok: Norok mungsa phano buljak manwi cháui man glak. Norokni bebak núkhungo’-no auang bángla se ma chá lainai.”

English: Then Moses called all the elders of Israel, and said to them, “Select lambs for yourselves according to your families, and kill the passover lamb.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Israilni okrarokno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok sak sakni núkhungni bororokno naiwi mera bwsa saiwi na di tei yókma salni mera bwsa rathar di.

English: Take a bunch of hyssop and dip it in the blood which is in the basin, and touch the lintel and the two doorposts with the blood which is in the basin; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.
Kokborok: Hisop jabra chopsa naui thẃi tonma a dangai-o abono chomsa di, hakhe a dangaini thẃino duglam sakani thongo phulwi rw di tei yang yangni kainwi duglamni thongla’-obo phulwi rw di, phiya sak sak norok khoroksa phano phataro ongkhorwi man glak.

English: For the Lord will pass through to slay the Egyptians; and when he sees the blood on the lintel and on the two doorposts, the Lord will pass over the door, and will not allow the destroyer to enter your houses to slay you.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Ijipsarokno butharna bagwi ha Ijip tẃi lái thángnai; duglam saka-o tei duglamni kainwi thongla-o thẃi nuk khe Subraiyung a duglamno haino lái thángnai tei butharnaino norokni nogo hapna rw glak.

English: You shall observe this rite as an ordinance for you and for your sons for ever.
Kokborok: Nwng o samungno dagimung hwnwi ma tẃinai tei nini nwsarokbo mol brúm brúmno ma tẃinai.

English: And when you come to the land which the Lord will give you, as he has promised, you shall keep this service.
Kokborok: Subraiyung norokno rwna hwnwi swmai tangma tẃi’-no a ha-o nwng sók phai phuru khe nwng o samungno ma tẃinai.

English: And when your children say to you, ‘What do you mean by this service?’
Kokborok: Norokni bwsarok norokno ‘O samung tamoni bagwi?’ hwnwi sẃng khe,

English: you shall say, ‘It is the sacrifice of the Lord's passover, for he passed over the houses of the people of Israel in Egypt, when he slew the Egyptians but spared our houses.’ ” And the people bowed their heads and worshiped.
Kokborok:
norok hai hwnwi ma sanai, “Omo wng kha Subraiyungni, yókmani rathar mung, tamni hwn khe Subraiyung, ha Ijip tẃi lái tháng phuru Israilrokni nokno haino lái tháng kha; ‘Bo Ijiprokno khe buthar kha, Chwngno khe haino karwi klang kha.’ ”
Israilrok bórokni khorok bomwi khulum lai kha.

English: Then the people of Israel went and did so; as the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
Kokborok: Hakhe Israil bororok thángwi haino khlai lai kha; Subraiyung Musi bai Aronno dagima tẃi’-no bórok khlai kha.

English: At midnight the Lord smote all the first-born in the land of Egypt, from the first-born of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the first-born of the captive who was in the dungeon, and all the first-born of the cattle.
Kokborok: Hor kuthugo kheno Subraiyung ha Ijipni bebak chwla bwsa sakrano buthar kha. Achukthayungo achukjak Pharaoni simi chengwi, romwi tubu-ui alongo chopjagwi tongnaini bwslabo thwi kha tei mal-mataroknibo swkang sakrarok butharjak bai kha.

English: And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt, for there was not a house where one was not dead.
Kokborok: Pharao hor kuthugono bacha kha, bini selengrokbo tei bebak Ijipsarokbo bacha kha; aro ha Ijip gwnangwi bebago’-no chirigwi kap laijak máng, tamni hwn khe núkhung khungsa-o phano thwi lai ya kwrwi kha.

English: And he summoned Moses and Aaron by night, and said, “Rise up, go forth from among my people, both you and the people of Israel; and go, serve the Lord, as you have said.
Kokborok: A horo’-no Pharao, Musi bai Aronno ring rw horwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bacha lang bai si di, ani bororokni oroni tháng si di, nwng tei nini bebak Israil bororok ongkhorwi tháng si di, phiya nwng sama hai kheno Subraiyungno khulumui lai si di.

English: Take your flocks and your herds, as you have said, and be gone; and bless me also!”
Kokborok: Nwng sama hai’-no norok mal-matarok, musuk-merarok tlangwi ongkhorwi tháng si di tei anibo hamari san si di!”

English: And the Egyptians were urgent with the people, to send them out of the land in haste; for they said, “We are all dead men.”
Kokborok: Ijipsarok o bororokno ha Ijipni tikhlai rohor laina bagwi do lai súkjak; hakhe bórok “Chwng bebakno kwthwi máng se” hwnwi sa kha.

English: So the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneading bowls being bound up in their mantles on their shoulders.
Kokborok: Bororok bórokni auang khlaima karaino moida bángla bai supung lai kha hakhe rí bai dul lai kha tei bangora boksaui tlang lai kha.

English: The people of Israel had also done as Moses told them, for they had asked of the Egyptians jewelry of silver and gold, and clothing;
Kokborok: Musi sama hai kheno Israilsarok khlai lai kha tei Ijipsarokni thani rangchak tei rukphaini kanmungrok tei rírok san lai kha.

English: and the Lord had given the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they let them have what they asked. Thus they despoiled the Egyptians.
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung Ijipsarokno Israilrokni bagwi khá phur rw kha, hakhe bórok sanma tẃi’-no Israilrokno rw lai kha. Abo hai kheno Israilrok Ijiipsarokni rangchak-ríchakrok tlangwi tháng kha.

English: And the people of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides women and children.
Kokborok: Israilrok Ramaseni lam rwui Sukot phaising lam rw lai kha. Aro bwrwirok bai chwrairokno karwi chwlarok simi khorok dok-rasai ká anw.

English: A mixed multitude also went up with them, and very many cattle, both flocks and herds.
Kokborok: Tei daljak kubun bororokbo kwbángma bórok bai baksa tháng lai kha. Tei kwbángma merarok, punrok mal-matarok bórok bai baksa lam rw lai kha.

English: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they had brought out of Egypt, for it was not leavened, because they were thrust out of Egypt and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any provisions.
Kokborok: Bórok ha Ijipni tubuma moida bánglano auang ser lai kha. Bórok achomsa se ha Ijipni ma ongkhor ma ba bórok auanrok tiyar khlai tubu-ui man li ya, eba auano buljaknobo bului tubu-ui man li ya; tamni hwn khe bórok sak sakno se tiyar wng lai man lai li ya.

English: The time that the people of Israel dwelt in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
Kokborok: Israilrok nokha bisi brwi-ra tei thamchi ha Ijibo tong lai kha,

English: And at the end of four hundred and thirty years, on that very day, all the hosts of the Lord went out from the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: tei nokha bisi brwira-thamchi páimani salo Subraiyungni bebak bororok ha Ijipni ongkhorwi tháng lai kha.

English: It was a night of watching by the Lord, to bring them out of the land of Egypt; so this same night is a night of watching kept to the Lord by all the people of Israel throughout their generations.
Kokborok: Subraiyung a horo se bórokno naikini khlaiwi ha Ijipni tikhlai-o. Hakhe a horono mol brúmni bagwi bebak Israil bororok, Subraiyungno bórokni naikinini bagwi yapharo.

English: And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron, “This is the ordinance of the passover: no foreigner shall eat of it;
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung, Musi bai Aronno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omorokno yókma salno tẃimani raidarok; khoroksa phatarsa phano abono ma chá glak;

English: but every slave that is bought for money may eat of it after you have circumcised him.
Kokborok: Tamo khe norok rang bai paimani bebak selengrok le bórokni sakur rajak hwn khe cháui mannai.

English: No sojourner or hired servant may eat of it.
Kokborok: Khoroksa mula khe chánairok eba haino srapsani bagwi phai tong phainairok le ma chá glak.

English: In one house shall it be eaten; you shall not carry forth any of the flesh outside the house; and you shall not break a bone of it.
Kokborok: Nok khungsa-o tiyar khlaijakma abo núkhung kaisa’-ono cháui ma páinai; aroni muihanno a nokni phataro tlangwi man glak. Norok a muini bekrengrokno ma swbai glak.

English: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.
Kokborok: Bebak Israil bororok abono ma tẃinai.

English: And when a stranger shall sojourn with you and would keep the passover to the Lord, let all his males be circumcised, then he may come near and keep it; he shall be as a native of the land. But no uncircumcised person shall eat of it.
Kokborok: Tumungno khoroksa swk phatarsa norokni bisingo kami kha phai khe tei norokni Subraiyungno bórina bagwi yókmani salno tẃina muchung khe norok bórokni núkhungni bebak chwlarokno sakur ma ra granai. Hakhe bono Israil ha-ono achainai Israilsa hai khe romwi mano tei yókma salni panda-o habwi mano. Tamo khe sakur rajak ya borok khoroksa phano abono cháui man glak.

English: There shall be one law for the native and for the stranger who sojourns among you.”
Kokborok: Tei ha-o achainai Israilrokbo haino ma khlainai tei buini hani kubun borok norokni aro kami kha phainairokbo haino ma khlainai.”

English: Thus did all the people of Israel; as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
Kokborok: Bebak Israilrokno Musi bai Aron dagima haino khlai lai kha.

English: And on that very day the Lord brought the people of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their hosts.
Kokborok: A salo’-no Subraiyung Israil bororokno tei bórokni bodolrok bai khe ha Ijipni phiyógwi tikhlai tubu kha.

English: The Lord said to Moses,
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino sa kha,

English: “Consecrate to me all the first-born; whatever is the first to open the womb among the people of Israel, both of man and of beast, is mine.”
Kokborok:
“Bebak chwla bwsa sakrano ani thani yaphar di. Tamni hwn ba bebak chwla sakra tei mal mataroknibo bebak chwlarok khe abo ani se.”
Auang bángla ter

English: And Moses said to the people, “Remember this day, in which you came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage, for by strength of hand the Lord brought you out from this place; no leavened bread shall be eaten.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bororokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “O salno muitu khlai di. Norok ha Ijipni ongkhormani sal, aro norok seleng wng lai ma tongmani. Subraiyung a salo’-no bini phankrak yak bai khe norokno phiyógwi tubu-o, mungsa buljak auang norok ma chá glak.

English: This day you are to go forth, in the month of Abib.
Kokborok: O salo’-no norok bwskang ma swkangnai, Abibni o talo.

English: And when the Lord brings you into the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he swore to your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, you shall keep this service in this month.
Kokborok: “Subraiyung nini chuchusongrokni thani kwrak khe norokno a piya bwtwi bai dut yorjak Kanansasongni ha, Hitisarokni hano, Amorsarokni, Hibisarokni tei Jebussarokni hano rwna hwnwi swmai tangmani, aphuru norok bisi brúmno o talo o terno khlai lai di.

English: Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a feast to the Lord.
Kokborok: Salsni jora norok auang bángla ma chá lainai, salsnini salo khe Subraiyungni mungwi chámung ter ma khlainai.

English: Unleavened bread shall be eaten for seven days; no leavened bread shall be seen with you, and no leaven shall be seen with you in all your territory.
Kokborok: Salsni norok mungsa bul yaui auang ma chá lainai, nini ha gwnangwi aro mungsa auano bulma manwi ma ton glak.

English: And you shall tell your son on that day, ‘It is because of what the Lord did for me when I came out of Egypt.’
Kokborok: Tei a salo norok bwsa chwlarokno saui ma khna rwnai, ‘Ha Ijipni nongkhorwi phai phuru Subraiyung ani bagwi tamo samung khlai kha abo se,’

English: And it shall be to you as a sign on your hand and as a memorial between your eyes, that the law of the Lord may be in your mouth; for with a strong hand the Lord has brought you out of Egypt.
Kokborok: tei, omo nini thani nini yago tei talikha bai mokol kwcharo khe khajak mari hai wngnai tei Subraiyungni kerong nini khugo tongna nangnai; tamni hwn khe Subraiyung nono yak kwrak bai ha Ijipni tikhlaiwi tubu kha.

English: You shall therefore keep this ordinance at its appointed time from year to year.
Kokborok:
Norok o dagimano bini chongjak jora-o bisi brúm brúmno ma tẃi lainai.
Swkang sakra

English: “And when the Lord brings you into the land of the Canaanites, as he swore to you and your fathers, and shall give it to you,
Kokborok: “Tei Bo nono tei nini chuchusongni thani rwna hwnwi kók rwma Kanansarokni ha-o Subraiyung norokno tubu-ui phai khe tei norokni yago rw phuru khe.

English: you shall set apart to the Lord all that first opens the womb. All the firstlings of your cattle that are males shall be the Lord's.
Kokborok: Norok bebak swkang achaima sakrano Subraiyungni bagwi khagwi ton di. Norokni bebak mal matarokni sakrarokbo Subraiyungni wngnai.

English: Every firstling of an ass you shall redeem with a lamb, or if you will not redeem it you shall break its neck. Every first-born of man among your sons you shall redeem.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Korai chirka chwla bwsa sakrano khe aboni sak slái-o mera bwsa rwi kiphilwi ma nanai. Phiya nwng a korai chirkano paiwi kiphilwi na ya hwn khe aboni gwdnano mukhrubwi rw di. Tei norokni chwla sakrano norok ma phiyóknai.

English: And when in time to come your son asks you, ‘What does this mean?’ you shall say to him, ‘By strength of hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt, from the house of bondage.
Kokborok: Tei ulo norokni bwsarok jora-o norokno ‘Omoni gujok tamo?’ hwnwi sẃng khe norok bono hai hwnwi ma sanai, ‘Subraiyung bini phankrak bai khe chwngno seleng khatiui ma tongma ha Ijipni phiyógwi tubu-ui phai kha.

English: For when Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the Lord slew all the first-born in the land of Egypt, both the first-born of man and the first-born of cattle. Therefore I sacrifice to the Lord all the males that first open the womb; but all the first-born of my sons I redeem.’
Kokborok: Tamni hwn khe Pharao kók khna ya wngwi chwngno phiyókna soi ya wng khe, Subraiyung ha Ijipni bebak chwla bwsa sakrano buthar kha bororokni sakrano tei mal matarokni sakranobo buthar kha. Abo bai’-no bebakni sakrano ang Subraiyungni thani ratharo; tamo khe ani chwla bwsa sakrarokno khe ang phiyógwi na-o.’

English: It shall be as a mark on your hand or frontlets between your eyes; for by a strong hand the Lord brought us out of Egypt.”
Kokborok:
Omo nini bagwi nini yago tei talikha-o, mokolni kwcharo mari hai khe ma khanai, tamni hwn khe Subraiyung phankrak bai khe ha Ijipni tikhlai tubu kha.”
Chumui budul bai hórni budul

English: When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God said, “Lest the people repent when they see war, and return to Egypt.”
Kokborok: Ha Ijipni habugra bororokno phiyógwi rohor phuru a lama sampha phano Kaitor bórokno ha Philistia-o thángma lama tẃi tlang rw li ya; tamni hwn khe Kaitor sa kha, “Bórok choba khlaina nangmano nuk khe, khá kiphilwi ha Ijibo kiphilwi thángnai se.”

English: But God led the people round by the way of the wilderness toward the Red Sea. And the people of Israel went up out of the land of Egypt equipped for battle.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Kaitor bórokno lama khon rwui hasing haphar tẃi khe Twijlang Kwchak phaising tlangwi tháng kha. Tei Israilrok ha Ijipni tháng phuru-o chobani bagwi manwirok khati laiwi’-no tiyar khe thángjak.

English: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him; for Joseph had solemnly sworn the people of Israel, saying, “God will visit you; then you must carry my bones with you from here.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Josepni bekrengno bo bai baksano tlang kha; tamni hwn khe Josep Israilrokno hai hwnwi kwrak khe sajak, “Kaitor norok bai malai phainai; aphuru khe norok ani bekrengno oroni norok bai baksa ma tlangnai.”

English: And they moved on from Succoth, and encamped at Etham, on the edge of the wilderness.
Kokborok: Tei bórok Sukotni nongkhorwi thángwi hasing hapharni páithak Ethamo thángwi batham khlai lai kha.

English: And the Lord went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, that they might travel by day and by night;
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung salo khe chumui budul wngwi bórokni bwskang bwskang thángwi lama soui tlang kha tei horo khe hórni budul wngwi pohor rwi tlang kha.

English: the pillar of cloud by day and the pillar of fire by night did not depart from before the people.
Kokborok: Salo khe chumui budul horo khe hórni budul khe bórokni bwskangni kutul yaui’-no tong kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Moses,
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino sa kha,

English: “Tell the people of Israel to turn back and encamp in front of Pi-ha-hiroth, between Migdol and the sea, in front of Baal-zephon; you shall encamp over against it, by the sea.
Kokborok: “Israil bororokno sa di, kiphilwi phaiwi Bal-Jephoni Migdol bai twijlang kwchar Pihahirotni bwskango batham khlai di. Nwng abono naharwi twijlang rukungo khe batham khlai di.

English: For Pharaoh will say of the people of Israel, ‘They are entangled in the land; the wilderness has shut them in.’
Kokborok: Tamni hwn ba Pharao, Israil bororokno hai hwnwi sanai, bórok a ha-o hai’-no kwcha rwjak kha; hasing haphar bai lama kasujak kha.

English: And I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and he will pursue them and I will get glory over Pharaoh and all his host; and the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord.” And they did so.
Kokborok: Ang Pharaoni bwkhano rak rwnai tei bo bórokno riphriknai tei habugra bai bini sengkrakrokni saka-o ang chẃngsacha wngnai. Hakhe Ijipsarok ano Subraiyung hwnwi sinai.” Israilrok bórok sajakma haino khlai lai kha.

English: When the king of Egypt was told that the people had fled, the mind of Pharaoh and his servants was changed toward the people, and they said, “What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Ijipni habugrano Israilrok khárwi tháng bai kha hwnwi sajak khe, Pharao bai bini tẃiphangrokni bwkha a borokrokni bagwi sláijak kha tei bórok sa lai kha, “Chwng tamo khlaijak lang, chwngno monwi tongnairokno bahai khe phiyógwi rwjak lang?”

English: So he made ready his chariot and took his army with him,
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bini korai malkhungrokno tiyar khlai kha tei bo bai baksa bini sengkrakrokno tẃi kha.

English: and took six hundred picked chariots and all the other chariots of Egypt with officers over all of them.
Kokborok: Tei saradok korai malkhungrokno tei kubun malkhungrokno naui tei bokhorokno tẃiwi tháng kha.

English: And the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt and he pursued the people of Israel as they went forth defiantly.
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung ha Ijipni habugra Pharaoni bwkhano rak rw kha tei bo kwplai khe ongkhorwi Israilrokno riphrigwi tháng kha.

English: The Egyptians pursued them, all Pharaoh's horses and chariots and his horsemen and his army, and overtook them encamped at the sea, by Pi-ha-hiroth, in front of Baal-zephon.
Kokborok: Ijipsarokni sengkrakrok bebak korai tei korai malkhungrok tei malkhung mal rwnairok, sengkrakrok, bórokno riphrigwi tháng kha tei bórok batham khlaima twijlang rukungni Pihahirotni gana Bal-jephono malai kha.

English: When Pharaoh drew near, the people of Israel lifted up their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians were marching after them; and they were in great fear. And the people of Israel cried out to the Lord;
Kokborok: Pharao samo sók phai khe; Israilsarok nuk kha Ijipsarok bórokno riphrigwi phai lai tong kha; hakhe bórok bebakno belai khe kirijak lai kha. Hakhe Israilsarok Subraiyungni thani chubachuni bagwi kap lai kha.

English: and they said to Moses, “Is it because there are no graves in Egypt that you have taken us away to die in the wilderness? What have you done to us, in bringing us out of Egypt?
Kokborok: Tei bórok Musino hwn lai kha, ha Ijibo chini bagwi mangkhor kwrwini bagwi da nwng chwngno o hasing hapharo thwina bagwi tubu-ui phai?

English: Is not this what we said to you in Egypt, ‘Let us alone and let us serve the Egyptians’? For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.”
Kokborok: Chwng nono ha Ijibo hai hwnwi sa lai ya da, “Chwngno orono klangwi tháng di, chwng haino Ijipsarokni seleng wngwi monwi máng thángnai. O hasing hapharo thwimani slái aro seleng wngwi Ijipsarokno monwi tongma phano ham kuk kha mun.”

English: And Moses said to the people, “Fear not, stand firm, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will work for you today; for the Egyptians whom you see today, you shall never see again.
Kokborok: Musi bororokno sa kha, “Ta kiri di, kwrak khe bacha di, norok nuknai, Subraiyung norokno mwthangna bagwi tini tamo samung khlai; norok tini nukma o Ijipsakrokno tei nuk phi glak.

English: The Lord will fight for you, and you have only to be still.”
Kokborok: Subraiyung norokni wngwi choba khlainai tei norok kwrak khe simi tongna nangnai.”

English: The Lord said to Moses, “Why do you cry to me? Tell the people of Israel to go forward.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino sa kha, “Norok tamo chubachuni bagwi ani thani kap lai? Israil bororokno yapri swkangwi tháng di hwnwi sa di.

English: Lift up your rod, and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, that the people of Israel may go on dry ground through the sea.
Kokborok: Nini Israilrok twijlang kwchar tẃi hakung kwran khe lái manna tẃi khe nini raidangno tisa di tei nini yakno twijlang phaising phologwi abono khaknwi khe khak di.

English: And I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians so that they shall go in after them, and I will get glory over Pharaoh and all his host, his chariots, and his horsemen.
Kokborok: Tei ang Ijipsarokno bwkha rak rwnai, hakhe bórok Israil borokni uklogo rwktharnai, tamo khe, ang bugra Pharaoni thani, bebak bini sengkrakrokni thani, bini malkhungrokni thani tei bini korai kanairokni thani mwkhang mannai.

English: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I have gotten glory over Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.”
Kokborok: Hakhe ang Pharaoni thani, bini korai malkhungrokni thani tei bini korai kanairokni thani mwkhang man ma nuk khe, Ijipsarok ang’-no Subraiyung hwnwi saui mannai.”

English: Then the angel of God who went before the host of Israel moved and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud moved from before them and stood behind them,
Kokborok: Hakhe Israilrokni bwskango khe tongnai Kaitorni suari konor yaui bórokni uklogo phai bacha phai kha; tei chumui budulbo haino konorwi phaiwi uklogo bathak phai kha.

English: coming between the host of Egypt and the host of Israel. And there was the cloud and the darkness; and the night passed without one coming near the other all night.
Kokborok: Ijipsarokni kwchar bai Israilrokni kwcharo phaiwi bacha phai kha. Aro Chumui mwnak kobol kha tei hopung’-no khoroksa tei khoroksa bai sederjak phano nuk lai li ya kheno hor ai kha.

English: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the Lord drove the sea back by a strong east wind all night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bini yakno twijlang phaising phologwi tisa kha; Subraiyung hopungno salkabaini nokbar sibcha rwui twijlangni twino saka phaising kasa rw kha, twijlangno hakung kwran khlai kha tei twirokno khaknwi khe khak kha.

English: And the people of Israel went into the midst of the sea on dry ground, the waters being a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.
Kokborok: Israil bororok twijlang kwchar tẃi hakung kwran khe láiwi tháng lai kha, twirok bórokni yaksi yagra-o hapung hai se wng si-o.

English: The Egyptians pursued, and went in after them into the midst of the sea, all Pharaoh's horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.
Kokborok: Ijipsarok bórokno riphrigwi thángwi twijlang bisingo habwi tháng lai kha. Ijipni habugrani bebak korairok, korai malkhungrok bebakno kwlwk bai kha.

English: And in the morning watch the Lord in the pillar of fire and of cloud looked down upon the host of the Egyptians, and discomfited the host of the Egyptians,
Kokborok: Tei phung aisirisiri, Subraiyung hórni budulo tong twtwi tei chumui budulo tongwi Ijipsarokni sengkrakrokno naikhlai kha tei Ijipsarokno bejua-o khiklai kha.

English: clogging their chariot wheels so that they drove heavily; and the Egyptians said, “Let us flee from before Israel; for the Lord fights for them against the Egyptians.”
Kokborok: Bo bórokni malkhungni gorgorirokno harpego kwlwk rw kha, abo bai bórok belai khe phan rwui béng lai kha; hakhe Ijipsarok sa lai kha, “Phai di chwng Israilrokni oroni khár lai sinai; tamni hwn khe Subraiyung se bórokni wngwi Ijipsarok bai choba khlai lai-o.”

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the sea, that the water may come back upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino sa kha, “Nwng twijlang bwkhak nini yak tisaui pholok di. Hakhe Ijipsarokni saka-o, bórokni malkhungrokno tei malkhung tẃinairokni saka-o twi kiphilwi phaiwi lomwi rw phai kha.”

English: So Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to its wonted flow when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled into it, and the Lord routed the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi twijlang bwkhak yak pholok kha, ai khe twi haino hai swkangni haino wng phi khua; Ijipsarok a twini yókna bagwi belai khe’-no chaitok lai kha, tamo khe Subraiyung bórokno twijlango swklwgwi rw kha.

English: The waters returned and covered the chariots and the horsemen and all the host of Pharaoh that had followed them into the sea; not so much as one of them remained.
Kokborok: Twi kiphilwi phaiwi malkhungrokno kobolwi pái kha, Israilrokno riphrigwi thángnai malkhung tẃinairok tei Ijipni sengkrakrok twijlango kwlwk bai kha, khoroksa phano yók li ya.

English: But the people of Israel walked on dry ground through the sea, the waters being a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.
Kokborok: Tamo khe twirok yagra yaksi hapung hai wng kha hakhe Israilrok twijlang kwchar tẃi khe hakung kwran hai khe lái tháng lai kha.

English: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day from the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the seashore.
Kokborok: Hakhe a salo Subraiyung Israilrokno Ijipsarokni yakni mwthang kha tei Israilrok bórokno twijlang rukungo kwthwi mang klaijak nuk kha.

English: And Israel saw the great work which the Lord did against the Egyptians, and the people feared the Lord; and they believed in the Lord and in his servant Moses.
Kokborok: Subraiyung bini phankrak bai khe Ijipsarokno mechenma nuk khe Subraiyungno kirijak lai kha hakhe bórok Subraiyung bai bini seleng Musino poito khlai lai kha.

English:
The Lord is my strength and my song,
and he has become my salvation;
this is my God, and I will praise him,
my father's God, and I will exalt him.
Kokborok:
Subraiyung se ani phan tei ani rwchapmung,
tei bo ani mwthangnai wng kha;
omono ani Kaitor tei ang bono’-no baikhenenai,
ani aphani Kaitor, Bono’-no ang lobnai.

English:
The Lord is a man of war;
the Lord is his name.
Kokborok:
Subraiyung wng kha choba khlainai;
bini mung wng kha Subraiyung.”

English:
“Pharaoh's chariots and his host he cast into the sea;
and his picked officers are sunk in the Red Sea.
Kokborok:
“Pharaoni malkhungrok bai bini sengkrakrokno
bo twijlango khiklai rw kha;
tei bini sengkrak bokhorokrok
twijlang kwcharo kwlwk bai kha.

English:
The floods cover them;
they went down into the depths like a stone.
Kokborok:
Twi kotor bórokno kobol kha;
holong kwlwk tẃi khe bórok kuthugo kwlwk kha.

English:
Thy right hand, O Lord, glorious in power,
thy right hand, O Lord, shatters the enemy.
Kokborok:
Subraiyung nini yagra yakni phan kirimasingcha,
Subraiyung, nini yagra yak arirokno swbaiwi rw kha.

English:
In the greatness of thy majesty thou overthrowest thy adversaries;
thou sendest forth thy fury, it consumes them like stubble.
Kokborok:
Nini suar kwplai bai khe nwng nono kebengnairokno norwi rohor kha.
Nwng nini jolimano rwui rohor khe abo bórokno chwkap hai khe sógwi pái-o.

English:
The enemy said, ‘I will pursue, I will overtake,
I will divide the spoil, my desire shall have its fill of them.
I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.’
Kokborok:
arirok hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘Ang riphrigwi bórokno romnai,
ang segwi tubuma mairokno banta khlainai,
ani muchung tẃi khe supungnai.
Ang ani seng tikhlainai, ani yak baithang’-no bórokno sokomornai.’

English:
Thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them;
they sank as lead in the mighty waters.
Kokborok:
Nwng nini nokbar bai uáisa swprak khe’-no twijlang bórokno kobolwi pái kha;
kirimasingcha twijlango bórok sorrok hai khe kwlwgwi pái kha.

English:
“Who is like thee, O Lord, among the gods?
Who is like thee, majestic in holiness,
terrible in glorious deeds, doing wonders?
Kokborok:
“O Subraiyung mwtairokni bere nwng hai tei sabo tong?
Sabo nwng hai chẃngsacha kwthar tong?
Samungo uanamasingcha
kwmamsingcha khe tangnai sabo tong?

English:
Thou didst stretch out thy right hand,
the earth swallowed them.
Kokborok:
Nwng nini yagra yakno pholok kha,
hakhe o ha bórokno monogwi pái kha.

English:
“Thou hast led in thy steadfast love the people whom thou hast redeemed,
thou hast guided them by thy strength to thy holy abode.

The peoples have heard, they tremble;
pangs have seized on the inhabitants of Philistia.
Kokborok:
Lukurok khnaui kiriui kwlwi lai kha;
Philistia-o tongnairok kirima chá kha.

English:
Terror and dread fall upon them;
because of the greatness of thy arm, they are as still as a stone,
till thy people, O Lord, pass by,
till the people pass by whom thou hast purchased.
Kokborok:
Bórokni uanama tei kirima hap kha;
o Subraiyung, bórok nini yakni phan nuk kha,
tei holong hai khe bórok raksa lai kha,
nwng bai paijak nini bororok lái tháng bai ya sak o Subraiyung.

English:
Thou wilt bring them in, and plant them on thy own mountain,
the place, O Lord, which thou hast made for thy abode,
the sanctuary, O Lord, which thy hands have established.
Kokborok:
Nwng bórokno bisingo tubunai tei
nini hathái-o khe songchauinai,
abo nwng nini nokni bagwi saiwi najak,
o Subraiyung nwng sak baithang’-no
o noktharno tang kha.

English: The Lord will reign for ever and ever.”
Kokborok:
Subraiyung mol brúmni bagwi
bugra chánai.”

English: For when the horses of Pharaoh with his chariots and his horsemen went into the sea, the Lord brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the people of Israel walked on dry ground in the midst of the sea.
Kokborok: Pharaoni korairok bini malkhungrok bai khe tei bini korai kanairok twijlango tháng phuru khe, Subraiyung twijlangni twirokno bórokni saka-o kiphil rwui tubu phi kha; tei Israil bororok khe twijlang kwchar tẃi hakung kwran khe lái tháng lai kha.

English: Then Miriam, the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and dancing.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Aronni bukhuk Miriam diyarijwk, kham bwsa yago na kha; hakhe bo bai baksa tei bebak bwrwirokbo kham bwsa khochaui bono rwktharwi tháng lai kha tei mwsa lai kha.

Miriam bórokni thani hai hwnwi rwchap kha:
“Subraiyungni thani rwchap di,
tamni hwn khe bo chẃngsacha khe kwplai wng kha;
korairok tei korai kanairoknobo
twijlango khiklai rw bai kha.”

English: Then Moses led Israel onward from the Red Sea, and they went into the wilderness of Shur; they went three days in the wilderness and found no water.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Musi Israilrokno twijlang kwchakni swkangwi tẃi kha; hakhe bórok Surni hasing hapharo tlangwi tháng kha; bórok saltham hasing hapharo him lai kha hai phano twi nuk li ya.

English: When they came to Marah, they could not drink the water of Marah because it was bitter; therefore it was named Marah.
Kokborok: Tei bórok Mara-o phai kha, a Marani twino bórok nungwi man li ya tamni hwn khe aroni twi belaino kwkha. Abo bai’-no abono “Mara” hwnwi mung pharjago.

English: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, “What shall we drink?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musino hai hwnwi hamya sa lai kha, “Chwng tamono nungnai?”

English:
And he cried to the Lord; and the Lord showed him a tree, and he threw it into the water, and the water became sweet.
There the Lord made for them a statute and an ordinance and there he proved them,
Kokborok:
Hakhe bo Subraiyungni thani suri kha tei Subraiyung bono buphang phangsa phunuk kha hakhe bo abono twi-o khiklai rw kha hakhe a twi kwtẃi wng kha.
Subraiyung aro bórokno raida kaisa tei dagimung tẃinani bagwi hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: saying, “If you will diligently hearken to the voice of the Lord your God, and do that which is right in his eyes, and give heed to his commandments and keep all his statutes, I will put none of the diseases upon you which I put upon the Egyptians; for I am the Lord, your healer.”
Kokborok: “Norok sói-sói khe norokni Subraiyung Kaitorni kókno khna khe tei bo chajakma tẃi khlai khe tei bini dagimarokno bwkha rw khe tei bini bebak raidarokno tẃi khe, Ijipsarokno rwmani mungsa-o bemukrokno ang norokno rw glak, tamni hwn khe ang’-no wng kha Hamya ham rwnai Subraiyung.”

English: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees; and they encamped there by the water.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Elimo sók phai kha, aro chiknwi twibru tei phangsni tal buphang tongo; tei bórok a twi rukungo batham khlai lai kha.

English: They set out from Elim, and all the congregation of the people of Israel came to the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they had departed from the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Bórok Elimni tháng lai kha tei Israilni bebak bororok hasinglwng haphar Sino sók phai kha, abo wng kha, Elim bai Sinaini kwcharo, omo bórok ha Ijipni ongkhorma nokha talnwini talni sal chibani sal klai-o.

English: And the whole congregation of the people of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness,
Kokborok: Israilrokni bebak bororok Musi bai Aronno hasing hapharo hamya hwnwi, hai hwnwi

English: and said to them, “Would that we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the fleshpots and ate bread to the full; for you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill this whole assembly with hunger.”
Kokborok: sa lai kha, “Subraiyung chwngno Ijibo buthar kheno ham kha mun. Chwng aro kisa le achugwi muihanrok ma chá kha mun tei kubun chámungrokbo ok pungwi ma chá kha mun. Tamo khe nwng chwngno o hasing hapharo chá rw yaui ok khui rwui butharna bagwi tubu kha.”

English: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a day's portion every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law or not.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang norokni bagwi nokhani chámung uatwi hai khe ua rwnai; tei bororok sal brúmno ongkhorwi salsani bagwi nangmano khochaui ma narwknai. Omo hai kheno bórok ani dagima kók khw de khwna khna ya amjogwi mannai.

English: On the sixth day, when they prepare what they bring in, it will be twice as much as they gather daily.”
Kokborok: Saldokni salo khe bórok salsani bagwi khochamani slái tei baknwi ma khochaui tiyar ma wngnai.”

English: So Moses and Aaron said to all the people of Israel, “At evening you shall know that it was the Lord who brought you out of the land of Egypt,
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bai Aron bebak Israil bororokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sal tháng khe norok saui mannai Subraiyung se norokno ha Ijipni tikhlai tubu-o,

English: and in the morning you shall see the glory of the Lord, because he has heard your murmurings against the Lord. For what are we, that you murmur against us?”
Kokborok: teini phungo khe norok Subraiyungni chẃngsachano nuknai, tamni hwn khe norok Subraiyungno hamya sa laimano bo khna kha, chwng ba sabo ba norok chwngno hamya hwn lai?”

English: And Moses said, “When the Lord gives you in the evening flesh to eat and in the morning bread to the full, because the Lord has heard your murmurings which you murmur against him—what are we? Your murmurings are not against us but against the Lord.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung norokno sarigo muihan chána rw kha tei phungo ok pung rwui chámung rw kha, tamni hwn khe norok Kaitorno hamya khe kók sa laimano Subraiyung khna kha; chwng ba sabo ba? Norok hamya sama chwngno sama klai ya abo Subraiyungno se hamya sama klai-o.”

English: And Moses said to Aaron, “Say to the whole congregation of the people of Israel, ‘Come near before the Lord, for he has heard your murmurings.’ ”
Kokborok: Musi Aronno hai hwnwi sa kha, Israilni bebak bororokno Subraiyungni samo phai di hwnwi sa di, tamni hwn khe bo norok hamya sa laimano khna kha.

English: And as Aaron spoke to the whole congregation of the people of Israel, they looked toward the wilderness, and behold, the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud.
Kokborok: Hakhe Aron Israilni bebak bororokno kók sa kheno bórok hasing haphar phaising naisik lai kha, hakhe nai di, Subraiyungni chẃngsacha chumui-o khe nukjak kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses,
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “I have heard the murmurings of the people of Israel; say to them, ‘At twilight you shall eat flesh, and in the morning you shall be filled with bread; then you shall know that I am the Lord your God.’ ”
Kokborok: “Ang Israil bororok hamya sa laimano khna kha, bórokno sa di, norok muphlik kaisani bisingo’-no muihan ma chá anw tei ai kheno norok chámung bai kupulung wngnai. Hakhe norok ano norokni Subraiyung Kaitor hwnwi sini anw.”

English: In the evening quails came up and covered the camp; and in the morning dew lay round about the camp.
Kokborok: Sarik wng kheno tokru phaiwi batham kitingwi kupulung wng phai kha tei phungo khe bathamo pantwi kupulung wng kha.

English: And when the dew had gone up, there was on the face of the wilderness a fine, flake-like thing, fine as hoarfrost on the ground.
Kokborok: A pantwini bepher ransaui tháng bai khe a hasing hapharo kahamma belep khe ha-o kothor kwsap hai klai tongo.

English: When the people of Israel saw it, they said to one another, “What is it?” For they did not know what it was. And Moses said to them, “It is the bread which the Lord has given you to eat.
Kokborok:
Israilrok abono nuk lai kheno bórok abo tamo sini lai li ya wngwi khoroksa tei khoroksano “Omo tamo?” hwnwi sẃng lai kha,
Musi bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “O chámung wng kha, Subraiyung norokno chána bagwi rwmani.

English: This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Gather of it, every man of you, as much as he can eat; you shall take an omer apiece, according to the number of the persons whom each of you has in his tent.’ ”
Kokborok: Subraiyung hai hwnwi dagi kha, norok bebakno sak sakni nangma swkno ma khochanai, núkhung mareui khoroksa khoroksani bagwi litar kwnwi wngnai.”

English: And the people of Israel did so; they gathered, some more, some less.
Kokborok: Hakhe Israil bororokbo haino khlai lai kha; baksa khe kwbáng tei baksa khe kisa khocha lai kha.

English: But when they measured it with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; each gathered according to what he could eat.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok ulo bebaknino rwtaui nai lai kha, kwbáng thumnairoknibo bára wngwi tong rwk li ya; kisa thumnairoknibo biyal wng li ya; bebakno bórok cháui manma tẃi’-no khocha lai kha.

English: And Moses said to them, “Let no man leave any of it till the morning.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Khoroksa phano abono phung jora narwgwi ta ton thung.”

English: But they did not listen to Moses; some left part of it till the morning, and it bred worms and became foul; and Moses was angry with them.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok Musini kókno khunju kan lai li ya; baksa baksa aboni bára phung jora ton lai kha, abo yóng kha ka tei sumwi pái kha; hakhe Musi bórokno jolijak kha.

English: Morning by morning they gathered it, each as much as he could eat; but when the sun grew hot, it melted.
Kokborok: Phung aisirisiri brúmno bórok abono cháui manma swkno khocha lai kha, tamo khe sal chugui tungwi phai khe abo kupru-ui thángo.

English: On the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers apiece; and when all the leaders of the congregation came and told Moses,
Kokborok: Sal dokni salo khe bórok teibo kwbáng bára khe thum lai kha, khoroksa khoroksani bagwi Omer kainwi wngnai. Dophani bebak tẃiphangrok phaiwi a kókno Musino saui khna rw kha.

English: he said to them, “This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Tomorrow is a day of solemn rest, a holy sabbath to the Lord; bake what you will bake and boil what you will boil, and all that is left over lay by to be kept till the morning.’ ”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung hai hwnwi dagi-o; khwna sói khe lenglanani sal, Subraiyungni bagwi kwthar lenglama sal; sóngwi narwkna tongmano sóng di, ruk di tei bebak bára tongmarok phung aima jora narwk di.”

English: So they laid it by till the morning, as Moses bade them; and it did not become foul, and there were no worms in it.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bórokno sama hai’-no phung jora ton kha, abo sum li ya tei yóngbo klai li ya.

English: Moses said, “Eat it today, for today is a sabbath to the Lord; today you will not find it in the field.
Kokborok: Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tini omono chá di, tini Subraiyungni bagwi lenglamani sal; tini hapharo norok abono man glak;

English: Six days you shall gather it; but on the seventh day, which is a sabbath, there will be none.”
Kokborok: tamo khe saldok sal jora abono thum di, phiya salsnini salo abo khe sabat salo abo aro kisa phano tong glak.”

English: On the seventh day some of the people went out to gather, and they found none.
Kokborok: Salsnini salo baksa bororok thumna bagwi tháng lai kha, tamo khe bórok kisabo manẃi li ya.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “How long do you refuse to keep my commandments and my laws?
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok tei bwswk sal ani dagimarokno tei kerongrokno tẃinani nai ya wngnai?

English: See! The Lord has given you the sabbath, therefore on the sixth day he gives you bread for two days; remain every man of you in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.”
Kokborok: Nai di, Subraiyung norokno sabat sal rw kha, aboni bagwino Subraiyung norokno saldokni salo salnwini bagwi chámung rw kha, bebak bororokno sak sakni nogono tong thung, salsnini salo khoroksa phano nokni ta nongkhor thung.”

English: So the people rested on the seventh day.
Kokborok: Hakhe bororok salsnini salo khe lengla lai kha.

English: Now the house of Israel called its name manna; it was like coriander seed, white, and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
Kokborok: Tabuk Israil bororok abono manna hwnwi mung phar lai kha; abo bwchlwi kuphur hai, abo piya bwtwi dalokjak biskut hai thogo.

English: And Moses said, “This is what the Lord has commanded: ‘Let an omer of it be kept throughout your generations, that they may see the bread with which I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.’ ”
Kokborok: Musi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung hai hwnwi dagi kha, norokni ulni bebak bosongrokno phunukna bagwi omer kwnwi Manna narwgwi ton di. Ijip hani tikhlai tubu phuru ang norokno hasing hapharo chá rwma chámungno nuk thung.”

English: And Moses said to Aaron, “Take a jar, and put an omer of manna in it, and place it before the Lord, to be kept throughout your generations.”
Kokborok: Musi Aronno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Jari tháisa narwgwi aro kisa manna daui norokni bosong brúmni bagwi Subraiyungni bwskango khe ton di.”

English: As the Lord commanded Moses, so Aaron placed it before the testimony, to be kept.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino dagima hai kheno Aron abono Swmai bokhokni bwskango khe beraui ton kha.

English: And the people of Israel ate the manna forty years, till they came to a habitable land; they ate the manna, till they came to the border of the land of Canaan.
Kokborok: Israil bororokno manna nokha bisi brwichi jora Kanan ha-o sók phai ya sakno chá rw kha; bórok Kanan hani páithak jora sók phai ya sakno ma chá kha.

English: (An omer is the tenth part of an ephah.)
Kokborok: Omer kaisa khe abo kwranno rwtaui kilo kaisa tei charra gram bai baksa.

English: All the congregation of the people of Israel moved on from the wilderness of Sin by stages, according to the commandment of the Lord, and camped at Rephidim; but there was no water for the people to drink.
Kokborok: Israilni bebak bororok hasing haphar Sinni ongkhorwi tháng kha, Subraiyungni dagima haino bórok aro-uro khe kutulwi tháng lai-o tei Rephidimo batham khlai lai kha, tamo khe aro bórokni bagwi nungnani twi kwrwi.

English: Therefore the people found fault with Moses, and said, “Give us water to drink.” And Moses said to them, “Why do you find fault with me? Why do you put the Lord to the proof?”
Kokborok:
Hakhe bororok Musino cha ya nuk lai kha tei hai hwnwi hwn lai kha, “Chwngno nungna bagwi twi rw di.”
Hakhe Musi bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, norok ani tamo cha ya nuk lai kha ba? Norok tamo Subraiyungno amjokna nai lai?

English: But the people thirsted there for water, and the people murmured against Moses, and said, “Why did you bring us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bororok aro belai khe twi kangjak lai kha tei Musino hai hwnwi hamya sa lai kha, “Nwng tamo chwngno, chini bwsarokno, mal-matarokno twi kang rwui butharna bagwi ha Ijipni tikhlai ma tubu lang?”

English: So Moses cried to the Lord, “What shall I do with this people? They are almost ready to stone me.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Subraiyungni thani hai hwnwi suri san kha, “Ang o bororok bai tamo khlainai? Bórok ano holong khitarwi butharna bagwi tiyar wng laijak.”

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Pass on before the people, taking with you some of the elders of Israel; and take in your hand the rod with which you struck the Nile, and go.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Israilni kisa bokhorokrokno tẃiwi nwng bororokni bwskang bwkhak lái tháng di. Nwng Nil twima-o swprakma a raidangno yago naui tháng di.

English: Behold, I will stand before you there on the rock at Horeb; and you shall strike the rock, and water shall come out of it, that the people may drink.” And Moses did so, in the sight of the elders of Israel.
Kokborok: Nai di, ang Horebo nini bwskango holong saka-o bachainai, hakhe bororok twi nungwi manna tẃi khe nwng a holongo bu di, hakhe aroni twi ongkhorwi phainai.” Hakhe Musi Israilni bokhorokrokni bwskango abo hai’-no khlai kha.

English: And he called the name of the place Massah and Meribah, because of the faultfinding of the children of Israel, and because they put the Lord to the proof by saying, “Is the Lord among us or not?”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo abono Masa tei Meriba hwnwi mung phar kha, tamni hwn ba Israilni bwsarok Subraiyungno, “Subraiyung chini bisingo to de tong kwrwi?” hwnwi amjogwi nai lai kha tei kók báng lai kha.

English: Then came Amalek and fought with Israel at Rephidim.
Kokborok: Ulo khe Amelekrok phaiwi Israilrok bai Rephidimo choba nang lai kha.

English: And Moses said to Joshua, “Choose for us men, and go out, fight with Amalek; tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in my hand.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Josuano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chini bagwi bororokno saiwi na di, ongkhorwi thángwi Amelekrok bai choba nangwi di, khwna-o ang thángwi Kaitorni raidang tẃiwi hathái bokhorogo bachauinai.”

English: So Joshua did as Moses told him, and fought with Amalek; and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josua Musi bai sajakma haino khlai kha. Amelekrok bai choba nangwi kha; phiya Musi, Aron tei Hur hatháini bokhorogo tháng kha.

English: Whenever Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed; and whenever he lowered his hand, Amalek prevailed.
Kokborok: Musi yak tisaui tong phuru khe Israilrok kwplai wngo, phiya bini yak tikhlai khe Amelekrok kwplai wngo.

English: But Moses' hands grew weary; so they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat upon it, and Aaron and Hur held up his hands, one on one side, and the other on the other side; so his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musini yak ruaijak kha, hakhe bórok holong tháisa naui bini yaphango ton lai kha hakhe bo aro achuk kha tei Aron bai Hur bini yakno tisaui ton lai kha, khoroksa yasa bwkhak tei khoroksa tei yasa bwkhak; hakhe sal tháng yasak’-no bini yak ragwi tong kha.

English: And Joshua mowed down Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josua Amelek tei bini bororokno seng bai mechenwi rw kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Write this as a memorial in a book and recite it in the ears of Joshua, that I will utterly blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omono muituni bagwi bijap kangsa-o swi di tei abono Josuano saui khwna rw di, ang Amelekrokni mungno o hani swkwma súgwi páinai.”

English: And Moses built an altar and called the name of it, The Lord is my banner,
Kokborok: Musi háyá kaisa snam kha tei bo abono “Subraiyung ani bana” wng kha hwnwi mung phar kha,

English: saying, “A hand upon the banner of the Lord! The Lord will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.”
Kokborok: tei bo hai hwnwi sa kha; “Subraiyungni bana saka-o yak kongsa tongo! Subraiyung Amelekrok bai kusung kusung choba khlainai.”

English: Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard of all that God had done for Moses and for Israel his people, how the Lord had brought Israel out of Egypt.
Kokborok: Musini bwkra Jethro, Midianrokni ochai, Kaitor Musini bagwi tamo khlai kha tei bini borok Israilni bagwi tamo khlai kha phiya bahai khe Subraiyung Israilrokno ha Ijipni tikhlaiwi tubu kha abono khna kha.

English: Now Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, had taken Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her away,
Kokborok: Hakhe Musini bwkra Jethro Musini bihik Jiporano tubu-ui phai kha, bo swkango kabu-ui phaijakmani

English: and her two sons, of whom the name of the one was Gershom (for he said, “I have been a sojourner in a foreign land”),
Kokborok: tei bini bwsa chwla khoroknwinobo tubujak kha, khoroksani mung khe Gersom (tamni hwn khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang buini ha-o haino kobonwi ma tongmani”),

English: and the name of the other, Eliezer (for he said, “The God of my father was my help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh”).
Kokborok: tei khoroksani mung khe Eliaser tamni hwn khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aphani Kaitor ano chuba kha tei Pharaoni sengnibo phiyók kha.”

English: And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife to Moses in the wilderness where he was encamped at the mountain of God.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musini bwkra Jethro Musini bwsarokno tei bihikno tubu-ui hasing haphar Kaitorni hathái-o Musi batham kha thani phai kha.

English: And when one told Moses, “Lo, your father-in-law Jethro is coming to you with your wife and her two sons with her,”
Kokborok: Tei aphuru Musino borok khoroksa hai hwnwi sa kha, “Naisik di, nwkra Jethro nihikno tei nwslasong khoroknwino tubu-ui phaiwi tongo.”

English: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and did obeisance and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare, and went into the tent.
Kokborok: Musi bwkra bai malaina bagwi tháng kha, Musi khorok bomwi khulumui bono motom su kha; bórok khoroksa tei khoroksano bahai tong hwnwi sẃng lai kha tei ríni nok bisingo habwi lai kha.

English: Then Moses told his father-in-law all that the Lord had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake, all the hardship that had come upon them in the way, and how the Lord had delivered them.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bwkrano bebakno saui khwna rw kha, Subraiyung Israilrokni bagwi Pharaono tei Ijipsarokno tamo khlai kha tei bórok bwswk lama-o tong kesep man kha, phiya bahai khe Subraiyung bórokno phiyók kha saui khwna rw kha.

English: And Jethro rejoiced for all the good which the Lord had done to Israel, in that he had delivered them out of the hand of the Egyptians.
Kokborok: Israilrokni bagwi Subraiyung bebak kaham khlaimano tei bahai khe bo Ijipsarokni yakni phiyók kha, abono tẃiwi Jethro tongthokjak kha.

English: And Jethro said, “Blessed be the Lord, who has delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jethro hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ijipsarokni yakni tei Pharaoni yakni norokno phiyóknai Subraiyung hamchajak.

English: Now I know that the Lord is greater than all gods, because he delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians, when they dealt arrogantly with them.”
Kokborok: Ang tabuk saui man kha, Subraiyung bebak mwtairokni slái kotor kuk, tamni hwn khe Ijipsarok bororokno hai’-no muchung tẃi thwng phuruno Subraiyung bórokno phiyók kha.”

English: And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, offered a burnt offering and sacrifices to God; and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before God.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musini bwkra Jethro Kaitorni thani sógwi tei ratharwi barokmung yaphar kha tei Aron Israilni bebak okrarok bai khe Kaitorni bwskango Musini bwkra bai chána phai lai kha.

English: On the morrow Moses sat to judge the people, and the people stood about Moses from morning till evening.
Kokborok: Teini salo Musi bororokno buj thumnani achuk kha, phiya bororok Musini samo phung aidropni simi sarik jora bachaui tong lai kha.

English: When Moses' father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, “What is this that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, and all the people stand about you from morning till evening?”
Kokborok: Musini bwkra bo bórokni bagwi tamo khlai bebak’-no nuk khe, bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng bororokni bagwi abo tamo khlai tong kha? Tamni nwng saichung ma achuk tei bororok nini bwskango phung aidropni simi sarik jora ma tong?”

English: And Moses said to his father-in-law, “Because the people come to me to inquire of God;
Kokborok: Musi bwkrano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tamni hwn khe bororok Kaitorni kók sina bagwi ani thani phai-o;

English: when they have a dispute, they come to me and I decide between a man and his neighbor, and I make them know the statutes of God and his decisions.”
Kokborok: Bórok uálai lai khe, ani thani phai lai-o, ang bórokni saboni cha cha ya swbai rw-o tei ang Kaitorni raidano tei Kaitor naimano saui man rw-o.”

English: Moses' father-in-law said to him, “What you are doing is not good.
Kokborok: Musini bwkra bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng khlai tongmani abo cha ya kho.

English: You and the people with you will wear yourselves out, for the thing is too heavy for you; you are not able to perform it alone.
Kokborok: Nwng tei nwng bai baksa bororok lengpranai, tamni hwn khe omo nini bagwi belaino hilik; nwng saichung omono khlai man ya.

English: Listen now to my voice; I will give you counsel, and God be with you! You shall represent the people before God, and bring their cases to God;
Kokborok: Tabuk ang samano khna di; ang nono kókbiti sanai tei Kaitorbo nwng bai tongnai, nwng bororokni wngwi Kaitorni bwskango bacha di, bórokni kókmungrokno Kaitorni thani tubu di;

English: and you shall teach them the statutes and the decisions, and make them know the way in which they must walk and what they must do.
Kokborok: hakhe nwng bórokno Kaitorni raidano phrwng di tei bini naimarokno sa di. Phiya bórok bu lama tẃi himnai tei tamo khlaithai abonobo phrwng di.

English: Moreover choose able men from all the people, such as fear God, men who are trustworthy and who hate a bribe; and place such men over the people as rulers of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
Kokborok: Aboni bisingo bebak borokni bisingni omo hai chúknai bororokno saiwi nai di, bórok Kaitorno kirinai ma wngnai, bwkha berai manjaknai borok, rang chámano selengnai borok, ma wngnai; abo hai bororokno borok khoroksaini, borok khoroksarani tei khorokbachini phiya khorokchini bokhorok khe chong di.

English: And let them judge the people at all times; every great matter they shall bring to you, but any small matter they shall decide themselves; so it will be easier for you, and they will bear the burden with you.
Kokborok: Bórok bororokno jora brúmno buj thum thung; bebak kók kutukrokno khe nini thani bórok tubu thung; hakhe nini bagwi kisa lainai tei bórok nwng bai baksa’-no poja tẃichrunai.

English: If you do this, and God so commands you, then you will be able to endure, and all this people also will go to their place in peace.”
Kokborok: Nwng omo hai khlai khe tei Kaitor nono daginai hakhe nwng soiwi mannai tei o bororokbo kwchang kwchang khe sak sakni nogo kiphilwi tháng anw.”

English: So Moses gave heed to the voice of his father-in-law and did all that he had said.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bwkra samani kókno khna kha hakhe bo sama hai’-no khlai kha.

English: Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.
Kokborok: Musi Israilrokni bisingni chúkjak bororokno saiwi na kha tei bórokni saka-o bokhorok chong kha, baksa khoroksaini bokhorok, baksa khoroksarani, baksa khorokbachini tei khorokchini, bokhorok wng kha.

English: And they judged the people at all times; hard cases they brought to Moses, but any small matter they decided themselves.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok bororokno buj thum lai máng tong lai kha; kutukrokno khe Musini thani tubu kha tamo khe tuk yarokno khe bórok bai bórok’-no kók sachu lai kha.

English: Then Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went his way to his own country.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Musi bwkrano thángna bagwi rwi rohor kha tei bo lam romwi sakni ha-o kiphilwi tháng kha.

English: On the third new moon after the people of Israel had gone forth out of the land of Egypt, on that day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.
Kokborok: Israil bororok ha Ijipni ongkhorwi thángmani talthamni swkangni salo, bórok Sinai hasinglwng hapharo phaiwi sók phai kha.

English: And when they set out from Rephidim and came into the wilderness of Sinai, they encamped in the wilderness; and there Israel encamped before the mountain.
Kokborok: Bórok Rephidimni ongkhorwi Sinai hasinglwng hapharo sók phai lai kha, bórok a hasinglwng hapharo batham khlai lai kha; bórok hathái bwskango khe batham khlai lai kha.

English: And Moses went up to God, and the Lord called to him out of the mountain, saying, “Thus you shall say to the house of Jacob, and tell the people of Israel:
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hathái-o Kaitor bai malaina tháng kha tei Subraiyung Musino hathái sakani ringhorwi hai hwnwi sa kha; “Nwng Jakobni núkhungno tei Israil bororoknobo hai hwnwi sa di;

English: You have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself.
Kokborok: Ang Ijipsarokno tamo khlai kha norok nuk kha tei toling bwsano bwkrango tẃima hai khe angbo norokno ani thani tubu-ui phai kha.

English: Now therefore, if you will obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my own possession among all peoples; for all the earth is mine,
Kokborok: Aboni bagwino tabuk norok ani kókno khunju kan khe tei ani swmaino tẃi khe, bebak bororokni bisingo norok ani borok wngnai; tamni hwn khe o ha ani máng se,

English: and you shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. These are the words which you shall speak to the children of Israel.”
Kokborok: tei norok simi se ani ochai wngwi ano monnai tei kwthar dopha wngnai. O kókrokno nwng Israilni bwsarokno ma sanai.”

English: So Moses came and called the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the Lord had commanded him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi phaiwi Israilni okrarokno ring kha tei Subraiyung bono dagimarokno bórokni bwskango sa kha.

English: And all the people answered together and said, “All that the Lord has spoken we will do.” And Moses reported the words of the people to the Lord.
Kokborok: Hakhe bebak bororok baksa khe hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Subraiyung samani bebak kókno’-no chwng khlainai” tei Musi bororokni kókno Subraiyungno saui khna rw kha.

English:
And the Lord said to Moses. “Lo, I am coming to you in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with you, and may also believe you for ever.”
Then Moses told the words of the people to the Lord.
Kokborok:
Tei Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang nwng bai kók sa lai phuru bororok khnana tẃi khe tei nono poito khlai phlai máng tongna tẃi khe ang chumui budulo khe phainai.”
Hakhe Musi Subraiyungni thani bororokni kókno sa kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow, and let them wash their garments,
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bororokni thani tháng di tei tini bai khwna bórokno kwthar khlai di tei bórokni rírokno sú rw di,

English: and be ready by the third day; for on the third day the Lord will come down upon Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.
Kokborok: tei salthamni salo khe tiyar tong di; tamni hwn khe, salthamni salo Subraiyung bebak bororokni mokol bwskango Sinai hathái-o ongkhorwi phainai.

English: And you shall set bounds for the people round about, saying, ‘Take heed that you do not go up into the mountain or touch the border of it; whoever touches the mountain shall be put to death;
Kokborok: Bororokni bagwi hathái kitingwi’-no mari rwui hai hwnwi sa di, ‘Norok hatháini saka-o thángna ta nai di, eba aboni yaphango phano yapri ta bera di; hathái-o kainai borok bo butharjaknai;

English: no hand shall touch him, but he shall be stoned or shot; whether beast or man, he shall not live.’ When the trumpet sounds a long blast, they shall come up to the mountain.”
Kokborok: khoroksani yak phano bono ta tang thung, wng ya khe bo holong khitharjaknai eba kogwi butharjaknai, mal mata phano wng thung eba borok phano wng thung, bo kwthang ma tong glak.’ Totro khorang kotor khe pung khe, bórok hathái jora ma phainai.”

English: So Moses went down from the mountain to the people, and consecrated the people; and they washed their garments.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hatháini ongkhorwi bororokni thani tháng lai kha tei bororokno kwthar khlai kha; tei bórok bórokni rírok sú lai kha.

English: And he said to the people, “Be ready by the third day; do not go near a woman.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bororokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Salthamni salo khe tiyar tong si di; bwrwirokni samo ta tháng si di.”

English: On the morning of the third day there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud upon the mountain, and a very loud trumpet blast, so that all the people who were in the camp trembled.
Kokborok: Saltham salni phung aisirisiri-o khe aro nokha gurum kha, phirang kok kha tei hathái saka-o chumui budul kosom kasa kha tei sotonwi totro pung kha, hakhe a bathamo tongnai bebak bororok kiriui kwlwi lai kha.

English: Then Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet God; and they took their stand at the foot of the mountain.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bororokno Kaitor bai malaina bagwi bathamni tikhlai tubu kha; tei bórok hatháini yaphango khe bacha lai kha.

English: And Mount Sinai was wrapped in smoke, because the Lord descended upon it in fire; and the smoke of it went up like the smoke of a kiln, and the whole mountain quaked greatly.
Kokborok: Tei Sinai hathái hoku bai kholopjak kha, tamni hwn khe Subraiyung hór bai khe aro ongkhorwi phai kha; tei aboni hoku thapa chwlani hoku ka tẃi khe saka-o kasa kha, hakhe hathái gwnangwi’-no jangrijak kha.

English: And as the sound of the trumpet grew louder and louder, Moses spoke, and God answered him in thunder.
Kokborok: Tei totroni khorang torsa rwk torsa rwk wng kha, hakhe Musi kók sa khe Kaitor bono phirang kokma tẃi khe uphilwi sa phi kha.

English: And the Lord came down upon Mount Sinai, to the top of the mountain; and the Lord called Moses to the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung Sinai hatháini buchugo khe ongkhorwi phai kha; Subraiyung Musino hathái buchugo ringhor kha, hakhe Musi kasaui tháng kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Go down and warn the people, lest they break through to the Lord to gaze and many of them perish.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ongkhorwi thángwi bororokno ragdama khe rwi di, wng ya khe bororok láiwi naina bagwi sederwi phaiwi komor baina phano tongo.

English: And also let the priests who come near to the Lord consecrate themselves, lest the Lord break out upon them.”
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyungni samo phainai ochairokbo kwthar wng thung wng ya khe Subraiyung bórokno majra rwnai.”

English: And Moses said to the Lord, “The people cannot come up to Mount Sinai; for thou thyself didst charge us, saying, ‘Set bounds about the mountain, and consecrate it.’ ”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Subraiyungno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bororok Sinai hathái jora phai man glak; tamni hwn khe nwngno chwngno ragdama khlaiwi ‘hathái kitingwi’-no mari rigwi nini bagwi khagwi kwthar khe ton di’ hwnwi sa kha.”

English: And the Lord said to him, “Go down, and come up bringing Aaron with you; but do not let the priests and the people break through to come up to the Lord, lest he break out against them.”
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung bono hai hwnwi sa kha “Yaphango thángwi Aronno nwng bai khe saka-o tubu-ui phai di; tamo khe ochairok tei bororok láiwi Subraiyungni thani ta phai thung, wng ya khe bo bórokno majra rwnai”.

English: So Moses went down to the people and told them.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hatháini ongkhorwi thángwi bórokno saui kha.

English: And God spoke all these words, saying,
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor o kókrokno hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
Kokborok: “Ang se norokni Subraiyung Kaitor, norok ha Ijibo khajagwi tong phuru khengwi tubunaisa.”

English: “You shall have no other gods before me.
Kokborok: “Ani bwskango norok tei kubun mwtairokno ta ton di.

English: “You shall not make for yourself a graven image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth;
Kokborok: “Norok sakni bagwi nokha sakani mungsa swkno kosol khe eba ha sakani eba khe twi bisingni mungsa swkno kosol khe mang ta tak di.”

English: you shall not bow down to them or serve them; for I the Lord your God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children to the third and the fourth generation of those who hate me,
Kokborok: Norok abórokno ta khulum di phiya tabo ta mon di; tamni hwn ba ang norokni Subraiyung Kaitor wng kha risi gwnang Kaitor ano khibinai buphasongni cha yani bagwi bwsarokno kusungtham tei kusung brwi majra rw-o.

English: but showing steadfast love to thousands of those who love me and keep my commandments.
Kokborok: Tamo khe ano hamjaknai tei ani kerongrokno tẃinairokno khe ang bosong kusungsara jora hamjago.

English: “You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain; for the Lord will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.
Kokborok: “Norok norokni Subraiyung Kaitorni mungno ahaino ta khu di; tamni hwn ba bini mungno ahai’-no nanairokno Subraiyung sátok yaui tong glak.”

English: “Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
Kokborok: “Sabat salno muitu ma khlainai tei kwthar khe ma tẃinai.”

English: Six days you shall labor, and do all your work;
Kokborok: Nokha saldok nwng sakni samungno tang di.

English: but the seventh day is a sabbath to the Lord your God; in it you shall not do any work, you, or your son, or your daughter, your manservant, or your maidservant, or your cattle, or the sojourner who is within your gates;
Kokborok: Tamo khe salsnini sal khe norokni Subraiyung Kaitorni bagwi sabat sal; a salo norok mungsa samung ta tang di, norokni sajla eba sajwk, norokni seleng eba selengjwk eba mal-matarok eba norokni nogo kubun phainairokbo samung ma tang glak.

English: for in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day; therefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day and hallowed it.
