Kokborok: Tamni hwn khe Subraiyung nokha saldogo ha, twijlang tei aro tongmarokno snam kha tei salsnini salo khe lengla kha; aboni bagwi Subraiyung sabat salno hamari rwui kwthar khlai kha.

English: “Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land which the Lord your God gives you.
Kokborok: “Nwma nwphano bórom khlai di, hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor norokno rwmani ha-o norok umor kwbáng thangwi mannai.

English: “You shall not kill.
Kokborok: “Nwng borok ta buthar di.

English: “You shall not commit adultery.
Kokborok: “Nwng buitali-buitala ta wng di.

English: “You shall not steal.
Kokborok: “Nwng ta khok di.

English: “You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
Kokborok: “Nwng nini nok arini borokni bagwi tatal khe kók ta sa di.

English: “You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, or his manservant, or his maidservant, or his ox, or his ass, or anything that is your neighbor's.”
Kokborok: “Nwng khoroksa swkni manwino phano ta khámai di; nwng buini bihikno ta khámai di, eba buini seleng eba selengjwkno eba bini musuk eba korai chirkano tei buini mungsa manwino ta khámai di.”

English: Now when all the people perceived the thunderings and the lightnings and the sound of the trumpet and the mountain smoking, the people were afraid and trembled; and they stood afar off,
Kokborok: Bororok nokha gurummani tei totro tammani khna kheno nokha philigwi phirang kokma nuk kha tei hoku dumsajak hathái nuk kheno kirijagwi buprajak lai kha tei hakchalo khe bachaui tong lai kha.

English: and said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will hear; but let not God speak to us, lest we die.”
Kokborok: Tei bórok Musino hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Nwng chwngno kók sa khe chwng khnanai; tamo khe Kaitorno le ta sa rw di, wng ya khe chwng thwi bainai.”

English: And Moses said to the people, “Do not fear; for God has come to prove you, and that the fear of him may be before your eyes, that you may not sin.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi bororokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ta kiri di; Kaitor norokno amjogwi nainani tei norok bono kiriui tong thung hwnwi se phai-o; hakhe norok sumsok khlai glak.”

English: And the people stood afar off, while Moses drew near to the thick darkness where God was.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Musi chumui mwnago Kaitorni samo thángwi tongwi phuru bororok khe hakchalo bachaui tongwi lai kha.

English: And the Lord said to Moses, “Thus you shall say to the people of Israel: ‘You have seen for yourselves that I have talked with you from heaven.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino, “Israil bororokno hai hwnwi sa di hwnwi dagi kha, ‘Norok mokol baithang nuk kha, ang nokha-o tong twtwi nwng bai kók samani.

English: You shall not make gods of silver to be with me, nor shall you make for yourselves gods of gold.
Kokborok: Norok norokni bagwi ani sak slái-o rukphaini eba rangchakni mwtairok ta tak di.

English: An altar of earth you shall make for me and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen; in every place where I cause my name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.
Kokborok: Ani bagwi hani háyá kaisa snam di, aboni saka-o norok sógwi yaphar di, kwthamungni yapharmung tei norokni merarok, musukrok ratharwi yaphar di; ang norokni bagwi rwma bebak khulum thani’-no ang nini thani phaiwi nono hamari rwnai.

English: And if you make me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stones; for if you wield your tool upon it you profane it.
Kokborok: Tumungno norok ani bagwi holongni háyá snam khe, rajak holong bai ta khlai di, tamni hwn khe norok holong saka-o sor bai khur khe norok abono ani samungni bagwi chúk ya khlai-o.

English: And you shall not go up by steps to my altar, that your nakedness be not exposed on it.’
Kokborok: Norok ani háyá-o yakálam tẃi kasaui ta tháng di, aro norokni sak bukchano ta phunuk di.’

English: “Now these are the ordinances which you shall set before them.
Kokborok: “Tabuk nwng bórokno o raidarokno rw di.

English: When you buy a Hebrew slave, he shall serve six years, and in the seventh he shall go out free, for nothing.
Kokborok: Nwng Hibru seleng pai khe, bo nokha bisidok jora nono monwi tongnai tei nokha bisisni-o khe bo mungsa rw yaui’-no yógwi thángnai.

English: If he comes in single, he shall go out single; if he comes in married, then his wife shall go out with him.
Kokborok: Bo saichung phai khe saichungno kiphilwi thángnai tei bo káijak hwn khe bini bihikbo bo bai baksano ongkhorwi thángnai.

English: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall be her master's and he shall go out alone.
Kokborok: Tumungno bini nokphang bono káiwi bwrwi rw khe, hakhe a bwrwi chwla bwsarok phano eba bwrwi bwsarok phano man khe, hakhe a bihik bai bwsarok abo nokphangni wngnai, a seleng sak saichung kheno ma ongkhorwi thángnai.

English: But if the slave plainly says, ‘I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free,’
Kokborok: Tamo khe a seleng srai-srai khe ang ani nokphangno hamjago tei ani hikno, ani bwsarokno hamjago hwnwi sa khe; ang yógwi tháng ya hwnwi sa khe,

English: then his master shall bring him to God, and he shall bring him to the door or the doorpost; and his master shall bore his ear through with an awl; and he shall serve him for life.
Kokborok: hakhe bini nokphang bono Kaitorni thani tubu-ui phainai tei bo bono duglamo eba duglamni thongla-o tubunai; hakhe bini nokphang bini khunjuno sor bai phugwi rwnai. Abo hai khe a seleng thwi yasakni bagwi bini seleng wng phlai sinai.

English: “When a man sells his daughter as a slave, she shall not go out as the male slaves do.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk borok bini bwsajwkno selengjwk wngna bagwi phal khe bo chwla selengrok yók tẃi khe yógwi man ya.

English: If she does not please her master, who has designated her for himself, then he shall let her be redeemed; he shall have no right to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt faithlessly with her.
Kokborok: Tumungno hik songjakna bagwi chongwi phaljak khe, phiya ba nokphang bono khá phurjak ya khe bo bono phiyógwi rw thung; bini nokphang bono buini borokni thani phalwi man glak, tamni hwn khe a nokphang bono bandi kha.

English: If he designates her for his son, he shall deal with her as with a daughter.
Kokborok: Khoroksa swk borok bo bwslani bagwi bwrwi seleng pai khe bo a bwrwino sajwk hai khe ma tonnai.

English: If he takes another wife to himself, he shall not diminish her food, her clothing, or her marital rights.
Kokborok: Tumungno bo tei khoroksano hik na phi khe, bo a swkangni bihikno swkango chá rw tẃi tei kan rw tẃi kheno chámung rwui kanmung rwui ma tonnai tei swkango hikni manthai tong tẃi kheno ma rwnai.

English: And if he does not do these three things for her, she shall go out for nothing, without payment of money.
Kokborok: Tumungno bo bono a mungthamrokno rw ya hwn khe, bihik bono mungsa rwyaui’-no yóknai.

English: “Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall be put to death.
Kokborok: “Tumung khoroksa swk khoroksa swkno bu khe tei a bujaknai borok thwi khe hakhe a bunai borok bo ma butharjakna nangnai.

English: But if he did not lie in wait for him, but God let him fall into his hand, then I will appoint for you a place to which he may flee.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo bono butharna hwnwi butharma ya deraui se hwn khe, Kaitor bini yapha-o se bono khiklai thung, hakhe ang nono, bo buro khárnai saui rwnai tei bo aro’-no khárwi yóknai.

English: But if a man willfully attacks another to kill him treacherously, you shall take him from my altar, that he may die.
Kokborok: Tamo khe khoroksa tei khoroksano jolima bai butharna hwnwi buthar khe, bo butharjaknai, yóknani bagwi ani háyá-o phano khár thung bono aroni tikhlai buthar di.”

English: “Whoever strikes his father or his mother shall be put to death.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk bo bini buma eba buphano buthar khe a borok ma butharjaknai.

English: “Whoever steals a man, whether he sells him or is found in possession of him, shall be put to death.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk bini nogo seleng khe tonnani eba phalnani bagwi borok khok khe a khoknai borok ma butharjaknai.

English: “Whoever curses his father or his mother shall be put to death.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk bini bumano eba buphano swrai khe bo ma butharjaknai.

English: “When men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist and the man does not die but keeps his bed,
Kokborok: “Bororok uálai laiwi khoroksa tei khoroksano holong bai khitar khe eba bini yak bai phano bu-ui hai phano a borok thwi li ya, tamo khe thongaro klaiwi ma tong khe,

English: then if the man rises again and walks abroad with his staff, he that struck him shall be clear; only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall have him thoroughly healed.
Kokborok: hakhe tumungno omo hai khe a borok hamwi bacha khe tei raidang sungwi himwi man khe, hakhe a swpraknai borok yóknai; tamo khe a borokni samung tangna jora keplemano bo su-ui ma rwnai; tei ham rwui rw suknani sak ma tharnai.

English: “When a man strikes his slave, male or female, with a rod and the slave dies under his hand, he shall be punished.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk bini selengno sorni raidang bai bu khe tei bini yago thwi khe bo majra ma chánai.”

English: But if the slave survives a day or two, he is not to be punished; for the slave is his money.
Kokborok: Tamo khe a seleng salsa eba salnwi jora thangwi tong khe bo majra ma chána nang glak; tamni hwn khe bini seleng wng kha bo bai paijak.

English: “When men strive together, and hurt a woman with child, so that there is a miscarriage, and yet no harm follows, the one who hurt her shall be fined, according as the woman's husband shall lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
Kokborok: “Bororok bú lai tok lai wng phuru sak kupulung bwrwi khoroksa bujagwi bini okni bwsa chokhrai kha, phiya aswk sago dera li ya hwn khe, a bwrwini bwsai bono sanmani swk tei buj thumnairok chongma swkno suwi ma rwnai.”

English: If any harm follows, then you shall give life for life,
Kokborok: Tumungno mungsa swk dera khe hakhe langmani bagwi langma,

English: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
Kokborok: mokolni bagwi mokol, buani bagwi bua, yakni bagwi yak, yakungni bagwi yakung,

English: burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
Kokborok: sókmani khe sókjaknai, kwsani bagwi kwsa tei swprakma bagwi swprakjakna nangnai.

English: “When a man strikes the eye of his slave, male or female, and destroys it, he shall let the slave go free for the eye's sake.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk bo bini selengsa eba selengjwkno mokolo bu khe tei bumani bagwi a mokol dera khe, bo mokolni bagwi a selengno ahai’-no phiyógwi ma rwnai.”

English: If he knocks out the tooth of his slave, male or female, he shall let the slave go free for the tooth's sake.
Kokborok: Tumungno bo bini selengni bua swkrai rw khe, seleng eba selengjwk phano wng thung bo a selengno bini bua swkraimani bai haino phiyógwi ma rwnai.

English: “When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox shall be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be clear.
Kokborok: “Musuk chwla masa khoroksa chwla eba bwrwino phano khaiwi buthar khe, a musukno holong khitarwi ma butharnai tei bini bahanno ma chá glak; phiya ba a musukni nokphang khe yóknai.”

English: But if the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past, and its owner has been warned but has not kept it in, and it kills a man or a woman, the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.
Kokborok: Tamo khe a musuk chwla swkangrogobo abo haino khai khai-o hwn khe, phiya nokphangno swkango’-no sajak, hai phano bo abono khaui ton ya hwn khe tei abo borok khoroksa swk chwla eba bwrwino phano khaiwi buthar khe a musukno holong khitarwi ma butharnai tei bini a nokphangbo ma butharjaknai.

English: If a ransom is laid on him, then he shall give for the redemption of his life whatever is laid upon him.
Kokborok: Phiya rang rwui butharjaknani yókna bagwi lama rwjak khe, bo rang tẃina nangma swk’-no ma rwnai.

English: If it gores a man's son or daughter, he shall be dealt with according to this same rule.
Kokborok: Tumungno musuk masa swk khoroksa swk chwla bwsa eba bwrwi bwsano buthar khe omobo abo haino wngnai.

English: If the ox gores a slave, male or female, the owner shall give to their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
Kokborok: A musuk mála, seleng eba selengjwkno buthar khe, a musukni nokphang selengni nokphangno, rukphai budul dulthamchi ma rwnai tei a musuk khe holong khitarwi butharjakna nangnai.

English: “When a man leaves a pit open, or when a man digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or an ass falls into it,
Kokborok: “Borok khoroksa swk hakhor kholop yaui ton khe eba hakhor khurwi abono kholop yaui haino ton khe aro musuk chwla masa phano eba koraichirka phano a hakhoro klai khe,

English: the owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money to its owner, and the dead beast shall be his.
Kokborok: A hakhorni nokphang bo a malni nokphangno rang ma rwnai; bo aboni nokphangno rang ma su-ui rwnai tei kwthwi mal khe bini wngnai.”

English: “When one man's ox hurts another's, so that it dies, then they shall sell the live ox and divide the price of it; and the dead beast also they shall divide.
Kokborok: “Khoroksani musuk mála tei khoroksanino khaiwi buthar khe, a khoroknwirok butharnai musukno kwthangno phalwi rang banta khe ma na lainai; tei a kwthwini bahanbo banta khe ma na lainai.”

English: Or if it is known that the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past, and its owner has not kept it in, he shall pay ox for ox, and the dead beast shall be his.
Kokborok: Tamo khe a musuk mála omo hai’-no khai khai laiwi butharma tong khe nokphang hui tonma kwrwi hwn khe, bo bini musuk mála kwthang masa rwui bórokno su-ui ma rwnai, tamo khe musuk kwthwi khe bini wngnai.

English: “If a man steals an ox or a sheep, and kills it or sells it, he shall pay five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep. He shall make restitution; if he has nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.
Kokborok: “Tumungno khoroksa swk borok bo musuk masa swk eba mera phano masa swk khok khe tei bo abono buthar khe eba phal khe bo musuk masani bagwi musuk mak ba tei mera masani bagwi mera mak brwi kiphilwi ma rwnai, a khokmano su-ui rwna bagwi bini mungsa kwrwi hwn khe hakhe bo sakno phalwi rang ma rwnai.”

English: If the stolen beast is found alive in his possession, whether it is an ox or an ass or a sheep, he shall pay double.
Kokborok: Tumungno sikhok nok khurwi hap phuru manjagwi bujak khe tei bujakma bai thwi khe bo thwima bai mungsa majra man glak.

English: “If a thief is found breaking in, and is struck so that he dies, there shall be no bloodguilt for him;
Kokborok: Tumungno salo hwn khe hakhe borok butharma swk majra wngnai; tei khokmano su-ui ma rwnai; bini sunani mungsa kwrwi hwn khe, hakhe khokmani bagwi bo ma phaljaknai.

English: but if the sun has risen upon him, there shall be bloodguilt for him.
Kokborok: Musuk, korai chirka eba mera khokmarok mungsa swk kwthang manjak khe, bo aboni kusungnwi ma su-ui rwnai.

English: “When a man causes a field or vineyard to be grazed over, or lets his beast loose and it feeds in another man's field, he shall make restitution from the best in his own field and in his own vineyard.
Kokborok: Khoroksa swk borok buini mai bwlwngo eba angur bwlwngo musuk mera chá rw khe eba phiyógwi rw khe bo bini mai bwlwngni tei angur bwlwngni kaham kuk bwthái rwui su-ui ma rwnai.

English: “When fire breaks out and catches in thorns so that the stacked grain or the standing grain or the field is consumed, he that kindled the fire shall make full restitution.
Kokborok: “Hór bachaui busulwngo hór nang khe, khoroksa swkni mai burung eba mai bwlwng kham khe, hakhe a sóknai borok bo bebak’-no su-ui ma rwnai.”

English: “If a man delivers to his neighbor money or goods to keep, and it is stolen out of the man's house, then, if the thief is found, he shall pay double.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk borok bo bini nok arini borok khoroksa swkni thani bini rangrok eba manwirok tisa rwi ton khe, hakhe abo a borokni nokni khokjak khe tei a khoknai sikhok manjak khe, bo aboni kusungnwi su-ui ma rwnai.”

English: If the thief is not found, the owner of the house shall come near to God, to show whether or not he has put his hand to his neighbor's goods.
Kokborok: Phiya a sikhok manjak ya hwn khe a nokni nokphang bo Kaitorni bwskango phaiwi bo a nok arini borokni manwirokno bo da yak rw kha, eba rw ya ma phunuknai.

English: “For every breach of trust, whether it is for ox, for ass, for sheep, for clothing, or for any kind of lost thing, of which one says, ‘This is it,’ the case of both parties shall come before God; he whom God shall condemn shall pay double to his neighbor.
Kokborok: “Su-ui rwna khe omo hai wngnai, musuk chwla phano wng thung, korai chirkani bagwi phano wng thung, merani bagwi phano, rí-chumrokni bagwi phano eba mungsa swk manwi kwmamarokni bagwi phano wng thung, omono a kwmama manwi hwnwi sa khe, manjaknai bai nokphang bórok kwnwnwi’-no Kaitorni bwskango ma phainai; Kaitor bai majra berjaknai borok bo’-no bini nok arini borokno kusungnwi bára khe su-ui ma ranai.”

English: “If a man delivers to his neighbor an ass or an ox or a sheep or any beast to keep, and it dies or is hurt or is driven away, without any one seeing it,
Kokborok: “Tumungno khoroksa swk borok bini nok arini borokno korai chirka phano eba musuk chwla phano eba mera phano mungsa swk mal-mata tonna bagwi rw khe tei abo nuk ya sini’-no thwi khe eba bujak khe, eba swkwma kha hwn khe,

English: an oath by the Lord shall be between them both to see whether he has not put his hand to his neighbor's property; and the owner shall accept the oath, and he shall not make restitution.
Kokborok: hakhe bo bini nokphangni manwi yak da rw rw ya sijakna bagwi a khoroknwirokni bisingo swmai ma tang lainai, a nokphangni thani swmai ma tangnai hakhe bo su-ui rwna nang glak.”

English: But if it is stolen from him, he shall make restitution to its owner.
Kokborok: Tamo khe abo bini aroni khokjak khe, bo aboni nokphangno su-ui ma rwnai.

English: If it is torn by beasts, let him bring it as evidence; he shall not make restitution for what has been torn.
Kokborok: Tumungno abo bolongni manwirok bai uátharjak khe, bo abono tubu-ui ma phunuk phainai, bo a uátharjakmano su-ui rwna nang glak.

English: “If a man borrows anything of his neighbor, and it is hurt or dies, the owner not being with it, he shall make full restitution.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk bo bini nok arini borokni aroni musuk-toknokrok solok na khe tei abo bujak khe eba thwi khe, nokphang aboni-o mungsa majra kwrwi, bo bebakno su-ui ma rwnai.”

English: If the owner was with it, he shall not make restitution; if it was hired, it came for its hire.
Kokborok: Tumungno nokphang arono tong khe su-ui rwna nang ya; abo keria nama hwn khe abo keriani a rangno suma hai wngnai.

English: “If a man seduces a virgin who is not betrothed, and lies with her, he shall give the marriage present for her, and make her his wife.
Kokborok: “Tumungno khoroksa swk kók sẃngjak ya bwrwi siklano belogwi a bwrwini sak tang trwng khe, bo bono káijakmungni hili ma rwnai tei bono’-no hik ma songnai.”

English: If her father utterly refuses to give her to him, he shall pay money equivalent to the marriage present for virgins.
Kokborok: Bwrwini bupha bono rwna soi súk ya khe, bo bwrwi siklano káijakmungni hili ma rwna nangma tẃi’-no ma rwnai.

English: “You shall not permit a sorceress to live.
Kokborok: “Norok swkal kókmung kwrwngrokno kwthang ta ton di.”

English: “Whoever lies with a beast shall be put to death.
Kokborok: “Khoroksa swk borok mal-matarok bai sak gwdalnairok ma butharjaknai.”

English: “Whoever sacrifices to any god, save to the Lord only, shall be utterly destroyed.
Kokborok: “Subraiyungno karwi tei kubun mwtairokni thani ratharwi yapharnairok sokomorjak suknai.”

English: “You shall not wrong a stranger or oppress him, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: “Norok buini hani bororokni hamya ta khlai di eba bono ta kusup di, tamni hwn khe norokbo salsa ha Ijibo buini borok tongmani.”

English: You shall not afflict any widow or orphan.
Kokborok: Norok khoroksa randijwkmarokno phano eba ma pha kwrwirokno ta sátok di.

English: If you do afflict them, and they cry out to me, I will surely hear their cry;
Kokborok: Norok bórokno sátok khe tei ani thani kap khe ang bórokni kapmano khnanai;

English: and my wrath will burn, and I will kill you with the sword, and your wives shall become widows and your children fatherless.
Kokborok: hakhe ang joliui tung phlainai tei ang norokno seng bai butharnai hakhe nihiksong randi wngnai tei nwsarok bupha kwrwi wngnai.

English: “If you lend money to any of my people with you who is poor, you shall not be to him as a creditor, and you shall not exact interest from him.
Kokborok: Nwng norokni bisingni, ani bororokni khoroksa swk bigrano rang solok rw khe, norok lakai rwnairok hai ta wng di, bini thani rangni yagulo rangni bwsa ta na di.

English: If ever you take your neighbor's garment in pledge, you shall restore it to him before the sun goes down;
Kokborok: Tumungno nwng norokni bisingni khoroksa swkni rírokno mungsa swkni kók khaui na khe abono sal tháng ya sini’-no kiphilwi rw di;

English: for that is his only covering, it is his mantle for his body; in what else shall he sleep? And if he cries to me, I will hear, for I am compassionate.
Kokborok: tamni hwn khe, bini sago kannani bagwi a rí kangsa susuk ja se, bo tamo bai thu janai? Bo ani thani kap khe, ang khnanai tamni hwn khe ang wng kha khairokma gwnang.

English: “You shall not revile God, nor curse a ruler of your people.
Kokborok: “Nwng Kaitorno ta kebeng di eba norokni, bororokni tẃiphangrokno ta swrai di.

English:
“You shall not delay to offer from the fulness of your harvest and from the outflow of your presses.
“The first-born of your sons you shall give to me.
Kokborok:
“Norokni maichlamni tei mai rwbamni mairokni, angur pheremani bwtwirokni rwnani ta ler di.
“Nini swkang sakrano ano ma rwnai.”

English: You shall do likewise with your oxen and with your sheep: seven days it shall be with its dam; on the eighth day you shall give it to me.
Kokborok: Norokni mal-matarokno tei meraroknobo abo hai’-no khlai di. Bumasong bai khe bórokno nokha salsni jora ton di, salcharni salo khe ani thani rw di.

English: “You shall be men consecrated to me; therefore you shall not eat any flesh that is torn by beasts in the field; you shall cast it to the dogs.
Kokborok: “Norok ani bagwi khalwi borok kwthar ma wngnai; abo bai’-no norok bolongni manwirok bai hapharo uátharjak muihanrokno ta chá di; abórokno sẃino chá rw di.”

English: “You shall not utter a false report. You shall not join hands with a wicked man, to be a malicious witness.
Kokborok: “Nwng tatal kók auai ta khlai di, nwng borok cha yarok bai kwthaui tatal kók ta sachru di.”

English: You shall not follow a multitude to do evil; nor shall you bear witness in a suit, turning aside after a multitude, so as to pervert justice;
Kokborok: Samung hamya tangna bagwi norok borok kwbángmarokno ta rwkthar di, eba bujsóino cha ya khlaina bagwi borok kwbángmarok bai pal khe kók tatal ta sa chru di.

English: nor shall you be partial to a poor man in his suit.
Kokborok: Eba buj khlai thani bigrarokno phikung khe ta nuk di.

English: “If you meet your enemy's ox or his ass going astray, you shall bring it back to him.
Kokborok: “Nini arini musuk phano eba bini korai chirka phano yógwi thángma nuk khe nwng abono romwi bini thani kiphilwi rw phai di.”

English: If you see the ass of one who hates you lying under its burden, you shall refrain from leaving him with it, you shall help him to lift it up.
Kokborok: Nono naislernaini koraichirka poja hilik tẃiwi klai tongmani nuk khe, nwng muchung ya phano abono tisaui sochaui rw di; haino nugwi lái ta tháng di.

English: “You shall not pervert the justice due to your poor in his suit.
Kokborok: Kwrwisa borok bujni bagwi tháng khe bono sói khe buj khlai ya ta wng di.

English: Keep far from a false charge, and do not slay the innocent and righteous, for I will not acquit the wicked.
Kokborok: Tatal khe majra bermarok bai hakchal khe tong di, borok sóino tei majra kwrwino haino ta buthar di, tamni hwn khe borok hamyano ang phiyók ya.

English: And you shall take no bribe, for a bribe blinds the officials, and subverts the cause of those who are in the right.
Kokborok: Norok rang ta chá di, abo buj thumnairokno mokol nuk ya khlai-o tei abo borok sóirokno bujsói man thani dera rw-o.

English: “You shall not oppress a stranger; you know the heart of a stranger, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: “Norok buini hani bororokno ta sátok di. Norok buini hani bororokni bwkhano saui mano tamni hwn khe norokbo salsa ha Ijibo buini hani borokno tongmani.”

English: “For six years you shall sow your land and gather in its yield;
Kokborok: “Bisidok cho yaui norok norokni hapharo mai khul khlai di tei manma jora tẃi bebakno thumwi rúng di.

English: but the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow, that the poor of your people may eat; and what they leave the wild beasts may eat. You shall do likewise with your vineyard, and with your olive orchard.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bisisnini bisi-o khe bathak di, aro karwi kabumano mungsano bo ta ra di. Norokni bisingni kwrwirok ma chána twi khe tei tong rwkma bárarokno bolongni mal-matarok chá thung. Nini angur bwlwngnobo abo haino khlai di tei Olib buphangroknobo haino khlai di.”

English: “Six days you shall do your work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; that your ox and your ass may have rest, and the son of your bondmaid, and the alien, may be refreshed.
Kokborok: “Nokha Salsni-o nokha saldok samung tang si di, abo hai khe nini samung tangnai selengrok, buini hani bororok lenglaui mannai. Nini musuk tei korai chirkarokbo lenglaui mannai.

English: Take heed to all that I have said to you; and make no mention of the names of other gods, nor let such be heard out of your mouth.
Kokborok: “Norok ang samani bebakno khna di. Tei kubun mwtairokni mung ta na di; bórokni mungrokno norokni khukni ta khna thung.”

English: “Three times in the year you shall keep a feast to me."
Kokborok: “Ani mungwi bisi-o uáitham chámung ter ma tẃinai.”

English: You shall keep the feast of unleavened bread; as I commanded you, you shall eat unleavened bread for seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in it you came out of Egypt. None shall appear before me empty-handed.
Kokborok: Norok auang bánglani terno ma tẃinai. Ang norokno dagi-o Abib talni chongjak jora-o norok salsni jora auang bángla ma chánai, norok a talo’-no ha Ijipni yógwi phai-o. Khoroksa phano yak bukcha khe ani thani phai man glak.

English: You shall keep the feast of harvest, of the first fruits of your labor, of what you sow in the field. You shall keep the feast of ingathering at the end of the year, when you gather in from the field the fruit of your labor.
Kokborok:
Norok ramani terno khlai di; norokni hapharo sarmani tháiskangni mai-khul kwtalni terno ma tẃinai.
Norok thansa khe raui pái phuru khe abono khlai di; norok bwtháirok thumwi pái khebo khlai di.

English: Three times in the year shall all your males appear before the Lord God.
Kokborok: Bisi-o uáitham norokni chwla bwsarok Subraiyung Kaitorno khulumna bagwi ma phainai.

English: “You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread, or let the fat of my feast remain until the morning.
Kokborok: “Norok mui ratharwi thẃi yaphar phuru khe chuan buljak auang ano ta yaphar di. O terni jora-o ani thani ratharwi yapharmani muini buphamrokno teini phung jora ma ton glak.

English:
“The first of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of the Lord your God.
“You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.
Kokborok:
“Norokni tháiskangni swkangni bwtháino norok Subraiyung Kaitorni thani ma tubunai.”
“Abuk twi cháui tong kho omo hai mera eba punsarokno norok ta song di.”

English: “Behold, I send an angel before you, to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place which I have prepared.
Kokborok: “Nai di, ang norokni bagwi tiyar khlaima ha-o tubuna bagwi tei lama-o tháng phuru norokno mwrwkna bagwi norokni slái swkango khe khoroksa suarino rwui rohornai.”

English: Give heed to him and hearken to his voice, do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression; for my name is in him.
Kokborok: Bono mokol kai di, bini kókrokno khna di. Bono ta kebeng di, ang se bono rohoro bo norok cha ya khlaimano nakar glak.

English: “But if you hearken attentively to his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
Kokborok: Phiya norok bini kók khna khe tei ang dagima hai bebak samung khlai khe, ang norokni bebak arirokni ari tei kebengnairokno kebengnai wngnai.

English: “When my angel goes before you, and brings you in to the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, and I blot them out,
Kokborok: Ani suari norokni slái swkang swkang thángwi norokno Amorsarokni ha-o tlangnai; Hitisarokno, Peressarokno tei Kanansarokno, Hibisarokno, Jebussarokno sokomornai.

English: you shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do according to their works, but you shall utterly overthrow them and break their pillars in pieces.
Kokborok: Bórokni mwtairokni thani khorok ta bom di eba bórokno tabo ta mon di tei bórokni samung khlaima tẃi ta khlai di. Bórokni holong thonglarokno swbai khibi di.

English: You shall serve the Lord your God, and I will bless your bread and your water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of you.
Kokborok: Ang norokni Subraiyung Kaitorno mon di, ang norokno mai, twi rwui hamari rwnai tei norokni kulum kwsarokno naui páinai.

English: None shall cast her young or be barren in your land; I will fulfil the number of your days.
Kokborok: Norokni bwrwirok khoroksabo bwsa chokhrai glak tei banjibo tong glak; ang norokni umorno kasuk rwnai.

English: I will send my terror before you, and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
Kokborok: Norokni slái swkang ang bororokni thani kirimung rwi rohorwi sikirinai, norok choba khlaimani bebak bororokno ang kwmam rwnai, ang norokni arirokno uphil rwnai tei norokni bwskangni khár rwui rohornai.

English: And I will send hornets before you, which shall drive out Hivite, Canaanite, and Hittite from before you.
Kokborok: Ang norokni arirokno tong kesebo khiklainai; ang norokni bwskango tháng khe Hibisarokno, Kanansarokno tei Hitisarokno khár rwui rohornai.

English: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and the wild beasts multiply against you.
Kokborok: Bolongni mal-matarok akarnai se tei habo komor ya tẃi khe ang bórokno nokha bisani bisingo rwui rohor glak.

English: Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you are increased and possess the land.
Kokborok: Norok erwi hano yago naui amanna wng ya sak, ang bórokno kisa kisa khe norokni bwskangni nornai.

English: And I will set your bounds from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the Euphrates; for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand, and you shall drive them out before you.
Kokborok: Ang norokni hano phuarnai. Twijlang kwchakni simi Philistiasarokni twijlang jora tei hasinglwngni simi Uphratis jora, ang norokni ha-o tongnairokno norokni yago rwnai tei norok bórokno norokni bwskangni riphrigwi rohornai.

English: You shall make no covenant with them or with their gods.
Kokborok: Norok bórok bai tei bórokni mwtairok bai kók ta kha lai di.

English: They shall not dwell in your land, lest they make you sin against me; for if you serve their gods, it will surely be a snare to you.”
Kokborok: Bórokno norokni ha-o ta tong rw di; wng ya khe bórok norokno ani thani sumsok khlai rwnai. Norok bórokni mwtairokno mon khe, abo norokni bagwi uarai se wngnai.

English: And he said to Moses, “Come up to the Lord, you and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel, and worship afar off.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Hathái saka-o nwng bai Aron, Nadab bai Abihu, tei khorok snichi Israilni tẃiphangrok ani thani phai di tei hakchalo tong twtwi’-no ano khulum di.

English: Moses alone shall come near to the Lord; but the others shall not come near, and the people shall not come up with him.”
Kokborok: Musi simino Subraiyungni samo phai di; tamo khe kubunrok samo ta phai di, bororokbo bo bai baksa hathái saka-o ta phai thung.”

English: Moses came and told the people all the words of the Lord and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said, “All the words which the Lord has spoken we will do.”
Kokborok: Musi phai kha, Hakhe bororokno Subraiyung samani bebak kókrokno tei dagimarokno saui khna rw kha; bebak bororok’-no khorang baksa khe hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Subraiyung samani bebak kókrok sa tẃi’-no chwng khlainai.”

English: And Moses wrote all the words of the Lord. And he rose early in the morning, and built an altar at the foot of the mountain, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi Subraiyungni bebak kókrokno swiwi narwk kha. Bo aidrobo bachaui hatháini yaphango háyá kaisa snam kha tei chinwi thongla Israil bosongni chinwino rwgwi tisa kha.

English: And he sent young men of the people of Israel, who offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen to the Lord.
Kokborok: Tei bo Israilni chwla siklarokno rwui rohor khe bórok sógwi yaphar kha tei Subraiyungni thani khakchangni yapharmungni musuk chwla ratharwi yaphar kha.

English: And Moses took half of the blood and put it in basins, and half of the blood he threw against the altar.
Kokborok: Musi kisa thẃirokni naui dangai-o khe ton kha tei baksa khe háyá-o sarwi rw kha.

English: Then he took the book of the covenant, and read it in the hearing of the people; and they said, “All that the Lord has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.”
Kokborok: Ulo khe bo swmaini bijapno naui lukuno poriui khna rw kha; hakhe bórok hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Subraiyung sama haino chwng bebakno khlainai tei chwng bebak kókno khnaui tongnai.”

English: And Moses took the blood and threw it upon the people, and said, “Behold the blood of the covenant which the Lord has made with you in accordance with all these words.”
Kokborok: Tei Musi thẃi naui bororokni saka-o sarwi rwui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, Swmaini o thẃino, omono Subraiyung o kókno tẃiwi kók rw-o.”

English: Then Moses and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel went up,
Kokborok: Hakhe, Musi, Aron, Nadab tei Abihu tei Israilni khoroksnichirok kasaui tháng lai kha,

English: and they saw the God of Israel; and there was under his feet as it were a pavement of sapphire stone, like the very heaven for clearness.
Kokborok: Bórok Israilni Kaitorno nuk kha; bini yakung yaphango holong kwkhrang saphir hai tei chuknoha chẃngma hai’-no kwchẃng.

English: And he did not lay his hand on the chief men of the people of Israel; they beheld God, and ate and drank.
Kokborok: Tei bo Israilni bokhorokrokni saka-o yak rw li ya; bórok Kaitorno nuk kha tei chá lai kha, nung lai kha.

English: The Lord said to Moses, “Come up to me on the mountain, and wait there; and I will give you the tables of stone, with the law and the commandment, which I have written for their instruction.”
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng hathái saka jora kasaui ani thani phai di tei aro naisingwi tong di; ang nono Kerong tei dagimungrok swijak holong bwlap rwnai.” Abo ang bórokno phrwngna bagwi swimani.

English: So Moses rose with his servant Joshua, and Moses went up into the mountain of God.
Kokborok: Hakhe bini seleng Josua bai bacha kha tei Musi Kaitorni hathái-o kasaui tháng kha.

English: And he said to the elders, “Tarry here for us, until we come to you again; and, behold, Aaron and Hur are with you; whoever has a cause, let him go to them.”
Kokborok: Musi okrarokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng norokni thani kiphilwi phai ya sak oro naisingwi tong di; ‘Nai di, Aron bai Hur norok bai tong rwk kha; norokni khoroksa swkni kók bángmarok tong khe a borok bórokni thani tháng thung.’ ”

English: Then Moses went up on the mountain, and the cloud covered the mountain.
Kokborok: Hakhe Musi hathái-o kasaui tháng khe chumui hatháino kobol kha.

English: The glory of the Lord settled on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days; and on the seventh day he called to Moses out of the midst of the cloud.
Kokborok: Sinai hathái-o Subraiyungni chẃngsacha tong phai kha, abo nokha saldok jora chumui bai kobolwi tong kha; ulo nokha salsnini salo khe Kaitor chumui bisingni Musino ring kha.

English: Now the appearance of the glory of the Lord was like a devouring fire on the top of the mountain in the sight of the people of Israel.
Kokborok: Tabuk Israilrok nuk kha hathái bokhorogo, Subraiyungni chẃngsacha, monoknai hór hai khe nukjak kha.

English: And Moses entered the cloud, and went up on the mountain. And Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.
Kokborok: Musi chumui bisingo habwi tháng kha tei hathái kuchugo kasaui kha. Musi aro nokha sal brwichi tei horbrwichi tongwi kha.

English: The Lord said to Moses,
Kokborok: Subraiyung Musino hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “Speak to the people of Israel, that they take for me an offering; from every man whose heart makes him willing you shall receive the offering for me.
Kokborok: “Israil bororokno sa di, bórok ani thani yapharmung tubu thung; bebak bororok bwkha-o muchungma tẃi yapharmano nwng ani bagwi yachak di.

English: And this is the offering which you shall receive from them: gold, silver, and bronze,
Kokborok: Tei omo hai yapharmarokno nwng bórokni aroni yachak di; rangchak, rukphai, pitol,

English: blue and purple and scarlet stuff and fine twined linen, goats' hair,
Kokborok: lilon kaham, kwkhrang, kormo tei kochual; tei pun buikhumuni rí takjak,

English: tanned rams' skins, goatskins, acacia wood,
Kokborok: mera bukur kwchak khlaijak; pun bukur kaham; akaia buphang;

English: oil for the lamps, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense,
Kokborok: Chatirokni bagwi thok, phuljaknai thokni bagwi mosla tei satrai sóknani bagwi motom manwi.

English: onyx stones, and stones for setting, for the ephod and for the breastpiece.
Kokborok: Oniks holongrok, ephod tei pannani holongrok tei khaklapni bagwi rí thepajaknairok.

English: And let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell in their midst.
Kokborok: Tei ang bórokni bisingo tongwi manna tẃi khe ani bagwi nokthar tang thung.

English: According to all that I show you concerning the pattern of the tabernacle, and of all its furniture, so you shall make it.
Kokborok: Ang nono phunukma hai kheno nini rínok tei aroni manwi-khwnwirokno norok ma snamnai.”

English: “They shall make an ark of acacia wood; two cubits and a half shall be its length, a cubit and a half its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.
Kokborok: “Akaia buphangni bokhok kaisa ma tonnai; lokma khe 45 inches kolok, 27 inches kuar tei 27 inches kuchuk ma wngnai.

English: And you shall overlay it with pure gold, within and without shall you overlay it, and you shall make upon it a molding of gold round about.
Kokborok: Bisingno rangchak kaham bai ma khobnai tei saka-obo rangchak bai ma khobnai tei buchugo gandar kitingwi’-no rangchak ma khobnai

English: And you shall cast four rings of gold for it and put them on its four feet, two rings on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side of it.
Kokborok: bwlamni bagwi aro rangchakni bukhunju kaibrwi ma thepanai tei yakungbo kongbrwi ma snamnai, babainwi-o bukhunju kainwi ma wngnai.

English: You shall make poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.
Kokborok: Akaia buphangni balphung ma snamnai, abono bo rangchak bai ma khobnai,

English: And you shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry the ark by them.
Kokborok: tei a balphungrokno a bokhokni babainwio’-no ma soranai,

English: The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be taken from it.
Kokborok: a balphungrokno a bukhunju’-ono ma tonnai; tei abono ma sokok glak.

English: And you shall put into the ark the testimony which I shall give you.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe a bokhokni bisingo ang rwna hwnmani a swmaini kóktháino ma tonnai.

English: And you shall make two cherubim of gold; of hammered work shall you make them, on the two ends of the mercy seat.
Kokborok: Hakhe nwng rangchakni maknwi cherub ma snamnai, abórokno rangchak tokhrubwi ma snamnai, abo khairokma achukthayungni yang yangni buchuk kwnwi-o ma tonnai.

English: Make one cherub on the one end, and one cherub on the other end; of one piece with the mercy seat shall you make the cherubim on its two ends.
Kokborok: Babaisa-o cherub masa tei babaisa-o tei masa cherubno ma tonnai; a cherub masa tei masa yang babai tei ayang babai khairokma achukthayungo ma tongnai.

English: The cherubim shall spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings, their faces one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubim be.
Kokborok: A cherubni bwkrangrok khe saka bwkhak pheheljak wngnai tei bórokni bwkrangrok bai khe a khairokma achukthayungno kholopnai. Bórokni mwkhang khe kaisa bai tei kaisa khe a khairokma achukthayungni bwkhak khe nahar laijak.

English: And you shall put the mercy seat on the top of the ark; and in the ark you shall put the testimony that I shall give you.
Kokborok: Tei nwng a khairokma achukthayungno a bokhokni saka-o ma tonnai; tei aboni bisingo khe ang rwna hwnmani a swmaino ton di.

English: There I will meet with you, and from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim that are upon the ark of the testimony, I will speak with you of all that I will give you in commandment for the people of Israel.
Kokborok: Ang nwng bai aro malainai tei a khairokma achukthayungni saka-o cherub maknwini kwcharo tongwi ang nono Israil bororokni bagwi ani bebak dagimungrokno rwnai.

English: “And you shall make a table of acacia wood; two cubits shall be its length, a cubit its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.
Kokborok: “Tei nwng akaia buphangni songphang kaisa snam di abo sentimitar
kolok, sentimitar
kuar tei sentimitar

English: You shall overlay it with pure gold, and make a molding of gold around it.
Kokborok: Nwng abono rangchak kaham bai khop di tei kitingwibo rangchak bai khop di.

English: And you shall make around it a frame a handbreadth wide, and a molding of gold around the frame.
Kokborok: Tei nwng mili mitar
kuar khlai bokhong ma snamnai, a bokhongno rangchak bai ma khobnai,

English: And you shall make for it four rings of gold, and fasten the rings to the four corners at its four legs.
Kokborok: tei aboni bagwi nwng baljaknai kaibrwi rangchakni bukhunju ma snamnai, yakung kongbrwi tongma a busup subrwi-o abórokno ton di.

English: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold, and the table shall be carried with these.
Kokborok: Nwng akaia buphangni balphungrokno ma snamnai tei abórokno rangchak bai ma khobnai. Songphangno abo bai ma balnai.

English: And you shall make its plates and dishes for incense, and its flagons and bowls with which to pour libations; of pure gold you shall make them.
Kokborok: Angur bwtwi yapharna bagwi, mairangrok, khurirok, lotarok tei dangairok snam di.

English: And you shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before me always.
Kokborok: Tei nwng a songphangni bwskango khe cho yaui auang ma yapharnai.

English: “And you shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The base and the shaft of the lampstand shall be made of hammered work; its cups, its capitals, and its flowers shall be of one piece with it;
Kokborok: “Rangchak kahamni thopna snam di. Tokjak rangchakni thopna snam di, abo khum bedek hai khe snamjak wngnai aro bubak tei bwkhrwi hai kaisa kaisa khe bedego tongnai.”

English: and there shall be six branches going out of its sides, three branches of the lampstand out of one side of it and three branches of the lampstand out of the other side of it;
Kokborok: Tei aroni bedek dekdok ongkhornai, abo bedek dek tham khe babaisani tei babaisani bedek dek tham ongkhornai.

English: three cups made like almonds, each with capital and flower, on one branch, and three cups made like almonds, each with capital and flower, on the other branch—so for the six branches going out of the lampstand;
Kokborok: Bedek dek thamni snamjak khum bubak tei bwkhrwi gwnangrok almon khum hai wngnai tei bedek dek thambo khumbar tei bwkhrwi gwnangrok almon khum hai wngnai, a thopnani abo hai khe bedek dekdok ongkhornai.

English: and on the lampstand itself four cups made like almonds, with their capitals and flowers,
Kokborok: Thopna bedego kaibrwi snamjak almon khum hai bubar bai bwkhrwi gwnang wngnai.

English: and a capital of one piece with it under each pair of the six branches going out from the lampstand.
Kokborok: Bojro kwtham bedekni páithak buchugo kaisa khe bwkhrwi tongnai.

English: Their capitals and their branches shall be of one piece with it, the whole of it one piece of hammered work of pure gold.
Kokborok: Thopna tei bini bedek bai bwkhrwirok kaisa khe tokjak rangchak kahamni ma wngnai.

English: And you shall make the seven lamps for it; and the lamps shall be set up so as to give light upon the space in front of it.
Kokborok: Tei nwng kaisni thopna gwnang thopnaphang snam di; tei bwskango pohor pirna tẃi khe abo thepajaknai.

English: Its snuffers and their trays shall be of pure gold.
Kokborok: Aboni romjaknai tei tonjaknai manwibo rangchak kaham bai snam di.

English: Of a talent of pure gold shall it be made, with all these utensils.
Kokborok: Thopna tei mairang lota snamna bagwi

English: And see that you make them after the pattern for them, which is being shown you on the mountain.
Kokborok: Hathái kuchugo ang nono phunukma hai kheno samung khlaina bagwi sakthar di.

English: Five curtains shall be coupled to one another; and the other five curtains shall be coupled to one another.
Kokborok: Rísa kangbano kwrẃngsa-o kangsa khe ma thepanai tei rísa kangba tei kwrẃngsa-o abo tẃi kheno supjaknai.

English: And you shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outmost curtain in the first set; and likewise you shall make loops on the edge of the outmost curtain in the second set.
Kokborok: Supjaknai swkangni páithak rísani buchugo rí kwkhrang bai bwlam snam di tei abo tẃi kheno tei kaisanobo abo haino ma khlainai.

English: Fifty loops you shall make on the one curtain, and fifty loops you shall make on the edge of the curtain that is in the second set; the loops shall be opposite one another.
Kokborok: Kangsa rísa-o bwlam lambachi snam di tei kubun rí supjakni páithak rísa-obo bachi bwlam snam di, phiya bwlam snamjakrok nahar laijak wngnai.

English: And you shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains one to the other with the clasps, that the tabernacle may be one whole.
Kokborok: Rangchakni biching snam di, a biching bai rísa supjak khaknwino kangsa khe phnangwi rwjaknai.

English: “You shall also make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle; eleven curtains shall you make.
Kokborok: “Pun buikhumu bai snamjak kangchisa, rísa ríni nok saka-o phinna bagwi snam di.

English: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains shall have the same measure.
Kokborok: Bebakno baksa khlai snam di, abo muk thamchi kolok tei muk brwi kuar wngnai.

English: And you shall couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and the sixth curtain you shall double over at the front of the tent.
Kokborok: Hakhe nwng a kangbano kangsa khe sup di tei kangdokno kwrẃngsa-o khe sup di. A kangdok bai snamjakno chabwi rínokni bwskangno kholop di.

English: And you shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outmost in one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which is outmost in the second set.
Kokborok: Tei nwng bwkhak kaisano khe buchugo bwlam lambachi snam di tei khaksaninobo buchugo bwlam lambachi snam di tei khaksaninobo buchugo bwlam lambachi snam di.

English: “And you shall make fifty clasps of bronze, and put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent together that it may be one whole.
Kokborok: Tei nwng pitolni kongbachi biching snam di tei a khaknwini bwlamo kaisa kholopjaknai abo tẃi bwlam snam di.

English: And the cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, of what remains in the length of the curtains of the tent shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle, on this side and that side, to cover it.
Kokborok: Hakhe sentimitar bara bachi kolok bwkhakni babainwino ríni nokni saka-o kholopjakna bagwi khachiui rw di.

English: And you shall make for the tent a covering of tanned rams' skins and goatskins.
Kokborok: “Nwng kholopjaknai kangnwi snam di, kangsa khe pun bukur pherejakni tei kangsa khe naithok bukurni abo sakani kholopjaknai wngnai.

English: “And you shall make upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.
Kokborok: “Ríni nokni núkhúngni bagwi akai ya buphangni bwklap snam di.

English: Ten cubits shall be the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the breadth of each frame.
Kokborok: A bwklap lapsa mitar kaibrwi kolok tei dokchi sentimitar kuar wngnai,

English: There shall be two tenons in each frame, for fitting together; so shall you do for all the frames of the tabernacle.
Kokborok: bwlap kwnwinino baksa khe thepanani bagwi kwnwi, kwnwi khe yakung tongna nangnai.

English: You shall make the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side;
Kokborok: Nwng rínokni bagwi nwichi bwlap snam di, abo nwichi bwlap salgrabai bwkhak wngnai;

English: and forty bases of silver you shall make under the twenty frames, two bases under one frame for its two tenons, and two bases under another frame for its two tenons;
Kokborok: tei a nwichi bwlapni yaphango brwichi rukphaini uásúng snamwi rw di, bwlap kaisani bagwi yakung kongnwini bagwi uásúng kwnwi tei kubun bwlapni bagwibo bórokni yakung kongnwi mareui’-no uásúng kwnwi kwnwi khe ma rwnai.

English: and for the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side twenty frames,
Kokborok: Tei rínokni tei kaisa-o salsibai bwkhakni bagwi bwlap lap nwichi;

English: and their forty bases of silver, two bases under one frame, and two bases under another frame;
Kokborok: o borokni bagwi kong brwichi uásúng nangnai; kaisa kaisa bwklapni bagwi uásúng kongnwi tei kubunrokni bagwibo kongnwi kongnwi uásúng.

English: and for the rear of the tabernacle westward you shall make six frames.
Kokborok: A rínokni salthángbaini uklokni bagwi lap dok bwklap ma khlainai.

English: And you shall make two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear;
Kokborok: Tei rínokni uklokni busup kwnwini bagwi kwnwi kwnwi bwklap snam di.

English: they shall be separate beneath, but joined at the top, at the first ring; thus shall it be with both of them; they shall form the two corners.
Kokborok: Busupni a bwklap khe khama bai saka bwkhak thepajak wngnai tei saka bai kwrwng laijak wngnai. A bwklap kwnwirok bai omo hai khe’-no busup kwnwi snamjaknai.

English: And there shall be eight frames, with their bases of silver, sixteen bases; two bases under one frame, and two bases under another frame.
Kokborok: Aro lap char bwklap wngnai tei a bwklaprokni bagwi rukphaini uásúng kongchidok wngnai, bwklap lapsani kwnwi-o uásúng tei lapsa bwklapni uásúng kongnwi wngnai.

English: “And you shall make bars of acacia wood, five for the frames of the one side of the tabernacle,
Kokborok: “Hakhe nwng rínokni bagwi kong ba akaia buphangni berepjaknai bokong ma snamnai, yasa bwkhakni bagwi bokong kong ba,

English: and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the side of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
Kokborok: tei kaisa bwkhakni bagwi tei bokong kong ba, uklokni salthángbaini bagwi.

English: The middle bar, halfway up the frames, shall pass through from end to end.
Kokborok: Kwcharni bokong khe abo yasani simi tei yasa jora kwrwngjak ma wngnai.

English: You shall overlay the frames with gold, and shall make their rings of gold for holders for the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
Kokborok: A bokhongrokno rangchak bai ma khobnai tei berepjaknaino kwcha rwnani bagwi rangchakni bokhong ma snamnai tei berepjaknairoknobo rangchak bai ma khobnai.”

English: And you shall erect the tabernacle according to the plan for it which has been shown you on the mountain.
Kokborok: “Hathái-o ang nono phunukma hai kheno nwng a rínokno ma songchanai.

English: “And you shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet stuff and fine twined linen; in skilled work shall it be made, with cherubim;
Kokborok: “Nwng kwkhrang, chaksru-sru tei kwchak bumulni mera buikhumuni khutwngni naithok khe takjak rísa snam di. Aro o cherubni mangtang ma snamnai.

English: and you shall hang it upon four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, with hooks of gold, upon four bases of silver.
Kokborok: Tei nwng abono rangchak khopjak akaia buphangni snamjak thong kaibrwi saka-o biching bai khachiui rw di tei rukphaini uásúngo abono achuk rwna nangnai.

English: And you shall hang the veil from the clasps, and bring the ark of the testimony in thither within the veil; and the veil shall separate for you the holy place from the most holy.
Kokborok: Rínokni núkhúngni bichingo khe a ríno khachiui rw di tei rí swngjakni bisingo khe swmai bokhokno ton di, a rí swngjak abo kwcharo tongwi kwthar kuk bai kwtharno khaknai.

English: You shall put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
Kokborok: Swmai bokhokni saka-o khe a khairokmung achukthaino kwthar kugo ma tonnai.

English: And you shall set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle opposite the table; and you shall put the table on the north side.
Kokborok: Rí swngjakni phataro tei rínokni yaksi bwkhak a songphangno ton di tei yagra bwkhak khe a thopnano ton di.

English: “And you shall make a screen for the door of the tent, of blue and purple and scarlet stuff and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework.
Kokborok: “Hapma duglamni bagwi rí swngjaknai ma snamnai, abo kwkhrang, chaksru-sru tei kwchak mera buikhumuni khutwng bai tei suchi bai rapjak wngna nangnai.

English: And you shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five bases of bronze for them.
Kokborok: A rí swngna bagwi akaia buphangni thong kongba nangnai tei a thongrokno rangchak bai khobna nangnai. Aboni bagwi biching ma snamna nangnai tei aboni bagwi pitolni uásúng kongba, ma khlainai.”

English: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Kokborok: Yaphango Kaitor nokharok tei hayungno snam kha.

English: The earth was without form and void, and darkness was upon the face of the deep; and the Spirit of God was moving over the face of the waters.
Kokborok: Hayung mungsa goron kwrwi bukcha tongma se, hakhe twi kuthuk khe mwnak koboljak; tei twirokni saka-o khe Kaitorni Phola birkitingwi tongmani.

English: And God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor “Pohor wng di” hwnwi sa kha, hakhe pohor wng kha.

English: And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
Kokborok: Tei Kaitor nuk kha a pohor kaham. Hakhe Kaitor pohor bai mwnakno swkak kha.

English: God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, one day.
Kokborok: Kaitor pohorno sal tei mwnakno khe hor hwnwi mung phar kha. Omo hai khe hor wng kha tei phung ai kha. Omo hai khe salsa wng kha.

English: And God said, “Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Twirokno khakna bagwi twirokni kwcharo kaisa bukcha wng thung.”

English: And God made the firmament and separated the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament. And it was so.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor twirokno swkagwi o bukchani saka-o kaisa tei kháma-o kaisa khlai kha. Hakhe abo hai’-no wng kha.

English: And God called the firmament Heaven. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.
Kokborok: Kaitor o bukchano khe nokha hwnwi mung rw kha. Sal thángwi phung ai kha tei salnwini sal wng kha.

English: And God said, “Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear.” And it was so.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nokharokni twirok kwrẃngsa-o khe thumjak thung tei hakung nukjak thung.” Hakhe ahai’-no wng kha.

English: God called the dry land Earth, and the waters that were gathered together he called Seas. And God saw that it was good.
Kokborok: Kaitor o ha kwranno khe Hakung tei twi thumjakrokno khe Twijlangrok hwnwi mung phar kha. Hakhe Kaitor nuk kha, abo kaham.

English: And God said, “Let the earth put forth vegetation, plants yielding seed, and fruit trees bearing fruit in which is their seed, each according to its kind, upon the earth.” And it was so.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “O ha-o bebak samrok, bwchlwi gwnang samphangrok tei bwthái tháinai buphangrok achai thung.” Hakhe abo hai’-no wng kha.

English: The earth brought forth vegetation, plants yielding seed according to their own kinds, and trees bearing fruit in which is their seed, each according to its kind. And God saw that it was good.
Kokborok: Hakhe o hayungo dal-bidal samrok, bwchlwi gwnangrok tei bwthái tháinai buphangrok wng kha tei sak sakni mangduk na kha. Hakhe Kaitor abórokno kaham nuk kha.

English: And there was evening and there was morning, a third day.
Kokborok: Hakhe hor wngwi phung ai kha tei salthamni sal wng kha.

English: And God said, “Let there be lights in the firmament of the heavens to separate the day from the night; and let them be for signs and for seasons and for days and years,
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nokhani bukcha-o sal bai horno swkakna bagwi pohor kaisa wng thung tei marini bagwi jorarokni bagwi salrok tei bisirokni bagwi

English: and let them be lights in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth.” And it was so.
Kokborok: tei ha saka-o pohor rwna bagwi nokha-o pohor wng thung.” Hakhe abo hai’-no wng kha.

English: And God made the two great lights, the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night; he made the stars also.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor pohor kotor kainwino snam kha. Pohor kotor kaisa khe salni bagwi tei kaisa lem pohor khe horni bagwi; bo athukiriroknobo snam kha.

English: And God set them in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth,
Kokborok: Bo a pohorrokno ha saka-o pohor wngna bagwi tei nokha saka-o khe ton kha,

English: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
Kokborok: tei abórokno sal tei horno tẃina bagwi tei pohor bai mwnakno swkakna bagwi ton kha, hakhe Kaitor abórokno kaham nuk kha.

English: And there was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.
Kokborok: Hor thángwi phung ai kha tei salbrwi sal wng kha.

English: And God said, “Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the firmament of the heavens.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Twi-o hámung sini ya langma gwnang malrok bai kupulung wng thung tei nokha-o khe birnai tokrok kupulung wng thung.”

English: So God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves, with which the waters swarm, according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. And God saw that it was good.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor twijlangni mal kotorrokno tei bebak twi-o tongnai langma gwnangrokno snam kha tei hámung sini ya birnai tokrokno snam kha. Hakhe Kaitor abórokno kaham nuk kha.

English: And God blessed them, saying, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor bórokno hamari rwui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Er di tei bángwi twijlangni twirogo supung di tei tokrokbo ha saka-o er thung.”

English: And there was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.
Kokborok: Hakhe hor wngwi phung ai kha tei salbani sal wng kha.

English: And God said, “Let the earth bring forth living creatures according to their kinds: cattle and creeping things and beasts of the earth according to their kinds.” And it was so.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “O ha saka-o bebak juda juda langma gwnang mal-matarok: Yabrwirok, ha-o malwi himnairok wng thung” hakhe abo hai’-no wng kha.

English: And God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds and the cattle according to their kinds, and everything that creeps upon the ground according to its kind. And God saw that it was good.
Kokborok: Kaitor hayungni yabrwi mal-matarokno sak sakni sak-mang rwui bebak ha-o tongnairokno tei malwi himnairokno snam kha. Kaitor nuk kha abo kaham.

English: Then God said, “Let us make man in our image, after our likeness; and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Phai di chwng bai kosol khe borokno snamnai. Chwng omo hai khe snamnai; twini árok, nokha-o birnai tokrok tei nokni, bolongni bebak yabrwi mal-matarok tei malwi himnairokni saka-o bórokni suar tongnai.”

English: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor borokno bono kosol khe snam kha, chwla tei bwrwi khe bórokno snam kha.

English: And God blessed them, and God said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth and subdue it; and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the air and over every living thing that moves upon the earth.”
Kokborok: Tei Kaitor hamari rwui bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Er di tei báng di, ha-o kupulung khlai di tei bórokno yapha-o ton di; phiya twijlangni árok tei nokhani toksa-toktẃirok tei ha saka-o tongnai langma gwnangrokno kók khna rwui ton di.”

English: And God said, “Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree with seed in its fruit; you shall have them for food.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ang o ha saka-o tongma bebak bwchlwi gwnang buphang-uáphangrok tei bwthái tháinai bebak buphangrokno, nini chámungni bagwi rw kha, abórokno nwng chá di.

English: And to every beast of the earth, and to every bird of the air, and to everything that creeps on the earth, everything that has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food.” And it was so.
Kokborok: Ha sakani bebak malrok nokhani bebak toksa-toktẃirok, ha sakani bebak malnairok tei bebak rangma gwnangrokno ang bórokni chámungni bagwi bebak sam kwkhrangrokno rw kha.” Hakhe abo hai’-no wng kha.

English: And God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, a sixth day.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor bo snamma bebak manwirokno naisigwi nai kha tei abórokno belai’-no kaham nuk kha. Hakhe sal tháng kha tei phung ai kha tei saldokni sal wng kha.

English: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.
Kokborok: Omo hai khe nokharokno tei hayungno tei aro bebak tongmarokno snam bai kha.

English: And on the seventh day God finished his work which he had done, and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had done.
Kokborok: Salsnini salo khe Kaitor bini a snammani samungrok pái kha. Bo bebak tangmani thagwi salsnini salo khe lengla kha.

English: So God blessed the seventh day and hallowed it, because on it God rested from all his work which he had done in creation.
Kokborok: Bo salsnini salno hamari rw kha tei juda khe khak kha, tamni hwn ba Kaitor a salo bebak samung tangma mwthagwi lenglama sal.

English:
These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created.
In the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens,
Kokborok: Omo hai khe’-no o nokharok tei hayung snammani láibuma. Subraiyung Kaitor nokha tei hayungno snam phuru

English: when no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up—for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was no man to till the ground;
Kokborok: ha saka-o buphang-uáphang mungsabo kwrwi kho, mungsa bwchlwibo pekhrup ya kho, tamni hwn ba Subraiyung Kaitor aphuru ha saka-o uatwi ua rw ya kho tei borok khoroksabo ha-o káimungrok kái ya kho;

English: but a mist went up from the earth and watered the whole face of the ground—
Kokborok: tamo khe ha bisingni twi choromsaui hano misiui rw kha.

English: then the Lord God formed man of dust from the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living being.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Subraiyung Kaitor hani kisicha ha naui borokno tak kha tei bini bukungo subui mwthangnai rangma daui rw kha; hakhe borok kwthang wng kha.

English: And the Lord God planted a garden in Eden, in the east; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor salkabai-o Eden hwnwi tháiblwng kaisa snam kha; tei bo takma a borokno aro ton kha.

English: And out of the ground the Lord God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food, the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
Kokborok: Bo aro naithok naithok tei kothok kothok bwthái buphangrok achai rw kha, tháiblwngni kwcharo khe langmani buphangno tei kaham hamya si rwnai Simaphangbo achai rw kha.

English: A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden, and there it divided and became four rivers.
Kokborok: Eden tháiblwngni twima kaisa yorwi aro twi nang rw-o. Abo aro khakjagwi twima prak brwi wng kha.

English: The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one which flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
Kokborok: Swkangni kaisani mung khe Pison; abo Habilani bebak saka tẃi yorjak, aro rangchak tongo;

English: and the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
Kokborok: a hani rangchak kaham; thok motom tei holong kaham monirokbo aro tongo.

English: The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one which flows around the whole land of Cush.
Kokborok: Tei kaisa twimani mung khe Gihon, abo ha Kus gwnangwi yoro.

English: And the name of the third river is Tigris, which flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
Kokborok: Tei kaisa twimani mung khe Taigris, abo Asiriyani salkabai tẃi yoro. Phiya tei kaisa khe Uphratis.

English: The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and keep it.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Kaitor a borokno tubu-ui Eden tháiblwng muphunna bagwi tei mwrwkna bagwi ton kha.

English: And the Lord God commanded the man, saying, “You may freely eat of every tree of the garden;
Kokborok: Subraiyung Kaitor a borokno hai hwnwi dagiui sa kha, “Nwng o tháiblwngni bebak buphangni bwtháino cháui mano,

English: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall die.”
Kokborok: tamo khe kaham hamya si rwnai buphangni bwtháino le ta chá di, tamni hwn khe nwng abono chámani salo’-no thwinai.”

English: Then the Lord God said, “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Subraiyung Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Borok saichung susuk khe tongmani hamya; ang bo bai mwchang rwui bono chubanai khoroksa snamnai.”

English: So out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to the man to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called every living creature, that was its name.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor ha naui ha sakani bebak mal-matarokno tei nokhani bebak toksa-toluarokno snam kha hakhe mung pharna bagwi bini thani tubu kha.

English: The man gave names to all cattle, and to the birds of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for the man there was not found a helper fit for him.
Kokborok: A borok bebak mal-matarokno toksa-toluarokno tei bolongni manwirokno mung phar kha; tamo khe abórokni bisingo kaisabo bono chubana bagwi bini mángsong mwchang li ya.

English: So the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and while he slept took one of his ribs and closed up its place with flesh;
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor a borokno sila charwi muktrwi tháng rw kha tei a borokni barani bekreng kongsa na kha tei abono bahan bai ból kha,

English: and the rib which the Lord God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Kaitor a borokni barani bekreng bai bwrwi khoroksa snamwi a borokni thani tubu kha.

English:
Then the man said,
“This at last is bone of my bones
and flesh of my flesh;
she shall be called Woman,
because she was taken out of Man.”
Kokborok:
Hakhe a borok hai hwnwi sa kha,
“Páithago omo ani bekrengni bekreng
tei ani bahanni bahan;
bini mung Bwrwi wngnai,
tamni hwn ba bo chwlani aroni karijakma se.”

English: Therefore a man leaves his father and his mother and cleaves to his wife, and they become one flesh.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi’-no borok ma-pha bai kagwi bini hik bai thansa wngo tei bórok mang kaisa wngo.

English: And the man and his wife were both naked, and were not ashamed.
Kokborok: Hakhe a chwla bai bihik khoroknwi’-no sago rí kwrwi tei labo lachi lai ya.

English: Now the serpent was more subtle than any other wild creature that the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, “Did God say, ‘You shall not eat of any tree of the garden’?”
Kokborok: Tabuk Subraiyung Kaitor aswk mal-mata snammarokni bere chibuk wng kha bandi kwrwng kuk. Bo bwrwino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor da norokno, ‘o tháiblwngni buphangrokni bwtháirokno norok ta chá di’ hwnwi hwn?”

English: And the woman said to the serpent, “We may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden;
Kokborok: Hakhe a bwrwi chibukno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng o tháiblwngni bebak buphangrokni bwtháirokno cháui mano;

English: but God said, ‘You shall not eat of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, neither shall you touch it, lest you die.’ ”
Kokborok: phiya ba Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘tháiblwng kwcharo tongma a buphangni bwtháino le ta chá di tabo ta tang di, hakhe nwng thwinai.’ ”

English: But the serpent said to the woman, “You will not die.
Kokborok: Phiya a chibuk bwrwino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng thwi glak:

English: For God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.”
Kokborok: tamni hwn ba, Kaitor si-o nwng o buphangni bwtháino chá khe nini mokol kiyoknai tei nwng Kaitor hai wngnai tei bubo kaham bubo hamya saui mannai.”

English: So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was to be desired to make one wise, she took of its fruit and ate; and she also gave some to her husband, and he ate.
Kokborok: A bwrwi a buphangno naithok nuk kha tei bini bwthái chá khe bwswk thokno tei siyan gwnang wng khe bwswk hamno hwnwi uansuk kha, hakhe bo aboni bwthái khagwi chá kha tei bwsainobo kisa chá di hwnwi rw khe bwsaibo abono chá kha.

English: Then the eyes of both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves aprons.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe khoroknwini mokol’-no kiyok kha, hakhe bórok sak lánga hwnwi saui man lai kha; hakhe bórok khwichang bwláirokno subui sakno ból lai kha.

English: And they heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day, and the man and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the Lord God among the trees of the garden.
Kokborok: A sarigo bórok Subraiyung Kaitorno tháiblwngo sarik sal kwchang dodo-o himgurungwi tongmani sari khna kha tei a chwla bai bihik tháiblwngni buphangrokni kotono khe Subraiyung Kaitor bai nukjakna kiri-lachiui huijak lai kha.

English: But the Lord God called to the man, and said to him, “Where are you?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyung Kaitor a chwlano ringwi hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng buro wng kha?”

English: And he said, “I heard the sound of thee in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháiblwngo ang nini khorang khna kha, ang nwng bai nukjakna kiriui lachiui ma huijak kha, tamni hwn ba ang sak lánga.”

English: He said, “Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten of the tree of which I commanded you not to eat?”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nono sak lánga hwnwi sabo sa lang ba? Ang nono ta chá di hwnwi samani o buphangni bwtháino da chá lang tamo?”

English: The man said, “The woman whom thou gavest to be with me, she gave me fruit of the tree, and I ate.”
Kokborok: A chwla hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang bai tong di hwnwi nwng ano rwmani a bwrwi se ano a bwtháino rw-o, hakhe ang ma chá kha.”

English: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “What is this that you have done?” The woman said, “The serpent beguiled me, and I ate.”
Kokborok:
Aphuru khe Subraiyung Kaitor a bwrwino hai hwnwi hwn kha, “Nwng tamo khlai kha?”
A bwrwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chibuk se ano belok khe ang ma chá kha.”
Kaitor majra sakhlaimani

English:
The Lord God said to the serpent,
“Because you have done this,
cursed are you above all cattle,
and above all wild animals;
upon your belly you shall go,
and dust you shall eat
all the days of your life.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor chibukno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng omo hai samung khlaimani bagwi nokni tei bolongni mal-matarokni slái’-bo nwng swraimung nang thung; tabukni simi nwng khaklap bai ma mal sinai; nwng thwi yasak’-no hadlwi ma chánai.

English:
I will put enmity between you and the woman,
and between your seed and her seed;
he shall bruise your head,
and you shall bruise his heel.”
Kokborok: Ang, nwng bai bwrwino kena wng rwnai, nini bosong bai bini bosongbo kena wng lainai; bo nini bokhorok swbainai tei nwng khe bini yakorai-o suknai.”

English:
To the woman he said,
“I will greatly multiply your pain in childbearing;
in pain you shall bring forth children,
yet your desire shall be for your husband,
and he shall rule over you.”
Kokborok: Bo bwrwino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng bwsa ogo tẃi phuru ang nini ok samano báng rwnai; nwng ok saui sak ma kengnai, hai twtwi phano nwng nwsaino khatungnai tei bo nono yago tonnai.”

English:
And to Adam he said,
“Because you have listened to the voice of your wife,
and have eaten of the tree
of which I commanded you,
‘You shall not eat of it,’
cursed is the ground because of you;
in toil you shall eat of it all the days of your life;
Kokborok: Hakhe bo Adamno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nihikni kók khna kha tei ang nono ‘ta chá di’ hwnmani buphangni bwtháino chá kha, nini bagwi o ha swraimung nang kha; kwthang tong lang sak’-no nwng kolomtwi klaiwi tangwi ma chá sinai;

English:
thorns and thistles it shall bring forth to you;
and you shall eat the plants of the field.
Kokborok: o ha-o jabrarok tei busurok bacha sinai; tei nwng hapharni dalo-samprarok ma chá sinai.

English:
In the sweat of your face
you shall eat bread
till you return to the ground,
for out of it you were taken;
you are dust,
and to dust you shall return.”
Kokborok: Nono takmani a ha-o kiphilwi tháng ya sak nwng khorokni kolomtwi yakungo khiklaiwi tangwi ma chá sinai; nini o hani mang, ha-ono kiphilwi ma tháng sinai.”

English: The man called his wife's name Eve, because she was the mother of all living.
Kokborok: Adam bihikno Ebi hwnwi mung phar kha, tamni hwn ba bo’-no bebak borokni bosongrokni buma.

English: And the Lord God made for Adam and for his wife garments of skins, and clothed them.
Kokborok:
Hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor Adam bai bihikni bagwi bukurni kanmung snamwi kan rwui rw kha.
Adam bai Ebi Tháiblwngni rwkhlaijakmani

English: Then the Lord God said, “Behold, the man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil; and now, lest he put forth his hand and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever” —
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di tabuk bórok chwng hai’-no wng kha, kaham hamya saui man kha, tabuk bo yak phologwi langma buphangni bwtháino khagwi chá khe mol brúm’-no kwthang tong phlainai”

English: therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from which he was taken.
Kokborok: aboni bagwi Subraiyung Kaitor bono Eden tháiblwngni phataro khe bono takma a ha-o hukba khlaina bagwi rwui rohor kha.

English: He drove out the man; and at the east of the garden of Eden he placed the cherubim, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to guard the way to the tree of life.
Kokborok: Bo borokno rwkhlaiwi rohor kha; hakhe Eden tháiblwngni salka bwkhak Cherubno tei bebak bwkhak’-no kitingwi hórjlai gwnang sengno ton kha. Langma rwnai buphangni samo thángwi man ya tẃi khe mwrwkna bagwi se abórokno tono.

English: Now Adam knew Eve his wife, and she conceived and bore Cain, saying, “I have gotten a man with the help of the Lord.”
Kokborok: Tabuk Adam Ebi bai thu khe-chá khe, Ebi sakderiya wngwi Kainno man kha, Ebi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyungni jarwi ang sajla khoroksa man kha.”

English: And again, she bore his brother Abel. Now Abel was a keeper of sheep, and Cain a tiller of the ground.
Kokborok: Bo bono Kain hwnwi mung phar kha. Tei haino hai bo Kainni takhuk Abelno man kha. Abel khe mera rinai tei Kain khe hukba khlaiwi chánai wng kha.

English: In the course of time Cain brought to the Lord an offering of the fruit of the ground,
Kokborok: Sal kisa lái khe Kain bini hapharni bwthái-bumukrokno Subraiyungni thani yapharna tubu kha.

English: and Abel brought of the firstlings of his flock and of their fat portions. And the Lord had regard for Abel and his offering,
Kokborok: Tei Abel khe merarokni bisingo swkang bwsa bá gramano tei bini buphamrokno Subraiyungni thani tubu kha. Subraiyung Abel tei bini yapharmano bwkha phurjak kha.

English: but for Cain and his offering he had no regard. So Cain was very angry, and his countenance fell.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Kain yapharmano khe bwkha phurjak li ya, hakhe Kain belai khe jolijak kha tei mwkhang som kha.

English: The Lord said to Cain, “Why are you angry, and why has your countenance fallen?
Kokborok: Subraiyung Kainno hai hwnwi hwn kha, “Nwng tamni aswk jolijak. Tamo nini mwkhang som lang?

English: If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is couching at the door; its desire is for you, but you must master it.”
Kokborok: Nwng samung kaham tang khe tamo yachakjak ya tong kha mun? Nwng samung kaham khlai ya wngmani bagwi sumsok nini duglamo habwi tong phai kha; abo nono se béngna nai-o, tamo khe nwng bono ma mechennai.”

English: Cain said to Abel his brother, “Let us go out to the field.” And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel, and killed him.
