Kokborok: Kain bini takhuk Abelno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Phai di hukba bwkhak tháng lai anw.” Hakhe hukba-o sógwi khe Kain bini takhuk Abelni bwkhak joliui bachasa kha tei buthar kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Cain, “Where is Abel your brother?” He said, “I do not know; am I my brother's keeper?”
Kokborok:
Subraiyung Kainno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nini takhuk Abel buko, biyang tháng kha?”
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang saui man ya. Ang ba tamo ani takhukno mwrwgwi da tong?”

English: And the Lord said, “What have you done? The voice of your brother's blood is crying to me from the ground.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tamo khlai kha de? Nini takhukni thẃi hani ano kabwi saui tong kha.

English: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
Kokborok: Nwng tabuk ha-o swraimung nang kha, nwng nwphayungno buthar phuru bono yachakna bagwi ha khua tẃi khe bo nini takhukni thẃino nungwi pái kha.

English: When you till the ground, it shall no longer yield to you its strength; you shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.”
Kokborok: Nwng o ha-o hukba khlai phano tei mungsa bwthái thái glak kha, nwng nok-huk kwrwi wngwi ha saka-o kitingwi ma tongnai.”

English: Cain said to the Lord, “My punishment is greater than I can bear.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kain Subraiyungno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani sátokjakmung khe ang soiwi manmani bára wng kha.

English: Behold, thou hast driven me this day away from the ground; and from thy face I shall be hidden; and I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will slay me.”
Kokborok: Nai di nwng ano o hani tei nini bwskangni hakchal khe rwkhlaiwi rohor kha, ang nok kwrwi wngwi ha gwnangwi kitingwi ma tong ja sinai tei ano nuknai borok ano butharnai.”

English: Then the Lord said to him, “Not so! If any one slays Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold.” And the Lord put a mark on Cain, lest any who came upon him should kill him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ahai le wng glak! Khoroksa swk Kainno buthar khe bo abo hai khe tei uáisni jora yagul sujaknai.” Hakhe bono nuknai khoroksabo ta buthar si thung hwnwi Subraiyung Kainni sago mari bu-ui rw kha.

English: Then Cain went away from the presence of the Lord, and dwelt in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kain Subraiyungni bwskangni nongkhorwi tháng kha tei Edenni salkabai, Nod hwnwi kaisa ha-o tongwi kha.

English: Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch; and he built a city, and called the name of the city after the name of his son, Enoch.
Kokborok: Kain bihik bai thu kha-chá kha, hakhe Enokno man kha tei bo kamiyung kaisa snam kha tei a kamiyungni mung bwsla Enokni mungwi phar kha.

English: To Enoch was born Irad; and Irad was the father of Me-huja-el, and Me-huja-el the father of Me-thusha-el, and Me-thusha-el the father of Lamech.
Kokborok: Enokni nogo Irad achai kha tei Irad wng kha Mehujaelni bupha tei Mehujael wng kha Methusaelni bupha, Methusael wng kha Lamekni bupha.

English: And Lamech took two wives; the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
Kokborok: Tei Lamek hik khoroknwi na kha, khoroksani mung khe Ada tei khoroksani mung khe Jilla.

English: Adah bore Jabal; he was the father of those who dwell in tents and have cattle.
Kokborok: Ada Jobalno man kha; bo wng kha ríni nogo tongnai tei mal-mata rinairokni bupha.

English: His brother's name was Jubal; he was the father of all those who play the lyre and pipe.
Kokborok: Bini takhukni mung khe Jubal, bo wng kha bebak chongpreng tei sumui tamnairokni bupha.

English: Zillah bore Tubal-cain; he was the forger of all instruments of bronze and iron. The sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
Kokborok: Jilla, Tubal-Kainno man kha; bo pitol bai sorni manwi taknai Tubal-Kainni bukhukni mung khe Nama.

English:
Lamech said to his wives:
“Adah and Zillah, hear my voice;
you wives of Lamech, hearken to what I say:
I have slain a man for wounding me,
a young man for striking me.

If Cain is avenged sevenfold,
truly Lamech seventy-sevenfold.”
Kokborok:
Kainno butharma yagul kusungsni bai sujak khe,
Lamekno buthar khe kusungsni-chisni butharwi yagul sujaknai.”

English: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and called his name Seth, for she said, “God has appointed for me another child instead of Abel, for Cain slew him.”
Kokborok: Adam bihik bai thu khe-chá khe bo sajla khoroksa man kha. Bono Set hwnwi mung phar kha. Tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ani bagwi Abelni sak slái-o tei khoroksa bwsa rw phi kha, tamni hwn ba Kain bono butharwi pái kha.”

English: To Seth also a son was born, and he called his name Enosh. At that time men began to call upon the name of the Lord.
Kokborok: Setbo sajla khoroksa man kha tei bo Enos hwnwi bini mung phar kha. Aphuruni simi’-no borokrok Subraiyungni mungno ringnani cheng lai-o.

English: This is the book of the generations of Adam. When God created man, he made him in the likeness of God.
Kokborok: Omo wng kha Adamni bosongrokni láibumarokni kók. Kaitor borokno snam phuru bo bono Kaitorno kosol khe snam kha.

English: Male and female he created them, and he blessed them and named them Man when they were created.
Kokborok: Bo bórokno chwla tei bwrwi khe snam kha tei bo bórokno hamari rw kha. Snamwi bórokno Borok hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: When Adam had lived a hundred and thirty years, he became the father of a son in his own likeness, after his image, and named him Seth.
Kokborok: Adamni umor nokha bisi sarathamchi phuru bo bupha wng kha. Bini sakno kosol khe’-no sajla khoroksa man kha tei bono Set hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: The days of Adam after he became the father of Seth were eight hundred years; and he had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo Adam teibo nokha bisi charra kwthang tong kha; bo teibo sajwk-sajla man kha.

English: Thus all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years; and he died.
Kokborok: Tei Adam nokha bisi chukurathamchi umoro khe thwi kha.

English: When Seth had lived a hundred and five years, he became the father of Enosh.
Kokborok: Setni umor nokha bisi saraba phuru sajla Enosno man kha.

English: Seth lived after the birth of Enosh eight hundred and seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Tei aboni ulo bo teibo nokha bisi charrasni thang lang kha tei bo teibo kubun sajwk-sajlarok man phi kha.

English: Thus all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years; and he died.
Kokborok: Tei nokha bisi chukurachinwi umoro khe thwi kha.

English: When Enosh had lived ninety years, he became the father of Kenan.
Kokborok: Enosni umor nokha bisi chukuchi phuru bo sajla Kenanno man kha.

English: Enosh lived after the birth of Kenan eight hundred and fifteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Tei aboni ulo teibo nokha bisi chukurachiba thang kha. Bo teibo sajwk-sajla man kha.

English: Thus all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years; and he died.
Kokborok: Tei Enosni umor nokha bisi chukuraba-o khe thwi kha.

English: When Kenan had lived seventy years, he became the father of Ma-halalel.
Kokborok: Kenanni umor nokha bisi snichi phuru Mahalalel hwnwi sajla khoroksa man kha.

English: Kenan lived after the birth of Ma-halalel eight hundred and forty years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Tei aboni ulo bo teibo nokha bisi charrabrwichi thang lang kha tei teibo sajwk-sajlarokno man kha.

English: Thus all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kenan umor nokha bisi chukurachi káui thwi kha.

English: When Ma-halalel had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Jared.
Kokborok: Mahalalelni umor nokha bisi dokchiba phuru bo sajla Jaredno man kha.

English: Ma-halalel lived after the birth of Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Bo aboni ulo teibo nokha bisi charrathamchi thang lang kha phiya teibo sajwk-sajlarok man kha.

English: Thus all the days of Ma-halalel were eight hundred and ninety-five years; and he died.
Kokborok: Mahalalelni umor nokha bisi charrachukuchiba-o khe thwi kha.

English: When Jared had lived a hundred and sixty-two years he became the father of Enoch.
Kokborok: Jaredni umor nokha bisi sara-dokchinwi phuru khe chwla bwsa Enokno man kha.

English: Jared lived after the birth of Enoch eight hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Tei aboni ulo Jared teibo nokha bisi charra thang lang kha tei bo sajwk-sajlarok man kha.

English: Thus all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years; and he died.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jaredni umor nokha bisi chukuradokchinwi-o khe thwi kha.

English: When Enoch had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Methuselah.
Kokborok: Enokni umor nokha bisi dokchiba phuru khe bo sajla Methuselano man kha.

English: Enoch walked with God after the birth of Methuselah three hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Enok Kaitor bai kwthaui nokha bisi thamra tong lang kha tei bo sajwk-sajlarokbo man phi kha.

English: Thus all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.
Kokborok: Bo nokha bisi thamradokchiba tong lang kha.

English: Enoch walked with God; and he was not, for God took him.
Kokborok: Bini thangsungo bo Kaitor bai kwthaui máng tongwi tháng kha. Hakhe, Kaitor bono tlangwi thángma bai bo nukjak li ya.

English: When Methuselah had lived a hundred and eighty-seven years, he became the father of Lamech.
Kokborok: Methuselani umor nokha bisi saracharchisni phuru bo sajla Lamekno man kha.

English: Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech seven hundred and eighty-two years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Methusela Lamek achaiwi tei nokha bisi sniracharchinwi thangwi tong lang kha tei bo teibo sajwk-sajlarok man phi kha.

English: Thus all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years; and he died.
Kokborok: Hakhe nokha bisi chukuradokchi-chuku umoro khe thwi kha.

English: When Lamech had lived a hundred and eighty-two years, he became the father of a son,
Kokborok: Lamekni umor nokha bisi sara-charchinwi phuru khe sajla khoroksa man kha.

English: and called his name Noah, saying, “Out of the ground which the Lord has cursed this one shall bring us relief from our work and from the toil of our hands.”
Kokborok: Tei hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung swraimani o ha-ono o bwsa chini bebak samung kwrak tangmani yakni chwngno phiyóknai,” hakhe bo bono Noa hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Lamech lived after the birth of Noah five hundred and ninety-five years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: Lamek Noa achaimani ulo teibo nokha bisi barachukuchiba thang lang kha, bo teibo sajwk-sajlarok man kha.

English: Thus all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years; and he died.
Kokborok: Tei bini umor nokha bisi snira tei snichisni-o khe thwi kha.

English: After Noah was five hundred years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
Kokborok: Noa nokha bisi bara umor wng phuru bo sajla khorok tham man kha. Bórokni mung khe Sem, Ham tei Japhet.

English: When men began to multiply on the face of the ground, and daughters were born to them,
Kokborok: Borokrok erwi ha saka-o kupulung wng kha, bórokni bwrwi bwsarok kwbángma achai kha.

English: the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were fair; and they took to wife such of them as they chose.
Kokborok: Kaitorni bwsarok, borokrokni bwsajwksongno naithokjak bai kha tei bórok hamjakma tẃi a bwrwirokno hik song kha.

English: Then the Lord said, “My spirit shall not abide in man for ever, for he is flesh, but his days shall be a hundred and twenty years.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani phola borokrok bai máng tong phlai glak, tamni hwn ba bo wng kha bahan bwthẃini, tamo khe bini umor tabukni simi nokha bisi sara-nwichi wng sinai.”

English: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of God came in to the daughters of men, and they bore children to them. These were the mighty men that were of old, the men of renown.
Kokborok: A jora-o tei aboni ulo o ha saka-o mang kotorrok tongmani, bórok wng kha borokrokni bwrwi bwsa bai Kaitorni bwsarok gwdalwi wngma bwsarok. Bórok’-no bisi kwbángma swkango phankrak tei mung gwnang borok hwnjaknairok.

English: The Lord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.
Kokborok: Subraiyung, ha saka-o borokrokni hamya-sitrarok bángmani nuk kha, bebak bórokni bwkha-o uansukmarok hamya máng se kupulung wngwi tongo.

English: And the Lord was sorry that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him to his heart.
Kokborok: O ha saka-o borokno snamwi Subraiyung bwkha nangjak kha tei abo bono bwkha hamya khlai kha.

English: So the Lord said, “I will blot out man whom I have created from the face of the ground, man and beast and creeping things and birds of the air, for I am sorry that I have made them.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang snamma borokrokno o ha sakani sokomorwi pái sinai tei mal-matarok, toksa-toktẃiroknobo sokomorwi pái sinai, tamni hwn ba omorokno snamwi cha se cha li ya, ani khá ham li ya.”

English: But Noah found favor in the eyes of the Lord.
Kokborok:
Tamo khe Subraiyung Noano khe kaham nuk kha.
Noani kothoma

English: These are the generations of Noah. Noah was a righteous man, blameless in his generation; Noah walked with God.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Noani bosongrok. Noa wng kha borok sói, bo a jorani borokni bisingo mungsa derama kwrwi borok; Noa Kaitor bai kwrwngwi tongo.

English: And Noah had three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
Kokborok: Noani sajla khorok tham tongo. Bórokni mungrok wng kha; Sem, Ham tei Japhet.

English: Now the earth was corrupt in God's sight, and the earth was filled with violence.
Kokborok: Aphuru Kaitorni mokolo o hani borokrok cha ya wng kha tei o ha-o tong kutung kupulung wng kha.

English: And God saw the earth, and behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted their way upon the earth.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor hano naikhlai naiwi nuk kha, nai di abo hamya bai kupulung wng kha, bebak borokrok’-no ha saka-o tongmung hamya khe máng tong lai kha.

English: And God said to Noah, “I have determined to make an end of all flesh; for the earth is filled with violence through them; behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor Noano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang bebak’-no sokomorwi pái sinai hwnwi bwkha-o chong kha, tamni hwn ba bórokni samungni bagwi o ha-o bu lai-tok lai bai pung súk kha; nai di bórokno ang o ha bai baksa’-no sokomor sinai.

English: Make yourself an ark of gopher wood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and out with pitch.
Kokborok: Nwng nini sakni bagwi Gophur buphangni rungtor snam di tei aro khotolrok snam di tei bising bai phataro pich silwi ma lepenai

English: This is how you are to make it the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its breadth fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
Kokborok: Nwng abono omo hai khe ma tannai kolok khe muk 300 , kebeng muk 50 tei kuchuk khe muk 30.

English: Make a roof for the ark, and finish it to a cubit above; and set the door of the ark in its side; make it with lower, second, and third decks.
Kokborok: Rungtorni núkhúngbo snam di tei núkhúngni muksani tola-o tailam tei bugurung narwgwi ton di. Aboni saka-o khe khotol kaitham snam di tei khaksa-o duglam narwk di.

English: For behold, I will bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh in which is the breath of life from under heaven; everything that is on the earth shall die.
Kokborok: Nai di, ha saka-o tongma bebak langma gwnangrokno sokomorna bagwi ang twi kotor rohornai, ha saka-o tongma bebak’-no thwi bainai.

English: But I will establish my covenant with you; and you shall come into the ark, you, your sons, your wife, and your sons' wives with you.
Kokborok: Tamo khe ang nwng bai swmai tháisa tangnai, nwng nihikno nwsarokno tei nahamjwksongno tẃiwi rungo hap di.

English: And of every living thing of all flesh, you shall bring two of every sort into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.
Kokborok: Tei nwng bebak langma gwnangrokno mwthangwi narwkna bagwi bojro-bojro khe rungtoro da di. Abórok chwla masa bwrwi masa khe wngna nangnai.

English: Of the birds according to their kinds, and of the animals according to their kinds, of every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of every sort shall come in to you, to keep them alive.
Kokborok: Mwthangwi narwkna bagwi toksa toktẃiroknobo pod thwgwi’-no tei mal-mataroknobo pod thwgwi phiya malnairoknobo hai’-no maknwi-maknwi khe pod thwgwi da phai di.

English: Also take with you every sort of food that is eaten, and store it up; and it shall serve as food for you and for them.”
Kokborok: Tei nini bai bórokni bagwi nangma bebak chámungrokbo na si di.”

English: Noah did this; he did all that God commanded him.
Kokborok:
Noa abo hai’-no khlai kha, bo bebakno Kaitor dagima tẃi’-no khlai kha.
Kok Borok Bible (Re-edited), Baibel Kwthar

English: Then the Lord said to Noah, “Go into the ark, you and all your household, for I have seen that you are righteous before me in this generation
Kokborok: Subraiyung Noano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tei nini nokni borokrok rungtoro hap si di, tamni hwn ba ang o bosongrokni bisingo nono borok sói nuk kha

English: Take with you seven pairs of all clean animals, the male and his mate; and a pair of the animals that are not clean, the male and his mate;
Kokborok: Nwng kwthar mal-matarokno jorsni na di, chwla tei bwrwi khe na di tei kwthar ya mal-matabo jorsa na di, chwla tei bwrwi khe na di

English: and seven pairs of the birds of the air also, male and female, to keep their kind alive upon the face of all the earth
Kokborok: Tei jorsni tokrokno o ha saka-o kwthang narwkna bagwi chwla tei bwrwi khe narwk di

English: For in seven days I will send rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living thing that I have made I will blot out from the face of the ground ”
Kokborok: O salsnini bisingo’-no ang ha saka-o sal brwichi tei horbrwichi uatwi ua rwnai, o ha sakani ang snamma bebak langma gwnangrokno hu-ui pái sinai ”

English: And Noah did all that the Lord had commanded him
Kokborok: Noa Subraiyung dagima tẃi’-no bebakno khlai bai kha

English: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came upon the earth
Kokborok: Ha saka-o twi kotor sók phai phuru Noani umor nokha bisi dok-ra wng kha

English: And Noah and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him went into the ark, to escape the waters of the flood
Kokborok: Noa bihik tei bwsajlasongno tei bini hamjwksongrokno tẃiwi twi kotorni yókna bagwi rungtoro hap lai kha

English: Of clean animals, and of animals that are not clean, and of birds, and of everything that creeps on the ground,
Kokborok: Kwthar mal-matarok tei kwthar yarok, tokrok tei ha saka-o malnai bebakno

English: two and two, male and female, went into the ark with Noah, as God had commanded Noah
Kokborok: maknwi-maknwi chwla bwrwi khe Kaitor Noano dagima tẃi’-no rungtoro hap lai kha

English: And after seven days the waters of the flood came upon the earth
Kokborok: Salsni ulo khe ha saka-o twi torsaui phai kha

English: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on that day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the windows of the heavens were opened
Kokborok: Noani umor nokha bisi dok-ra phuru, talnwini talni tal chisnini salo khe, bebak kuthukni twimukrok pekhogwi nongkhor kha tei nokhani tailamrokbo kiyok kha

English: And rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights
Kokborok: Ha saka-o sal brwichi horbrwichi uatwi ua kha

English: On the very same day Noah and his sons, Shem and Ham and Japheth, and Noah's wife and the three wives of his sons with them entered the ark,
Kokborok: A salo’-no Noa tei bini bwsarok Sem, Ham, Japhet tei Noani bihik, bahamjwksong rungtoro habo,

English: they and every beast according to its kind, and all the cattle according to their kinds, and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth according to its kind, every bird according to its kind, every bird of every sort
Kokborok: bórok mung thwgwi tongma mal-matarok tei bebak musuk-misiprok, bebak ha-o malnairok tei bebak tokrokbo

English: They went into the ark with Noah, two and two of all flesh in which there was the breath of life
Kokborok: bórok Noa bai baksa khe rungtoro hap bai kha, langma gwnangrok bebak’-no bojoro-bojoro khe hap kha

English: And they that entered, male and female of all flesh, went in as God had commanded him; and the Lord shut him in
Kokborok: Kaitor bono dagima hai khe’-no bebak langma gwnangrokno chwla bwrwi khe hap kha, hakhe Subraiyung phatar tẃi khe dugar berebwi rw kha

English: The flood continued forty days upon the earth; and the waters increased, and bore up the ark, and it rose high above the earth
Kokborok: Nokha sal brwichi jora twi torsa kha, twi kwbángma wng khe rungtor kuphungsa kha, abo ha bai kak kha

English: The waters prevailed and increased greatly upon the earth; and the ark floated on the face of the waters
Kokborok: Twi belai khe thuk kha, hakhe twi saka-o rungtor kuphungsaui tong kha

English: And the waters prevailed so mightily upon the earth that all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered;
Kokborok: Ha saka-o twi aswk khe torsa kha, nokha tolani bebak hathái kuchukrok lomsa bai kha

English: the waters prevailed above the mountains, covering them fifteen cubits deep
Kokborok: Hathái kuchukrok lom súgwi twi teibo mitar kaichi chuksa kha

English: And all flesh died that moved upon the earth, birds, cattle, beasts, all swarming creatures that swarm upon the earth, and every man;
Kokborok: Ha saka-o tongma bebak langma gwnangrok thwi bai kha, tokrok musuk-misiprok, mal-matarok, malnairok tei bebak borokrok thwi bai kha

English: everything on the dry land in whose nostrils was the breath of life died
Kokborok: Hakungo tongnai bebak langma gwnangrok thwi bai kha

English: He blotted out every living thing that was upon the face of the ground, man and animals and creeping things and birds of the air; they were blotted out from the earth  Only Noah was left, and those that were with him in the ark
Kokborok: Ha saka-o tongnai bebak langma gwnangrok, borokrok, mal-mata tei ha-o malnai tei toksa-toktẃirok páiwi tháng kha, bórok hani sokomorwi páijak kha  Noa tei bo bai baksa rungtoro tongnairok simi se tong rwk si-o

English: And the waters prevailed upon the earth a hundred and fifty days
Kokborok: Ha saka-o nokha sal sara-bachi jora twi tong kha

English: But God remembered Noah and all the beasts and all the cattle that were with him in the ark. And God made a wind blow over the earth, and the waters subsided;
Kokborok: Aboni ulo Subraiyung Noano muitu khlai kha tei bo bai baksa tongnai bebak mal-matarokno, musuk-misiprok, toksa-toktẃirokno muitu khlai kha, Kaitor nokbar sip rw kha tei twirok bathak kha.

English: the fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed, the rain from the heavens was restrained,
Kokborok: Nokhani uatwirok thak kha tei twimuk twirokbo yormani thak kha.

English: and the waters receded from the earth continually. At the end of a hundred and fifty days the waters had abated;
Kokborok: O ha sakani twirok kechepsana chengsaui tong kha. Nokha sal sara-bachi ká khe’-no twi kechep kha

English: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest upon the mountains of Ararat.
Kokborok: tei aboni ulo talsnini talo tei sal chisnini salo rungtor Ararat hathái-o thángwi kwchaui kha.

English: And the waters continued to abate until the tenth month; in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains were seen.
Kokborok: Nokha talchi jora twi ran kha, talchini puilani salo hatháini bokhorok nukjak kha.

English: At the end of forty days Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made,
Kokborok: Aboni ulo sal brwichini salo Noa bini snammani rungtorni tailamno phiyok kha

English: and sent forth a raven; and it went to and fro until the waters were dried up from the earth.
Kokborok: tei tokha masa phiyógwi rohor kha; abo ayang-uyang ha kwran naitugwi birwi tong kha.

English: Then he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters had subsided from the face of the ground;
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe twi de ran kha nainani bagwi pharuk masano phiyógwi rohor kha;

English: but the dove found no place to set her foot, and she returned to him to the ark, for the waters were still on the face of the whole earth. So he put forth his hand and took her and brought her into the ark with him.
Kokborok: hai phano o pharuk bájaknai kwrwi wngwi abo hai’-no hai rungtoro kiphilwi phai kha, tamni hwn ba aphuru phano ha sakani twi kechep súk ya kho. Hakhe bo abono yak phologwi rungtor bisingo sitok kha.

English: He waited another seven days, and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark;
Kokborok: Aboni ulo bo tei salsni naisigwi tong kha tei hai’-no hai pharukno phiyógwi rohor-u kha;

English: and the dove came back to him in the evening, and lo, in her mouth a freshly plucked olive leaf; so Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth.
Kokborok: o pharuk sarigo bini thani kiphilwi phai kha tei búkhúgo tháistwi buphangni bwlái kwlwi tubu-ui phai kha, abo bai Noa ha sakani twi ran kha hwnwi saui man kha.

English: Then he waited another seven days, and sent forth the dove; and she did not return to him any more.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo bo tei salsni naisingwi tong phi kha tei hai’-no hai pharukno phiyógwi rohor kha, aphuru khe abo tei bini aro kiphilwi phai li ya.

English: In the six hundred and first year, in the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were dried from off the earth; and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and behold, the face of the ground was dry.
Kokborok: Nokha bisi dok-rani puilani bisi-o tei puilani talo tei puilani salo bebak twirok hani ran kha; Noa rungtorni núkhúng phiyogwi nahar khe, hani twirok ranjak nuk kha.

English: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.
Kokborok: Talnwi talni sal nwichisnini salo twirok ran bai kha.

English: Then God said to Noah,
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Kaitor Noano hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “Go forth from the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons' wives with you.
Kokborok: “Nwng bai nihik tei nwslasong tei bórokni bihiksong bebak’-no rungtorni ongkhorwi phai di.

English: Bring forth with you every living thing that is with you of all flesh—birds and animals and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth—that they may breed abundantly on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply upon the earth.”
Kokborok: Nini rungtorni bebak langma gwnang mal-matarok tei tokrokno tẃiwi nongkhor si di, bebak malnai tei birnairok bórok ernai; abórok o hayungo erwi pung si thung.”

English: So Noah went forth, and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Noani bihik bwsa tei bahamjwksong nongkhor lai kha.

English: And every beast, every creeping thing, and every bird, everything that moves upon the earth, went forth by families out of the ark.
Kokborok: Joto mal-matarok tei bebak birnairok, malnairok, yóngsa-kuksarok bebak’-no bojro-bojro khe rungtorni phataro nongkhor lai kha. Noa ratharwi yapharmani

English: Then Noah built an altar to the Lord, and took of every clean animal and of every clean bird, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
Kokborok: Hakhe Noa Subraiyungni bagwi háyá kaisa tang kha; hakhe bebak mal-mata tei tokrokni bere barokna bagwi chúkjak kwtharrokno háyá-o sógwi barok kha.

English: And when the Lord smelled the pleasing odor, the Lord said in his heart, “I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the imagination of man's heart is evil from his youth; neither will I ever again destroy every living creature as I have done.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Subraiyung aboni báhaino belai’-no motomjak kha, hakhe Subraiyung bini khá bisingo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang o hano tei swraimung rw li ya, tamni hwn ba borokrok achai phuruni simi’-no cha ya wngwi phai kha. Aboni bagwi omo hai khe hayungni langma gwnangrokno tei sokomor li ya.

English: While the earth remains, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night, shall not cease.”
Kokborok: O hayung tong sak maiphang-khulphang tei muikwthwng-tháikwthwng káinani tei thumnani jora tongnai. Tungmani mol tei kwchangni molbo tongnai, abo hai khe sal tei hor wng máng tongnai.”

English: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth.
Kokborok: Kaitor Noa bai bwsarokno hamari rwui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bwsa kwbáng man di, er di tei hano kupulung khlai di.

English: The fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth, and upon every bird of the air, upon everything that creeps on the ground and all the fish of the sea; into your hand they are delivered.
Kokborok: Ha sakani bebak mal-matarok, malnairok tei toksa toktẃirok, twini árok nono ma kirinai, abórok bebak’-no nini yago yapharjak kha.

English: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you; and as I gave you the green plants, I give you everything.
Kokborok: Bebak langma gwnangrokno; tei dalo-samprarok bebak’-no nini chámung wngnai, ang nono abórok bebak’-no rw kha.

English: Only you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood.
Kokborok: Tamo khe nwng thẃi gwnang kwthang manwi simi ta chá di, tamni hwn ba thẃi-o se langma tongo.

English: For your lifeblood I will surely require a reckoning; of every beast I will require it and of man; of every man's brother I will require the life of man.
Kokborok: Khoroksa swk nini thẃino kochok rw khe ang abono muitu tonnai. Mal-matarok phano eba borok phano bini takhukno buthar khe ang aboni bagwi butharwi yagul sunai.

English: Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed; for God made man in his own image.
Kokborok: Khoroksa swk borok buthar khe bo tei kubun borok bai butharjaknai; tamni hwn ba borok Kaitorno kosol khe snamjak.

English: And you, be fruitful and multiply, bring forth abundantly on the earth and multiply in it.”
Kokborok: “Hakhe, nwng bwsa kwbángma man di tei ha gwnangwi erwi o hano kupulung khlai di.”

English: Then God said to Noah and to his sons with him,
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor Noa tei bo bai baksa tongnai bini bwsarokno hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “Behold, I establish my covenant with you and your descendants after you,
Kokborok: “Nai di, ang nwng bai tei nini ulni bosongrok bai swmai tang kha,

English: and with every living creature that is with you, the birds, the cattle, and every beast of the earth with you, as many as came out of the ark.
Kokborok: nwng bai baksa tongnai langma gwnang bebak mal-matarok bai, toksa-toktẃirok bai musuk-misiprok tei nwng bai baksa rungtorni ha-o nongkhornai bebak bai’-no swmai tango.

English: I establish my covenant with you, that never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of a flood, and never again shall there be a flood to destroy the earth.”
Kokborok: Ang nwng bai swmai tango, ang tei bebak langma gwnangrokno twi kotor bai khe ha sakani sokomor li ya tei o hano ang twi kotor bai tei sokomor glak kha.”

English: And God said, “This is the sign of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all future generations
Kokborok: Tei Kaitor hai hwnwi sa phi kha, “Omo nwng bai ani bisingo tei nini logini bebak langma gwnangrokni bisingo tei thinangni bosongrokni bagwi o swmaini mari rw kha

English: I set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.
Kokborok: Ang ani chokhrengno nokha-o ton kha, omo’-no ang swngchar bai swmai tangmani mari wngnai.

English: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow is seen in the clouds,
Kokborok: Ang nokha-o chumui kasa rw phuru, o chokhreng nukjak khe,

English: I will remember my covenant which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall never again become a flood to destroy all flesh.
Kokborok: Ang nwng bai tei o ha saka-o tongma bebak langma gwnangrok bai swmai tangmano muitu khlainai, hakhe bebak langma gwnangrokno sokomorna bagwi twi tor rw glak.

English: When the bow is in the clouds, I will look upon it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.”
Kokborok: Chumui-o chokhreng wng khe ang abono naisigwi Kaitor bai bebak ha saka-o kwthang tongnairok swmai tangmano muitu khlainai.”

English: God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant which I have established between me and all flesh that is upon the earth.”
Kokborok:
Kaitor Noano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo’-no ha saka-o tongma bebak langma gwnangrok bai ang swmai tangmani mari.”
Noa tei bwsarok

English: The sons of Noah who went forth from the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.
Kokborok: Rungtorni nongkhornai Noani bwsarok wng kha Sem, Ham tei Japhet. Ham wng kha Kananni bupha.

English: These three were the sons of Noah; and from these the whole earth was peopled.
Kokborok: O khorok thamrok khe Noani bwsarok; Bórokni bising tẃi’-no o ha saka-o borok kupulung wngo.

English: Noah was the first tiller of the soil. He planted a vineyard;
Kokborok: Noa wng kha swkang ha muphunwi núkhung khlainaisa. Bo’-no swkang angur phang lwng khlai gra-o;

English: and he drank of the wine, and became drunk, and lay uncovered in his tent.
Kokborok: bo angur bwtwi nung kha, hakhe phek kha tei ríni nogo sago rí kwrwi khe thu-ui tong kha.

English: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brothers outside.
Kokborok: Kananni bupha Ham buphano sago rí kwrwi nuk kha, hakhe phataro thángwi bini tei khoroknwi takhuksongno saui kha.

English: Then Shem and Japheth took a garment, laid it upon both their shoulders, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of their father; their faces were turned away, and they did not see their father's nakedness.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Sem bai Japhet rí kangsa naui khoroknwini phairukungo boksaui uklok khe him laiwi thángwi buphano rí bólwi rw lai kha; bórokni bumkhang uphiljak wngwi bórok buphani rí kwrwino mokol bai nuk li ya.

English:
When Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him, he said, “Cursed be Canaan;
a slave of slaves shall he be to his brothers.”
Kokborok:
Noa angur bwtwi phekmani swra khe bwsla kusu bono tamo khlai kha saui manẃi hai hwnwi sa kha; “Kanan swraimung nangnai;
bo bini takhuksongni selengni seleng wngnai.”

English: After the flood Noah lived three hundred and fifty years.
Kokborok: Twi kotorni ulo Noa teibo nokha bisi thamrabachi thangwi tong lang kha.

English: All the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years; and he died.
Kokborok: Tei bini umor nokha bisi chukurabachi-o khe thwi kha.

English: These are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth; sons were born to them after the flood.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Noani bwsarok Sem, Ham tei Japhetni bosongrok. Twi kotorni ulo se bórokni bwsarok achai-o.

English: The sons of Japheth  Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
Kokborok: Japhetni bwsarok wng kha  Gomer, Magog, Madai, Jaban, Tubal, Mesek tei Tiras.

English: The sons of Gomer  Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
Kokborok: Gomerni bwsarok wng kha  Askenas, Riphat tei Togarma.

English: The sons of Javan  Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
Kokborok: Jabanni bwsarok wng kha  Elisa, Tarsis, Kitim tei Dodanim.

English: From these the coastland peoples spread. These are the sons of Japheth in their lands, each with his own language, by their families, in their nations.
Kokborok: Omorokni’-no twijlang rukungni tei twi kwcharni ha-o tongnairokni yaphangni bórok pírjago. Omorokni dopha juda-o tongma tei kubun harogo tongnairok Japhetni bosongrok. Bórok juda juda sak-sakni kók bai kók sa lai-o.

English: The sons of Ham  Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Hamni bwsarok  Kus, Ijip, Put tei Kanan.

English: The sons of Cush  Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah  Sheba and Dedan.
Kokborok: Kusni bwsarok wng kha  Seba, Habila, Sabta, Rama tei Sabteka. Ramani bwsarok wng kha  Seba bai Dedan.

English: Cush became the father of Nimrod; he was the first on earth to be a mighty man.
Kokborok: Kus khe Nimrodni bupha wng kha, bo’-no ha saka-o swkang phan gwnang kuk borok wngma klai-o.

English: He was a mighty hunter before the Lord; therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the Lord.”
Kokborok: Bo wng kha Subraiyungni thani belai’-no poluan kotor kuk; aboni bagwi’-no “Nimrod hai Subraiyungni thani poluan kotor kuk” hwnwi kók sajago.

English: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, and Accad, all of them in the land of Shinar.
Kokborok: Bini ha Babel, Erek tei Akadni simi chengwi Sinarni bebak harok.

English: From that land he went into Assyria, and built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah, and
Kokborok: Bo aroni Asiriya-o tháng kha tei Ninobi, Rehobot-Ir, Kala tei.

English: Resen between Nineveh and Calah; that is the great city.
Kokborok: Ninobi bai Kalani kwchar Resenno tisa kha, abo wng kha kamiyung kotor.

English: Egypt became the father of Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naph-tuhim,
Kokborok: Ijipni bosongrok wng kha  Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naptuhim,

English: Pathrusim, Casluhim (whence came the Philistines), and Caphtorim.
Kokborok: Path-rusim, Kasluhim, (o bosongni’-no Philistiasarok phai-o) tei Kapthorim.

English: Canaan became the father of Sidon his first-born, and Heth,
Kokborok: Kananni bwsarok  Bwsa okra Sidon, bo’-no okra tei Het,

English: and the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
Kokborok: tei Jebussa, Amorsa, Girgassa,

English: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
Kokborok: Hibisa, Arkitsa, Sinitsa,

English: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Afterward the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.
Kokborok: Arbadsa, Semarisa tei Hamathisa. Yagulo khe Kananni juda juda bosongrok yang yang koproi kha.

English: And the territory of the Canaanites extended from Sidon, in the direction of Gerar, as far as Gaza, and in the direction of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
Kokborok: Kanansongni ha khe Sidonni simi chengwi Gerar babai Gaja jora tei Sodom, Gomora Adma, Jeboim jora tei Lasa jora khu-ui o.

English: These are the sons of Ham, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
Kokborok: Omorok’-no bosongrok, juda juda hodarok tei juda juda ha mareui bórok Hamni bosongrok.

English: To Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the elder brother of Japheth, children were born.
Kokborok: Japhetni takhuk okra Sem wng kha bebak Eberrokni bosongni yaphang.

English: The sons of Shem  Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.
Kokborok: Semni bwsarok khe  Elam, Asur, Arapaksad, Lud tei Aram.

English: The sons of Aram  Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
Kokborok: Aramni bwsarokni mung wng kha  Uj, Hul, Gether tei Mesek.

English: Arpachshad became the father of Shelah; and Shelah became the father of Eber.
Kokborok: Arpaksad wng kha Selani bupha; Sela khe Eberni bupha.

English: To Eber were born two sons  the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and his brother's name was Joktan.
Kokborok: Eberni bwsa chwla khoroknwi tongo  khoroksani mung khe Peleg, tamni hwn ba a jora’-ono o ha khakjago tei khoroksani mung khe Joktan.

English: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
Kokborok: Joktanni bwsarok wng kha  Almodad, Selep, Hajarmabet, Jera,

English: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
Kokborok: Hadoram, Ujal, Dikla,

English: Obal, Abima-el, Sheba,
Kokborok: Obal, Abimael, Seba,

English: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab; all these were the sons of Joktan.
Kokborok: Ophir, Habila tei Jobab; bórok bebak’-no Joktanni bwsarok máng.

English: The territory in which they lived extended from Mesha in the direction of Sephar to the hill country of the east.
Kokborok: Bórok tongma ha salkabaini hathái kami Mesani simi Sephar jora.

English: These are the sons of Shem, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Semni bwsarok, bórokni hoda mareui tei ha thwgwi’-no tongo tei sak sakni kók bai kók sa laiwi tongo.

English: These are the families of the sons of Noah, according to their genealogies, in their nations; and from these the nations spread abroad on the earth after the flood.
Kokborok: O dopha thwgwi bosongni bwtang thwgwi’-no o borokrok bebak’-no Noani bosong máng. Twi kotor wngmani yagulo ha sakani bebak dopharok’-no Noani bwsarokni bosongni nongkhorma máng se.

English: Now the whole earth had one language and few words.
Kokborok: Yaphango ha gwnangwi’-no borokrokni kókmung-kókrok thansa se tei kóktháirokbo kisa.

English: And as men migrated from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
Kokborok: Borokrok salkabaini thángwi bórok Babilonni hapharno nuk kha tei aro’-no kami kha ka.

English: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
Kokborok: Bórok khoroksa tei khoroksano hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Phai di chwng it tagwi sógwi rak rw lai anw.” Hakhe bórok nok tangna bagwi it tei abono chwrap rwna bagwi belengtwi man kha.

English: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Phai di chwng chini bagwi kamiyung tang lai anw tei nokha jora kuchuk khe nok kuchuk khungsa tisa lai anw tei chini mung rw lai anw, omo hai khe chwng ha gwnangwi pisa-piring wng ya tẃi kurum-kuthum khe tongwi man lai anw.”

English: And the Lord came down to see the city and the tower, which the sons of men had built.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Subraiyung borokrok tang laimani u kamiyungno tei a nok kuchukno naina bagwi nongkhorwi phai kha.

English: And the Lord said, “Behold, they are one people, and they have all one language; and this is only the beginning of what they will do; and nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
Kokborok: Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di bórok borok kaisa se tei bórokni kókmungbo kaisa; tei omono bórok tamo khlaino aboni cheng grama se, phiya bórok khlaina hwnwi chap laimano tei samung cha rwui man glak kha.

English: Come, let us go down, and there confuse their language, that they may not understand one another's speech.”
Kokborok: Phai di, chwng nongkhorwi thángwi bórokni kókno gothok lai ya khlaiwi rwui khoroksa bai tei khoroksano tamo sa kha romwi man lai ya khlai rw lai anw.”

English: So the Lord scattered them abroad from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city.
Kokborok: Omo hai khe Subraiyung bórokno ha gwnangwi’-no pisarwi rw kha tei bórok o kamiyung tisama yakarwi tháng lai kha.

English: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth; and from there the Lord scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi’-no abono Babel hwnwi mung pharjago, tamni hwn ba Subraiyung aro’-no ha gwnangwini borokrokno kók gothok lai ya khlaiwi rw-o tei Subraiyung aroni’-no bórokno ha gwnangwi koproi rwui rw-o.

English: These are the descendants of Shem. When Shem was a hundred years old, he became the father of Arpachshad two years after the flood;
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Semni bosongrok   twi kotorni nokha bisik nwini ulo Semni umor nokha bisi sara phuru bo Arpaksad hwnwi bwsa chwla khoroksano man kha;

English: and Shem lived after the birth of Arpachshad five hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: tei Sembo Arpaksad achaimani ulo nokha bisik bara thangwi tongwi tei kubun chwla tei bwrwi bwsarokno man lang kha.

English: When Arpachshad had lived thirty-five years, he became the father of Shelah;
Kokborok: Arpaksadni umor nokha bisi thamchiba phuru khe bo bwsa chwla Selano man kha;

English: and Arpachshad lived after the birth of Shelah four hundred and three years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo bo tei nokha bisik brwithamra thangwi tongwi tei kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Shelah had lived thirty years, he became the father of Eber;
Kokborok: Selani umor nokha bisik thamchi phuru bo bwsa chwla Eberno man kha,

English: and Shelah lived after the birth of Eber four hundred and three years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo Eber achaimani nokha bisi brwiratham kwthang tongwi Sela teibo kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Eber had lived thirty-four years, he became the father of Peleg;
Kokborok: Eberni umor nokha bisi thamchibrwi phuru bo bwsa chwla Pelegno man kha;

English: and Eber lived after the birth of Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo bo teibo nokha bisik brwirathamchi thangwi tongwi tei kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Peleg had lived thirty years, he became the father of Reu;
Kokborok: Pelegni umor nokha bisi thamchi phuru bo bwsa chwla Reuno man kha,

English: and Peleg lived after the birth of Reu two hundred and nine years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo bo teibo nokha bisik nwirachuku thangwi tongwi tei kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Reu had lived thirty-two years, he became the father of Serug;
Kokborok: Reuni umor nokha bisi thamchinwi phuru, bo bwsa chwla Serugno man kha;

English: and Reu lived after the birth of Serug two hundred and seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo bo tei nokha bisik nwirasni thangwi tongwi tei kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Serug had lived thirty years, he became the father of Nahor;
Kokborok: Serugni umor nokha bisi thamchi phuru bo bwsa chwla Nahorno man kha;

English: and Serug lived after the birth of Nahor two hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo tei nokha bisi nwira thangwi tongwi tei kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Nahor had lived twenty-nine years, he became the father of Terah;
Kokborok: Nahorni umor nokha bisi nwichichuku phuru, bo bwsa chwla Terano man kha;

English: and Nahor lived after the birth of Terah a hundred and nineteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
Kokborok: aboni ulo bo tei nokha bisi sarachichuku thangwi tongwi tei kubun bwsarokno man kha.

English: When Terah had lived seventy years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
Kokborok: Terani umor nokha bisi snichi phuru khe bo Abram, Nahor tei Haranni bupha wng kha.

English: Now these are the descendants of Terah. Terah was the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran was the father of Lot.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Terani bosongrok. Tera wng kha Abram, Nahor tei Haranni bupha; tei Haran khe Lotni bupha.

English: Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
Kokborok: Haran bupha kwthang tongsani’-no bini achaima ha Kolodiarokni Ur kamiyungo thwi kha.

English: And Abram and Nahor took wives; the name of Abram's wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor's wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
Kokborok: Abram Saraino káijak kha tei Nahor khe Haranni bwsajwk Milkano káijak kha, bo wng kha Iskanibo bupha.

English: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.
Kokborok: Sarai khe banji; bwsa man ya.

English: Terah took Abram his son and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife, and they went forth together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
Kokborok: Tera, bwsla Abram tei busuk Haranni bwsla Lotno tei bahamjwk Sarai Abramni bihikno tẃiwi Kolodiarokni hani Ur kamiyungni nongkhorwi Kanan ha-o thángna bagwi lam rwui tháng kha. Bórok kwchalma Haran jora tháng kha tei aro kami khaui lai kha.

English: The days of Terah were two hundred and five years; and Terah died in Haran.
Kokborok: Tera aro nokha bisik nwiraba wng khe Harano thwi kha.

English: Now the Lord said to Abram, “Go from your country and your kindred and your father's house to the land that I will show you.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nini sakni hano yakarwi sandai-masarokno tei nwphani nokno yakarwi ang nono phunukna hwnmani a ha-o tháng di.

English: And I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you, and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.
Kokborok: Hakhe ang nono dopha kotor snamnai, ang nono hamari rwnai, nini mungno kuchuk khlainai, omo hai khe nwng kaisa hamari wngnai.

English: I will bless those who bless you, and him who curses you I will curse; and by you all the families of the earth shall bless themselves.”
Kokborok:
Nono hamari rwnairokno ang hamari rwnai,
tei nono swrainairokno ang swrainai;
tei nini bising tẃi khe ha sakani
bebak núkhungno ang hamari rwnai.”

English: So Abram went, as the Lord had told him; and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.
Kokborok: Subraiyung tháng di hwnwi dagima tẃi’-no Abram tháng kha; tei Lotbo bo bai baksa tháng kha. Abram Haranni nongkhorwi tháng phuru bini umor nokha bisi snichiba ká kha.

English: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all their possessions which they had gathered, and the persons that they had gotten in Haran; and they set forth to go to the land of Canaan. When they had come to the land of Canaan,
Kokborok: Abram bihik Saraino tei bini takhukni bwsla Lotno tei bórok Haranno thummani bebak rang-mairok tei borokrokno tlangwi tháng lai kha; hakhe bórok ha Kananno thángna bagwi lam rw lai kha. Bórok ha Kananno phai khe,

English: Abram passed through the land to the place at Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time the Canaanites were in the land.
Kokborok: Abram a ha tẃi lái phuru Sekemni More-o uk buphang tong thani bathago. A jora-o Kananni borokrok aro tong kho.

English: Then the Lord appeared to Abram, and said, “To your descendants I will give this land.” So he built there an altar to the Lord, who had appeared to him.
Kokborok: Aphuru Subraiyung Abramno nukjagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini bosongno ang o hano rwnai.” Hakhe bo bo bai nukjaknai Subraiyungni bagwi aro háyá kaisa snam kha.

English: Thence he removed to the mountain on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east; and there he built an altar to the Lord and called on the name of the Lord.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe bo Bethelni salkabai hathái bwkhak kutulwi tháng kha tei Bethelni salthángbai-o tei Aini khe salkabai-o ríni nok tang kha, aro bo Subraiyungni bagwi háyá snam kha tei Subraiyungni mungno ring kha.

English: And Abram journeyed on, still going toward the Negeb.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Abram thángte-thángte ha Negeb bwkhak tháng rw rwk kha.

English: Now there was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to sojourn there, for the famine was severe in the land.
Kokborok: Tabuk ha Kananno asari nang kha, aro belai khe asari nangma bagwi Abram ha Ijibo sal kisa tongwina bagwi tháng kha.

English: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to Sarai his wife, “I know that you are a woman beautiful to behold;
Kokborok: Ha Ijibo sógwi twtwi wng khe bo bihik Saraino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng wng kha bwrwi naithok;

English: and when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife’; then they will kill me, but they will let you live.
Kokborok: hakhe, ha Ijipni borokrok nono nuk khe, ‘Omo nini hik’-no’ hwnwi sa anw tei ano butharwi páiwi nono khe buthar glak.

English: Say you are my sister, that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared on your account.”
Kokborok: Nwng ani bukhuk se hwnwi sa ja di, hakhe nini jarwi ang kwthang narwkjak anw phiya angbo kaham’-no tong anw.”

English: When Abram entered Egypt the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.
Kokborok: Abram ha Ijibo habwi khe’-no ha Ijipni borokrok a bwrwino belai khe naithokjak lai kha.

English: And when the princes of Pharaoh saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh. And the woman was taken into Pharaoh's house.
Kokborok: Pharaoni tẃiphangrok bono nuk kha tei bini naithokmani kókrokno Pharaono saui lai kha. Hakhe a bwrwi Pharaoni nogo tlangjak kha.

English: And for her sake he dealt well with Abram; and he had sheep, oxen, he-asses, menservants, maidservants, she-asses, and camels.
Kokborok: Bini bagwi Pharao Abramno kaham khe ton kha tei bono merarok, musukrok, korai chwlarok, selengrok, selengjwkrok, korai bumarok tei mangingangrok rw kha.

English: But the Lord afflicted Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram's wife.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Pharao Abramni bihik Saraino tlangmani bagwi Subraiyung bini sago tei bini nogo tongnai bebakni saka-o belaino bemuk hamya rwui rohor kha.

English: So Pharaoh called Abram, and said, “What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Abramno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ano tamo khlai kha de? Nwng tamo omo nini hik se hwnwi sa ya?

English: Why did you say, ‘She is my sister,’ so that I took her for my wife? Now then, here is your wife, take her, and be gone.”
Kokborok: Nwng tamo ‘Omo ani bukhuk se’ hwnwi sa? Aboni bagwi se ang ani hik snamna bagwi ang bono tlango, hakhe iko nini hikno tlangwi tháng si di.”

English: And Pharaoh gave men orders concerning him; and they set him on the way, with his wife and all that he had.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Pharao bini borokrokno dagiui rw kha; tei bórok bono bini hani kariui rohor kha bini bihik tei bini tongma bebak’-no rwui rohor kha.

English: So Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the Negeb.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abram ha Ijipni nongkhorwi tháng kha, bo tei bihik tei bini bebak tongmarokno tei Lotno tlangwi Negebo tháng lai kha.

English: Now Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.
Kokborok: Abram merarok, punrok tei mal-matarok bai gwnang wng kha, rangchak rukphaibo wng kha.

English: And he journeyed on from the Negeb as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,
Kokborok: Bo Negebni simi Bethel bwkhak tháng kha, hakhe swkango bo ríni nok tang thani sógwi kha. Abo Bethel bai Aini kwcharo,

English: to the place where he had made an altar at the first; and there Abram called on the name of the Lord.
Kokborok: bo aro’-no swkang háyá snamo; tei aro’-no Abram Subraiyungno khulumo.

English: And Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents,
Kokborok: Abram bai baksa thángnai Lotnibo merarok, punrok, mal-matarok tei bini hai’-no sakni núkhung bai selengrok tongo,

English: so that the land could not support both of them dwelling together; for their possessions were so great that they could not dwell together,
Kokborok: aboni bagwi oro khoroknwi kwrẃngsa khe tongnani ha khochor kha; bórokni mal-matarok belai bángmani bagwi thansa khe kwrẃngsa-o tongwi man li ya,

English: and there was strife between the herdsmen of Abram's cattle and the herdsmen of Lot's cattle. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites dwelt in the land.
Kokborok: hakhe, Abramni mal-mata mwrwknairok bai Lotni mal-mata mwrwknairok uálai kha, a jora-o Kanansarok bai Peressarok a ha-o tongo.

English: Then Abram said to Lot, “Let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herdsmen and my herdsmen; for we are kinsmen.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Abram Lotno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng bai ang le uálaima cha ya tei nini mal-mata mwrwknairok bai ani mal-mata mwrwknairokbo uálaimabo cha ya; tamni hwn khe chwng wng kha sandai se.

English: Is not the whole land before you? Separate yourself from me. If you take the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if you take the right hand, then I will go to the left.”
Kokborok: O bebak ha nini bwskango ya diba? Nwng ang bai nok juda wng si di. Nwng yaksi bwkhak tháng khe ang yagra bwkhak thángnai; eba nwng yagra bwkhak tháng khe ang yaksi bwkhak thángnai.”

English: And Lot lifted up his eyes, and saw that the Jordan valley was well watered everywhere like the garden of the Lord, like the land of Egypt, in the direction of Zoar; this was before the Lord destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.
Kokborok: Hakhe Lot ayang-uyang nahar kha tei nuk kha Jordan rukung ha kaham, bebago twi nangjak, Subraiyungni tháiblwng hai, ha Ijipni Juar phaisingni ha hai, omo wng kha Subraiyungni mokol bwskango bo sokomorma kamiyung Sodom bai Gomora.

English: So Lot chose for himself all the Jordan valley, and Lot journeyed east; thus they separated from each other.
Kokborok: Hakhe Lot Jordan rukungni bebakno saui na kha tei bo salkabai bwkhak tháng kha, omo hai khe bórok khoroknwi kak lai kha.

English: Abram dwelt in the land of Canaan, while Lot dwelt among the cities of the valley and moved his tent as far as Sodom.
Kokborok: Abram Kananni ha-o tong kha, Lot khe harungni kamiyungo tong kha tei Sodomni samo khe ríni nok tang kha.

English: Now the men of Sodom were wicked, great sinners against the Lord.
Kokborok: Sodomni borokrok khe belai’-no hamya tei Subraiyungni thani belai khe sumsok khlai kha.

English: The Lord said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, “Lift up your eyes, and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward;
Kokborok: Lot bo bai kagwi tháng khe Subraiyung Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tabuk tongmani oroni kaham khe nahar di, salgrabai, salsibai tei salkabai phiya salthángbai bwkhak nahar di;

English: for all the land which you see I will give to you and to your descendants for ever.
Kokborok: tamni hwn khe, nwng tabuk nuhurma-o harokno ang nono tei nini bosongrokno rw phlainai.

English: I will make your descendants as the dust of the earth; so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your descendants also can be counted.
Kokborok: Ang nini bosongrokno o ha sakani hasingsa hai khe er rwnai; ha sakani hasingsano khoroksa swk lekhaui man khe nini bosongroknobo lekhaui mannai.

English: Arise, walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I will give it to you.”
Kokborok: Bacha di, tabuk o ha tongsak’-no kebeng-kosong himwi nai di, tamni hwn khe ang nono omono rwnai.”

English: So Abram moved his tent, and came and dwelt by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron; and there he built an altar to the Lord.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abram aroni kutulwi tháng kha tei Mamreni uk buphangrok tongma Hebrono tongwi kha; aro bo Subraiyungni bagwi háyá kaisa snamwi kha.

English: In the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Ched-or-laomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim,
Kokborok: Sinarni habugra, Armaphel, Ariok Elasarni habugra, Elamni habugra Khedorlaomer tei Goimni habugra Tidalsongni jora-o,

English: these kings made war with Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).
Kokborok: o habugrarok Sodomni habugra Bera bai choba nang lai kha, Gomorani habugra Birsa, Adamani habugra Sinab, Jeboimni habugra Semeber tei Belani (abo Joar se) habugrarok bai khe choba nang lai kha.

English: And all these joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Salt Sea).
Kokborok: A bebak kwthajak phanrok Sidimni harungo (abo wng kha sómni twijlang) kuthum lai kha.

English: Twelve years they had served Ched-or-laomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
Kokborok: Nokha bisi chiknwi bórok Khedorlaomerno monwi tong kha, tamo khe, nokha bisi chitham phuru khe bórok kebengwi bacha lai kha.

English: In the fourteenth year Ched-or-laomer and the kings who were with him came and subdued the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,
Kokborok: Nokha bisi chibrwi phuru khe Khedorlaomer bai bini kwtha laijak habugrarok bórokni sengkrakrokno tubu-ui Asterot-Karnaimni Rephaimno, Jujimni Hamno, Sabe-kiriyathaimni Emimno

English: and the Horites in their Mount Seir as far as El-paran on the border of the wilderness;
Kokborok: tei Horsong tongmani hathái Seirni simi hasing panthorni páithak Elparan jorani hano mechen kha

English: then they turned back and came to Enmishpat (that is, Kadesh), and subdued all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who dwelt in Hazazon-tamar.
Kokborok: ulo khe bórok uphilwi Enmispato kiphilwi phai kha (abo wng kha Kades), bórok bebak Amaleksarokni hano segwi na kha tei Hajajon-tamaro tongnai Amorsaroknobo mechen kha.

English: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out, and they joined battle in the Valley of Siddim
Kokborok: Hakhe Sodomni habugra, Gomorani habugra, Admani habugra, Jeboimni habugra tei Belani (abo wng kha Joar) habugrarok Sidimni harungo ongkhorwi thángwi

English: with Ched-or-laomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar, four kings against five.
Kokborok: Elamni habugra Khedorlaomer, Goimni habugra Tidal, Sinarni habugra Amraphel tei Elasarni habugra Arioksong bugra khorokba habugra bai nang lai kha.

English: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits; and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, and the rest fled to the mountain.
Kokborok: Hakhe Sidimni harung khe alkatrani hakhor bai máng se kupulung; Sodomni habugra bai Gomorani habugrasong chobani khárwi tháng phuru khe bórok baksa hakhoro klai kha, tamo khe tei baksa khe hathái-o khárwi yók kha.

English: So the enemy took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their provisions, and went their way;
Kokborok: A kebengnairok Sodom bai Gomorani bebak manwirok na kha tei chámungrokbo tlangwi tháng bai kha;

English: they also took Lot, the son of Abram's brother, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.
Kokborok: bórok Abramni takhukni bwsla Sodomo tongnai Lotno tei bini manwi-khwnwiroknobo tlangwi tháng lai kha.

English: Then one who had escaped came, and told Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and of Aner; these were allies of Abram.
Kokborok: Tamo khe borok khoroksa yógwi phaiwi Hibrusa, Abramno bebakno sa phai kha, bo Amorsa Mamresongni Uk buphangrok tongma-o tongo; Mamre tei bini takhuk Eskol bai Anersong khe Abram bai kwthajak.

English: When Abram heard that his kinsman had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, three hundred and eighteen of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
Kokborok: Abram bini takhukni bwslano romwi tlangjak kha hwnwi khna kheno, bo bini núkhungni bebak choba kwrwngrokno kwrẃngsa-o khe ring kha, bórok bebagwi khorokthamra chichar ká-o, bórok bórokno riphrigwi Dan jora tháng lai kha.

English: And he divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and routed them and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
Kokborok: Aro bo bini borokrokno gut-gut khe khak kha, horo khe arirokno swpragwi mechen kha. Bo bai bini borokrok bórokno Damaskosni salgrabai Hoba jora riphrigwi rwi kha.

English: Then he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his kinsman Lot with his goods, and the women and the people.
Kokborok: Hakhe tlangwi thángjakmani manwirok kiphilwi na phi kha tei bo bini takhukni bwsla Lotno tei bini manwirokno, bwrwirokno tei bebak borokrokno kiphilwi tubu kha.

English: After his return from the defeat of Ched-or-laomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King's Valley).
Kokborok: Abram, Khedorlaomer bai bini pal habugrarokno mechenwi kiphilwi phai khe, Sodomni habugra Sabeni harungo bo bai malaina tháng kha (Abo habugrani harung).

English: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine; he was priest of God Most High.
Kokborok: Salemni habugra Melkisedek auang tei angur bwtwi tubu-ui phai kha; bo wng kha Kaitor Kotorni Ochai.

Bo bono hamari rwui hai hwnwi sa kha,                                                                                             “Nokha bai ha snamnaisa Kaitor Kotor,                                                                                  Abramno hamari rw thung;

English:
and blessed be God Most High,
who has delivered your enemies into your hand!”
And Abram gave him a tenth of everything.
Kokborok: tei arirokno nini yago yapharwi mechen rwnai Kaitor Kotor lobjak thung!” Hakhe Abram bono bebakni chi-o kaisa khe rw kha.

English: And the king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the persons, but take the goods for yourself.”
Kokborok: Sodomni habugra Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “ani borokrokno ano rwui rw di, tamo khe manwi-khwnwirokno khe nwng’-no na bai di.”

English: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, “I have sworn to the Lord God Most High, maker of heaven and earth,
Kokborok: Abram Sodomni habugrano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nokha bai ha snamnaisa Kaitor Kotor Subraiyungni mungwi ang swmai tangwi sa-o,

English: that I would not take a thread or a sandal-thong or anything that is yours, lest you should say, ‘I have made Abram rich.’
Kokborok: ang nini khutwng twngsa swk eba yaklap buduk twngsa phano narwk glak, omo hai khe nwng ‘ang se Abramno gwnang khlai rw-o’ hwnwi saui man glak.

English: I will take nothing but what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me; let Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre take their share.”
Kokborok: Ang ani bagwi mungsabo na glak. Ani siklarok chámarokno simi se ang nai-o, ang bai pal thángnairok; Aner, Eskol tei Mamresongno simi bórokni manthaino rwui rw di.”

English: After these things the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision, “Fear not, Abram, I am your shield; your reward shall be very great.”
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe, Abramno Subraiyung imang-mukthang khe hai hwnwi samano khna kha, “Ta kiri di, Abram, Ang nini phui; nini sokat belai kotor wngnai.”

English: But Abram said, “O Lord God, what wilt thou give me, for I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Abram hai hwnwi sa kha, “O Subraiyung Kaitor, nwng ano tamo rwnai, tamni hwn ba ang tabukbo bwsa se kwrwi kho, ani bebakni mannai wngnai le Damaskosni Eliaser se?”

English: And Abram said, “Behold, thou hast given me no offspring; and a slave born in my house will be my heir.”
Kokborok: Abram hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, nwng ano bwsa-bẃtẃi se rw ya kho; tei ani nogo achainai seleng se ani bebakno mannai.”

English: And behold, the word of the Lord came to him, “This man shall not be your heir; your own son shall be your heir.”
Kokborok: Nai di hai hwnwi Subraiyungni kók bini thani sók phai kha, “O borok nini bosongni manthaino man glak; nini sakni tei okni bwsa se nini bosongni mannanino mannai.”

English: And he brought him outside and said, “Look toward heaven, and number the stars, if you are able to number them.” Then he said to him, “So shall your descendants be.”
Kokborok: Tei bo bono phataro tubu-ui hai hwnwi sa phai kha, “Nwng nokha saka bwkhak naisa di tei nwng athukirirokno lekhaui da man nai di.” Hakhe bo bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini bosongbo abo hai’-no wngnai”.

English: And he believed the Lord; and he reckoned it to him as righteousness.
Kokborok: Abram Subraiyungno bwkha bera kha; tei aboni bagwi Subraiyung bono bwkha phurjak kha tei bono borok kaham hwnwi yachak kha.

English: And he said to him, “I am the Lord who brought you from Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess.”
Kokborok: Tei bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang’-no Subraiyung, nono Koldiarokni ha Ur hani o hano rwnani tei tongna bagwi ringwi tubunai.”

English: But he said, “O Lord God, how am I to know that I shall possess it?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “O Subraiyung Kaitor, ang abono mannai hwnwi bahai khe saui mannai?”

English: He said to him, “Bring me a heifer three years old, a she-goat three years old, a ram three years old, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.”
Kokborok: Bo bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani thani nokha bisik thamni musuk masa, pun masa tei mera masa, tokthu masa tei pharuk bwsa masa tubu-ui phai di.”

English: And he brought him all these, cut them in two, and laid each half over against the other; but he did not cut the birds in two.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo abórokno bebakno tubu-ui phai kha tei kochoknwi khe rathar kha, khaksa khaksano nahar lalai khe beraui rw kha; tamo khe tokrokno le bo kochoknwi khlai li ya.

English: And when birds of prey came down upon the carcasses, Abram drove them away.
Kokborok: A kwthwirokni saka-o sikruk klai phai kha, tamo khe abórokno Abram rwgwi rohor kha.

English: As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell on Abram; and lo, a dread and great darkness fell upon him.
Kokborok: Sal tháng rw rwk wng kheno, Abram belai khe muktrwi phaijak kha; hakhe bini sago kirima tei mwnakma phai sók phai kha.

English: Then the Lord said to Abram, “Know of a surety that your descendants will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs, and will be slaves there, and they will be oppressed for four hundred years;
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini bwsarok buini ha-o phatarni borok wngwi ma tongwinai; bórok aro seleng wngwi ma tongwinai tei bisibrwira belai khe sátokjagwi ma tongwinai;

English: but I will bring judgment on the nation which they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions.
Kokborok: tamo khe bórokno seleng khati rwnairokno ang majra rwnai tei bórok a hani nongkhorwi phai phuru khe bórok kwbángma rangchak-ríchak tlangwi thángnai.

English: As for yourself, you shall go to your fathers in peace; you shall be buried in a good old age.
Kokborok: Nwng sakbo umor kolok wngwi tongwi kwchang kwchang khe chakra umoro thwiwi phobjaknai.

English: And they shall come back here in the fourth generation; for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.”
Kokborok: Nini bosongrok oro kiphilwi phai ya sini aro bosong kusungbrwi kánai; tamni hwn ba, Amorsarok belai borok hamya wng súgwi majra berjakna nang súk ya sak ang bórokno rwkhlai glak.”

English: When the sun had gone down and it was dark, behold, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces.
Kokborok: Sal thángwi, mwnak wng khe, hoku nongkhorjak hórni twk tei hór chẃngjak homchang nukjagwi a ratharjak muini kwchar tẃi láiwi tháng kha.

English: On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram, saying, “To your descendants I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates,
Kokborok: A salo Subraiyung Abram bai hai hwnwi saui swmai tang kha, “Nini bosongrokno ang o hano rw-o; Ijipni twimani simi Uphratis twima jorani harok,

English: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
Kokborok: Kenini ha, Kenijisasongni ha, Kadmosongni,

English: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
Kokborok: Hitisongni, Peressongni, Rephaimsongni,

English: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites and the Jebusites.”
Kokborok: Amorsasongni, Kanansasongni, Girgassasongni tei Jebussasongni ha.”

English: Now Sarai, Abram's wife, bore him no children. She had an Egyptian maid whose name was Hagar;
Kokborok: Tabuk Abramni bihik khe Sarai bwsa man ya. Bini khoroksa Ijipjwk seleng tongo bini mung khe Hagar;

English: and Sarai said to Abram, “Behold now, the Lord has prevented me from bearing children; go in to my maid; it may be that I shall obtain children by her.” And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.
Kokborok: hakhe Sarai Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung ano bwsa man ya khe ton kha; nwng ani selengjwkni thani tháng di, abo hai khe ang bini bising tẃi khe bwsa manẃi mano.” Tei Abram Sarai samani kókno khna kha.

English: So, after Abram had dwelt ten years in the land of Canaan, Sarai, Abram's wife, took Hagar the Egyptian, her maid, and gave her to Abram her husband as a wife.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abram Kanan ha-o nokha bisi chi jora tong kha, Abramni bihik Sarai Ijipni bwrwi selengjwk Hagarno bwsai Abramno hik na di hwnwi rwui rw kha.

English: And he went in to Hagar, and she conceived; and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked with contempt on her mistress.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo Hagarni thani tháng kha tei bo sakderiya wng kha; Hagar sakderiya wngma bai, bo bini nokphangjwkno sak nangjak ya wng kha.

English: And Sarai said to Abram, “May the wrong done to me be on you! I gave my maid to your embrace, and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked on me with contempt. May the Lord judge between you and me!”
Kokborok: Hakhe Sarai Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ano hamya khlaima nini sago nang thung! Ang ani selengjwkno nini khuri-o rw kha tei bo sakno sakderiya wngma nugwi, bo ano naislerjak kha Subraiyung nono bai ano buj thum thung!”

English: But Abram said to Sarai, “Behold, your maid is in your power; do to her as you please.” Then Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she fled from her.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Abram Saraino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di nini selengjwk le nini yapha-o se; nwng bono nini muchung tẃi’-no khlai di.” Hakhe Sarai bono tekto rw kha hakhe bo Saraini aroni khár kha.

English: The angel of the Lord found her by a spring of water in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur.
Kokborok: Subraiyungni suari Hagarno hasingkhongni kwrẃngsa twimuk tongma-o nuk kha, a twimuk Surni lama-o klai-o.

English: And he said, “Hagar, maid of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going?” She said, “I am fleeing from my mistress Sarai.”
Kokborok:
Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Hagar, Saraini selengjwk, nwng buroni phaiwi buro thángna naiwi tong?”
Hagar hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang ani bugrajwk Saraini aroni khárma se.”

English: The angel of the Lord said to her, “Return to your mistress, and submit to her.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyungni suari bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini bugrani thani kiphilwi thángwi bini thani nwng Sakno yapharwi di.”

English: The angel of the Lord also said to her, “I will so greatly multiply your descendants that they cannot be numbered for multitude.”
Kokborok: Subraiyungni suari bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nini bosongno aswk kwbáng khe er rwnai abórokno lekhaui pái man glak.”

English: And the angel of the Lord said to her, “Behold, you are with child, and shall bear a son; you shall call his name Ishmael; because the Lord has given heed to your affliction.
Kokborok: Subraiyungni suari bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di nini ogo tabuk bwsa tongo, nwng bwsa chwla bánai, nwng bono Ismael hwnwi mung phar di; tamni hwn ba Subraiyung nini tektono nuk kha,

English: He shall be a wild ass of a man, his hand against every man and every man's hand against him; and he shall dwell over against all his kinsmen.”
Kokborok: Bo borokrokni bisingo bolongni korai hai wngnai, bini yak bebakno’-no kebengnai tei bebak borokrokbo bono kebengnai; tei bo bebak bini borokrok bai kebengwi tongnai.”

English: So she called the name of the Lord who spoke to her, “Thou art a God of seeing”; for she said, “Have I really seen God and remained alive after seeing him?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Hagar bini thani kók sanai Subraiyungno suriui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng wng kha nuknaisa Kaitor”; phiya Hagar hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang kubui’-no da Kaitorno nuk kha tei bono nugwibuda thangwi tong kho?”

English: Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; it lies between Kadesh and Bered.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi a twikhor Bir-Lahairoi hwnjago; abo Kades bai Beredni kwcharo.

English: And Hagar bore Abram a son; and Abram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.
Kokborok: Hagar Abramni bagwi bwsa chwla khoroksa bá kha; Abram, Hagar bámani bini bwslano Ismael hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to Abram.
Kokborok: Hagar Ismaelno bá phuru Abramni umor bisicharchidok wng kha.

English: When Abram was ninety-nine years old the Lord appeared to Abram, and said to him, “I am God Almighty; walk before me, and be blameless.
Kokborok: Abramni umor nokha bisi chukuchichuku phuru Subraiyung Abram bai nukjak kha tei bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Kwplaisa Kaitor; Ani kók khna di tei samung cha ya ta khlai di.

English: And I will make my covenant between me and you, and will multiply you exceedingly.”
Kokborok: Ang nwng bai swmai tangnai tei nono kwbángma khe er rwnai.”

English: Then Abram fell on his face; and God said to him,
Kokborok: Hakhe Abram bono ha-o botok khe klaiwi khulum kha; tei Kaitor bono hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “Behold, my covenant is with you, and you shall be the father of a multitude of nations.
Kokborok: “Nai di ang nwng bai swmai tango, nwng kwbángma bosongni bupha wngnai.

English: No longer shall your name be Abram, but your name shall be Abraham; for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations.
Kokborok: Nini mung Abram tong glak kha, tamo khe Abraham se wngnai; tamni hwn ba ang nono dopha kwbángmani bupha snam kha.

English: I will make you exceedingly fruitful; and I will make nations of you, and kings shall come forth from you.
Kokborok: Ang nono bwthái gwnang khlainai; tei ang nini aroni dopharok khlainai. Tei nini aroni habugrarok phainai.

English: And I will establish my covenant between me and you and your descendants after you throughout their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your descendants after you.
Kokborok: “Ang nwng bai ani swmaino narwknai tei mol brúmni bagwi nini bosongrokbo ang ani swmaino tẃinai. Ang nini tei nini bosongni Kaitor wngnai.

English: And I will give to you, and to your descendants after you, the land of your sojournings, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession; and I will be their God.”
Kokborok: Nwng tabuk Kanan ha-o tongwi tongo, o hano ang nono tei nini thinangni bosongno mol brúmni bagwi rwnai tei ang bórokni Kaitor wngnai.”

English: And God said to Abraham, “As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your descendants after you throughout their generations.
Kokborok: Kaitor Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwngbo hai’-no ani swmaino ma tẃinai, nwng tei nini bosongrok, bórokni ulni bosongrok kusung kusungo bebak tẃi phlaiwi ma thángnai.

English: This is my covenant, which you shall keep, between me and you and your descendants after you   Every male among you shall be circumcised.
Kokborok: Omono wng kha ani a swmai, nwng tei nini ulo khe nini bosongrok ma tẃinai; nini bisingni bebak chwla bwsarok’-no sakur ma rajaknai.

English: You shall be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.
Kokborok: Nini sakurno ma ranai, abo’-no ang nwng bai swmai tang laimani a mari wngnai

English: He that is eight days old among you shall be circumcised; every male throughout your generations, whether born in your house, or bought with your money from any foreigner who is not of your offspring,
Kokborok: Nini bisingni nokha salcharni borok wng khe’-no sakur ma ranai; nini núkhungo achainai, eba nini bosongni ya tamo khe phatarni nini nogo rang bai paiwi tubujaknai seleng bebak chwla bwsarokno,

English: both he that is born in your house and he that is bought with your money, shall be circumcised. So shall my covenant be in your flesh an everlasting covenant.
Kokborok: nini nogo achainai eba rang bai paiwi tubujaknai seleng phano sakur ma rajaknai.

English: Any uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.”
