Kokborok: Khoroksa swk chwla bwsa sakur rajak ya hwn khe bo bini borokrokni bisingni swkagwi khibijaknai, tamni hwn ba bo ani swmaino tẃi li ya.”

English: And God said to Abraham, “As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.
Kokborok: Kaitor Abramno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tei nini nihik Saraino Sarai hwnwi ta ring si di, tamo khe bini mung Sara se wng sinai.

English: I will bless her, and moreover I will give you a son by her; I will bless her, and she shall be a mother of nations; kings of peoples shall come from her.”
Kokborok: Ang bono hamari rwnai tei ang bini tẃi khe nono chwla bwsa khoroksa rwnai; Ang bono hamari rwnai tei bo dopharokni buma wngnai; phiya bini aroni’-no borokrokni habugrarok wngnai.”

English: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said to himself, “Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham ha-o botok khe klaiwi khulum kha tei bwkha bising bising mwnwiwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Umor nokha bisi sarani borok chakra wngwi buda bwsa manno kho? Sarani umor nokha bisi chukuchi wngwi bo bwsa da man sino?”

English: And Abraham said to God, “O that Ishmael might live in thy sight!”
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham Kaitorno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ismael nini mokol bwskango tongwi umor kolok wng thung!”

English: God said, “No, but Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasing covenant for his descendants after him.
Kokborok: Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi, kók khe ahai, nini hik Sara bwsa chwla khoroksa mannai tei nwng bono Isak hwnwi mung phar di. Ang bo bai khe tei bini ulni bini bosongrok bai khe mol brúmni bagwi swmai tangnai.

English: As for Ishmael, I have heard you; behold, I will bless him and make him fruitful and multiply him exceedingly; he shall be the father of twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation.
Kokborok: Nwng Ismaelni bagwi koimano ang khna kha, Ang bono hamari rwnai tei bosong kwbángma er rwnai, Ang bono bosong kotor khlainai tei khorokchinwi bugrarokni bupha wngnai.

English: But I will establish my covenant with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this season next year.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe ang Isak bai se ani swmaino songchanai, khali omo hai jora’-ono Sara nini bagwi bánai.”

English: When he had finished talking with him, God went up from Abraham.
Kokborok: Kaitor bo bai kók sa bai khe Abrahamni aroni tháng kha.

English: Then Abraham took Ishmael his son and all the slaves born in his house or bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham's house, and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very day, as God had said to him.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Kaitor Abramno sama tẃi’-kheno bo bwsa chwla Ismaelno tei bini nogo tongma bebak chwlarokno phiya bini-o achai phaima paiwi tubujaknai selengrokno a salo’-no sakur ra bai kha.

English: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
Kokborok: Abraham sákolomni sakur rajak phuru bini umor nokha bisi chukuchichuku ká kha.

English: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
Kokborok: Tei Ismael sakur rajak phuru bini umor khe nokha bisi chitham ká kha.

English: That very day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised;
Kokborok: A Sal kaisa’-ono Abraham bai bini bwsla Ismael sakur rajak lai-o;

English: and all the men of his house, those born in the house and those bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.
Kokborok: bini nokni bebak chwlarokbo tei bini nogo achainairok tei buini hani rang bai khe paiwi tubujaknairokbo bo bai baksa khe’-no sakur rajak bai-o.

English: And the Lord appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the door of his tent in the heat of the day.
Kokborok: Mamreni Uk buphangrok tong thani-o bo tong phuru Subraiyung bo bai nukjak kha, salni tung kukma jora-o aphuru bo bini rí nokni duglamo achugwi tongo.

English: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, three men stood in front of him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them, and bowed himself to the earth,
Kokborok: Bo naisigwi naiwi nuk kha, bini bwskango chwlaktham borok bacha laijak. Bórokno nuk’-kheno bo rínokni duglamni nongkhorwi thángwi bórok bai malaina tháng kha tei ha-o bolokhógwi khulumui

English: and said, “My lord, if I have found favor in your sight, do not pass by your servant.
Kokborok: hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani bugra, ano nwng khá phur khe, nini o selengno hai’-no lái ta tháng di.

English: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree,
Kokborok: Norokni yakung yak sú rwna bagwi twi kisa tubu grana; norok o buphang saklomo bathak laisok gra di,

English: while I fetch a morsel of bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on—since you have come to your servant.” So they said, “Do as you have said.”
Kokborok: nini selengni nogo yapri khiklai kha ba, ang norokni bagwi chámungbo kisa tubunai abo bai norok lama himnani phan man anw.” Hakhe bórok, “Nwng sama tẃi’-no khlai si di” hwnwi sa kha.

English: And Abraham hastened into the tent to Sarah, and said, “Make ready quickly three measures of fine meal, knead it, and make cakes.”
Kokborok: Abraham doui rínok bisingo habwi kha tei Sarano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng dodro yakaktham moida kaham naui sapigwi kisa auang snam gra di.”

English: And Abraham ran to the herd, and took a calf, tender and good, and gave it to the servant, who hastened to prepare it.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham doui musukrok ton thani tháng kha tei milik kuphung musuk bwsa masa romwi tubu-ui selengno rw phai kha; bo abono doui kwrak kha.

English: Then he took curds, and milk, and the calf which he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree while they ate.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo a muihan sóngjakno, doi bai dutno bórokno rw phai kha; hakhe bo bórok chá laiwi tong phuru buphang saklomo bórokni samo bachaui tong kha.

English: They said to him, “Where is Sarah your wife?” And he said, “She is in the tent.”
Kokborok: Bórok bono hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nini nihik Sara le buko biyang?” “Bo rínok bisingo se,” hwnwi bo sa kha.

English: The Lord said, “I will surely return to you in the spring, and Sarah your wife shall have a son.” And Sarah was listening at the tent door behind him.
Kokborok:
Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nini thani sachlang jora-o sók phai phinai, nini nihik Sara chwla bwsa khoroksa mannai.”
Aphuru Sara rínokni duglamni ayang naro tong twtwi khnaui tongo.

English: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in age; it had ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.
Kokborok: Aphuru Abraham bai Sara chakrajak tei Sarabo bini rí thakjak.

English: So Sarah laughed to herself, saying, “After I have grown old, and my husband is old, shall I have pleasure?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Sara saichung saichung mwnwiwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang tabuk chakra súgwi tháng kha tei ani bwsaibo haino chakra wng kha, ang tei khá da khatung sino?”

English: The Lord said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh, and say, ‘Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?’
Kokborok: Subraiyung Abrahamno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tamo Sara mwnwiwi ‘Ang tabuk aswk chakraui bwsa da manno kho, hwnwi sa?’

English: Is anything too hard for the Lord? At the appointed time I will return to you, in the spring, and Sarah shall have a son.”
Kokborok: Subraiyung khlaiwi man ya omo hai mungsa phano to de tong ba? chongjak jora’-ono ang nini thani phainai, sachlang jora-o khe Sara chwla bwsa khoroksa mannai.”

English: But Sarah denied, saying, “I did not laugh”; for she was afraid. He said, “No, but you did laugh.”
Kokborok:
Tamo khe Sara gosi li ya tei “Ang mwnwi ya ble” hwnwi sa kha, tamni hwn ba bo kirijak kha. Tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi nwng kubui’-no mwnwi kha.”
Abraham Sodomni bagwi koimani

English: Then the men set out from there, and they looked toward Sodom; and Abraham went with them to set them on their way.
Kokborok: Hakhe a borokrok aroni lam rwui tháng lai kha tei bórok Sodom bwkhak nahar lai kha, phiya Abraham bórokno tunna bagwi bórok bai baksa tháng kha.

English: The Lord said, “Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang khlaina naiwi tongmano Abrahamno khogwi tonnai hai da?

English: seeing that Abraham shall become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall bless themselves by him?
Kokborok: Bini bosongrok kotor wngnai tei dopha kwrak wngnai tei bini bising tẃi khe swngcharni bebak dopharokno ang hamari rwnai;

English: No, for I have chosen him, that he may charge his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing righteousness and justice; so that the Lord may bring to Abraham what he has promised him.”
Kokborok: I’nhi ang bono chong kha, bo bini bwsarokno tei bini bosongrokno ani kók nanani bagwi tei samung sói khlaina, bujsói tẃinano dagi rwna bagwi se ang chongo; bórok abo hai khe khlai khe Subraiyung Abraham khlaina hwnwi swmai tangma tẃi’-no bini bagwi khlainai.”

English: Then the Lord said, “Because the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great and their sin is very grave,
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sodomni tei Gomorani bagwi belai’-no kók hamya tongo, bórokni sumsok belai’-no báng kha.

English: I will go down to see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry which has come to me; and if not, I will know.”
Kokborok: Bórok bebakno da ang hwnma hai’-no khlai bai kha, ang thángwi naiwi sinai. Tei abo swk da abo swk ya, ang sinai.”

English: So the men turned from there, and went toward Sodom; but Abraham still stood before the Lord.
Kokborok: Hakhe a borokrok aroni nongkhorwi Sodom bwkhak tháng lai kha; phiya Abraham khe Subraiyungni bwskango bachaui’-no tong kha.

English: Then Abraham drew near, and said, “Wilt thou indeed destroy the righteous with the wicked?
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham samo thángwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng borok hamyarok bai baksa borok kahamroknobo de sokomornai?

English: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city; wilt thou then destroy the place and not spare it for the fifty righteous who are in it?
Kokborok: Rom di, kamiyungo khorokbachi borok kaham tongo; hakhe nwng aro tongnai a borok khorokbachirokni bagwi a kamiyungno narwk glak da?

English: Far be it from thee to do such a thing, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous fare as the wicked! Far be that from thee! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?”
Kokborok: Nwng kubui’-no borok sutrangrokno borok hamyarok bai baksa le buthar glak. Borok kahamrokno borok hamyarok bai baksa khe le nwng nuk glak! O bebak hano buj thumnaisa sói khe’-no buj thum glak diba?”

English: And the Lord said, “If I find at Sodom fifty righteous in the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tumungno Ang Sodomni kamiyungo khorokbachi borok kaham nuk khe, a borokni bagwi a bebak kamiyungno mwthangnai.”

English: Abraham answered, “Behold, I have taken upon myself to speak to the Lord, I who am but dust and ashes.
Kokborok: Abraham hai hwnwi sa phi kha, “Subrai ang sak baithangno ma sa phiro, nai di ang le mungsabu ya hadlwi tei thapla ja se.

English: Suppose five of the fifty righteous are lacking? Wilt thou destroy the whole city for lack of five?” And he said, “I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.”
Kokborok: Rom di, borok khorokbachi kánani bagwi aro tei khorokba borok’-no ká ya wng khe le? nwng a khorokba kwrwi wngwi, khorokbachi ká súk yani bagwi bebak kamiyungno sokomornai hai da?” Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aro borok khorokbrwichiba tong khe’-no ang sokomor glak.”

English: Again he spoke to him, and said, “Suppose forty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of forty I will not do it.”
Kokborok: Bo hai’-no hai hwnwi sa phi kha “Rom di aro borok khorokbrwichi simi borok kaham tong si-o.” Hakhe Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aro borok kaham khorokbrwichi tong khe’-no ang sokomor glak.”

English: Then he said, “Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak. Suppose thirty are found there.” He answered, “I will not do it, if I find thirty there.”
Kokborok:
Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa-u kha “Subraiyung ta joli ja di, ang ma sa phiro. Rom di aro borok kaham khorokthamchi tong kha ta.”
Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang aro borok khorokthamchi tong khe’-no abo hai khlai glak.”

English: He said, “Behold, I have taken upon myself to speak to the Lord. Suppose twenty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of twenty I will not destroy it.”
Kokborok:
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung ang nono kók sa phir ja-o. Aro khoroknwichi’-no tong kha ta.”
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang khoroknwichi borok kaham nuk khe’-no sokomor glak.”

English: Then he said, “Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak again but this once. Suppose ten are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of ten I will not destroy it.”
Kokborok:
Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha “Subraiyung ta joli ja di ang tei uáisa’-no sa phi si anw. Aro borok kaham khorokchi’-no tong kha ta?”
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aro khorokchi’-no borok kaham tong khe ang sokomor glak.”

English: And the Lord went his way, when he had finished speaking to Abraham; and Abraham returned to his place.
Kokborok: Abraham bai kók sa laiwi pái khe Subraiyung tháng kha; hakhe Abraham nogo kiphilwi phai kha.

English: The two angels came to Sodom in the evening; and Lot was sitting in the gate of Sodom. When Lot saw them, he rose to meet them, and bowed himself with his face to the earth,
Kokborok: Sarigo Suari khoroknwi Sodomo phai kha; aphuru Lot Sodomni kamiyungni duglamo achugwi tongo. Lot bórokno nuk’-kheno bórok bai malaina bagwi bachaui tháng kha tei ha-o botok khe bolokhógwi khulumui hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: and said, “My lords, turn aside, I pray you, to your servant's house and spend the night, and wash your feet; then you may rise up early and go on your way.” They said, “No; we will spend the night in the street.”
Kokborok:
“Ani bugrarok, ang norokno koi ja-o, ani nogo kasa lai phai jaui horo tongwi tháng lai ja di, yakung yak sú phai lai di; hakhe norok aidrobo bachaui norokni thángna lama-o thángwi man anw.”
Hakhe bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi chwng lampra’-ono hor ai lai anw.”

English: But he urged them strongly; so they turned aside to him and entered his house; and he made them a feast, and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo bórokno belai khe koi kha, hakhe bórok bini nogo habwi kasaui lai kha; bo bórokno chámung rw kha, auang bángla snamjakrok rw kha, hakhe bórok chá lai kha.

English: But before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, all the people to the last man, surrounded the house;
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok thu lai ya kho sini’-no, a kamiyungni chwlarok, Sodomni sikla tei chakra chwlarok, bebak borokrok phaiwi a nokno chorsa khe kitingwi gerep lai kha,

English: and they called to Lot, “Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, that we may know them.”
Kokborok: hakhe bórok Lotno ringwi hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Tini horo nini nogo phainai a chwlarok biyang tháng bai kha? Bórokno phataro tikhlai tubu-ui phai di, chwng bórok bai thu lai-chá lai khlai grana.”

English: Lot went out of the door to the men, shut the door after him,
Kokborok: Lot phataro a borokrokni thani nongkhorwi tháng kha tei bo nongkhor khe uklok tẃi dugar berebwi rwui

English: and said, “I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly.
Kokborok: bo a borokrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Takhukrok ang norokno koi ja-o, bórokno ahai ta khlai ja di.

English: Behold, I have two daughters who have not known man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please; only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof.”
Kokborok: Nai di, ani sajwk sikla khoroknwi tongo, tabukbo bórok kwthar kho; bórokno norokni thani tikhlai rw phai anw, norok muchung tẃi’-no khlai di; tamo khe bórok ani nogo se bom phai laijama ba, a borokrokno le mungsa ta khlai ja di.”

English: But they said, “Stand back!” And they said, “This fellow came to sojourn, and he would play the judge! Now we will deal worse with you than with them.” Then they pressed hard against the man Lot, and drew near to break the door.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwngno ta kebeng di!” Tei bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “O borok buini hani phaimani se, bo se tabuk buj thumnai! Chwng tabuk nono se o borokni slái’-bo sitra rwnai.” Bórok Lotno dagar lai kha tei duglam swbaina bagwi seder lai kha.

English: But the men put forth their hands and brought Lot into the house to them, and shut the door.
Kokborok: Tamo khe a khoroknwi borokrok Lotno yak phologwi bisingo sotonwi nahorwi duglam soui rw kha.

English: And they struck with blindness the men who were at the door of the house, both small and great, so that they wearied themselves groping for the door.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok nok duglamni phataro tongnai bebak chakra-kusu borokrokno mokol nuk ya khlai rw kha, hakhe bórok tei duglam naitugwi man lai li ya.

English: Then the men said to Lot, “Have you any one else here? Sons-in-law, sons, daughters, or any one you have in the city, bring them out of the place;
Kokborok: Hakhe a borok khoroknwirok Lotno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Oro nini chamarisarok, sajlasongrok, eba tei nini khoroksa swk o kamiyungo to de tong? Tong khe bórokno oroni kariui tlang si di,

English: for we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before the Lord, and the Lord has sent us to destroy it.”
Kokborok: chwng omono sokomorna naiwi tong kha, tamni hwn ba Subraiyung o borokrokni belai samung hamyarokni kókno khna kha tei Sodomno sokomorna bagwi se Subraiyung chwngno rwui rohoro.”

English: So Lot went out and said to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters, “Up, get out of this place; for the Lord is about to destroy the city.” But he seemed to his sons-in-law to be jesting.
Kokborok: Hakhe Lot bini sajwksongno káijakna bagwi kók kha laijak chamarirokno thángwi hai hwnwi saui kha, “Do di! O kamiyungni nongkhor lai di; Subraiyung o kamiyungno sokomornani naiwi tong kha.” Tamo khe bwchamarisong bórokno hai’-no kara thwngwi se sa khna hwnwi uansuk lai tong kha.

English: When morning dawned, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Arise, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be consumed in the punishment of the city.”
Kokborok: Phung ai khe’-no suarirok Lotno hai hwnwi dagiui sa kha, “Bacha di, nihikno tei oro tongma nini sajwk khoroknwino tẃiwi nongkhor di, wng ya khe o kamiyungno sokomorma bai baksa norokbo pái bainai.”

English: But he lingered; so the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the Lord being merciful to him, and they brought him forth and set him outside the city.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo lerwi tong kha; tamo khe Subraiyung bono khairok kha, hakhe borokrok bono, bihikno tei bwsajwk khoroknwirokno yak romwi kamiyungni tikhlai tubu kha.

English: And when they had brought them forth, they said, “Flee for your life; do not look back or stop anywhere in the valley; flee to the hills, lest you be consumed.”
Kokborok: Bórok bórokno tikhlai tubu phuru hai hwnwi sa kha, “Thangnani bagwi se khár di, uklok phaising uphilwi ta nahar di tei o harungo kwrẃngsa swgo tabo ta bathak di; o hatháirogo khár di, wng ya khe norokbo khamwi páinai.”

English: And Lot said to them, “Oh, no, my lords;
Kokborok: Hakhe Lot bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi omo hai ta khlai di ani bugrarok;

English: behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life; but I cannot flee to the hills, lest the disaster overtake me, and I die.
Kokborok: nai di nwng nini o selengno belai khe khairogwi nai khlai kha tei ano mwthang kha, tamo khe, ang hatháirogo le khárwi thángwi man glak, hamya se ano rom phainai kha tei ang thwinai kha.

English: Behold, yonder city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one. Let me escape there—is it not a little one?—and my life will be saved!”
Kokborok: Nai di, u kamiyung khe sampha-o tei abo kwbo kẃlẃksasa. Aro phano ano thángna rw ja si di, ahai khe le ani langma thangwi tong rwknai! abo sói’-no kẃlẃksasa ya diba?”

English: He said to him, “Behold, I grant you this favor also, that I will not overthrow the city of which you have spoken.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Do wng si thung, ang lon kha, nwng samani a kamiyungno ang sokomor glak.

English: Make haste, escape there; for I can do nothing till you arrive there.” Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.
Kokborok:
Do di, aro khárwi tháng di, nwng aro sógwi ya sak ang mungsa khlai ya.”
Aboni bagwino a kamiyungno Joar hwnjago.
Sodom bai Gomora sokomorjakmani

English: The sun had risen on the earth when Lot came to Zoar.
Kokborok: Lot Joaro sók phai kheno sal kasaui phai kha.

English: Then the Lord rained on Sodom and Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the Lord out of heaven;
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung Sodom bai Gomorani saka-o nokhani gwndwk tei hórni uatwi ua rw kha;

English: and he overthrew those cities, and all the valley, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.
Kokborok: tei bo a kamiyungrokno tei bebak harungrokno sokomor kha, bebak borokrok bai baksa aroni ha-o achainai bebakno’-no sokomor bai kha.

English: But Lot's wife behind him looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
Kokborok: Phiya Lotni uklogo tongnai bihik uklogo uphilwi nahar kha tei bo sómni thongla chaui tháng kha.

English: And Abraham went early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the Lord;
Kokborok: Teini phungo khe Abraham doui Subraiyung bai bacha lai thani-o tháng kha;

English: and he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and toward all the land of the valley, and beheld, and lo, smoke of the land went up like the smoke of a furnace.
Kokborok: hakhe bo, Sodom bai Gomora tei bebak harungno naikhlaiwi nai kha tei nuk kha, hani hoku kasaui tongo, abo thapa kotorni hoku ka tẃi wngwi tong kha.

English: So it was that, when God destroyed the cities of the valley, God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities in which Lot dwelt.
Kokborok:
Hakhe Kaitor harungni kamiyungrokno sokomor phuru omo hai wng kha, Kaitor Abrahamni kókno muitu khlaiwi, kamiyungrokno sokomorna swkang a kamiyungni Lotno kariui rohor kha.
Moabsong bai Ammonsongni bosong chengmani

English: Now Lot went up out of Zoar, and dwelt in the hills with his two daughters, for he was afraid to dwell in Zoar; so he dwelt in a cave with his two daughters.
Kokborok: Hakhe Lot Joarni nongkhorwi thángwi bwsajwksong khoroknwino tẃiwi hathái-o tongwi kha, tamni hwn ba bo Joaro tongnani kirijak kha; hakhe bo bwsajwk khoroknwino tẃiwi aro hakhoro khe tongwi kha.

English: And the first-born said to the younger, “Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us after the manner of all the earth.
Kokborok: A bukhuk okra kusuno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chini apha chakra wng kha tei o hani tongmung tẃi khoroksa chwla oro phano tong phainai kwrwi.

English: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, that we may preserve offspring through our father.”
Kokborok: Phai di chwng aphano chuak khanwi phek rwui bo bai thu-ui bini tẃi khe bosong narwk lai anw.”

English: So they made their father drink wine that night; and the first-born went in, and lay with her father; he did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
Kokborok: Hakhe a horo bórok buphano chuak khanwi phek rw lai kha; tei a bukhuk okra bupha bai thu-ui kha; bo buphuru thu kha tei bachaui tháng kha bupha saui man ya.

English: And on the next day, the first-born said to the younger, “Behold, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine tonight also; then you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve offspring through our father.”
Kokborok: Teini salo khe a bukhuk okra kusuno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang miya horo apha bai thu kha; tinibo bono chuak khan lai anw; hakhe nwng thángwi bo bai thu-ui di, hakhe chwng bini tẃi khe chini bosong narwgwi man anw.”

English: So they made their father drink wine that night also; and the younger arose, and lay with him; and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
Kokborok: Hakhe a horobo bórok buphano chuak khan rwui phek rw lai kha; tei a bukhuk kusu bo bai thángwi thu-ui kha. Bo buphuru thu phaiwi bachaui tháng kha si rwk li ya.

English: Thus both the daughters of Lot were with child by their father.
Kokborok: Omo hai kheno Lotni bwsajwk khoroknwirok bupha bai khe ogo bwsa wng kha.

English: The first-born bore a son, and called his name Moab; he is the father of the Moabites to this day.
Kokborok: Okrani okni bono Moab hwnwi mung phar kha; bono tabuk tongnai Moabrokni bupha

English: The younger also bore a son, and called his name Ben-ammi; he is the father of the Ammonites to this day.
Kokborok: kusubo chwla bwsa’-no man kha tei Benammi hwnwi mung phar kha, bono tabukni Ammonsongni bupha.

English: From there Abraham journeyed toward the territory of the Negeb, and dwelt between Kadesh and Shur; and he sojourned in Gerar.
Kokborok: Abraham aroni Negeb amchoi bwkhak tháng kha tei Kades bai Surni kwcharo tongwi kha; ulo khe Geraro tongwi kha.

English: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, “She is my sister.” And Abimelech king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.
Kokborok: Abraham bini bihik Sarano “Bo ani bukhuk se” hwnwi sa kha. Hakhe Gerarni habugra Abimelek Sarano bini thani tubu rw kha.

English: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, “Behold, you are a dead man, because of the woman whom you have taken; for she is a man's wife.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Kaitor horsa Abimelekni thani imango nukjak phaiwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di nwng thwinai tamni hwn ba nwng tubumani o bwrwi wng kha tei khoroksani bihik se.”

English: Now Abimelech had not approached her; so he said, “Lord, wilt thou slay an innocent people?
Kokborok: Hai phano Abimelek a bwrwini thani tháng ya kho; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Subraiyung, nwng borok kahamno hai’-no butharnai hai da?

English: Did he not himself say to me, ‘She is my sister’? And she herself said, ‘He is my brother.’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this.”
Kokborok: ‘Bo ani bukhuk se’ hwnwi bo se ano sa ya diba? O bwrwi bo sakno ‘Bo ani takhuk se’ hwnwi sa bla. Ang omo sói bwkha srai-srai khe se khlai-o tei ang mungsabo cha ya khlai ya.”

English: Then God said to him in the dream, “Yes, I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart, and it was I who kept you from sinning against me; therefore I did not let you touch her.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor bono imango hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’n ang saui mano, nwng omo bwkha srai-srai kheno khlai-o tei ang se nono sumsok samungni kati rwui tono; abo bai’-no ang nono bini sak tang rw yaui tono.

English: Now then restore the man's wife; for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you, and you shall live. But if you do not restore her, know that you shall surely die, you, and all that are yours.”
Kokborok: Tabuk nwng bihikno kiphilwi rw di; bo wng kha diyari, hakhe bo nini bagwi suri khe nwng thangnai. Nwng bono kiphilwi rw ya khe si di nwng thwinai’-no thwinai tei nini borokrokbo bebakno thwinai.”

English: So Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and told them all these things; and the men were very much afraid.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abimelek phung aidrobo bacha kha tei bini bebak selengrokno ringwi a bebak kókrokno sa kha; tei borokrok bebakno belai khe kirijak lai kha.

English: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said to him, “What have you done to us? And how have I sinned against you, that you have brought on me and my kingdom a great sin? You have done to me things that ought not to be done.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Abimelek Abrahamno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng chwngno tamo khlai lang? Ang bahai nini thani cha ya khlaiwi nwng ani bagwi tei ani hani bagwi aswk hamya sumsokno tubu rw lang? Khlaithai kwrwino nwng ani thani khlai kha.”

English: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What were you thinking of, that you did this thing?”
Kokborok: Phiya Abimelek Abrahamno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng twma khákaui omo hai khlai lang?”

English: Abraham said, “I did it because I thought, There is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.
Kokborok: Abraham hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo hai ma khlaima le, ang oro Kaitorno kirinai se kwrwina tei ani hikni bagwi bórok ano butharnai hwnwi se ma khlai-o.

English: Besides she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.
Kokborok: Abono karwi bo ani bukhuk’-no kwbo klai-o, bo ani aphani bwsajwk, tamo khe amani bwsajwk ya; tei ang bono káijak kha.

English: And when God caused me to wander from my father's house, I said to her, ‘This is the kindness you must do me   at every place to which we come, say of me, He is my brother.’ ”
Kokborok: Phiya Kaitor ano aphani nokni rwi rohor phuru, ang bono sa kha, ‘Nwng kháirogwi ano omo hai ma khlainai; chini bebak tháng thani’-no ano nwng ‘Bo ani takhuk se’ hwnwi ma sanai.”

English: Then Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and male and female slaves, and gave them to Abraham, and restored Sarah his wife to him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abimelek, musukrok, merarok selengrok tei selengjwkrokno tubu-ui Abrahamno rw kha, phiya Sarano kiphilwi rw kha.

English: And Abimelech said, “Behold, my land is before you; dwell where it pleases you.”
Kokborok: Abimelek hai hwnwi sa kha, “Naisik di o nini mokol bwskangni harok bebak ani máng; nwng muchungma’-ono tong di.”

English: To Sarah he said, “Behold, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; it is your vindication in the eyes of all who are with you; and before every one you are righted.”
Kokborok: Bo Sarano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ang nini takhukno rukphai budul dulsaisa rw kha; abo norok bai tongnai bebak borokni thani nini mungsa majra kwrwi hwnwi sijak rwna mari se; bebakno sinai nwng mungsa cha ya khlai ya.”

English: Then Abraham prayed to God; and God healed Abimelech, and also healed his wife and female slaves so that they bore children.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham Kaitorni thani suri kha; tei Kaitor Abimelekno, bihikno tei selengjwkrokno ham rw kha bórok bwsarokbo man bai kha.

English: For the Lord had closed all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.
Kokborok: Tamni hwnma le Abrahamni bihik Sarani bagwi bini nokni bebak bwrwirokno Subraiyung bwsa man ya khlai rw kha.

English: The Lord visited Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did to Sarah as he had promised.
Kokborok: Subraiyung kók sa langma tẃi’-no Sara bai malai phai kha tei swmai tangma tẃi’-no bo Sarani bagwi khlai kha.

English: And Sarah conceived, and bore Abraham a son in his old age at the time of which God had spoken to him.
Kokborok: Sara ogo bwsa wng kha tei Kaitor bono samani jora’-ono a chakra umor phuru Abrahamni bagwi chwla bwsa bá kha.

English: Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore him, Isaac.
Kokborok: Abrahamni bagwi Sara bámani a chwla bwsano Isak hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
Kokborok: Kaitor bono dagima hai kheno, Abraham bwsla Isakno achaiwi nokha salcharo sakur ra kha.

English: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
Kokborok: Bwsla Isak achai phuru Abrahamni umor nokha bisi sara ká kha.

English: And Sarah said, “God has made laughter for me; every one who hears will laugh over me.”
Kokborok: Sara hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ano mwnwimung khlai kha; ani o kókno khnanairok le bebakno ano mwnwinai.”

English: And she said, “Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would suckle children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Abrahamno sabo Sara bwsano abuk chá rwnai hwnwi sa kha mun? Hai phano bini umor chakrajak phano ang bini bagwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá kha.”

English: And the child grew, and was weaned; and Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
Kokborok: A chwrai tor kha tei abuk yakar khe Abraham, Isak abuk yakarmani salo chámung panda kotor kaisa khlai kha.

English: But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, playing with her son Isaac.
Kokborok: Salsa, Sara bwsla Isakno Ijipjwkma Hagar Abrahamni bagwi chwla bwsa bámani a chwraisano Isak bai khe thwng laiwi tongmano nuk kha.

English: So she said to Abraham, “Cast out this slave woman with her son; for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Sara, Abrahamno hai hwnwi sa kha, “O selengjwkmano bwsa bai khe tikhlai rohor di; tamni hwn ba o selengjwkmani bwsla ani angsajla Isak bai baksa buphani manthai bantano man glak.”

English: And the thing was very displeasing to Abraham on account of his son.
Kokborok: Omo belai kheno Abrahamni bwkha-o nang kha tamni hwn khe, Ismaelbo bini’-no bwsa ba.

English: But God said to Abraham, “Be not displeased because of the lad and because of your slave woman; whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for through Isaac shall your descendants be named.
Kokborok: Tamo khe, Kaitor Abrahamno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng a chwraini bagwi tei nini selengjwkmani bagwi bwkha hamya ta wng di, Sara nono samani hai’-no nwng khlai di, tamni hwn ba Isakni bising tẃi se nini bosongni mung pharjaknai.

English: And I will make a nation of the son of the slave woman also, because he is your offspring.”
Kokborok: Tei selengjwkmani bwsani bosongnobo ang dopha snamnai, tamni hwn ba bo’-bo nini’-no bwsa.”

English: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and took bread and a skin of water, and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed, and wandered in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham phung aidrobo bachaui chámung tei bukurni twi dajaknai-o twi daui Hagarno rw kha, bo bwsano phikungo bárwi rwui rohor kha. Hagar lam rwui thángwi Birsebani hasinglwng bwkhak tháng kha.

English: When the water in the skin was gone, she cast the child under one of the bushes.
Kokborok: Bukurni twi tonjaknaini a twi pái khe Hagar a chwraino jabralwngni saklomo klangwi tháng kha.

English: Then she went, and sat down over against him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot; for she said, “Let me not look upon the death of the child.” And as she sat over against him, the child lifted up his voice and wept.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe bo thángwi kisa hakchalo achugwi tongwi kha, abo chalmani koslu kusungsa kok hórwi man anw; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “O chwrai thwimano ang mokol baithang bahai khe nainai.” Bo bwsani bwkhak khe achugwi tong phuru a chwrai khorang torsaui kap kha.

English: And God heard the voice of the lad; and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven, and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Fear not; for God has heard the voice of the lad where he is.
Kokborok: Kaitor a chwraini khorangno khna kha; tei Kaitorni suari chuknoha-o tong twtwi Hagarno hai hwnwi sakhlai kha, “Nini tamo dukhu tong Hagar? Ta kiri di; Kaitor a chwrai tongmani aroni bini khorangno khna kha.

English: Arise, lift up the lad, and hold him fast with your hand; for I will make him a great nation.”
Kokborok: Bacha di, a chwraino nini yak bai kwbaksaui bam di; tamni hwn ba ang bono dopha kotor snamnai.”

English: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; and she went, and filled the skin with water, and gave the lad a drink.
Kokborok: Kaitor bini mokol nuk rw kha hakhe samo’-no twikhor khorsa nuk kha tei bo aro thángwi a bukurni twi dajaknaino twi supungwi tubu-ui bwslano khan kha.

English: And God was with the lad, and he grew up; he lived in the wilderness, and became an expert with the bow.
Kokborok: Kaitor a chwrai bai tong kha tei chwraibo torsa kha; bo hasinglwngo tong kha tei belai’-no badukhung kwrwng wng kha.

English: He lived in the wilderness of Paran; and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Bo Paranni hasinglwngo tong kha tei buma bini hik khlaina bagwi ha Ijipni bwrwino tubu kha.

English: At that time Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do;
Kokborok: A jora-o Abimelek bini sengkrak bokhorok Phikol bai khe thángwi Abrahamno hai hwnwi saui kha, “Kaitor nini bebak samungo’-no tongo;

English: now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my offspring or with my posterity, but as I have dealt loyally with you, you will deal with me and with the land where you have sojourned.”
Kokborok: hakhe Kaitorni bwskango swmai tang di, nwng ano ani bwsarokno eba ani bosongrokno bandi glak. Ang nono bórom khlaiwi phai kha, abo bai’-no swmai tangwi sa di nwng’-bo ano tei nwng tabuk tongmani o hano bórom khlai tongnai.”

English: And Abraham said, “I will swear.”
Kokborok: “I’n ang tangnai,” hwnwi Abraham sa kha.

English: When Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well of water which Abimelech's servants had seized,
Kokborok: Abraham, Abimelekni selengrok bini twikhor segwi namani kókno sa khe,

English: Abimelech said, “I do not know who has done this thing; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.”
Kokborok: Abimelek hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo sabo khlai kha ang saui man ya; nwngbo ano sa ya kho ba, ang o kókno tini jora khw se khna ya kho.”

English: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham mera tei musuk chwlarokno Abimelekno rw kha tei o khoroknwirok swmai kaisa tang lai kha.

English: Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart.
Kokborok: Abraham mera bwrwi mak snino bodolni khak kha.

English: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs which you have set apart?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Abimelek Abrahamno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng tamo o mera bwrwi mak snino bodolni khakma le tamni bagwi sla?”

English: He said, “These seven ewe lambs you will take from my hand, that you may be a witness for me that I dug this well.”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “O mera bwrwi mak sni bodolni khakma merano nwng ani yakni yachak di, omo bai’-no ang’-no o twikhor khurmani nwng mokol baithang nukmani mari wngnai.”

English: Therefore that place was called Beer-sheba; because there both of them swore an oath.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwino abo Birseba hwnjago; tamni hwn ba oro’-no a khoroknwirok swmai tang lai-o.

English: So they made a covenant at Beer-sheba. Then Abimelech and Phicol the commander of his army rose up and returned to the land of the Philistines.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Birseba-o swmai tang lai kha. Aboni ulo khe Abimelek bini sengkrak bokhorok Phikolno tẃiwi bini ha Philistia-o kiphilwi tháng kha.

English: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the Lord, the Everlasting God.
Kokborok: Abraham a Birseba-o Tamarisk buphang káiwi Mol brúmni Kaitorno khulumo.

English: And Abraham sojourned many days in the land of the Philistines.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham ha Philistia-o sal kwdwkma tong kha.

English: After these things God tested Abraham, and said to him, “Abraham!” And he said, “Here am I.”
Kokborok: Aboni sal kisa ulo khe Kaitor Abrahamno amjogwi nai kha, bo bono “Abraham” hwnwi ring kha, hakhe Abraham khorang yachagwi “Ang oro se!” hwnwi sa kha.

English: He said, “Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering upon one of the mountains of which I shall tell you.”
Kokborok: Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwslano na di, nini hamjak kukma khoroksa susuk nwsla Isakno tẃiwi ha Moria-o thángwi ang phunukma a hathái-o tlangwi tanwi sógwi ma barogwinai.”

English: So Abraham rose early in the morning, saddled his ass, and took two of his young men with him, and his son Isaac; and he cut the wood for the burnt offering, and arose and went to the place of which God had told him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham phung aidrop bacha kha tei bini korai chirka saka-o manwirok boksa kha, bo bai baksa tei khoroknwi chwla siklano tei bwsla Isakno tẃi kha; sógwi barokna bagwi bo bol tẃi kha tei Kaitor bono tháng di hwnwi sama hathái phaising lam rwui tháng kha.

English: On the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place afar off.
Kokborok: Salthamni salo khe Abraham tháng di hwnjakma a hatháino naisik horwi hakchal khe nuhur kha.

English: Then Abraham said to his young men, “Stay here with the ass; I and the lad will go yonder and worship, and come again to you.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Abraham bo bai baksa tlangma a sikla khoroknwino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Oro korai chirka bai baksa tong rwk di; ang bai chwraisa simi aro thángwi khulumui norokni thani kiphilwi phai anw.”

English: And Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering, and laid it on Isaac his son; and he took in his hand the fire and the knife. So they went both of them together.
Kokborok: Abraham sógwi barokna bagwi tlangma a bolno naui bwsla Isakno tẃi rw kha; tei bo sak khe bini yago hór bai dasano tẃi kha.

English: And Isaac said to his father Abraham, “My father!” And he said, “Here am I, my son.” He said, “Behold, the fire and the wood; but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?”
Kokborok: Isak bupha Abrahamno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Apha,” hakhe bupha hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’n tamo sajla?” Isak hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nai di hór bai bol simi se tongo; tamo khe sógwi barokna bagwi mera le buko biyang?”

English: Abraham said, “God will provide himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son.” So they went both of them together.
Kokborok: Abraham hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sajla Kaitor baithang’-no, sógwi barokna bagwi jugaliui rwnai.” Hakhe bórok phasaknwi’-no tháng lai kha.

English: When they came to the place of which God had told him, Abraham built an altar there, and laid the wood in order, and bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar, upon the wood.
Kokborok: Kaitor samani hathái-o bórok sók phai khe, Abraham aro háyá kaisa snam kha tei bolrokno jai khe bera kha tei bwsa Isakno khaui háyá-o bolrokni saka-o khe boksa kha.

English: Then Abraham put forth his hand, and took the knife to slay his son.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham bwslano tanna bagwi dasano kariui har kha.

English: But the angel of the Lord called to him from heaven, and said, “Abraham, Abraham!” And he said, “Here am I.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyungni suari chuknohani ringwi bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Abraham, Abraham!” hakhe bo sa kha, “I’n ang oro se.”

English: He said, “Do not lay your hand on the lad or do anything to him; for now I know that you fear God, seeing you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.”
Kokborok: Kaitor hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng chwraini saka-o yak ta tisa di, bono mungsa ta khlai di; tamni hwn khe, ang saui man kha nwng Kaitorno kiri’-ono, ang nuk kha nwng nwsla khoroksa susuknobo ano rwnani yak kwcha li ya.”

English: And Abraham lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, behind him was a ram, caught in a thicket by his horns; and Abraham went and took the ram, and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.
Kokborok: Abraham bokhorok tisaui naisigwi nai kha, hakhe bini uklogo budukrok bai khe bokorongo kubuljak mera masa nuk kha tei Abraham thángwi a merano romwi tubu-ui bwslani sak slái-o a merano sógwi yaphar kha.

English: So Abraham called the name of that place The Lord will provide; as it is said to this day, “On the mount of the Lord it shall be provided.”
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi Abraham a hatháino Subraiyung se Jugaliui rwnai hwnwi mung phar kha; a kók tabuk jorabo sajagwi’-no tongo, “Subraiyungni hathái-o Jugaliui rwjaknai.”

English: And the angel of the Lord called to Abraham a second time from heaven,
Kokborok: Subraiyungni suari chuknohani tei uáisa Abrahamno ringwi sa kha,

English: and said, “By myself I have sworn, says the Lord, because you have done this, and have not withheld your son, your only son,
Kokborok: “Ang baithangni mung bai’-no swmai tangwi sa-o, nwng omo tẃi samung tang kha, nwsla khoroksa susukno narwgwi ton li ya,

English: I will indeed bless you, and I will multiply your descendants as the stars of heaven and as the sand which is on the seashore. And your descendants shall possess the gate of their enemies,
Kokborok: Ang nono hamari rw sinai’-no, ang nini bosongno nokhani athukiri hai khe tei twijlang rukungni hasing hai khe er rwnai. Tei nini bosongrok bórokni arirokni hapmani duglamno seknai,

English: and by your descendants shall all the nations of the earth bless themselves, because you have obeyed my voice.”
Kokborok: phiya nini bosongrokni tẃi khe ha sakani bebak dopharok hamari mannai, tamni hwn ba nwng ani kókno khna kha.”

English: So Abraham returned to his young men, and they arose and went together to Beer-sheba; and Abraham dwelt at Beer-sheba.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham bo bai baksa khe tubuma a sikla khoroknwirokno kabuma-o kiphilwi phai kha tei pal khe lam rwui Birseba-o tháng lai kha; tei Abraham Birseba-o kami khaui tongwi kha.

English: Now after these things it was told Abraham, “Behold, Milcah also has borne children to your brother Nahor
Kokborok: Aboni sal kisa ulo khe Abraham hai hwnwi sajak kha, “Nai di Milka nini takhuk Nahorni bagwi bwsarok bá kha.

English: Uz the first-born, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram,
Kokborok: Uj wng kha okra, buphayung khe Buj, Kemuel khe Aramni bupha,

English: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”
Kokborok: tei Kesed, Hajo, Pildas, Jidlap tei Bethuel.”

English: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham's brother.
Kokborok: Bethuel khe Rebekani bupha. Milka-o khorokcharno Abrahamni takhuk Nahorni bagwi bá kha.

English: Moreover, his concubine, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah
Kokborok: Abono karwibo bini hik kotoi Reuma khe, Teba, Goham, Tahas tei Makano bá kha.

English: Sarah lived a hundred and twenty-seven years; these were the years of the life of Sarah.
Kokborok: Sara nokha bisi sara tei nwichisni jora kwthang tong lang kha; Sara omo swk bisi tong lang kha.

English: And Sarah died at Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan; and Abraham went in to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
Kokborok: Bo ha Kananni Hebrono thwi-o tei Abraham bini bagwi bwkha khamwi kap kha.

English: And Abraham rose up from before his dead, and said to the Hittites,
Kokborok: Abraham a kwthwino klangwi Hitisarokni thani thángwi hai hwnwi saui kha.

English: “I am a stranger and a sojourner among you; give me property among you for a burying place, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.”
Kokborok: “Ang buini hani borok norokni ha-o tong janai se; ani bwskangni mang kwthwino phobna bagwi ano ha kisa rw ja di.”

English: The Hittites answered Abraham,
Kokborok: Hakhe Hitisarok Abrahamno hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “Hear us, my lord; you are a mighty prince among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our sepulchres; none of us will withhold from you his sepulchre, or hinder you from burying your dead.”
Kokborok: “Hodasa chini kók khna di; nwng chini bisingo phankrak tẃiphang. Nwng nini muchungma mangkhoro’-no nini mang kwthwi phob di; chini bisingo khoroksa phano bini mangkhoro’-no rw yaui tong glak eba nini kwthwino phob rw yaui tong glak.”

English: Abraham rose and bowed to the Hittites, the people of the land.
Kokborok: Abraham bachaui a hani borok Hitisongno khulum kha.

English: And he said to them, “If you are willing that I should bury my dead out of my sight, hear me, and entreat for me Ephron the son of Zohar,
Kokborok: Tei bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok ani mang kwthwino oro phob rwna lon khe, ani kók khna ja di; Joharni bwsla, Ephronni

English: that he may give me the cave of Mach-pelah, which he owns; it is at the end of his field. For the full price let him give it to me in your presence as a possession for a burying place.”
Kokborok: Makpelani hakhorno ano rwna bagwi saui rw di; abo bini hani páithago. A hano ang a mangkhorno paiwi namani marini bagwi bebak rang norokni bwskango’-no bono rwui páinai.”

English: Now Ephron was sitting among the Hittites; and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the hearing of the Hittites, of all who went in at the gate of his city,
Kokborok: Ephron Hitirokni bisingo’-no achukjak, kamiyungni duglamo aro thángnai bebak Hitisarokni bwskango Hitisa Ephron Abrahamno sa kha,

English: “No, my lord, hear me; I give you the field, and I give you the cave that is in it; in the presence of the sons of my people I give it to you; bury your dead.”
Kokborok: “I’nhi hoda ani kók khna gra di; ang nono a hanobo rw-o tei a hakhornobo rw-o; ang omo ani borokrokni bwskango’-no rw-o; nini mang kwthwino phob di.”

English: Then Abraham bowed down before the people of the land.
Kokborok: Hakhe Abraham a hani borokrokno khulum kha.

English: And he said to Ephron in the hearing of the people of the land, “But if you will, hear me; I will give the price of the field; accept it from me, that I may bury my dead there.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bebak lukurokno khna rwui Ephronno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng muchung khe ani kók khna ja di, ang ani mang kwthwino oro phobwi manna bagwi ang o hani rang rwmano yachak ja di.”

English: Ephron answered Abraham,
Kokborok: Ephron Abrahamno hai hwnwi sa kha,

English: “My lord, listen to me; a piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver, what is that between you and me? Bury your dead.”
Kokborok: “Hoda, ani kók khna ja di; ha phalasani rang khe brwi-ra rukphai budul se, abo nini bai ani bisingo ba tamo? Nini kwthwino phob di.”

English: Abraham agreed with Ephron; and Abraham weighed out for Ephron the silver which he had named in the hearing of the Hittites, four hundred shekels of silver, according to the weights current among the merchants.
Kokborok: Abraham, Ephron bai lon lai kha; bo Ephronno Hitisarokno sama swk’-no rw kha, abo ra brwi rukphai belep, a jora-o bepar thwngnairokni rwta tẃi khe abono rwta kha.

English: So the field of Ephron in Mach-pelah, which was to the east of Mamre, the field with the cave which was in it and all the trees that were in the field, throughout its whole area, was made over
Kokborok: Mamreni salkabai-o Ephronni Makpelani ha tei aro tongma hakhor tei charani buphangrok, bebakno,

English: to Abraham as a possession in the presence of the Hittites, before all who went in at the gate of his city.
Kokborok: Hitisarokni bwskango’-no tei bebak kamiyungni duglamo thángnairokni bwskango Abrahamni hwnwi rwjak kha.

English: After this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Mach-pelah east of Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Abraham bihik Sarano Mamreni salkabaini Makpela-o tongma a hakhoro phob kha, (abo wng kha Hebron) abo Kanan ha-o.

English: The field and the cave that is in it were made over to Abraham as a possession for a burying place by the Hittites.
Kokborok: Aroni ha tei aro tongma hakhor bebak Hitisarokni yakni paiwi Abraham bebakni nokphang wng kha.

English: Now Abraham was old, well advanced in years; and the Lord had blessed Abraham in all things.
Kokborok: Abraham chakra wng kha, umor pái rw rwk kha; Subraiyung Abrahamno bebago’-no hamari rw kha.

English: And Abraham said to his servant, the oldest of his house, who had charge of all that he had, “Put your hand under my thigh,
Kokborok: Abraham bini nokni seleng kwcham kuk, bebakni barjano berjak a selengno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini yakno ani yaphago ton di,

English: and I will make you swear by the Lord, the God of heaven and of the earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I dwell,
Kokborok: ang nono ha tei chuknohani Subraiyung Kaitorni mungwi swmai tang rwui sa-o, nwng ang tongma o ha Kananni bwrwirokno ani sajlani bagwi hamjwk naui man glak,

English: but will go to my country and to my kindred, and take a wife for my son Isaac.”
Kokborok: tamo khe nwng ani ha-o thángwi ani sajla Isakni bagwi ani sandai masarokni bisingni se angsajla Isakno káina bagwi bwrwi ma tubunai.”

English: The servant said to him, “Perhaps the woman may not be willing to follow me to this land; must I then take your son back to the land from which you came?”
Kokborok: A seleng bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Rom di a bwrwi ang bai baksa phaina nai li ya; hakhe ang ba tamo nwslano da nwng phaimani a ha-o tlangwi thángnai tamo?”

English: Abraham said to him, “See to it that you do not take my son back there.
Kokborok: Abraham bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, nwng ani sajlano le aro kiphilwi ta tlang phi di.

English: The Lord, the God of heaven, who took me from my father's house and from the land of my birth, and who spoke to me and swore to me, ‘To your descendants I will give this land,’ he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.
Kokborok: Ano ani aphani nokni tei ani achaima hani tikhlai tubunai chuknohani Subraiyung Kaitor, bo ano hai hwnwi saui swmai tang kha, ‘Nini bosongno ang o hano rwnai,’ bo’-no nini swkango khe bini suarino rohornai tei nwng aroni ani sajlani bagwi hamjwk mannai.

English: But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you will be free from this oath of mine; only you must not take my son back there.”
Kokborok: Aroni bwrwi nwng bai baksa phainani lon ya hwn khe, hakhe nwng ani o swmaini yakni yóknai; nwng ani sajlano simi aro kiphilwi ta tlang di.”

English: So the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master, and swore to him concerning this matter.
Kokborok: Hakhe a seleng bini nokphang Abrahamni yaphungo yak ton kha tei o kókno tẃiwi bini thani swmai tang kha.

English: Then the servant took ten of his master's camels and departed, taking all sorts of choice gifts from his master; and he arose, and went to Mesopotamia, to the city of Nahor.
Kokborok: Hakhe a seleng bini bugrani mangingang mak chi naui lam rw kha, bo bini bugrani aroni hilini bagwi manwi kaham-kaham tlang kha; hakhe bo lam rwui Nahorni kamiyung Mesopotamia-o tháng kha.

English: And he made the camels kneel down outside the city by the well of water at the time of evening, the time when women go out to draw water.
Kokborok: Bo kamiyungni phataro twikhor gandaro khe mangingangrokno yáskú sung rw bai kha, aphuru sarik jora, a jora-o bwrwirok twi khókna phai-o.

English: And he said, “O Lord, God of my master Abraham, grant me success today, I pray thee, and show steadfast love to my master Abraham.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo hai hwnwi suri kha, “O Subraiyung, ani nokphang Abrahamni Kaitor, nwng tini ani samung cha rw ja di, ang nono koi ja-o tei ani bugra Abrahamno hamjakmano phunuk ja di.

English: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.
Kokborok: Nai di ang twikhor gana-o bachaui tongo tei kamiyungni bwrwirok twi khókna bagwi phaiwi tongo.

English: Let the maiden to whom I shall say, ‘Pray let down your jar that I may drink,’ and who shall say, ‘Drink, and I will water your camels’—let her be the one whom thou hast appointed for thy servant Isaac. By this I shall know that thou hast shown steadfast love to my master.”
Kokborok: Ang bórokni bisingni’-no khoroksano, ‘Nini golano mekhengwi ano kisa twi khan rw ja di,’ hwnwi sa khe bo, ‘Nwng di tei nini mangingangrokni bagwibo ang twi khógwi tubunai,’ hwnwi sa khe, bo’-no nini seleng Isakni bagwi nini chongmani bwrwi wng si thung. Omo hai wng khe, nwng ani nokphangno khá súgo hwnwi sinai.”

English: Before he had done speaking, behold, Rebekah, who was born to Bethuel the son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham's brother, came out with her water jar upon her shoulder.
Kokborok: Bo suri bai ya sini’-no Rebeka phairukungo twini gola tẃiwi aro sók phai kha. Bo Abrahamni takhuk Nahorni bwsla Bethuelni bwsajwk, bini bihik wng kha Milka.

English: The maiden was very fair to look upon, a virgin, whom no man had known. She went down to the spring, and filled her jar, and came up.
Kokborok: Bo wng kha belai bwrwi naithok, bo khoroksa chwla bai phano gwdal ya kho. Bo twikhoro thángwi gola-o twi supungwi kiphilwi phai kha.

English: Then the servant ran to meet her, and said, “Pray give me a little water to drink from your jar.”
Kokborok: Hakhe seleng khachigwi thángwi bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini golani twi ano kisa khan ja di.”

English: She said, “Drink, my lord”; and she quickly let down her jar upon her hand, and gave him a drink.
Kokborok: Bo, “Takhuk nwng ja di” hwnwi sa kha; hakhe bo dodro-dodro khe golano yago khe tikhlaiwi bono twi khan kha.

English: When she had finished giving him a drink, she said, “I will draw for your camels also, until they have done drinking.”
Kokborok: Bo bono twi khanwi pái khe hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nini mangingangrokni bagwibo twi khógwi tubunai bórok nungwi mansak’-no nung thung.”

English: So she quickly emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to the well to draw, and she drew for all his camels.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo doui a golano bukcha khe twikhoro hai’-no hai twi khókna tháng kha bo bini bebak mangingangrokni bagwi twi khók kha.

English: The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether the Lord had prospered his journey or not.
Kokborok: A borok siring siring khe a bwrwini tongmungrokno naichomwi Kaitor ba bini phaimani samung cha da cha rwno cha rw ya naiwi tong kha.

English: When the camels had done drinking, the man took a gold ring weighing a half shekel, and two bracelets for her arms weighing ten gold shekels,
Kokborok: A mangingangrok twi nung khe’-no a borok sekel khaksa rangchakni longphu rw kha, sekel kaichi yaktwk kongnwini bagwi rangchakni mathiya rwui

English: and said, “Tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your father's house for us to lodge in?”
Kokborok: bo a bwrwino hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng saboni sajwk wng kha? Nwphani nogo ang bai ani borokrokni tongthai da manno?”

English: She said to him, “I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.”
Kokborok: Bwrwima bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang khe Milka, Nahorni bagwi bámani bwsla Bethuelni bwsajwk se.”

English: She added, “We have both straw and provender enough, and room to lodge in.”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwibo sa kha, “Chini nogo nara tei chwkap kwbángma tongo tei tongna bagwibo nok kwbángma tongo.”

English: The man bowed his head and worshiped the Lord,
Kokborok: Hakhe a borok yáskú sungwi Subraiyungno suriui

English: and said, “Blessed be the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken his steadfast love and his faithfulness toward my master. As for me, the Lord has led me in the way to the house of my master's kinsmen.”
Kokborok: hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani nokphang Abrahamni Subraiyung Kaitorno lobo, bo ani nokphangno hamjakma tei sói khe naimano yakar ya. Anobo hai’-no Subraiyung ani nokphangni jaiti-masani nok lama bwkhak lama soui tubu-ui phai kha.”

English: Then the maiden ran and told her mother's household about these things.
Kokborok: Hakhe a bwrwima khachigwi thángwi bumani nokni bebak borokrokno a kókrokno saui khna rwui kha.

English: Rebekah had a brother whose name was Laban; and Laban ran out to the man, to the spring.
Kokborok: Rebekani takhuk khoroksa tongo bini mung wng kha Laban; Laban a twikhor tongma-o a borokni thani khachigwi tháng kha.

English: When he saw the ring, and the bracelets on his sister's arms, and when he heard the words of Rebekah his sister, “Thus the man spoke to me,” he went to the man; and behold, he was standing by the camels at the spring.
Kokborok: Laban bini bukhuk Rebekani thani longphu tei yakni mathiyano nuk khe tei a borok bahanokjwkno tamo sa kha a kókrokno khna khe, bo bini thani tháng kha, bo a borokno bini mangingangrokni gandaro twikhor samo khe bachaui tongjak nugwi kha.

English: He said, “Come in, O blessed of the Lord; why do you stand outside? For I have prepared the house and a place for the camels.”
Kokborok: Bo bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “O Subraiyung bai naikhlaijaknaisa, phai di, tamo phataro bachaui tong lang? Ang nini bagwi ani nogo tongthai tiyar khe ton kha tei nini mangingangrokni bagwibo tongthai tongo.”

English: So the man came into the house; and Laban ungirded the camels, and gave him straw and provender for the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.
Kokborok: Hakhe a borok nok bisingo hap kha; hakhe Laban mangingangrokni phikungni manwirokbo kariui chwkap tei bwlái kwranrok chána bagwi rw kha. Bo bai bini borokrokni bagwi yakung súnani twi rw kha.

English: Then food was set before him to eat; but he said, “I will not eat until I have told my errand.” He said, “Speak on.”
Kokborok:
Ulo khe bini thani chámung tubu-ui rw phai kha; tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani kók sajaknairok sa bai ya sak ang chá glak.”
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Do hakhe sa di.”

English: So he said, “I am Abraham's servant.
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Abrahamni seleng.

English: The Lord has greatly blessed my master, and he has become great; he has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, menservants and maidservants, camels and asses.
Kokborok: Subraiyung, ani nokphangno hamari kwbángma rw kha, bo kotor wng kha; bo bono musuk, merarok, rangchak-rukphairok, selengrok, selengjwkrok, mangingangrok, korai chirkarok rw kha.

English: And Sarah my master's wife bore a son to my master when she was old; and to him he has given all that he has.
Kokborok: Hakhe ani nokphangni bihik umor chakra súk phuru ani nokphangni bagwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá kha; bono bo bini bebakno rw kha.

English: My master made me swear, saying, ‘You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I dwell;
Kokborok: Ani nokphang ano hai hwnwi swmai tang rw kha, ‘Ani sajlani bagwi tabuk ang tongmani a ha Kananni bwrwino hamjwk ta na di;

English: but you shall go to my father's house and to my kindred, and take a wife for my son.’
Kokborok: tamo khe nwng ani Aphani ha-o ani jaiti-masarok tongma ha-o thángwi ani sajlani bagwi hamjwk naiwi di.’

English: I said to my master, ‘Perhaps the woman will not follow me.’
Kokborok: Ang ani nokphangsano hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘A bwrwi ang bai phai ya wngwi mano.’

English: But he said to me, ‘The Lord, before whom I walk, will send his angel with you and prosper your way; and you shall take a wife for my son from my kindred and from my father's house;
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo ano hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘Ang tẃimani Subraiyung, bini suarino nwng bai rohornai tei nini samungno cha rwnai; nwng ani sajlani bagwi ani jaiti-masani bisingni, ani aphani nokni hamjwk tubu di;

English: then you will be free from my oath, when you come to my kindred; and if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath.’
Kokborok: omo hai khe nwng ani jaiti-masani aro thángwi nwng soi yarok wngjagwi kiphilwi phai khe se yóknai.’

English: “I came today to the spring, and said, ‘O Lord, the God of my master Abraham, if now thou wilt prosper the way which I go,
Kokborok: “Aboni bagwi ang tini a twikhor rukungni samo phaiwi khá bising bising hai hwnwi suri kha, ‘O Subraiyung, ani nokphang Abrahamni Kaitor, nwng ani o phaimanino samung cha rw ja di,

English: behold, I am standing by the spring of water; let the young woman who comes out to draw, to whom I shall say, “Pray give me a little water from your jar to drink,”
Kokborok: nai di ang o twikhorni samo bachaui tongo; kamiyungni khoroksa swk bwrwi sikla twi khókna phai khe ang bono hai hwnwi sanai, “Ano nini golani twi kisa khan ja di,”

English: and who will say to me, “Drink, and I will draw for your camels also,” let her be the woman whom the Lord has appointed for my master's son.’
Kokborok: phiya bo ano “Nung di, ang nini mangingangrokni bantabo twi khógwi rw anw,” hwnwi sanai bwrwi’-no subrai ani bugrani bwslani bagwi chongma bwrwi wng thung.’

English: “Before I had done speaking in my heart, behold, Rebekah came out with her water jar on her shoulder; and she went down to the spring, and drew. I said to her, ‘Pray let me drink.’
Kokborok: “Ang hai hwnwi siring siring khe surima thak ya sini’-no Rebeka phairukungo gola boksaui phaiwi twikhor bwkhak twi khókna bagwi tháng kha, ang bono ‘ano kisa twi khan ja di’ hwnwi sa kha.

English: She quickly let down her jar from her shoulder, and said, ‘Drink, and I will give your camels drink also.’ So I drank, and she gave the camels drink also.
Kokborok: Bo doui bini golano phairukungni tikhlaiwi, ‘Nung di’ hwnwi gola mekhengwi sa kha, ang nini mangingangroknobo twi khannai’ hwnwi sa kha. Hakhe ang twi nung kha tei bo mangingangroknobo twi khan kha.

English: Then I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ She said, ‘The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore to him.’ So I put the ring on her nose, and the bracelets on her arms.
Kokborok: Ang bono sẃng kha, ‘Nwng saboni bwsajwk?’ Bo sa kha, ‘Ani apha wng kha Nahor bai Milkani bwsla Bethuel.’ Hakhe ang bini bukungo longphu kan rwui rw kha tei yaktwgo mathiya soraui rw kha.

English: Then I bowed my head and worshiped the Lord, and blessed the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me by the right way to take the daughter of my master's kinsman for his son.
Kokborok: Ang khorok bomwi Subraiyungno khulum kha, ano ani nokphangni jaiti-masani thani lama soui tlangwi nokphangni bwslani bagwi hamjwk man rwui rwnai ani nokphang Abrahamni Subraiyung Kaitorno ang lob kha.

English: Now then, if you will deal loyally and truly with my master, tell me; and if not, tell me; that I may turn to the right hand or to the left.”
Kokborok: Tabuk nwng ani nokphangno sói khe nainani tei kubui khe samung tangna chong khe ano sa si di. I’n da, I’nhi sa di hakhe ang tamo khlainai khlai ya chongnani.”

English: Then Laban and Bethuel answered, “The thing comes from the Lord; we cannot speak to you bad or good.
Kokborok: Laban bai Bethuel hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo Subraiyungni aroni se phai-o; chwng nono hamyabo saui man ya kahambo saui man ya.

English: Behold, Rebekah is before you, take her and go, and let her be the wife of your master's son, as the Lord has spoken.”
Kokborok: Iko Rebeka nini bwskango’-no, bono nwng bai tlangwi tháng si di, Subraiyung sama tẃi’-kheno bono nini nokphangni sajlani hik snamwi si di.”

English: When Abraham's servant heard their words, he bowed himself to the earth before the Lord.
Kokborok: Abrahamni seleng a kókno khna khe’-no, bo yáskú sungwi Subraiyungno khulum kha.

English: And the servant brought forth jewelry of silver and of gold, and raiment, and gave them to Rebekah; he also gave to her brother and to her mother costly ornaments.
Kokborok: Hakhe a seleng rírok tei rangchak rukphaini uakhum-toyarok kanmungrok tikhlaiwi Rebekano rw kha. Bo bini takhuk bai bini bumanobo kanmung kaham rw kha.

English: And he and the men who were with him ate and drank, and they spent the night there. When they arose in the morning, he said, “Send me back to my master.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Abrahamni seleng tei bo bai baksa tongnai a borokrok chá lai kha-nung lai kha tei horo arono tong lai kha, bórok phungo bacha khe, “Ang ani nokphangni thani kiphilwi tháng sinai” hwnwi bo sa kha.

English: Her brother and her mother said, “Let the maiden remain with us a while, at least ten days; after that she may go.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Rebekani takhuk bai buma hai hwnwi sa kha, “Rebeka chwng bai khe salsni eba salchi jora chwng bai tong gra si thung aboni ulo khe bo tháng si thung.”

English: But he said to them, “Do not delay me, since the Lord has prospered my way; let me go that I may go to my master.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe a seleng hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ano ta dwk rw si di; tamni hwn ba Subraiyung ani samungo hamari rw kha, ano nokphangni thani tháng rw si di.”

English: They said, “We will call the maiden, and ask her.”
Kokborok: Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng a bwrwimano ringwi sẃngnai.”

English: And they called Rebekah, and said to her, “Will you go with this man?” She said, “I will go.”
Kokborok:
Hakhe bórok Rebekano ringwi “Nwng o borok bai baksa tháng sinai da?” hwnwi sẃng kha,
Rebeka hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’n ang thángnai.”

English: So they sent away Rebekah their sister and her nurse, and Abraham's servant and his men.
Kokborok: Bórok bórokni bukhuk Rebekano tei bono naikini khlainaino Abrahamni seleng tei bo bai baksa phainai a borokrok bai khe rwui rohor kha.

English: And they blessed Rebekah, and said to her, “Our sister, be the mother of thousands of ten thousands; and may your descendants possess the gate of those who hate them!”
Kokborok: Aphuru bórok Rebekano hai hwnwi hamari rwui sa kha, “Chini hanok chisai chisainini buma wng thung, tei nini bosongrok bórokni arirokni kamiyungrokno segwi naui man thung!”

English: Then Rebekah and her maids arose, and rode upon the camels and followed the man; thus the servant took Rebekah, and went his way.
Kokborok: Hakhe Rebeka bai bono naikini khlainaijwkma bachaui mangingangrokni saka-o kasaui a borokrokni utung utung khe tháng lai kha; omo hai kheno a seleng Rebekano tẃiwi bini thángthai-o tháng kha.

English: Now Isaac had come from Beer-lahai-roi, and was dwelling in the Negeb.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Isak Bir-Lahairoini phaiwi Negebo tong phai kha.

English: And Isaac went out to meditate in the field in the evening; and he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, there were camels coming.
Kokborok: Isak sarigo haphar bwkhak himgurungna thángmani; hakhe bo bokhorok tisaui naisik khe mangingangrokno phaiwi tongma nuhur kha.

English: And Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she alighted from the camel,
Kokborok: Rebeka Isakno phaima nuk khe’-no mangingangni nongkhor kha,

English: and said to the servant, “Who is the man yonder, walking in the field to meet us?” The servant said, “It is my master.” So she took her veil and covered herself.
Kokborok: tei selengno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Chini phaising himwi phainai a borok sabo?” Hakhe a seleng “Bo ani nokphang” hwnwi sa kha. Hakhe bo bini rí hungjaknai bai mwkhang kholop kha.

English: And the servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.
Kokborok: A seleng Isakno bo khlaimarokno saui khna rw kha.

English: Then Isaac brought her into the tent, and took Rebekah, and she became his wife; and he loved her. So Isaac was comforted after his mother's death.
Kokborok: Hakhe Isak Rebekano ríni nok bisingo tubu-ui phai kha tei bono hik song kha, Rebeka bini bihik wng kha; Isak Rebekano hamjak kha. Omo hai khe Isak buma thwimani yagulo bwkha kwchang kha.

English: Abraham took another wife, whose name was Keturah.
Kokborok: Abraham tei khoroksa bwrwino káijak kha tei bini mung khe Ketura.

English: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
Kokborok: Bo Simran, Joksan, Medan, Midian, Isbak tei Suano bini bagwi bá kha.

English: Jokshan was the father of Sheba and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were Asshurim, Letushim, and Le-ummim.
Kokborok: Joksan khe, Seba bai Dedanni bupha. Dedanni bwsarok khe Asurim, Letusim, Leomim.

English: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
Kokborok: Midianni bwsarok wng kha, Epha, Epher, Hanok, Abida tei Elda. Omorok bebak’-no Keturani bwsarok.

English: Abraham gave all he had to Isaac.
Kokborok: Abraham bini tong kwrwi bebakno Isakno rw bai kha.

English: But to the sons of his concubines Abraham gave gifts, and while he was still living he sent them away from his son Isaac, eastward to the east country.
Kokborok:
Bo kwthang tong phuru bini tei kubun bihik kotoisongrok bámani bini bwsaroknobo hilirok rw bai kha. Hakhe bo a bwsarokno bwsla Isak bai hakchal khe salkabaini ha-o rwui rohor kha.
Abraham thwi kha tei phobjak kha

English: These are the days of the years of Abraham's life, a hundred and seventy-five years.
Kokborok: Abraham nokha bisi sarasnichiba thangwi tong lang kha.

English: Abraham breathed his last and died in a good old age, an old man and full of years, and was gathered to his people.
Kokborok: Abraham bini umor rá súgwi, umor pái súgwi’-no thwi kha tei bini borokrok bai tongwi kha.

English: Isaac and Ishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Mach-pelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, east of Mamre,
Kokborok: Isak bai Ismael bini bwslasong bono Makpelani hakhoro phob kha, abo wng kha salkabaini haphar Hitisa Joharni bwsla Ephronni ha panthor,

English: the field which Abraham purchased from the Hittites. There Abraham was buried, with Sarah his wife.
Kokborok: Abo khe Abraham Hitisasongni aroni paimani ha; Abraham bai bihik Sara khoroknwi’-no arono phobjago.

English: After the death of Abraham God blessed Isaac his son. And Isaac dwelt at Beer-lahai-roi.
Kokborok: Abraham thwimani yagulo khe Kaitor Isakno hamari rw kha. Tei Isak Bir-Laharoi o tongwi kha.

English: These are the descendants of Ishmael, Abraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's maid, bore to Abraham.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Ijipjwk Hagar, Sarani selengjwk bámani Abrahamni bwsla Ismaelni bosongrok

English: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, named in the order of their birth   Nebaioth, the first-born of Ishmael; and Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
Kokborok: Bórokni mungrok khe, mareui swkang achaigranaini simi; Nebaiot khe Ismaelni bwsa okra; teirok khe Kedar, Adbil, Mibsam,

English: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
Kokborok: Misma, Duma, Massa,

English: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
Kokborok: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphis tei Kedema.

English: These are the sons of Ishmael and these are their names, by their villages and by their encampments, twelve princes according to their tribes.
Kokborok: Omorokno Ismaelni bwsarok, bórokni mung rwgwi’-no bórokni kamirokni mungbo pharjago tei bosong thwgwi khorokchinwi bugra chongjak.

English: (These are the years of the life of Ishmael, a hundred and thirty-seven years; he breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his kindred.)
Kokborok: (Ismael nokha bisi sara tei thamchisni umor phuru-o thwi kha tei bini sandairok bai baksa tongwi kha.)

English: They dwelt from Havilah to Shur, which is opposite Egypt in the direction of Assyria; he settled over against all his people.
Kokborok: Bini bosongrok Habila bai Surni amchoini kwcharo, Ijipni salkabai-o tei Asiriya thángma lama-o sandairok bai pal khe kami kha lai tong lai kha.

English: These are the descendants of Isaac, Abraham's son   Abraham was the father of Isaac,
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Abrahamni bwsla Isakni bosongrok; Abraham wng kha Isakni bupha,

English: and Isaac was forty years old when he took to wife Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean of Paddan-aram, the sister of Laban the Aramean.
Kokborok: Isakni, Padan-aramni Aramiasa Bethuelni bwsajwkno tei Labanni bahanokno káijak phuru Isakni umor nokha bisi brwichi ká kha.

English: And Isaac prayed to the Lord for his wife, because she was barren; and the Lord granted his prayer, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
Kokborok: Rebeka bwsa man yani bagwi, Isak Subraiyungni thani suri kha; hakhe Subraiyung bini surimano khna kha tei bihik Rebeka sakderiya wng kha.

English: The children struggled together within her; and she said, “If it is thus, why do I live?” So she went to inquire of the Lord.
Kokborok: Bini ogo bwsa khoroknwirok dundrok lai kha; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo hai wng khe, bahai khe ang thangwi tongnai?” Hakhe bo Subraiyungno sẃngnani tháng kha.

English:
And the Lord said to her,
“Two nations are in your womb,
and two peoples, born of you, shall be divided;
the one shall be stronger than the other,
the elder shall serve the younger.”
Kokborok:
Hakhe Subraiyung bono hai hwnwi sa kha,
“Nini ogo dopha kwnwirok tongo,
nwng kwtha lai ya dopha kwnwino bánai,
khoroksa tei khoroksani slái raknai;
okra kusuno ma monnai.”

English: When her days to be delivered were fulfilled, behold, there were twins in her womb.
Kokborok: Bini sak keng phuru khe, bo bwsa apher bá kha.

English: The first came forth red, all his body like a hairy mantle; so they called his name Esau.
Kokborok: Swkang achaigranai khe kwchak tei bini bwsak buikhumu gwnang rí hai, abo bai’-no bono Esau hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Afterward his brother came forth, and his hand had taken hold of Esau's heel; so his name was called Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe tei khoroksa Esauni yakoraino kwrak khe romwi nongkhorwi phai kha, hakhe bono Jakob hwnwi mung phar kha. Bórok achai phuru Isakni umor nokha bisi dokchi ká kha.

English: When the boys grew up, Esau was a skilful hunter, a man of the field, while Jacob was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.
Kokborok: A chwrairok tor kha lok kha, Esau khe poluan kaham wng kha, bo phataro tongnano hamjago, Jakob khe kwchang kwchang borok, nogo máng tongo.

English: Isaac loved Esau, because he ate of his game; but Rebekah loved Jacob.
Kokborok: Isak Esauno hamjago, tamni hwn ba bo Esau butharmani muihan thokjago; Rebeka khe Jakobno hamjago.

English: Once when Jacob was boiling pottage, Esau came in from the field, and he was famished.
Kokborok: Salsa Jakob tha sóngwi tongo, Esau khe mui rwgwi phai-o, bo belai khe ok khuijak.

English: And Esau said to Jacob, “Let me eat some of that red pottage, for I am famished!” (Therefore his name was called Edom. )
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Esau Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang belai ok khui tong kha, ano kisa u tha sóngjakno chá rw ja di.” (Aboni bagwino bono Idom hwnjago.)

English: Jacob said, “First sell me your birthright.”
Kokborok: Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Swkang nwng ano nini okrani manthaino phalwi rw gra di.”

English: Esau said, “I am about to die; of what use is a birthright to me?”
Kokborok: Esau hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang thw se thwinai kha, okrani manthai ba ani tamo chúk?”

English: Jacob said, “Swear to me first.” So he swore to him, and sold his birthright to Jacob.
Kokborok:
Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Swkang ano swmai tangwi kók rw gra di.”
Hakhe tei Jakobno bini achaimani manthaino bo phalwi rw kha.

English: Then Jacob gave Esau bread and pottage of lentils, and he ate and drank, and rose and went his way. Thus Esau despised his birthright.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob bono auang kisa tei tha sóngmani bwtwino rw kha. Hakhe Esau cháui nungwi bini achaimani manthaino khibiui rw kha.

English: Now there was a famine in the land, besides the former famine that was in the days of Abraham. And Isaac went to Gerar, to Abimelech king of the Philistines.
Kokborok: Abrahamni jora-o asari wngmano karwi tei philsa aro asari wng phi kha. Hakhe Isak Philistiani habugra Abimelekni ha Geraro tháng kha.

English: And the Lord appeared to him, and said, “Do not go down to Egypt; dwell in the land of which I shall tell you.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Isak bai nukjagwi hai hwnwi sa phai kha, “Ha Ijibo ta tháng di; ang tong di hwnwi samani a ha-ono tong di.

English: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you, and will bless you; for to you and to your descendants I will give all these lands, and I will fulfil the oath which I swore to Abraham your father.
Kokborok: O ha-ono tongwi tong di ang nwng bai’-no tongnai tei nono hamari rwnai; nono tei nini bosongno o harokno bebakno rwnai, ang nwpha Abraham bai swmai tangmano narwknai.

English: I will multiply your descendants as the stars of heaven, and will give to your descendants all these lands; and by your descendants all the nations of the earth shall bless themselves
Kokborok: Ang nini bosongrokno athukiri báng tẃi khe er rwnai tei o harokno nini bosongrokno rwnai; nini bosongni tẃi khe hani bebak dopharok hamari mannai;
