English: because Abraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.”
Kokborok: tamni hwn khe Abraham ani kók khna kha, ani bebak kerongno tẃi kha tei dagimarokno khna kha.”

English: So Isaac dwelt in Gerar.
Kokborok: Isak Geraro kami khaui tong kha.

English: When the men of the place asked him about his wife, he said, “She is my sister”; for he feared to say, “My wife,” thinking, “lest the men of the place should kill me for the sake of Rebekah”; because she was fair to look upon.
Kokborok: Aroni borokrok bono bihikni kókno sẃng khe, bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bo ani hanok se” tamni hwn ba bo “Ani hik” hwnwi sanani kiri jago, “Oroni borokrok ano Rebekani bagwi butharnai” hwnwi bo uansuk kha, tamni hwn ba Rebeka wng kha bwrwi naithok.

English: When he had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out of a window and saw Isaac fondling Rebekah his wife.
Kokborok: Bo aro sal kolokma romwi tong phuru salsa Philistiani habugra Abimelek tailam tẃi naharwi Isak bihik Rebekani bwsak mihimwi tongma nuk kha.

English: So Abimelech called Isaac, and said, “Behold, she is your wife; how then could you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac said to him, “Because I thought, ‘Lest I die because of her.’ ”
Kokborok:
Hakhe Abimelek Isakno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di bo nihik se; hakhe nwng bahai khe, ‘Bo ani hanok se’ hwnwi saui man?”
Isak bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang bini bagwi ma de ma thwi langno hwnwi ma uansuk kha?”

English: Abimelech said, “What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us.”
Kokborok: Abimelek hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng chwngno tamo khlai kha? Khoroksa swk nini hik bai sak gwdalwi man kha mun tei abo bai chini thani sumsok tubuma klai kha mun.”

English: So Abimelech warned all the people, saying, “Whoever touches this man or his wife shall be put to death.”
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi Abimelek bini borokno hai hwnwi saui hus khe rw kha, “Khoroksa swk phano o borokno eba bini bihikni sak tangna ta tháng di, tang khe bo ma butharjaknai.”

English: And Isaac sowed in that land, and reaped in the same year a hundredfold. The Lord blessed him,
Kokborok: Isak o ha-o mai-khul kái kha tei kwbángma mai-khulrok man kha. Hakhe Subraiyung bono kwbángma hamari rw kha,

English: and the man became rich, and gained more and more until he became very wealthy.
Kokborok: hakhe bo belai’-no gwnang wngsa kha tei rang kwbáng aji kha, hakhe bo khoroksa rang gwnang wng kha.

English: He had possessions of flocks and herds, and a great household, so that the Philistines envied him.
Kokborok: Bini kwbángma musuk-merarok wng kha teibo kwbángma seleng-selengjwkrok wng kha tei Philistiasarok abono nugwi mokol kiyórjak kha.

English: (Now the Philistines had stopped and filled with earth all the wells which his father's servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father.)
Kokborok: (Aboni bagwi bini bupha Abrahamni molni bebak twikhor khurjakrokno Philistiasarok ha bai khe muthubwi rw kha.)

English: And Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us; for you are much mightier than we.”
Kokborok: Tei Abimelek Isakno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chini oroni tháng di, tamni hwn ba nwng chini slái phan gwnang kuk wng kha.”

English: So Isaac departed from there, and encamped in the valley of Gerar and dwelt there.
Kokborok: Yagulo khe Isak aroni kutulwi thángwi Gerarni hapharo ríni nok tangwi tongwi kha.

English: And Isaac dug again the wells of water which had been dug in the days of Abraham his father; for the Philistines had stopped them after the death of Abraham; and he gave them the names which his father had given them.
Kokborok: Tei Isak hai’-no hai buphani molni twikhorno khur phi kha, tamni hwn ba Philistiasarok Abraham thwimani ulo abono muthup kha. Bo bupha mung pharma hai’-no mung phar phi kha.

English: But when Isaac's servants dug in the valley and found there a well of springing water,
Kokborok: Tei Isakni selengrok hapharo twikhor khur khe aro twimuk kahamni twi man kha,

English: the herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Isaac's herdsmen, saying, “The water is ours.” So he called the name of the well Esek, because they contended with him.
Kokborok: aro Gerarni mal-mata mwrwknairok bai Isakni mal-mata mwrwknairok uálai lai kha tei “Omo chini twi se” hwnwi sa lai kha. Aboni bagwi o twikhorni mung Esek hwnwi phar kha, tamni hwn ba abono tẃi ma uálai kha.

English: Then they dug another well, and they quarreled over that also; so he called its name Sitnah.
Kokborok: Aphuru bórok tei khorsa twikhor khur-u kha, arobo hai’-no ma uálai-u kha, aboni bagwi abono Sitna hwnwi mung rw kha.

English: And he moved from there and dug another well, and over that they did not quarrel; so he called its name Rehoboth, saying, “For now the Lord has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.”
Kokborok: Tei bo aroni kutulwi thángwi tei khorsa twikhor khur rw kha, aro khe bo ma uálai li ya, aboni bagwi bo abono Rehobot mung phar kha tei hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tabuk le Subraiyung chwngno tongthai rw kha, chwng o ha-o hamari kupulung wngnai.”

English: From there he went up to Beer-sheba.
Kokborok: Aroni khe bo Birseba o toksaui tháng kha.

English: And the Lord appeared to him the same night and said, “I am the God of Abraham your father; fear not, for I am with you and will bless you and multiply your descendants for my servant Abraham's sake.”
Kokborok: Tei Subraiyung a horo bo bai nukjagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nini Apha Abrahamni Kaitor; ta kiri di, tamni hwn ba ang nwng bai tongo, ang nono hamari rwui tei nini bosongno ani seleng Abrahamni jarwi er rwnai.”

English: So he built an altar there and called upon the name of the Lord, and pitched his tent there. And there Isaac's servants dug a well.
Kokborok: Aphuru bo aro Subraiyungni mungwi háyá kaisa tang kha tei ríni nok tang kha. Aro Isakni selengrok twikhor khorsa khur-u kha.

English: Then Abimelech went to him from Gerar with Ahuzzath his adviser and Phicol the commander of his army.
Kokborok: Abimelek, bono kókbiti sanai Ahujat tei bini sengkrak bokhorok Phikolno tẃiwi Isak bai malaina bagwi Gerarni phai kha.

English: Isaac said to them, “Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Isak bórokno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng tamo tabuk ang bai malaina phai, nwng ba swkango ano naislerjagwi nini aroni rwkhlai rohor kha bla?”

English: They said, “We see plainly that the Lord is with you; so we say, let there be an oath between you and us, and let us make a covenant with you,
Kokborok: Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng srai-srai khe nuk kha Subraiyung nwng bai tongo; hakhe phai di nwng bai chwng kók kha lai anw, swmai kaisa tang lai anw,

English: that you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you and have done to you nothing but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of the Lord.”
Kokborok: nwng chwngno mungsa hamya ta khlai di, chwngbo nini mungsa hamya khlai yaba, chwng nono mungsa khlai yaui kwchang kwchang khe’-no thángna rw kha. Tabuk Subraiyung nono hamari rw kha.”

English: So he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bórokno chá rw kha khan rw kha.

English: In the morning they rose early and took oath with one another; and Isaac set them on their way, and they departed from him in peace.
Kokborok: Teini phung aidrobo khe khoroksa tei khoroksa bai khe swmai tang lai kha. Isak bórokno lama naharwi rohor kha tei bórok kwchang kwchang khe tháng lai kha.

English: That same day Isaac's servants came and told him about the well which they had dug, and said to him, “We have found water.”
Kokborok: A salo khe Isakni selengrok phaiwi bórok khurma a twikhorni kókno sa phai kha. Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng twi man kha.”

English: He called it Shibah; therefore the name of the city is Beer-sheba to this day.
Kokborok: Bo a twikhorni mung “Siba” hwnwi phar kha; abo hai kheno a kamiyungni mung tabuk jora Birseba hwnjago.

English: When Esau was forty years old, he took to wife Judith the daughter of Be-eri the Hittite, and Basemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite;
Kokborok: Esauni umor nokha bisi brwichi phuru bo Hitisa Berini bwsajwk Judit tei Hitisa Elonni bwsajwk Basematno hik na-o;

English: and they made life bitter for Isaac and Rebekah.
Kokborok: bórok Isak bai Rebekani thangsungo belai khe tekto rw kha.

English: When Isaac was old and his eyes were dim so that he could not see, he called Esau his older son, and said to him, “My son”; and he answered, “Here I am.”
Kokborok: Isak umor chakra wng kha tei mokolbo nuk ya wng kha. Bo bwsajla okra Esauno ringhorwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Angsajla” hakhe bwsajla, “I’n ang oro se” hwnwi sa kha.

English: He said, “Behold, I am old; I do not know the day of my death.
Kokborok: Isak hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang chakra laha; ang buphuru bo thwino si ya,

English: Now then, take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field, and hunt game for me,
Kokborok: hakhe tabuk nwng nini mui kokjaknai badukhung bai chakhlarok naui hapharo nongkhorwi tháng di tei ani bagwi mui butharwi tubu di,

English: and prepare for me savory food, such as I love, and bring it to me that I may eat; that I may bless you before I die.”
Kokborok: tei ang thokjakma hai khe mui sóngwi ani thani tubu-ui phai di, abo cháui pái khe ang thwinani swkang nono ani páithakni hamari rwnai.”

English: Now Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. So when Esau went to the field to hunt for game and bring it,
Kokborok: Yang khe Rebeka, Isak bwsla Esauno sama kókrokno khna kha. Hakhe Esau bolongo mui butharwi tubuna bagwi tháng khe,

English: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, “I heard your father speak to your brother Esau,
Kokborok: Rebeka bwsla Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwphano nwta Esauno hai hwnwi samani khna kha,

English: ‘Bring me game, and prepare for me savory food, that I may eat it, and bless you before the Lord before I die.’
Kokborok: ‘Ani bagwi mui butharwi tubu-ui abono sóngwi rw phai di; hakhe ang abono chá bai khe, thwinani swkang Subraiyungni bwskango khe hamari rwnai.’

English: Now therefore, my son, obey my word as I command you.
Kokborok: Tabuk aboni bagwi sajla ani kókno khna di tei ang sama tẃi’-no khlai di.

English: Go to the flock, and fetch me two good kids, that I may prepare from them savory food for your father, such as he loves;
Kokborok: Tháng di, mera bodol tongma-o thángwi mera bwsa kaham maknwi romwi tubu-ui phai di, abo bai khe ang nwpha thokjak tẃi’-kheno sóngwi tiyar khlainai;

English: and you shall bring it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies.”
Kokborok: bo thwina swkang abono cháui nono hamari rwui manna tẃi khe nwng abono tubu-ui phai di.”

English: But Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, “Behold, my brother Esau is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Jakob buma Rebekano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ata Esauni le bwsago buikhumu máng tei ani le bwsak milik se.

English: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be mocking him, and bring a curse upon myself and not a blessing.”
Kokborok: Apha ano tangwi naiwi saui man khe ang bono bandima se klai anw tei ani bagwi hamari wng yaui swraimung se wng anw.”

English: His mother said to him, “Upon me be your curse, my son; only obey my word, and go, fetch them to me.”
Kokborok: Buma bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini a bati ani sago nang thung, sajla; ani kók simi se khna di, tháng di, ano mera bwsarokno simi tubu-ui rw phai di.”

English: So he went and took them and brought them to his mother; and his mother prepared savory food, such as his father loved.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo thángwi romwi tubu-ui bumano rw phai kha; tei bupha thokjak tẃi’-kheno buma abono sóng kha.

English: Then Rebekah took the best garments of Esau her older son, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob her younger son;
Kokborok: Rebeka bini nogo tongma bwsla okra Esauni rí kaham kuk’-no naui, bwsla kusu Jakobno chum rw kha;

English: and the skins of the kids she put upon his hands and upon the smooth part of his neck;
Kokborok: a mera bwsani bukurno bini yago tei gwdnani bahan milik tongma jidolo bólwi rw kha;

English: and she gave the savory food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
Kokborok: hakhe bo kothok khe sóngma chámung tei auangrokno bwsla Jakobni yago yapharwi rw kha.

English: So he went in to his father, and said, “My father”; and he said, “Here I am; who are you, my son?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob buphani aro thángwi “Apha” hwnwi hwn phai khe; bo “I’n tamo, sajla, nwng sabo wng kha?” hwnwi hwn kha.

English: Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau your first-born. I have done as you told me; now sit up and eat of my game, that you may bless me.”
Kokborok: Jakob buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwsla okra Esau se. Ang nwng sama tẃi’-no khlai kha; ano hamari rwui manna tẃi khe, nwng bacha di achugwi ang mui romwi tubumano chá di.”

English: But Isaac said to his son, “How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?” He answered, “Because the Lord your God granted me success.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe Isak bwslano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sajla nwng bahai khe omo aswk doui manẃi phai man lang?” Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini Subraiyung Kaitor se ano samung cha rw-o.”

English: Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Come near, that I may feel you, my son, to know whether you are really my son Esau or not.”
Kokborok: Isak Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani samo phai di, nwng angsajla Esau de Esau ya, tangwi nai grana.”

English: So Jacob went near to Isaac his father, who felt him and said, “The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob bupha Isakni samo tháng kha, hakhe bo bono tangwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Khorang khe Jakobni khorang, tamo khe yakrok khe Esauni yakrok se.”

English: And he did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like his brother Esau's hands; so he blessed him.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bono siniui man li ya, tamni hwn ba bini yakrok khe bini bwta Esauni yakrok hai buikhumu gwnang. Hakhe bo bono hamari rw kha,

English: He said, “Are you really my son Esau?” He answered, “I am.”
Kokborok: bo bono hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng kubui’-no ani sajla Esau da?” Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’n ang’-no.”

English: Then he said, “Bring it to me, that I may eat of my son's game and bless you.” So he brought it to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.
Kokborok: Hakhe Isak hai hwnwi sa kha, “Abono ani thani tubu di, ani sajla mui rommano cháui pái khe ang nono hamari rwnai.” Hakhe bo abono bini thani tubu khe bo abono chá kha; tei angur bwtwibo rw phai khe abono nung kha.

English: Then his father Isaac said to him, “Come near and kiss me, my son.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bupha Isak bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Sajla, samo phai di, ano motom su phai di.”

English:
So he came near and kissed him; and he smelled the smell of his garments, and blessed him, and said,
“See, the smell of my son
is as the smell of a field which the Lord has blessed!
Kokborok: Hakhe bo samo phaiwi bono motom su kha; bo bini rírokno motom su-ui hamari rwui hai hwnwi sa kha “Nai di ani sajlani báhai, Subraiyung hamari rwma hapharni motom báhai!

English:
May God give you of the dew of heaven,
and of the fatness of the earth,
and plenty of grain and wine.
Kokborok: Kaitor nono nokhani pantwi rwui, hapharno ha kaham khlai thung, bo nono mai tei angur bwtwi kwbáng khe rw thung!

English:
Let peoples serve you,
and nations bow down to you.
Be lord over your brothers,
and may your mother's sons bow down to you.
Cursed be every one who curses you,
and blessed be every one who blesses you!”
Kokborok: Dopharok nini seleng wng thung, borokrokno nono ma khulum thung, nini sandairokno tei nwmani bosongrok ma khulum thung. Nono swrainairok swraimung nang thung tei nono hamari rwnairok hamari rwjak thung!”

English: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, when Jacob had scarcely gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
Kokborok: Isak Jakobno hamari rwui pái kha tei bo bupha Isakni aroni tháng khe’-no bwta Esau mui romwi kiphil kha.

English: He also prepared savory food, and brought it to his father. And he said to his father, “Let my father arise, and eat of his son's game, that you may bless me.”
Kokborok: Bo’-bo chámung kothok khe sóngwi buphani thani tubu-ui phai kha. Bo buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Apha ano hamari rwui manna tẃi khe bachaui achugwi chá di ang nini bagwi muihan sóngwi tubu kha.”

English: His father Isaac said to him, “Who are you?” He answered, “I am your son, your first-born, Esau.”
Kokborok:
Bupha Isak bono hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng sabo?”
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwsla, okrasa Esau.”

English: Then Isaac trembled violently, and said, “Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him?—yes, and he shall be blessed.”
Kokborok: Isak belai khe bwsak kwlwijagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Hakhe nwng phai ya sini ani thani a mui tubu-ui ang chámani tei ang hamari ma rwmani abo sabo sla? I’n a hamari bini’-no wng phlainai.”

English: When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried out with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father, “Bless me, even me also, O my father!”
Kokborok: Esau buphani o kók khna kheno, chirikhógwi khá hamya wngwi belai khe kap kha tei buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Apha anobo hamari rw ja di!”

English: But he said, “Your brother came with guile, and he has taken away your blessing.”
Kokborok: Isak hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwphayung phaiwi ano bandi phai khe, bo nini hamarino tlangwi tháng kha.”

English: Esau said, “Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright; and behold, now he has taken away my blessing.” Then he said, “Have you not reserved a blessing for me?”
Kokborok: Esau hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bono gothok rwuino Jakob hwnwi mung pharma klai ya da? Bo ano omo bai khe uáinwi bandi kha. Bo ani okrani manthainobo sek kha; nai di tabuk khe ani hamarino naui pái kha.” Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha “Nwng ani banta hamari khatiui ton li ya da?”

English: Isaac answered Esau, “Behold, I have made him your lord, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants, and with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?”
Kokborok: Isak Esauno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ang bono nini nokphang snamwi pái kha, bini bebak takhuksongno bini seleng wngna rw kha, bono mai tei angur bwtwirok bebak rw bai kha, sajla ang nini bagwi tei tamo khlai man sinai?”

English: Esau said to his father, “Have you but one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, O my father.” And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
Kokborok: Esau buphano hai hwnwi sa phi kha, “Apha nini ba hamari kaisa’-no da tong? Apha, anobo hamari rw ja di.” Esau khorang kotor khe kap kha.

English:
Then Isaac his father answered him  
“Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall your dwelling be,
and away from the dew of heaven on high.
Kokborok:
Hakhe bupha Isak bono hai hwnwi sa kha  
“Nai di nini haphar kaham wng glak,
aro nokhani pantwibo klai glak.

English:
By your sword you shall live,
and you shall serve your brother;
but when you break loose
you shall break his yoke from your neck.”
Kokborok:
Nwng nini seng bai se ma chánai,
tei nwng nini nwphayungno ma monnai;
tamo khe nwng dundroksaui bini seleng
wngwi tongmani yakni yóknai.”

English: Now Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, “The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob.”
Kokborok: Hakhe tabuk bupha Isak Jakobno a hamari rwmani bagwi Esau Jakobno naislerjak kha tei bo bwkha bising-bising hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aphani thwinani sal sampha kha, aphuru khe angphayung Jakobno butharwi páinai.”

English: But the words of Esau her older son were told to Rebekah; so she sent and called Jacob her younger son, and said to him, “Behold, your brother Esau comforts himself by planning to kill you.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bwsla okra Esauni a kókrok Rebekano sajak kha; hakhe bo bwsla kusu Jakobno ring rw horwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di nwta Esau nono butharwi páinai hwnwi bwkha chabwi tong kha.

English: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice; arise, flee to Laban my brother in Haran,
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi sajla nwng tabuk ani kók khna di; nwng ani ata Labanni thani Haranno khárwi tháng di,

English: and stay with him a while, until your brother's fury turns away;
Kokborok: Nwtani thamchi kwchang khlai ya sak aro’-no tong gra di;

English: until your brother's anger turns away, and he forgets what you have done to him; then I will send, and fetch you from there. Why should I be bereft of you both in one day?”
Kokborok: nwta jolima pogwi tei nwng bono khlaimarokno pogwi tháng ya sak aro’-no tong gra di; ulo ang khoroksa swkno rohorwi nono kiphilwi tubu-ui phai anw. Tamo ang salsani salo’-no bwsa khoroknwirokno’-no kwmana klai lang?”

English: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, “I am weary of my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women such as these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Rebeka Isakno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Esau Hitini bwrwirokno káijakma a bihiksongrokni bagwi lengpro kha. Abo hai kheno Jakobbo khoroksa swk Hitini bwrwirok bai káijak khe ani langma-o tamo kaham wng sinai?”

English: Then Isaac called Jacob and blessed him, and charged him, “You shall not marry one of the Canaanite women.
Kokborok: Hakhe Isak Jakobno ringhorwi hamari rw kha tei hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng khoroksa Kananjwkno phano ta káijak di.

English: Arise, go to Paddan-aram to the house of Bethuel your mother's father; and take as wife from there one of the daughters of Laban your mother's brother.
Kokborok: Bachaui Padan-aramo nwmani bupha Bethuelni nogo tháng di; aroni’-no nwmani bwta Labanni bwsajwk khoroksa swkno káijagwi di.

English: God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, that you may become a company of peoples.
Kokborok: Kwplaisa Kaitornono lukurokni bodol wngwi manna tẃi khe tei dopha kwbángmani bupha wngna bagwi hamari rw thung tei bwsa kwbángma rw thung.

English: May he give the blessing of Abraham to you and to your descendants with you, that you may take possession of the land of your sojournings which God gave to Abraham!”
Kokborok: Kaitor Abrahamno rwmani ha nwng tongwi tongmani hano mannani hamari nono tei nini bosongno rw thung!”

English: Thus Isaac sent Jacob away; and he went to Paddan-aram to Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, Jacob's and Esau's mother.
Kokborok: Abo hai khe Isak Jakobno rwui rohor kha; bo Padan-aramni Labanni aro tháng kha, bo wng kha Bethuelni bwsla Aramsa, Jakob bai Esauni buma Rebekani bwta.

English: Now Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him away to Paddan-aram to take a wife from there, and that as he blessed him he charged him, “You shall not marry one of the Canaanite women,”
Kokborok: Esau nuk kha, Isak Jakobno hamari rwui Padan-aramo thángwi aroni bwrwino káijak di hwnwi sa phuru-o hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng Kananni khoroksa swk bwrwi bai phano ta káijak di,”

English: and that Jacob had obeyed his father and his mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
Kokborok: tei Jakob bo bupha bai bumani kók khnaui Padan-aramo tháng kha.

English: So when Esau saw that the Canaanite women did not please Isaac his father,
Kokborok: Hakhe Esau bupha Isak Kananjwkno khá phurjak ya nuk kha,

English: Esau went to Ishmael and took to wife, besides the wives he had, Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael Abraham's son, the sister of Nebaioth.
Kokborok: Esau Ismaelni thani thángwi bini bwrwino karwi tei khoroksa bwrwi na phi kha, bini tongma hikno karwi Abrahamni bwsla Ismaelni bwsajwk Mahalatno káijak kha, bo Nebaiotni bahanok.

English: Jacob left Beer-sheba, and went toward Haran.
Kokborok: Jakob Birsebani nongkhorwi Haran bwkhak lam rw kha.

English: And he came to a certain place, and stayed there that night, because the sun had set. Taking one of the stones of the place, he put it under his head and lay down in that place to sleep.
Kokborok: Bo kwrẃngsa-o phai sók phai khe sal hap kha, horo arono tong kha, bo aroni holongdap dulsa naui aro’-no kobongwi rokoui muktrwi tháng kha.

English: And he dreamed that there was a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven; and behold, the angels of God were ascending and descending on it!
Kokborok: Hakhe bo imango yakhili kaisa ha saka-o songchajak nuk kha, abo chuknoha-o nangwijak; hakhe nai di Kaitorni suarirok aboni saka-o kasa lai-o tei nongkhor lai-o khe tong lai-o!

English: And behold, the Lord stood above it and said, “I am the Lord, the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac; the land on which you lie I will give to you and to your descendants;
Kokborok: Hakhe nai di Subraiyung aboni saka-o bachajak khe hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwpha Abrahamni Subraiyung Kaitor tei Isakni Kaitor; nwng rokoui tongmani o hano ang nono tei nini bosongrokno rwnai;

English: and your descendants shall be like the dust of the earth, and you shall spread abroad to the west and to the east and to the north and to the south; and by you and your descendants shall all the families of the earth bless themselves.
Kokborok: tei nini bosongrok ha sakani hadlwi hai khe bángnai tei nini bosongrok salkabai, salthángbai tei salgrabai salsibai bwkhak bórokni hano phuarnai. Hakhe nini bising tẃi’-no nini bosongrokni bising tẃi khe ha sakani bebak dopharokno hamari rwnai.

English: Behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land; for I will not leave you until I have done that of which I have spoken to you.”
Kokborok: Nai di, ang nwng bai tongo nwng tháng thani’-no ang nono hamyani kati rwui tonnai. Ang nono o ha-ono ha-o kiphilwi tubu phinai. Ang nono samarok khlai pái yasak’-no ang nono yakar glak.”

English: Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, “Surely the Lord is in this place; and I did not know it.”
Kokborok: Jakob thumani sichaui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kubui’-no Subraiyung oro tongo; ang omono saui man li ya.”

English: And he was afraid, and said, “How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo kirijagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo bwswk kirimasingcha! Omo Kaitorni nok wng ya wng glak; omono chuknohani duglam wng kha.”

English: So Jacob rose early in the morning, and he took the stone which he had put under his head and set it up for a pillar and poured oil on the top of it.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob teini phung aidrobo bacha kha tei bo kobongmani a holongno thongla kuchuk snamwi aboni saka-o thok lu-ui rw kha.

English: He called the name of that place Bethel; but the name of the city was Luz at the first.
Kokborok: Tei bo aboni mung “Bethel” hwnwi phar kha; tamo khe swkango a kamiyungni mung Luj se tongmani.

English: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, “If God will be with me, and will keep me in this way that I go, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,
Kokborok: Jakob Subraiyungni mungwi swmai tangwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tumung ang ani lama tháng thani-o Kaitor ang bai baksa tongwi ano hamyani kati rwui tong khe tei kanmung, chámungrok rw khe,

English: so that I come again to my father's house in peace, then the Lord shall be my God,
Kokborok: phiya ang kaham kwrwng khe aphani nogo kiphilwi man khe nwng ani Kaitor wngnai,

English: and this stone, which I have set up for a pillar, shall be God's house; and of all that thou givest me I will give the tenth to thee.”
Kokborok: tei o holong thongla ang tisama Kaitorni nok wngnai; tei nwng ano rwmarokni bebakni’-no, ang chi-o kaisa rwnai.”

English: Then Jacob went on his journey, and came to the land of the people of the east.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob thángte thángte salkabaini borokrok tongmani ha-o sók phai kha.

English: As he looked, he saw a well in the field, and lo, three flocks of sheep lying beside it; for out of that well the flocks were watered. The stone on the well's mouth was large,
Kokborok: Bo aro hapharo twikhor khorsa nuk kha, abono kiting laiwi mera bodol kwthamrok roko laijak; aroni’-no merarokno twi khanjago. Twikhor khe holong bai khe kholopjak,

English: and when all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the mouth of the well, and water the sheep, and put the stone back in its place upon the mouth of the well.
Kokborok: bebak mera bodolrok aro sók phai khe mera mwrwknairok o holongno norwi merarokno twi khano; aboni ulo khe abono kholobwi ton phiro.

English: Jacob said to them, “My brothers, where do you come from?” They said, “We are from Haran.”
Kokborok:
Jakob mera mwrwknairokno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Takhukrok norok buroni wng bai kha?”
Hakhe bórok “Chwng Haranni se” hwnwi sa kha.

English: He said to them, “Do you know Laban the son of Nahor?” They said, “We know him.”
Kokborok:
Bo bórokno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Norok Nahorni bwsla Labanno si da sini?”
Hakhe bórok “I’n chwng sini-o” hwnwi sa lai kha.

English: He said to them, “Is it well with him?” They said, “It is well; and see, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep!”
Kokborok:
Bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha,
“Bo kaham kwrwngda tong?” Bórok sa kha, “I’n bo kaham kwrwngno tongo; iko naisik di bwsajwk Rahel merarok bai baksa phaiwi tongo!”

English: He said, “Behold, it is still high day, it is not time for the animals to be gathered together; water the sheep, and go, pasture them.”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di tabukbo sal kolokma tong kho, tabuk merarokno thumna jora wng ya kho; merarokno twi khanwi mwrwgwi phi di.”

English: But they said, “We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together, and the stone is rolled from the mouth of the well; then we water the sheep.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Mera bodolrok bebakno kwrẃngsa khe kuthum khe se chwng twikhor kholopma holongno noro; hai khe se merarokno twi khano.”

English: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with her father's sheep; for she kept them.
Kokborok: Bo bórok bai kók sa laiwi tong phuru Rahel buphani merarokno tubu-ui phai kha; tamni hwn ba bo buphani merarokno mwrwgo.

English: Now when Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother's brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother's brother, Jacob went up and rolled the stone from the well's mouth, and watered the flock of Laban his mother's brother.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakob bini bumani takhuk Labanni bwsajwk Rahelno nuk khe, bo bumani takhuk Labanni merarokno tubu-ui phai kha, hakhe Jakob thángwi a twikhorni kholopma holongno norwi bumani takhuk Labanni merarokno twi khan kha.

English: Then Jacob kissed Rachel, and wept aloud.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Rahelno motom su-ui khorang kotor khe kap kha.

English: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's kinsman, and that he was Rebekah's son; and she ran and told her father.
Kokborok: Jakob Rahelno bo bini buphani jaiti, Rebekani bwsla se hwnwi sa kha; hakhe bo khachigwi thángwi buphano saui kha.

English: When Laban heard the tidings of Jacob his sister's son, he ran to meet him, and embraced him and kissed him, and brought him to his house. Jacob told Laban all these things,
Kokborok: Laban bini bukhukni bwsla Jakobni kókno khna kheno bo bo bai malaina khachigwi tháng kha, hakhe malaiwi kwbagwi motom su kha tei nogo tubu-ui phai kha. Jakob Labanno bebak kókrokno sa bai khe,

English: and Laban said to him, “Surely you are my bone and my flesh!” And he stayed with him a month.
Kokborok: Laban bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng sói ani bahan-bwthẃi se!” Hakhe bo bo bai talsa tong kha.

English: Then Laban said to Jacob, “Because you are my kinsman, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Laban Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ani sandai ba, nwng ano hai’-no da monthai klai ba? Sa di nini mula bwswk wngnai?”

English: Now Laban had two daughters; the name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
Kokborok: Labanni bwsajwksong khoroknwi tongo; okrani mung khe Lea tei kusuni mung khe Rahel.

English: Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful and lovely.
Kokborok: Leani khe mokol naithok ya tamo khe Rahel khe sakduk tei bwrwibo naithok.

English: Jacob loved Rachel; and he said, “I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.”
Kokborok: Jakob khe Rahelno hamjago; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwsajwk kusu Rahelni bagwi nokha bisi sni nono monwi tongnai.”

English: Laban said, “It is better that I give her to you than that I should give her to any other man; stay with me.”
Kokborok: Hakhe, Laban hai hwnwi sa kha “Kubun chwlano rwmani slái nono rwmani’-no ham kugo; ang bai’-no tong di.”

English: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Jakob nokha bisi sni Rahelni bagwi monwi tong kha, bo bono aswk hamjakmani bagwi aswk salno sal kisicha se wngjak si-o.

English: Then Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife that I may go in to her, for my time is completed.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Labanno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani bwrwino ano rw si di, ang bono tẃiwi tong sinai, ang kók khamani jora pái kha.”

English: So Laban gathered together all the men of the place, and made a feast.
Kokborok: Hakhe Laban aroni bebak borokrokno thum kha tei chámung panda kaisa khlai kha.

English: But in the evening he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob; and he went in to her.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Laban sal tháng khe Leano Jakobni thani tubu-ui rw phai kha; tei Jakob bo bai thu kha.

English: (Laban gave his maid Zilpah to his daughter Leah to be her maid.)
Kokborok: (Laban Jilpano bwsajwk Leani selengjwk wngna bagwi rw kha.)

English: And in the morning, behold, it was Leah; and Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?”
Kokborok: Phung ai khe nuk kha abo Lea se; aphuru khe Jakob Labanno sa kha, “Nwng omo ano tamo khlai lang? Nwng tamo ano bandi lang? ang Rahelni bagwi monma ya diba?”

English: Laban said, “It is not so done in our country, to give the younger before the first-born.
Kokborok: Laban sa kha, “Chini o ha-o okrano kái yaui kusuno káiwi man ya.

English: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years.”
Kokborok: Omo bai nokha salsni pung gra di, ano tei nokha bisisni mon khe ang nono tei khoroksanobo rwnai.”

English: Jacob did so, and completed her week; then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel to wife.
Kokborok: Jakobbo ahai’-no khlai kha, bini salsnino supung khe, Laban bono bwsajwk Rahelno kái rw kha.

English: (Laban gave his maid Bilhah to his daughter Rachel to be her maid.)
Kokborok: (Laban Bilhano bwsajwk Rahelni bagwi selengjwk rw kha.)

English: So Jacob went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and served Laban for another seven years.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Rahel baibo thu kha, bo Rahelno Leani slái hamjak kuk kha, phiya Labanno tei nokha bisi sni monwi tong kha.

English: When the Lord saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.
Kokborok: Subraiyung Leano norjagwi tongma nuk kha, hakhe Bo bono bwsa man rw kha. Rahel khe banji wng kha.

English: And Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben; for she said, “Because the Lord has looked upon my affliction; surely now my husband will love me.”
Kokborok: Lea sakderiya wngwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá kha tei bo bono Ruben hwnwi mung phar kha; tamni hwn ba Lea sa kha, “Subraiyung ang sátokjakmano nuk kha; tabuk le ani angsai sói khe ano hamjaknai.”

English: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Because the Lord has heard that I am hated, he has given me this son also”; and she called his name Simeon.
Kokborok: Bo hai’-no hai sakderiya wngwi tei khoroksa chwla bwsa bá khua; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang norjakmano Subraiyung khna kha, bo ano o bwsanobo rw kha”; tei bo bini mung Simion hwnwi phar kha.

English: Again she conceived and bore a son, and said, “Now this time my husband will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons”; therefore his name was called Levi.
Kokborok: Haino hai bo sakderiya wngwi tei khoroksa chwla bwsa bá phi kha; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tabuk le ani angsai ang bai tong sinai, tamni hwn ba ang bini bagwi chwla bwsa khoroktham bá kha”; aboni bagwi bini mung Lebi wngnai.

English: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, “This time I will praise the Lord”; therefore she called his name Judah; then she ceased bearing.
Kokborok: Haino hai bo sakderiya wngwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá phi kha, hakhe hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tabuk le ang Subraiyungno lob sinai”, aboni bagwi bo bono Juda hwnwi mung phar kha; hakhe bo bwsa thak kha.

English: When Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, she envied her sister; and she said to Jacob, “Give me children, or I shall die!”
Kokborok: Rahel Jakobni bagwi bwsa man li ya wngwi bibino músú kha; tei Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ano bwsa rw di, wng ya khe ang thwi sinai!”

English: Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel, and he said, “Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”
Kokborok: Jakob Rahelno jolijagwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang ba Kaitor da tamo, nono bwsa man ya khlainai?”

English: Then she said, “Here is my maid Bilhah; go in to her, that she may bear upon my knees, and even I may have children through her.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Iko ani selengjwk Bilha bini thani tháng di, bo ani bagwi bwsa man khe ang buma wngwi mannai.”

English: So she gave him her maid Bilhah as a wife; and Jacob went in to her.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bini selengjwk Bilhano hik songna bagwi rw kha tei Jakob bo bai thu kha chá kha.

English: And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
Kokborok: Bilha sakderiya wngwi Jakobni bagwi sajla khoroksa bá kha.

English: Then Rachel said, “God has judged me, and has also heard my voice and given me a son”; therefore she called his name Dan.
Kokborok: Hakhe Rahel hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ano sói khe buj khlai kha tei ani khorang khnaui ano sajla khoroksa rw kha”; Hakhe bo bono Dan hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Rachel's maid Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son.
Kokborok: Rahelni selengjwk sakderiya wngwi Jakobni bagwi tei khoroksa bwsa bá phi kha.

English: Then Rachel said, “With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister, and have prevailed”; so she called his name Naphtali.
Kokborok: Hakhe Rahel hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang belai kwrak khe ani bukhuk bai ma nang lai kha tei ang pái kha”; hakhe bo bono Naptali hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: When Leah saw that she had ceased bearing children, she took her maid Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as a wife.
Kokborok: Lea bwsa thak kha hwnwi saui man kheno bo bini selengjwk Jilpano bwsai Jakobno hik song di hwnwi rw kha.

English: Then Leah's maid Zilpah bore Jacob a son.
Kokborok: Hakhe Leani selengjwk Jilpa Jakobni bagwi chwla bwsa khoroksa man kha.

English: And Leah said, “Good fortune!” so she called his name Gad.
Kokborok: Tei Lea hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani talikha kaham!” hakhe bo bono Gad hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Leah's maid Zilpah bore Jacob a second son.
Kokborok: Leani selengjwk Jilpa tei khoroksa chwla bwsa bá phi kha.

English: And Leah said, “Happy am I! For the women will call me happy”; so she called his name Asher.
Kokborok: Lea hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang bwkha tongthok kha! tamni hwn ba bwrwirok ano tongthokjak hwnnai”; hakhe bo bono Aser hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: In the days of wheat harvest Reuben went and found mandrakes in the field, and brought them to his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah, “Give me, I pray, some of your son's mandrakes.”
Kokborok: Gom ramani jora-o Ruben gom bwlwngo thángwi bo Duaim manẃi buma Leani thani tubu-ui rw phai kha. Rahel Leano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwslani Duaimno ano kisa rw ja di.”

English: But she said to her, “Is it a small matter that you have taken away my husband? Would you take away my son's mandrakes also?” Rachel said, “Then he may lie with you tonight for your son's mandrakes.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Lea hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ani bwsaino na kha, omoba kisa-misani kók diba? Tabuk nwng ani sajlani Duaimnobo segwi naui páinai hai da?” Rahel hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nwslani Duaimno ano rw khe, nwng tini horo Jakob bai thu-ui mano.”

English: When Jacob came from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him, and said, “You must come in to me; for I have hired you with my son's mandrakes.” So he lay with her that night.
Kokborok: Jakob sarigo habani phai khe’-no Lea thángwi bo bai malaiwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tini horo ang bai se ma thu phainai, tamni hwn khe ang nono ani sajlani Duaim bai paiwi na kha,” hakhe bo a horo bo bai thu kha.

English: And God hearkened to Leah, and she conceived and bore Jacob a fifth son.
Kokborok: Kaitor Leani Surimano khna kha, hakhe bo sakderiya wngwi Jakobni bagwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá kha tei abo bai bwsa khorokba wng kha.

English: Leah said, “God has given me my hire because I gave my maid to my husband”; so she called his name Issachar.
Kokborok: Lea hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ani manthai rw kha, tamni hwn ba ang ani selengjwkno ani bwsaino rw kha”; hakhe bo bono Isakar hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: And Leah conceived again, and she bore Jacob a sixth son.
Kokborok: Lea sakderiya wngwi tei khoroksa chwla bwsa bá-u kha, abo khorokdok klai-o.

English: Then Leah said, “God has endowed me with a good dowry; now my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons”; so she called his name Zebulun.
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ano hili kaham rw kha, tabuk angsai ano bórom khlai sinai, tamni hwn khe ang bini bagwi chwla bwsa khorokdok bá kha”; hakhe bo bono Jebulun hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Afterwards she bore a daughter, and called her name Dinah.
Kokborok: Ulo khe bo tei khoroksa bwsa bwrwi bá kha bini mung khe Dina hwnwi phar kha.

English: Then God remembered Rachel, and God hearkened to her and opened her womb.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor Rahelno muitu khlai kha tei Kaitor bo ringmano khnaui bini khum kiyok rw kha.

English: She conceived and bore a son, and said, “God has taken away my reproach”;
Kokborok: Bo sakderiya wngwi chwla bwsa khoroksa bá kha tei hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ani lachimungno norwi pái kha.”

English: and she called his name Joseph, saying, “May the Lord add to me another son!”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bono Josep hwnwi mung phar kha tei hai hwnwi sa kha “Subraiyung ano tei khoroksa chwla bwsa rw ja thung!”

English: When Rachel had borne Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, “Send me away, that I may go to my own home and country.
Kokborok: Rahel Josepno bámani ulo khe, Jakob Labanno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani sakni ha-o, sakni nogo thángna bagwi ano yakarwi rwui rohor si di.

English: Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, and let me go; for you know the service which I have given you.”
Kokborok: Ani hiksongno tei ani bwsarokno ano rw si di, ang bórokni bagwi se nini oro samung tangwi tongo, ang tháng sinai. Nwng saui mano ang bwswk khe nono mon kha.”

English: But Laban said to him, “If you will allow me to say so, I have learned by divination that the Lord has blessed me because of you;
Kokborok: Tamo khe Laban bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ano sa di hwn khe ang diyari ka rwui saui man kha Subraiyung ano nini bagwi hamari rw kha;

English: name your wages, and I will give it.”
Kokborok: nini dormai bwswk ano sa di ang abono rwui rwnai.”

English: Jacob said to him, “You yourself know how I have served you, and how your cattle have fared with me.
Kokborok: Jakob bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng sak’-no si-o ang nono bwswk khe mon kha tei nini mal-matarok ang mwrwk phuru er kha abo nwng’-no si-o.

English: For you had little before I came, and it has increased abundantly; and the Lord has blessed you wherever I turned. But now when shall I provide for my own household also?”
Kokborok: Swkango ang phai ya sini nini kisicha se tongmani ang phaiwi se nini abórok aswk kwbáng khe ero, ang tháng thani’-no Subraiyung nono hamari rw kha. Hakhe buphuru ba ang ani núkhungni bagwi khati sinai?”

English: He said, “What shall I give you?” Jacob said, “You shall not give me anything; if you will do this for me, I will again feed your flock and keep it:
Kokborok:
Bo hai hwnwi sa kha “Ang nono tamo rwnai?”
Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ano mungsa phano rwna nang glak; tumung nwng ani o kók bai lon khe ang mungsabo na ya, ang nini merarokno mwrwk phinai:

English: let me pass through all your flock today, removing from it every speckled and spotted sheep and every black lamb, and the spotted and speckled among the goats; and such shall be my wages.
Kokborok: Tini ano nini mal-matarokni kwchar tẃi him rw di tei ang bebak mera kosomrokno tei merarokno, punroknibo bebak merengrokno tei mari gwnangrokno nanai. Abórok ani dormai wng si thung.

English: So my honesty will answer for me later, when you come to look into my wages with you. Every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats and black among the lambs, if found with me, shall be counted stolen.”
Kokborok: Ang sói da sói ya nwng ani manthaino phaiwi nai phai khe’-no nuknai. Mereng ya tei mari gwnang punrok tei mera kosomrokno phaiwi ani oro nuk phai khe ano khok kha hwnwi nwng rom si di.”

English: Laban said, “Good! Let it be as you have said.”
Kokborok: Laban hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang soi-o, nwng sama hai’-no wng thung.”

English: But that day Laban removed the he-goats that were striped and spotted, and all the she-goats that were speckled and spotted, every one that had white on it, and every lamb that was black, and put them in charge of his sons;
Kokborok: Tamo khe Laban a salo’-no bebak punjua mereng tei brang brang mari gwnang tei bebak puma mereng mari gwnangrokno tei kuphur gwnangrokno nor kha, bo mera kosomroknobo nor kha; bo bwslasongno abórokni barja ber kha;

English: and he set a distance of three days' journey between himself and Jacob; and Jacob fed the rest of Laban's flock.
Kokborok: aboni ulo khe a mal-matarokno tẃiwi salthamni lama hakchalo thángwi Jakobni aroni tháng kha. Jakob Labanni tei tongma merarokno mwrwk kha.

English: Then Jacob took fresh rods of poplar and almond and plane, and peeled white streaks in them, exposing the white of the rods.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakob poplarni almon tei plan buphangrokni bedek kwthang naui abórokno bukur khugwi latha kuphur khlai kha tei belengtwi kuphur kari kha tei.

English: He set the rods which he had peeled in front of the flocks in the runnels, that is, the watering troughs, where the flocks came to drink. And since they bred when they came to drink,
Kokborok: Bo a bukur khukma dangrokno a merarokno twi khan rwma jidoloni twilamo khe ton kha, ayang tẃi twi kochokkhlaiwi phai-o. Bórok aro twi nung laina kuthum lai phai phuru khe merarok ogo bwsa wngo,

English: the flocks bred in front of the rods and so the flocks brought forth striped, speckled, and spotted.
Kokborok: hakhe a dangrokni bwskango ogo chunairokni bwsarok achai khe brang brang mari metrengrok tei merengrok bá o.

English: And Jacob separated the lambs, and set the faces of the flocks toward the striped and all the black in the flock of Laban; and he put his own droves apart, and did not put them with Laban's flock.
Kokborok: Ulo Jakob a mera bwsarokno khagwi tono tei pun mera bodolrokno Labanni mereng tei kosom mera punrokni phaising; tei bo saknino khe Labanni bodol bai ton yaui khagwi tono.

English: Whenever the stronger of the flock were breeding Jacob laid the rods in the runnels before the eyes of the flock, that they might breed among the rods,
Kokborok: Mera bodolni raktho raktho merarok ogo chu laima jora-o khe mera bodolrokni bwskango khe a dangrokno twilamni búkhúgo khe tono,

English: but for the feebler of the flock he did not lay them there; so the feebler were Laban's, and the stronger Jacob's.
Kokborok: tamo khe mera bodolrokni kebel kebel merarok ogo chu laima jora-o khe abono aro ton ya; hakhe raktho rakthorok khe merarok khe Jakobni tei kebel kebelrok khe Labanni wngo.

English: Thus the man grew exceedingly rich, and had large flocks, maidservants and menservants, and camels and asses.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob belai khe cha sa khe, bini mal-matarok selengrok, selengjwkrok, mangingangrok korai chirkarok kwbángma wng kha.

English: Now Jacob heard that the sons of Laban were saying, “Jacob has taken all that was our father's; and from what was our father's he has gained all this wealth.”
Kokborok: “Jakob chini aphani bebakno na bai kha; bini rang rírok bebakno chini aphani aroni máng se.” hwnwi Labanni bwslasong sa laiwi tongmano Jakob khna kha.

English: And Jacob saw that Laban did not regard him with favor as before.
Kokborok: Jakob naiwi nai ba Laban bono swkango bono nuk tẃi khe nuk li ya.

English: Then the Lord said to Jacob, “Return to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nini nwphani tei nini sandai masarokni ha-o kiphilwi tháng di. Ang nwng bai baksa tongnai.”

English: So Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah into the field where his flock was,
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Rahel bai Leano bini merarok tongmani a hapharo phai di hwnwi ring rw rohor kha tei

English: and said to them, “I see that your father does not regard me with favor as he did before. But the God of my father has been with me.
Kokborok: bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwpha swkango ang bai tongma tẃi khe tei tabuk tong li ya; tamo khe ani aphani Kaitor le ang bai’-no tongwi phai-o.

English: You know that I have served your father with all my strength;
Kokborok: Norok khoroknwi’-no si-o, ang nwphani bagwi bebak phan rwui samung tangwi phai kha;

English: yet your father has cheated me and changed my wages ten times, but God did not permit him to harm me.
Kokborok: aile bo khe ano bandi kha tei uáichi ani dormaino slái kha, tamo khe Kaitor bono ani mungsa hamya khlai rw li ya.

English: If he said, ‘The spotted shall be your wages,’ then all the flock bore spotted; and if he said, ‘The striped shall be your wages,’ then all the flock bore striped.
Kokborok: Bo ‘A merengrok nini manthai wngnai’ hwn khe bebakno mari gwnang máng bá kha, phiya ‘Brang brang bumul gwnangrok nini manthai wngnai’ hwn khe bebakno brang brang bumul gwnang mera bwsa bá kha.

English: Thus God has taken away the cattle of your father, and given them to me.
Kokborok: Omo hai kheno Kaitor nwphani pun merarokno naui ano rwui rw kha.

English: In the mating season of the flock I lifted up my eyes, and saw in a dream that the he-goats which leaped upon the flock were striped, spotted, and mottled.
Kokborok: Pun merarok ogo bwsa wng laima jora-o, ang imang kaisa nuk kha, punjuarok, pun-merajwkrokni saka-o kasanairok bebakno merengrok, metrengrok, brang brang marirok gwnang máng.

English: Then the angel of God said to me in the dream, ‘Jacob,’ and I said, ‘Here I am!’
Kokborok: Kaitorni suari imango ang bai nukjagwi ‘Jakob’ hwnwi ring khe ang ‘I’n ang orono!’ hwnwi sa kha,

English: And he said, ‘Lift up your eyes and see, all the goats that leap upon the flock are striped, spotted, and mottled; for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you.
Kokborok: hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha ‘Khorok tisaui naisik di, bebak pun mera bodolni pun merajwkrokni saka kanai punjua merarok bebakno metrengrok, brang brang mari gwnangrok máng. Tamni hwn ba ang Laban nono bebak khlaimarokno nuk kha.

English: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and made a vow to me. Now arise, go forth from this land, and return to the land of your birth.’ ”
Kokborok: Ang wng kha Bethelni Kaitor, nwng holongni thongla káiwi thok lu-ui ani thani swmai tangma a Bethelo nwng bai nukjaknai a Kaitor. Hakhe tabuk nwng bachaui o hani nongkhorwi nini achaima ha-o kiphilwi tháng si di.’ ”

English: Then Rachel and Leah answered him, “Is there any portion or inheritance left to us in our father's house?
Kokborok: Hakhe Rahel bai Lea bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chini aphani nogo chini bagwi aro mungsa phano mannani kwrwi kha da?

English: Are we not regarded by him as foreigners? For he has sold us, and he has been using up the money given for us.
Kokborok: Chwng bo bai buini hani borokrok hai khe se tonjago ahai ya diba? Bo chwngno phalwi rw kha, chwngno phalwi rang manma bebak rangrokbo swbaiwi tong kha.

English: All the property which God has taken away from our father belongs to us and to our children; now then, whatever God has said to you, do.”
Kokborok: Kaitor chini aphani aroni tlangwi thángma rangrok bebakno chini tei chini bwsarokni máng se; hakhe tabuk Kaitor nono samani tẃi’-no khw se khlai si di.”

English: So Jacob arose, and set his sons and his wives on camels;
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob tiyar wngwi bwsarokno tei bihiksongno mangingang saka-o kasa rw bai kha;

English: and he drove away all his cattle, all his livestock which he had gained, the cattle in his possession which he had acquired in Paddan-aram, to go to the land of Canaan to his father Isaac.
Kokborok: tei bo bini tong kwrwi pun-merarok mal-matarokno bebakno bo Padan-aramo yago chopma mal-matarokno béngkhlaiwi bupha Isakni Kanan ha-o thángna bagwi lam rw kha.

English: Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel stole her father's household gods.
Kokborok: Laban khe bini merarokni buikhumu rana bagwi thángo, aphuru khe Rahel buphani núkhungni mwtairokno khok kha.

English: And Jacob outwitted Laban the Aramean, in that he did not tell him that he intended to flee.
Kokborok: Jakob, Aramiasa Labanno bo khárna naimano si rw li ya.

English: He fled with all that he had, and arose and crossed the Euphrates, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.
Kokborok: Bo bini tong kwrwi tẃi naui khár lang kha tei Uphratis twimano bárwi hachuk phaisingni ha Giliad bwkhak lam rw kha.

English: When it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob had fled,
Kokborok: Nokha salthamni ulo khe Jakob khár kha hwnwi Laban sajak kha.

English: he took his kinsmen with him and pursued him for seven days and followed close after him into the hill country of Gilead.
Kokborok: Laban bini borokrokno tẃiwi salsni romwi Jakobno hathái ha Giliad jora thángwi bono slap tẃi slap tẃi wngwi kha.

English: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night, and said to him, “Take heed that you say not a word to Jacob, either good or bad.”
Kokborok: A horo Kaitor Aramiasa Labanni thani phaiwi bo bai imango nukjagwi bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Khna di, nwng Jakobno tháisa phano kaham hamya ta sa di.”

English: And Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsmen encamped in the hill country of Gilead.
Kokborok: Hakhe Laban Jakobno slabwi kha. Aphuru Jakob khe hachuk saka-o bini rí nok swngwi tong kha, hakhe Labanbo hachuk saka-o bini borokrok bai khe aro sók phai kha.

English: And Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done, that you have cheated me, and carried away my daughters like captives of the sword?
Kokborok: Laban Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tamoni bagwi ano bandi lang tei ani sajwksongno choba-o romwi tlangjaknairok hai khe tubu-ui phai lang?

English: Why did you flee secretly, and cheat me, and did not tell me, so that I might have sent you away with mirth and songs, with tambourine and lyre?
Kokborok: Nwng ba tamo ano sa yaui sereksa khe bandiui ma khár lang? Ang ba bwkha srangwi’-no, rwchapma bai kham bai khum bai khe nono rohorwi man kha mun ya diba?

English: And why did you not permit me to kiss my sons and my daughters farewell? Now you have done foolishly.
Kokborok: Nwng ano tamoni angsukrokno tei sajwksongno motom su rwui nairwgwi rohorna rw li ya wng lang? Obo le nwng butuani samung tang kha.

English: It is in my power to do you harm; but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Take heed that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad.’
Kokborok: Nono dera rwnani ani phan tongo; tamo khe nwphani Kaitor ano miya horo nono ‘Mungsa kaham phano eba hamya phano ta sa di’ hwnwi sa kha.

English: And now you have gone away because you longed greatly for your father's house, but why did you steal my gods?”
Kokborok: Ang saui mano nwng nwphani hano belai khe khátangjak kha, phiya ba nwng tamo ani nokni mwtairoknoba ma khok lang?”

English: Jacob answered Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I thought that you would take your daughters from me by force.
Kokborok: Jakob Labanno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nwsajwksongno ani aroni segwi na anw hwnwi khákaui ang kiri kha.

English: Any one with whom you find your gods shall not live. In the presence of our kinsmen point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
Kokborok: Tamo khe nwng oro tongnairokni bere khoroksa swkni aro nini mwtairokno man khe bono butharwi páijaknai. Oro bebak ani borokrokni bwskango, oro nini mungsa swk da tong nai di, tong khe abono naui pái di.” Tamo khe Rahel Labanni núkhungni mwtairokno khokma khe Jakob saui man ya.

English: So Laban went into Jacob's tent, and into Leah's tent, and into the tent of the two maidservants, but he did not find them. And he went out of Leah's tent, and entered Rachel's.
Kokborok: Hakhe Laban Jakobni ríni nogo; Leani ríni nogo; tei khoroknwi selengjwkrokni ríni nogobo naitugwi kha, hai phano bo bini mwtairokno man li ya. Ulo khe Leani ríni nokni ongkhorwi Rahelni ríni nogo tháng kha.

English: Now Rachel had taken the household gods and put them in the camel's saddle, and sat upon them. Laban felt all about the tent, but did not find them.
Kokborok: Rahel núkhungni a mwtairokno khogwi mangingang saka-o achukthai snamjak rí bisingo huiwi a saka-o achugwi tong kha. Laban ríni bebak nogono naituk kha, tamo khe man li ya.

English: And she said to her father, “Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me.” So he searched, but did not find the household gods.
Kokborok: Rahel buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ano ta joli di, tamni hwn ba ang nini bwskango bachaui man glak kho, ang sak tangwi man ya wngwi tongo.” Hakhe bo naituk phano bini núkhungni mwtairokno man li ya.

English: Then Jacob became angry, and upbraided Laban; Jacob said to Laban, “What is my offense? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob jolijak kha tei Laban bai uálai kha; aphuru khe Jakob Labanno hai hwnwi hwn kha, “Ang tamo samung hamya tang kha ba? Bubo tẃi cha yani bagwi aswk joliui ano ma riphrigwi phai lang ba?

English: Although you have felt through all my goods, what have you found of all your household goods? Set it here before my kinsmen and your kinsmen, that they may decide between us two.
Kokborok: Nwng tabuk ani nok gwnangwini bebak manwirogo naitugwi naiwi nini nokni tamo manwino man lang ba? Tabuk omono nini borok bai ani borokrokni bwskango ton si di, bórokno saboni cha cha ya chong si thung.

English: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and your she-goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams of your flocks.
Kokborok: O nokha bisik nwichi ang nwng bai se tongo; nini merarok tei punjwkrokni bwsabo chokhrai ya tei angbo nini mera bodolni mera masabo chá ya.

English: That which was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it myself; of my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
Kokborok: Bolongni yabrwirok bai mera masa swk phano butharjak khe, ang abono nini thani tubu ya; tamo khe ang se baithang abono nono su-ui rw-o. Salo da khokjak horo da khokjak nwng abórokno ani thani kiphilwi na-o.

English: Thus I was; by day the heat consumed me, and the cold by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.
Kokborok: Salo khe ang belai satung manjak kha tei hor kwbángma’-no kwchangma manjak kha, ang thu-ui man li ya.

English: These twenty years I have been in your house; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
Kokborok: O nokha bisik nwichi ang nini nogo máng se; nwsajwk khoroknwini bagwi ang nono nokha bisi chibrwi mon kha tei nokha bisi dok nini mal-matarokno mwrwk kha, nwng ani mulani rang uáichi slái kha.

English: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God saw my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.”
Kokborok: Ani aphani Kaitor tei Abraham bai Isakni Kaitor ang bai kwrwi hwn khe nwng bu masíngo’-no ano yak bukcha khe rwkhlaiwi rohor kha mun. Tamo khe Kaitor ani tong kesepno tei ani samung tangmano nugwi thángnai horo nono khorai kha.”

English: Then Laban answered and said to Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day to these my daughters, or to their children whom they have borne?
Kokborok: Laban Jakobno hai hwnwi kiphilwi sa kha, “O bwrwimasong ani sajwksong máng; bórokni bwsarokbo ani’-no, o mal-matarokbo ani mángno, oro nukmani bebakno ani máng se. Tamo khe tinini salo ang o ani sajwksongno eba bórok bámani bwsarokno tamo khlaiwi man sinai?

English: Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I; and let it be a witness between you and me.”
Kokborok: Phai di tabuk nwng bai ang kók khaui swmai tang sinai; abo nwng bai ani sakki wng si thung.”

English: So Jacob took a stone, and set it up as a pillar.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob holongdap kaisa naui thongla kái kha.

English: And Jacob said to his kinsmen, “Gather stones,” and they took stones, and made a heap; and they ate there by the heap.
Kokborok: Jakob bini sakni borokrokno “Holongrok thum di,” hwnwi sa khe bórok holong naui rúngwi chong chong khe tisa kha; tei bórok a holong burungni samo khe chá lai kha.

English: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.
Kokborok: Laban abono Jagar-Sahadutha hwnwi mung phar kha; tamo khe Jakob khe abono Galid hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Laban said, “This heap is a witness between you and me today.” Therefore he named it Galeed,
Kokborok: Laban hai hwnwi sa kha, “O holongni burung’-no tini nwng bai ang swmai tangmani mari.” Aboni bagwino bo abono Galid hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: and the pillar Mizpah, for he said, “The Lord watch between you and me, when we are absent one from the other.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo holong káimano khe Mijpa hwnwi hwn kha; tamni hwn ba “Subraiyung se nwng bai ang kak laima yagulo chwngno nainaisa.

English: If you ill-treat my daughters, or if you take wives besides my daughters, although no man is with us, remember, God is witness between you and me.”
Kokborok: Tumung nwng ani sajwksongno sátok khe eba ani sajwksong tong twtwi tei kubun bwrwirok na khe, muitu narwk di chwng bai tei kubun borok kwrwi phano, chwng khoroknwini bisingo Kaitor tongo.”

English: Then Laban said to Jacob, “See this heap and the pillar, which I have set between you and me.
Kokborok: Hakhe Laban Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini bai ani kwcharo o holong rúngjak bai thonglano naisik di.

English: This heap is a witness, and the pillar is a witness, that I will not pass over this heap to you, and you will not pass over this heap and this pillar to me, for harm.
Kokborok: O rúngjak holong burung bai thongla wng kha chini bagwi kók sachu rwnai, ang o holong burung tei thonglano láiwi nini ayang tháng glak tei nwng’-bo o holong rúngjak bai thonglano basu-ui hamya samungni bagwi ani yang phai man glak.

English: The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.” So Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac,
Kokborok: Abrahamni Kaitor, Nahorni Kaitor chwng khoroknwino buj thumnai.” Hakhe Jakob bupha Isak khulumma Kaitorni mungwi o swmaino narwknai hwnwi kwrak khe swmai tang kha,

English: and Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and called his kinsmen to eat bread; and they ate bread and tarried all night on the mountain.
Kokborok: hakhe Jakob mui masa ratharwi hathái-o barogwi bini borokrokno auang chánani kobok hor kha; bórok chá lai kha tei horo a hathái-o hopung tong lai kha.

English: Early in the morning Laban arose, and kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them; then he departed and returned home.
Kokborok: Teini phung aisirisiri Laban bacha kha tei thángwi busuksongno, bwsajwksongno motom su kha tei hamari rw kha; hakhe bo aroni lam rwui nogo kiphilwi phai kha.

English: Jacob went on his way and the angels of God met him;
Kokborok: Jakob lama tẃi thángwi tong phuru Kaitorni suarirok bo bai phaiwi malai phai kha;

English: and when Jacob saw them he said, “This is God's army!” So he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
Kokborok: Jakob bórokno nuk khe hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo Kaitorni sengkrak se!” Hakhe bo a jagani mung Mahanaim hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother in the land of Seir, the country of Edom,
Kokborok: A borokrok bini takhuk Esauno Idomni Seir ha-o hai hwnwi saui borok rwi rohor kha.

English: instructing them, “Thus you shall say to my lord Esau: Thus says your servant Jacob, ‘I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed until now;
Kokborok: Norok ani nokphang Esauno hai hwnwi sa di, “Nini seleng Jakob omo hai sa-o; ‘Ang buini ha-o Labanni aro tabuk jora tong kha;’

English: and I have oxen, asses, flocks, menservants, and maidservants; and I have sent to tell my lord, in order that I may find favor in your sight.’ ”
Kokborok: tei ani musuk chwlarok korai chirkarok, pun tokrok chwla tei bwrwi selengrok tongo. Ang nwng bai khairokjakna bagwi se borok rwi rohoro.”

English: And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, “We came to your brother Esau, and he is coming to meet you, and four hundred men with him.”
Kokborok: A borokrok kiphilwi phaiwi Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng nini takhuk Esauni thani tháng kha, bo nwng bai malaina bagwi phaiwi tongo. Bo bai baksa borok khorokbrwi-ra tongo.”

English: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed; and he divided the people that were with him, and the flocks and herds and camels, into two companies,
Kokborok: Jakob kiriui uanajak kha, hakhe bo bo bai baksa tongma borokrokno khaknwi khe swkak kha tei merarok, punrok mal-matarok tei mangingangroknobo haino swkak kha,

English: thinking, “If Esau comes to the one company and destroys it, then the company which is left will escape.”
Kokborok: bo hai hwnwi uansuk kha, “bodol kaisano swprak phaisani tei kaisa bodol khárwi mannai.”

English: And Jacob said, “O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, O Lord who didst say to me, ‘Return to your country and to your kindred, and I will do you good,’
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “O ani chuchu Abrahamni Kaitor tei apha Isakni Kaitor, ‘nini sakni ha-o tei sakni borokrokni thani kiphilwi tháng di, ang nini kaham khlainai’ hwnwi ano sanai Subraiyung;

English: I am not worthy of the least of all the steadfast love and all the faithfulness which thou hast shown to thy servant, for with only my staff I crossed this Jordan; and now I have become two companies.
Kokborok: ang le nini Khairokma tei sói khe naijakna chúk ya, nini seleng se, raidang kongsa simi yago khe nongkhorwi Jordan lái thángma se, tabuk ani bodol kwnwi wng kha.

English: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I fear him, lest he come and slay us all, the mothers with the children.
Kokborok: Ani takhuk Esauni yakni ano phiyók ja di hwnwi ang suri ja-o, tamni hwnma le bo chwngno swpragwi chwngno bebakno sokomorwi páinai tei bwrwi bwsa chwrai bwsarokno buda butharwi páilangno hwnwi, ang kiri-o.”

English: But thou didst say, ‘I will do you good, and make your descendants as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.’ ”
Kokborok: Tamo khe nwng, “Ang nini kaham khlainai tei nini bosong twijlangni hasing hai khe ernai, abono lekhaui pái rwi man glak” o kókbo sa kha.

English: So he lodged there that night, and took from what he had with him a present for his brother Esau,
Kokborok: A horo bo arono tong kha tei bini takhuk Esauni bagwi bini aro tongmarokni bisingni’-no,

English: two hundred she-goats and twenty he-goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
Kokborok: makranwi punjwk tei mak chinwi punjua, makranwi merajwk tei mak chinwi mera chwla,

English: thirty milch camels and their colts, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty she-asses and ten he-asses.
Kokborok: mak thamchi abuktwi rwnai mangingang tei abórokni bwsa, mak brwichi musuk bwrwi tei mak chi musuk chwla, mak nwichi korai chirka bwrwi tei mak chi korai chirka chwlano saiwi na kha.

English: These he delivered into the hand of his servants, every drove by itself, and said to his servants, “Pass on before me, and put a space between drove and drove.”
Kokborok: Bo abórokno gut gut khe khak kha, kaisa kaisa gutni bagwi khoroksa khoroksa selengno barja berwi rw kha. Bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani slái swkang swkang tháng lai di, kaisa gut bai tei kaisa gutno hakchal khe béng di.”

English: He instructed the foremost, “When Esau my brother meets you, and asks you, ‘To whom do you belong? Where are you going? And whose are these before you?’
Kokborok: Bo swkangni a selengno hai hwnwi dagiui sa kha, “Ani takhuk nwng bai malaiwi ‘Norokni nokphang sabo tei buro thángnai? Tei o mal-matarok saboni’ hwnwi sẃng khe

English: then you shall say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob; they are a present sent to my lord Esau; and moreover he is behind us.’ ”
Kokborok: nwng hai hwnwi sa di ‘omorok nini seleng Jakobni se; bo omorokno bini bugra Esauno se hili rohoro; tei bo chini uklogo khe phaiwi tongo.’ ”

English: He likewise instructed the second and the third and all who followed the droves, “You shall say the same thing to Esau when you meet him,
Kokborok: Aboni ulninobo abo hai’-no dagiui sa kha tei kubun mal-matarokni barja gwnangroknobo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo haino norok Esau bai malai khe ma sanai,

English: and you shall say, ‘Moreover your servant Jacob is behind us.’ ” For he thought, “I may appease him with the present that goes before me, and afterwards I shall see his face; perhaps he will accept me.”
Kokborok: haibo hwnwi sa di, ‘nini seleng Jakob chini uldrobo’-no phaiwi tongo.’ Tamni hwn ba bo uansuk kha, ‘Ang omo hilirok bai bini bwkhano methebwi mannai, bo ano yachagwi phano mano.’ ”

English: So the present passed on before him; and he himself lodged that night in the camp.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bini slái swkango hilirok rwui rohor kha tei a horo bo a bini ríni nogono tong rwk kha.

English: The same night he arose and took his two wives, his two maids, and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
Kokborok: A horo’-no Jakob bachaui bihik khoroknwino tei khoroknwi selengjwkrok phiya khorokchisa bwsarokno tẃiwi Jabok twimano bárwi tháng kha.

English: He took them and sent them across the stream, and likewise everything that he had.
Kokborok: Abórokno twi bár rwui rohor baiwi bo bini tong kwrwi tẃi bebakno rwui rohor kha.

English: And Jacob was left alone; and a man wrestled with him until the breaking of the day.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Jakob saichung uklogo tong rwk kha; hakhe borok khoroksa phaiwi bo bai aiyasak sólai phai kha.

English: When the man saw that he did not prevail against Jacob, he touched the hollow of his thigh; and Jacob's thigh was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.
Kokborok: A borok nai ba Jakobno mechenwi se man ya wngwi tong kha, hakhe bo bini yaphungni jorani bekrengo yak koson kha, hakhe sólaiwi tong phuru’-no Jakobni yaphungni jora báiwi tháng kha.

English: Then he said, “Let me go, for the day is breaking.” But Jacob said, “I will not let you go, unless you bless me.”
Kokborok:
Aphuru khe a borok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ano yakar di, sal pasaui tong kha.”
Hakhe Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ano hamari rw ya sak ang nono yakar ya.”

English: And he said to him, “What is your name?” And he said, “Jacob.”
Kokborok: Hakhe a borok “Nini mung tamo” hwnwi sẃng khe “Jakob” hwnwi bo sa kha.

English: Then he said, “Your name shall no more be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with men, and have prevailed.”
Kokborok: A borok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini mung tei Jakob wng glak kha, Nwng Kaitor bai tei borok bai choba khlaiwi mungnwio’-no pái kha, aboni bagwi nini mung Israil wngnai.”

English: Then Jacob asked him, “Tell me, I pray, your name.” But he said, “Why is it that you ask my name?” And there he blessed him.
Kokborok:
Hakhe Jakob bono hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nini mung le tamo ano sa ja di.”
Tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng tamo ani mung sinani muchung?” Hakhe bo Jakobno hamari rw kha.

English: So Jacob called the name of the place Peniel, saying, “For I have seen God face to face, and yet my life is preserved.”
Kokborok: “Ang dak-dak khe mokol baithang Kaitorno nugwibo ang kwthang tong kha” aboni bagwi Jakob abono Peniel hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: The sun rose upon him as he passed Penuel, limping because of his thigh.
Kokborok: Jakob Penuelni kutul khe’-no sal kasa kha tei bo bini yaphungni bagwi khorabe khorabe khe ma him kha.

English: Therefore to this day the Israelites do not eat the sinew of the hip which is upon the hollow of the thigh, because he touched the hollow of Jacob's thigh on the sinew of the hip.
Kokborok: Tabuk jorabo Israilrok yaphungni jorani bahanno chá ya; tamni hwn ba Jakob le a yaphung jora’-ono swprakjago.

English: And Jacob lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two maids.
Kokborok: Jakob naiwi nuk kha, Esau bini borok khorokrabrwirokno tẃiwi phaimani nuhur kha, hakhe bo bwsarokno, Lea, Rahel tei khoroknwi selengjwkrokno khaknwi khe khagwi rw kha.

English: And he put the maids with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
Kokborok: Bo selengjwkrok bai bórokni bwsarokno ton kha tei aboni ulo khe Lea bai bini bwsarokno tei uklogo khe Rahel bai Josep.

English: He himself went on before them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
Kokborok: Jakob khe bórok bebakni slái bwskango, bo bini takhukni bwskango sógwi khe’-no uáisni ha-o klaiwi botok khe khulum kha.

English: But Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Esau bo bai malaina bagwi khachigwi tháng kha tei kotogo kwbagwi motom su kha tei bórok kap lai kha.

English: And when Esau raised his eyes and saw the women and children, he said, “Who are these with you?” Jacob said, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”
Kokborok:
Esau yang yang naiwi nai khe bwrwirokno tei chwrairokno nuk khe hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng bai baksa o borokrok sabo wng bai kha?”
Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bórok khe, Kaitor khairogwi nini o selengno rwmani bwsarok se.”

English: Then the maids drew near, they and their children, and bowed down;
Kokborok: Hakhe selengjwkrok, bwsarok bai phaiwi bono khulum phai kha.

English: Leah likewise and her children drew near and bowed down; and last Joseph and Rachel drew near, and they bowed down.
Kokborok: Hakhe Lea bai bwsarok phaiwi khulum phai kha, bebakni ulo khe Jakob bai Rahel phaiwi khulum phai kha.

English: Esau said, “What do you mean by all this company which I met?” Jacob answered, “To find favor in the sight of my lord.”
Kokborok:
Esau hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Ang tei kubun bodolrok malaimarok le tamo? Abórokni gujok ba tamo wng lang?”
Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Abo nini bwkha manna bagwi se.”

English: But Esau said, “I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself.”
Kokborok: Esau hai hwnwi sa kha, “Takhuk, ani kwbángma’-no tongo, ninino nwng’-no na di.”

English: Jacob said, “No, I pray you, if I have found favor in your sight, then accept my present from my hand; for truly to see your face is like seeing the face of God, with such favor have you received me.
Kokborok: Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi, ang nono koi ja-o, nwng ano bwkha phurwi’-no tong khe nwng ang rwmani ani yakni hilino yachak ja di. Ang nini mwkhangno Kaitorni mwkhang hai khe nugo. Nwng ano tabuk belai kheno kaham khe na kha.

English: Accept, I pray you, my gift that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough.” Thus he urged him, and he took it.
Kokborok: Ang nini bagwi tubuma hilino yachak ja di; Kaitor ano khairogwi tongmani bai ani bebak nangmarokno Kaitor ano rw bai kha.” Bo yachak yasak’-no Jakob bono koi máng tong kha.

English: Then Esau said, “Let us journey on our way, and I will go before you.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Esau hai hwnwi sa kha, “Phai di tháng lai si anw, ang lam rw gra anw.”

English: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail, and that the flocks and herds giving suck are a care to me; and if they are overdriven for one day, all the flocks will die.
Kokborok: Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng saui mano chwrairok kebel máng, merarok, mal-matarok bórokni bwsa kwlwiroknobo ma uansugo, bórok salsa ma him khe bebakno thwi se pái bainai.

English: Let my lord pass on before his servant, and I will lead on slowly, according to the pace of the cattle which are before me and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.”
Kokborok: Nwng ani slái swkang tháng gra si di, ang twrwk twrwk khe phai anw. Ang mal-mata tei bwsarokno tẃiwi ang doui manma tẃi’-no doui phaiwi nono Seiro malai phai anw.”

English: So Esau said, “Let me leave with you some of the men who are with me.” But he said, “What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.”
Kokborok:
Esau hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani kisa borokno nwng bai klang anw.”
Tamo khe Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ano chajak khe se wng kha, abo ba tamo nangnai?”

English: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
Kokborok: Hakhe, Esau Salono bini lama Seir bwkhak tháng kha.

English: But Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house, and made booths for his cattle; therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
Kokborok: Jakob khe Sukoto tháng kha tei aro bini bagwi tei bini mal-matarokni bagwi nok tangwi kha. Aboni bagwino abono Sukot hwnwi mung pharjago.

English: And Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, on his way from Paddan-aram; and he camped before the city.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Padan-aramni phaiwi Kanan hani Sekemni kamiyungo kaham kwrwng khe sók phaiwi aro batham khlai kha.

English: And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, he bought for a hundred pieces of money the piece of land on which he had pitched his tent.
Kokborok: Sekemni bupha Hamorni bwsarokni aroni a hano rukphai khók sara rwui paiwi na kha.

English: There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
Kokborok: Bo aro háyá kaisa tisa kha tei abono El-Elohe-Israil hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Now Dinah the daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to visit the women of the land;
Kokborok: Salsa Jakob bai Leani bwsajwk Dina, a hani bwrwirok bai malaina bagwi tháng kha.

English: and when Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the land, saw her, he seized her and lay with her and humbled her.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Hamorni bwsla Sekem Hibisa, a hani bugrani bwsla bono nuk khe tlangwi thángwi bono romphreui thu kha.

English: And his soul was drawn to Dinah the daughter of Jacob; he loved the maiden and spoke tenderly to her.
Kokborok: Bo Jakobni bwsajwkno bwkha phurjak kha tei hamjak kha; bini bwkha manna bagwi bo kók kwtẃi kwtẃi sa kha.
