English: So Shechem spoke to his father Hamor, saying, “Get me this maiden for my wife.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Sekem bupha Hamorno “Nwng ano o bwrwi bai ma káinai” hwnwi sa kha.

English: Now Jacob heard that he had defiled his daughter Dinah; but his sons were with his cattle in the field, so Jacob held his peace until they came.
Kokborok: Jakob, Sekem bwsajwkno mung hamya man rwmano khna kha; tamo khe bwsarok aphuru mal-matarok mwrwgwi tongwi-o aboni bagwi bórok kiphil yasakno kwchang kwchang kheno tong gra kha.

English: And Hamor the father of Shechem went out to Jacob to speak with him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Sekemni bupha Hamor Jakob bai kók sa laina bagwi tháng kha.

English: The sons of Jacob came in from the field when they heard of it; and the men were indignant and very angry, because he had wrought folly in Israel by lying with Jacob's daughter, for such a thing ought not to be done.
Kokborok: Jakobni bwsarok abono khnaui mal-mata mwrwkmani kiphilwi phai lai kha; bórok bwkha hamjak li ya tei belai khe jolijak bai kha, tamni hwn khe bo Jakobni bwsajwkno romphreui bo bai thu-ui Israilrokno lachi rw kha.

English: But Hamor spoke with them, saying, “The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter; I pray you, give her to him in marriage.
Kokborok: Phiya ba Hamor bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Angsla Sekemni bwkha le nwsajwkno khátang súgwi tong kha, bono bo bai kái si di.

English: Make marriages with us; give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.
Kokborok: Phai di chwng bai kái lai si di; nwsajwksongno chini bwsa chwlarok bai kái lai anw tei chini sajwksong baibo nwslasongno kái si di.

English: You shall dwell with us; and the land shall be open to you; dwell and trade in it, and get property in it.”
Kokborok: Norok chwng bai’-no thansa khe tong di, norok tongna muchung thani’-no tong di, pabo pai di, phabo phal di, sak sakni bagwibo aji di.”

English: Shechem also said to her father and to her brothers, “Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give.
Kokborok: Sekem Dinani bupha tei bini takhuksongrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok ano lon bai di, hakhe ang norok sanmani tẃi’-no rwnani.

English: Ask of me ever so much as marriage present and gift, and I will give according as you say to me; only give me the maiden to be my wife.”
Kokborok: Norok tamo hili nai sa di, bwrwini bagwi kái laimungni hilibo norok ano sanma swk’-no rwnai. Norok ano bo bai simi kái di.”

English: The sons of Jacob answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.
Kokborok: Sekem bórokni bukhuk Dinano dera rwmani bagwi, Jakobni bwsarok bwsla Sekem bai bupha Hamorno bandiui sa kha.

English: They said to them, “We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us.
Kokborok: Bórok bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng o samung khlaiwi man ya, chwng sakur rajak yarokni thani chini bukhukno rwui man ya, abo hai khe chini lachimung wngnai se.

English: Only on this condition will we consent to you: that you will become as we are and every male of you be circumcised.
Kokborok: Omo hai hwn khe simi se wngwi mano, norokbo chwng hai ma wngnai bebak chwlarok sakur ma rajaknai.

English: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters to ourselves, and we will dwell with you and become one people.
Kokborok: Abo hai khe se chwng norok bai kái laiwi tei káijak laiwi mannai tei chwng orono tong phlaiwi norok bai bosong kaisa wngnai.

English: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our daughter, and we will be gone.”
Kokborok: Phiya norok chwng samano lon yaui sakur rajak ya khe chwng bono tlangwi thángwi páinai.”

English: Their words pleased Hamor and Hamor's son Shechem.
Kokborok: Bórokni kókno Hamor bai bwsla Sekem chajak lai kha.

English: And the young man did not delay to do the thing, because he had delight in Jacob's daughter. Now he was the most honored of all his family.
Kokborok: A siklasa dodro a samungno khlai kha, tamni hwn khe bo Jakobni bwsajwkno belai kheno hamjago. Tabuk bo khe bini nokni bórijak kuknai.

English: So Hamor and his son Shechem came to the gate of their city and spoke to the men of their city, saying,
Kokborok: Hakhe Hamor bai bwsla Sekem kamiyungni duglamo phaiwi bórokni kamiyungni borokrokno hai hwnwi sa phai kha,

English: “These men are friendly with us; let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for behold, the land is large enough for them; let us take their daughters in marriage, and let us give them our daughters.
Kokborok: “O borokrok chwng bai kwthaui tongnai máng se; bórok chini ha-ono tong thung, manwirokbo pai thung phal thung, nai di o habo torma ba bórokni bagwi tongna khochor glak. Chwngbo bórokni bwrwi bwsarok bai káijaknai, chini bwrwi bwsaroknobo bórok bai kái lainai.

English: Only on this condition will the men agree to dwell with us, to become one people: that every male among us be circumcised as they are circumcised.
Kokborok: Tamo khe o borokrok chwng bai thansa dopha kaisa wngna khe, chwng bórok sakur rajakma hai’-no chwngbo sakur ma rajaknai.

English: Will not their cattle, their property and all their beasts be ours? Only let us agree with them, and they will dwell with us.”
Kokborok: Omo hai khe bórokni mal-matarok, rang-rírok chini wng glak diba? Phai di bórokbo chwng bai tongwi manna tẃi khe chwng bebakno lon lai anw.”

English: And all who went out of the gate of his city hearkened to Hamor and his son Shechem; and every male was circumcised, all who went out of the gate of his city.
Kokborok: Kamiyungni bebak borokrok Hamor bai Sekem samano soi lai kha tei kamiyungni duglamni phataro thángnai bebak chwla bwsarok sakur rajak bai kha.

English: On the third day, when they were sore, two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, took their swords and came upon the city unawares, and killed all the males.
Kokborok: Salthamni salo, bórokni bebak chwlarok sakur rajagwi kwsa manjak máng, aphuru Jakobni bwslasong khoroknwi Simion bai Lebi Dinani takhuksong, bórokni seng naui tepsa khe kamiyungo habwi kha tei bebak chwlarokno butharwi pái kha.

English: They slew Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword, and took Dinah out of Shechem's house, and went away.
Kokborok: Bórok Hamor bai bwsla Sekemnobo seng bai tantharwi pái kha. Hakhe bórok Dinano Sekemni nokni tikhlaiwi nongkhorwi tháng kha.

English: And the sons of Jacob came upon the slain, and plundered the city, because their sister had been defiled;
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakobni bwslasong kwthwi tong thani phaiwi kamiyungni manwi-khwnwirok sekproui tlangwi tháng kha; tamni hwnma le bórok bahanokno dera rw kha.

English: they took their flocks and their herds, their asses, and whatever was in the city and in the field;
Kokborok: Bórok mal-matarok, pun merarok, korai chirkarok kamiyungo tei hapharo tongma bebakno naui pái kha,

English: all their wealth, all their little ones and their wives, all that was in the houses, they captured and made their prey.
Kokborok: bórokni bebak rang-puisarok, bórokni chwrai khonairok tei bwrwirok, romwi nokni tong kwrwi bebakno romwi tlangwi tháng kha.

English: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “You have brought trouble on me by making me odious to the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites; my numbers are few, and if they gather themselves against me and attack me, I shall be destroyed, both I and my household.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakob Simion bai Lebino hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok oro ani saka-o digi-sigi tubu kha; tabuk Kanansong Peressong tei ha-o tongnai bebakno ano hamya sanai kha. Ani borokrokbo báng tha ya; Bórok bebakno ano thansa khe swprak khe chini bebak núkhung se komornai.”

English: But they said, “Should he treat our sister as a harlot?”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bo chini hanokno le sak phalnaijwkrok hai da ma tong?”

English: God said to Jacob, “Arise, go up to Bethel, and dwell there; and make there an altar to the God who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau.”
Kokborok: Kaitor Jakobno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bacha di, Bethelo thángwi tongwi si di; tei nwng nini takhuk Esauni aroni khárwi tháng phuru nwng bai nukjaknai a Kaitorni bagwi háyá snam di.”

English: So Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, “Put away the foreign gods that are among you, and purify yourselves, and change your garments;
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob bini nokni borokrokno tei bo bai tongnai bebak borokrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norokni bisingo tongma buini mwtairokno khibi bai di tei sak sak kwthar wng bai di tei norokni rírok slái bai di;

English: then let us arise and go up to Bethel, that I may make there an altar to the God who answered me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone.”
Kokborok: chwng oroni nongkhorwi Bethelo thángnai tei aro thángwi ang tong kesebo tong phuru ano chubanai tei ang tháng thani ang bai baksa thángnai a Kaitorni háyá kaisa snamnai.”

English: So they gave to Jacob all the foreign gods that they had, and the rings that were in their ears; and Jacob hid them under the oak which was near Shechem.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Jakobni yago bebak buini mwtairokno tei bórokni khunju kanma manwirokno yapharwi rw bai kha; Jakob abórokno Sekem gandarni uk buphang yaphango khe phob bai kha.

English: And as they journeyed, a terror from God fell upon the cities that were round about them, so that they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.
Kokborok: Bórok thángwi tong phuru bórokni gana-gini tongma kamiyungrogo Kaitorni kirima bórokni thani hap kha, hakhe bórok Jakobni bwsarokno riphrik rak li ya.

English: And Jacob came to Luz (that is, Bethel), which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him,
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob bini bororokno tubu-ui Lujo sók phai kha, (abono’-no Bethel hwno), omo wng kha ha Kanano,

English: and there he built an altar, and called the place El-bethel, because there God had revealed himself to him when he fled from his brother.
Kokborok: bo aro háyá kaisa snam kha tei El-Bethel hwnwi mung phar kha, tamni hwn khe bo bini takhukni aroni khárwi tháng phuru Kaitor bo bai aro nukjak kha.

English: And Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, died, and she was buried under an oak below Bethel; so the name of it was called Allon-bacuth.
Kokborok: Rebekano nainaijwkma Debora aro thwi kha hakhe Bethelni salsibaini uk buphang yaphango bono phob kha. Hakhe abono Alon-bakut hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: God appeared to Jacob again, when he came from Paddan-aram, and blessed him.
Kokborok: Jakob Padan-aramni kiphil phuru Kaitor haino hai nukjak phirwi bono hamari rw kha.

English: And God said to him, “Your name is Jacob; no longer shall your name be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name.” So his name was called Israel.
Kokborok: Hakhe Kaitor bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini mung wng kha Jakob, nwng tei Jakob hwnwi nungjak glak kha, nini mung Israil hwnjak sinai.” Hakhe bini mung Israil wng kha.

English: And God said to him, “I am God Almighty: be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall spring from you.
Kokborok: Kaitor bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Kwplaisa Kaitor nwng ham di tei er di; dopha kaisa tei dopharok nini aroni nongkhornai, habugrarok nini aroni nongkhornai.

English: The land which I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you, and I will give the land to your descendants after you.”
Kokborok: Abraham bai Isakno rwma a hano ang nono tei nini yakchu-o nini bosongrokno rwnai.”

English: Then God went up from him in the place where he had spoken with him.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Kaitor bo bai kók sa laimani aroni thángwi pái kha.

English: And Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a pillar of stone; and he poured out a drink offering on it, and poured oil on it.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob, Kaitor bo bai kók sama jidolo kaisa holongni thongla kái kha; aboni saka-o khe nungmung bai thok lukhlai rwui yaphar kha.

English: So Jacob called the name of the place where God had spoken with him, Bethel.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob, Kaitor bo bai malaiwi kók sa phaima jagano Bethel hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Then they journeyed from Bethel; and when they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel travailed, and she had hard labor.
Kokborok: Abo pái khe bórok Bethelni lam rwui tháng lai kha; Ephrato sógwinani kisa chalwi tong kho, aphuru Rahel sak kengnani ok sajak kha, bo belai kheno bohok sajak kha.

English: And when she was in her hard labor, the midwife said to her, “Fear not; for now you will have another son.”
Kokborok: Bo belai khe bohok sajagwi tong phuru, kumajwk bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ta kiri di; nwng tabuk tei khoroksa chwla bwsa mannai.”

English: And as her soul was departing (for she died), she called his name Ben-oni; but his father called his name Benjamin.
Kokborok: Bini rangma chona naiwi tong phuru bo a bwsano Benoni hwnwi mung phar kha; tamo khe absani bupha khe bono Benjamin hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: So Rachel died, and she was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem),
Kokborok: Hakhe Rahel thwi kha tei Ephratni lama-o phobjak kha (abono Betlehem hwno),

English: and Jacob set up a pillar upon her grave; it is the pillar of Rachel's tomb, which is there to this day.
Kokborok: Jakob, bini a mangkhoro thongla kaisa kái kha; abo tabukbo Rahelni simalwng hwnjago.

English: Israel journeyed on, and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.
Kokborok: Israil aroni kutulwi Edarni nok kuchukni ayang naro ríni nok songchaui kha.

English:
While Israel dwelt in that land Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine; and Israel heard of it.
Now the sons of Jacob were twelve.
Kokborok:
Israil a ha-o tong phuru, Ruben buphani khoroksa hik kotoi Bilha bai thu lai-chá lai kha; hakhe Israil a kókno khna kha.
Jakobni khe bwsla khorokchinwi tongo.

English: The sons of Leah: Reuben (Jacob's first-born), Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
Kokborok: Leani bwsarok khe: Ruben (Jakobni bwsla okra) Simion, Lebi, Juda, Isakar tei Jebulun.

English: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
Kokborok: Rahelni bwsarok khe: Josep tei Benjamin.

English: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel's maid: Dan and Naphtali.
Kokborok: Rahelni selengjwk Bilhani bwsarok khe: Dan tei Naptali.

English: The sons of Zilpah, Leah's maid: Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan-aram.
Kokborok: Leani selengjwk Jilpani bwsarok khe: Gad tei Aser. Omorokno Jakobni bwsarok, bórok Padan-aramo achainairok.

English: And Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, or Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had sojourned.
Kokborok: Jakob, Abraham bai Isak tong langma Mamre eba Kiriat-arba-o (abo khe Hebron) sók phai kha, arono Abraham tei Isak tong lango.

English: Now the days of Isaac were a hundred and eighty years.
Kokborok: Aphuru Isakni umor nokha bisi sara tei charchi wng kha.

English: And Isaac breathed his last; and he died and was gathered to his people, old and full of days; and his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
Kokborok: Isak chakrasuk khe thwi kha; tei bini borokrokni thani tlangjak kha, bwsarok Esau bai Jakob bono hakhoro phob kha.

English: These are the descendants of Esau (that is, Edom).
Kokborok: Omorokno Esauni bosongrok, (bórokno Idom bo hwno.)

English: Esau took his wives from the Canaanites: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, Oholibamah the daughter of Anah the son of Zibeon the Hivite,
Kokborok: Esau Kananni bwrwirok bai káijak kha; Hitisa Elonni bwsajwk Ada, Hibisa Jibeonni bwsla Anani bwsajwk Oholibama,

English: and Basemath, Ishmael's daughter, the sister of Nebaioth.
Kokborok: Tei Ismaelni bwsajwk, Nebaiotni bukhuk Basemat;

English: And Adah bore to Esau, Eliphaz; Basemath bore Reuel;
Kokborok: Ada Esauni bagwi Eliphasno bá kha tei Basemat khe Reuelno bá kha,

English: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
Kokborok: tei Jeus khe Jalam tei Korano bá kha. Omorok wng kha Esauni bwsarok, bórok bebakno ha Kanano achainairok.

English: Then Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, his cattle, all his beasts, and all his property which he had acquired in the land of Canaan; and he went into a land away from his brother Jacob.
Kokborok: Hakhe Esau bini núkhungni bebak borokrokno, mal-matarok tei musuk-merarok tei ha Kanano ajima bebak rang-rírok tẃiwi bo bini takhuk Jakobni aroni hakchalma ha-o thángwi pái kha.

English: For their possessions were too great for them to dwell together; the land of their sojournings could not support them because of their cattle.
Kokborok: Bórokni mal-mata, manwi-khwnwi belai bángmani bagwi bórok kwrẃngsa-o tong laiwi man lai li ya. Mal-matarok bángmani bagwi a ha-o chak lai ya wng kha.

English: So Esau dwelt in the hill country of Seir; Esau is Edom.
Kokborok: Hakhe Esau hatháini ha Seiro tong kha; Esauno Idom.

English: These are the descendants of Esau the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir.
Kokborok: Omorokno Esauni bosongrok, Idom bosongrokni bupha. Bórok Seir hathái-o tongnairok

English: These are the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz the son of Adah the wife of Esau, Reuel the son of Basemath the wife of Esau.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Esauni bwsarokni mungrok: Eliphas, bo khe Esauni bihik Adani bwsla, Reuel, bo khe Esauni bihik Basematni bwsla.

English: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
Kokborok: Eliphasni bwsarok khe, Teman, Omar, Jepho, Gatam tei Kenaj.

English: (Timna was a concubine of Eliphaz, Esau's son; she bore Amalek to Eliphaz.) These are the sons of Adah, Esau's wife.
Kokborok: (Timna khe Eliphasni hik kotoi, bo khe Eliphasni bagwi Amalekno bá kha.) Omorok wng kha Esauni bihik Adani bwsarok.

English: These are the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These are the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha, Reuelni bwsarok; Nahat, Jera, Samma tei Mijja. Omorok wng kha Esauni bihik Basematni bwsarok.

English: These are the sons of Oholibamah the daughter of Anah the son of Zibeon, Esau's wife: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha, Jibeonni bwsla Anani bwsajwk Esauni bihik Oholibamani bwsarok: Bo Esauni bagwi Jeus, Jalam tei Korano bá kha.

English: These are the chiefs of the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz the first-born of Esau: the chiefs Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha, Esauni bosongrokni bosong-okrarok. Esauni bwsla okra Eliphasni bosongrokni khe; Teman, Omar, Jepho, Kenaj,

English: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek; these are the chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Adah.
Kokborok: Kora Gatam tei Amalek; omorok wng kha ha Idomni Eliphasni bosong-okrarok; bórok khe Adani bwsarok.

English: These are the sons of Reuel, Esau's son: the chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah; these are the chiefs of Reuel in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
Kokborok: Omorok khe Esauni bwsla Reuelni bwsarok: bosong-okra Nahat, Jera, Sama tei Mijja. Omorok wng kha ha Idomni Reuelni bosong-okrarok; bórok khe Esauni bihik Basematni bwsarok.

English: These are the sons of Oholibamah, Esau's wife: the chiefs Jeush, Jalam, and Korah; these are the chiefs born of Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau's wife.
Kokborok: Esauni bihik Oholibamani bwsarok: bórokni bosong okrarok wng kha; Jeus, Jalam tei Kora; Bórok wng kha Anani bwsajwk, Esauni bihik Oholibamani ogo achainai bosong okrarok.

English: These are the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these are their chiefs.
Kokborok: Bórok bebakno Esauni (abo khe Idom) bwsarok tei bosong-okrarok.

English: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha a ha-o tongnai Horisa Seirni bwsarok; Lotan, Sobal, Jibeon, Ana,

English: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; these are the chiefs of the Horites, the sons of Seir in the land of Edom.
Kokborok: Dison, Ejer tei Disan; Omorokno wng kha ha Idomni Horisa Seirni bwsarokni bosong-okrarok.

English: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Heman; and Lotan's sister was Timna.
Kokborok: Lotanni bwsarok khe Hori bai Heman; Lotanni bukhukni mung khe Timna.

English: These are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
Kokborok: Sobalni bwsarok wng kha: Alban, Manahat, Ebal, Sepho tei Onam.

English: These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah; he is the Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness, as he pastured the asses of Zibeon his father.
Kokborok: Jibionni bwsarok wng kha: Aia tei Ana; O Ana’-no hapharo bupha Jibeonni korai chirkarokno mwrwgwi tong phuru twi kutung khe chersaui tongmano mannai.

English: These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah.
Kokborok: Anani bwsla khe Dison tei bwsajwk khe Oholibama.

English: These are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
Kokborok: Omorok khe Disonni bwsarok: Hemdan, Esban, Ithran tei Cheran.

English: These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
Kokborok: Omorok khe Ejerni bwsarok: Bilhan, Jaban tei Akan.

English: These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
Kokborok: Disanni bwsarok khe: Uj tei Aran.

English: These are the chiefs of the Horites: the chiefs Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Horisarokni bosong-okrarok: Bosong-okra Lotan, Sobal, Jibeon, Ana,

English: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan; these are the chiefs of the Horites, according to their clans in the land of Seir.
Kokborok: Dison, Ejer tei Disan; bórok ha Seiro bórokni bosong thwgwini bosong okra wngnairok.

English: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom, before any king reigned over the Israelites.
Kokborok: Bórokno Israilrokni saka-o khoroksa phano bugra chá ya sini swkang ha Idomo bugra chánairok.

English: Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom, the name of his city being Dinhabah.
Kokborok: Beorni bwsla Bela ha Idomo bugra chá kha, bini kamiyungni mung khe Dinhaba.

English: Bela died, and Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his stead.
Kokborok: Bela thwi khe bini yakchu-o Bojra hani Jerani bwsla Jobab habugra chá kha.

English: Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his stead.
Kokborok: Jobab thwi kha, hakhe ha Temanni Husam habugra chá kha.

English: Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, reigned in his stead, the name of his city being Avith.
Kokborok: Husam thwi kha, hakhe Moabo Midiansongno mechennai Bedadni bwsla Hadad habugra chá kha, bini kamiyung khe Abit.

English: Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his stead.
Kokborok: Hadad thwi kha, hakhe Masrekani Samla habugra chá kha.

English: Samlah died, and Shaul of Rehoboth on the Euphrates reigned in his stead.
Kokborok: Salma thwi kha hakhe Rehobotni Saul Uphratisni habugra chá kha.

English: Shaul died, and Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his stead.
Kokborok: Saul thwi kha hakhe, Akborni bwsla Balhanan habugra chá kha.

English: Baal-hanan the son of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his stead, the name of his city being Pau; his wife's name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab.
Kokborok: Akborni bwsla Balhanan thwi khe Hadar habugra chá kha, bini kamiyungni mung khe Pau; bini bihikni mung khe Mehetabel, Matred bai Mejahabni bwsajwk.

English: These are the names of the chiefs of Esau, according to their families and their dwelling places, by their names: the chiefs Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
Kokborok: Omorok wng kha Esau bosongni bosong-okrarokni mungrok, bórokni núkhung, tongthairokno rwksaui bórokni mungrok: bosong-okra Timna, Alba, Jethet,

English: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
Kokborok: Oholibama, Ela, Pinon,

English: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
Kokborok: Kenaj Teman, Mibsar

English: Magdiel, and Iram; these are the chiefs of Edom (that is, Esau, the father of Edom), according to their dwelling places in the land of their possession.
Kokborok: Magdiel tei Iram; omorok khe Idomni bokhorokrok (Esauno Idomni bupha), bórokni bosongrogo bórok bosong-okrarok.

English: Jacob dwelt in the land of his father's sojournings, in the land of Canaan.
Kokborok: Jakob bupha tong langma ha Kanano kami khaui tongnani cheng kha.

English:
This is the history of the family of Jacob.
Joseph, being seventeen years old, was shepherding the flock with his brothers; he was a lad with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father's wives; and Joseph brought an ill report of them to their father.
Kokborok:
Omo wng kha Jakobni núkhungni láibumani kothomarok.
Josep nokha bisi chisnini chasloksa chwrai, bini takhuksong bai khe merarok, punrok mwrwgo, abórok wng kha buphani hik kotoi Bilha bai Jilpani bwsarok. Bo bini takhuksongrok samung cha ya khlaima kókrokno buphani thani saui rw phai-o.

English: Now Israel loved Joseph more than any other of his children, because he was the son of his old age; and he made him a long robe with sleeves.
Kokborok: Israil, Josepno bini tei bwsarokni slái hamjak kugo, tamni hwn ba bo Josep khe bini belai chakra jora-o manmani sajla; hakhe bono kamchwlwi lolang naithok kangsa subwi rw kha.

English: But when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him, and could not speak peaceably to him.
Kokborok: Phiya ba Josepni takhukrok bórokni slái bupha bono hamjago hwnwi naislerjak kha tei bo bai kwchang kwchang kók sa laiwi tongwi man li ya.

English: Now Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers they only hated him the more.
Kokborok: Josep imang kaisa nuk kha tei a imangno bini takhuksongno saui khna rw khe, bórok bono teibo naislerjak kha.

English: He said to them, “Hear this dream which I have dreamed:
Kokborok: Bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang tamo imang nuk kha abo norok khna ja bai di:

English: behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and lo, my sheaf arose and stood upright; and behold, your sheaves gathered round it, and bowed down to my sheaf.”
Kokborok: Nai di chwng bebakno gom bwlwngo gom motha kha laiwi tongmani; ani motha thóng-thóng khe bachasa kha; nai ba norokni motharok khe ani mothano kiting lai kha tei abono khulum lai kha.”

English: His brothers said to him, “Are you indeed to reign over us? Or are you indeed to have dominion over us?” So they hated him yet more for his dreams and for his words.
Kokborok: Bini takhuksong bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng de chini bugra wng suknai tamo?” Eba nwng da chini bokhorok wngwi chwngno kók khna rwnai wngnai? Hakhe bórok bono bini imang bai bini kókrokni bagwi teibo naisler lai kha.

English: Then he dreamed another dream, and told it to his brothers, and said, “Behold, I have dreamed another dream; and behold, the sun, the moon, and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo tei kaisa imang nuk phir kha tei a kókno bini takhuksongno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di a imango sal, tal tei athukiri kolchisarok ano khulum lai kha.”

English: But when he told it to his father and to his brothers, his father rebuked him, and said to him, “What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow ourselves to the ground before you?”
Kokborok: Bo a kókno bini buphano tei bini takhuksongno sa khe bupha bono hai hwnwi kulwi hwn kha, “Nwng omo tamo imang nuk lang? Ang, nwma tei nini takhuksongrok phaiwi ha-o botok khe nono klaiwi ma khulumnai hai da?”

English: And his brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept the saying in mind.
Kokborok: Hakhe bini takhuksongrok bono músú lai kha tamo khe bupha khe a samani a kókrokno bwkha-o chu-ui ton kha.

English: Now his brothers went to pasture their father's flock near Shechem.
Kokborok: Salsa bini takhuksongrok buphani pun, merarok mwrwkna bagwi Sekemo tháng kha

English: And Israel said to Joseph, “Are not your brothers pasturing the flock at Shechem? Come, I will send you to them.” And he said to him, “Here I am.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Israil Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nini takhuksongrok Sekemo merarok, punrok mwrwgwi tong ya da? Phai gra di ang nono bórokni aro rohor grana.”

English: So he said to him, “Go now, see if it is well with your brothers, and with the flock; and bring me word again.” So he sent him from the valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
Kokborok:
Hakhe bupha bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di tei nwtasongrok tei mal-matarok bebak kaham kwrwngda nai di; hakhe kiphilwi phaiwi ano sa phai phi di.”
Hakhe bo bono Hebron harungo rwi rohor kha, hakhe bo Sekemo sók phai kha.

English: And a man found him wandering in the fields; and the man asked him, “What are seeking?”
Kokborok: Aro sógwi khe bo yang yang tháng trwngwi tongwi kha, hakhe aphuru borok khoroksa bono nugwi hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nwng tamo naituk?”

English: “I am seeking my brothers,” he said, “tell me, I pray you, where they are pasturing the flock.”
Kokborok: “Ang ani takhuksongrokno se naitugwi tongo,” hwnwi bo sa kha. “Bórok buro mera mwrwk laiwi tong khna ano kisa sa ja di,” hwnwibo bo sa kha.

English: And the man said, “They have gone away, for I heard them say, ‘Let us go to Dothan.’ ” So Joseph went after his brothers, and found them at Dothan.
Kokborok: A borok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bórok oroni swkang’-no tháng bai kha, tamni hwn ba ang bórokno ‘Tho Dothanno tháng lainai’ hwnwi samano khna kha.” Hakhe Josep bini takhuksongno naitugwi thángwi Dothanno bórok bai malai kha.

English: They saw him afar off, and before he came near to them they conspired against him to kill him.
Kokborok: Bórok hakchalo tongsani’-no bono nuhur lai kha tei bo bórokni aro sógwi ya sini’-no bono butharwi páinai hwnwi kók chap lai kha,

English: They said to one another, “Here comes this dreamer.
Kokborok: Bórok bo’-bo bono hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Imang nuknaisa phai-o.

English: Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits; then we shall say that a wild beast has devoured him, and we shall see what will become of his dreams.”
Kokborok: Phai di, tabuk bono buthar laiwi kaisa hakhoro khiklaiwi rw lai anw; tei chwng bono bolongni manwirok uátharwi pái kha hwnwi sa lai anw, hakhe bini a imang tamo wng chwng nuk anw.”

English: But when Reuben heard it, he delivered him out of their hands, saying, “Let us not take his life.”
Kokborok: Phiya Ruben bórok sa laima a kókno khnaui Josepno mwthangna bagwi nai kha tei “Phai di bono buthar lai li ya,” hwnwi sa kha.

English: And Reuben said to them, “Shed no blood; cast him into this pit here in the wilderness, but lay no hand upon him”—that he might rescue him out of their hand, to restore him to his father.
Kokborok: Ruben bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Thẃi le ta kochok rw di; bono yakbo ta rw di, tamo khe oro haphar bukcha-o hakhor tongma-o khiklaiwi rw di” bo bono mwthangwi buphani thani phiyógwi rohorna bagwi se omo hai khe sa-o.

English: So when Joseph came to his brothers, they stripped him of his robe, the long robe with sleeves that he wore;
Kokborok: Josep bini takhuksongni aro sók phai kheno, bórok bini a kamchwlwi lolang yaktwk kolokno khugwi na lai kha;

English: and they took him and cast him into a pit. The pit was empty, there was no water in it.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok bono romwi hakhoro khiklaiwi rw lai kha. U hakhoro khe twi kwrwi, bukcha.

English: Then they sat down to eat; and looking up they saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing gum, balm, and myrrh, on their way to carry it down to Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok chá laina achuk phuru khe; tei nuk kha, Ismaelsasong bwtang-bwtang khe dolsa Giliadni Ijibo tháng laiwi tongo. Bórokni mangingangrogo khe belengtwi motomrok tei satrairok teibo motom manwirok boksajak.

English: Then Judah said to his brothers, “What profit is it if we slay our brother and conceal his blood?
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Juda bini takhuksongno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng chini takhukno butharwi tamo mannai tei thẃi khiklai laiwi le tamo mannai?

English: Come, let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him, for he is our brother, our own flesh.” And his brothers heeded him.
Kokborok: Phai di, chwng bono Ismaelsarokni yago phalwi rw lai anw; chwng bono yak rw lai li ya, tamni hwn khe bo’-bo chini takhukno ba, chini sakni’-no bahan se ba.” Hakhe bini takhuksongrok lon lai kha.

English: Then Midianite traders passed by; and they drew Joseph up and lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver; and they took Joseph to Egypt.
Kokborok: Ha Midianni kisa bepar thwngnairok samo sók phai khe, bórok Josepno twikhorni tisa lai kha tei lepchinwi rukphaini sekel bai khe Ismaelrokni yago phalwi rw kha; hakhe bórok Josepno ha Ijibo tlangwi tháng kha.

English: When Reuben returned to the pit and saw that Joseph was not in the pit, he rent his clothes
Kokborok: Ruben a twikhoro phaiwi Josepno aro nuk li ya hwn khe bini ríno chir kha

English: and returned to his brothers, and said, “The lad is gone; and I, where shall I go?”
Kokborok: tei bini takhukrokni aro thángwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “A chwraisa ba aro kw se kwrwi kha; ang ba buro thángwi tamo khlai sinai?”

English: Then they took Joseph's robe, and killed a goat, and dipped the robe in the blood;
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórok pun masa rathar laiwi aboni thẃi-o Josepni ríno chomsa kha;

English: and they sent the long robe with sleeves and brought it to their father, and said, “This we have found; see now whether it is your son's robe or not.”
Kokborok: bórok a kamchwlwi lolangno buphani thani tlangwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng omono man kha; nai di omoba tamo nwslani da wng kha?”

English: And he recognized it, and said, “It is my son's robe; a wild beast has devoured him; Joseph is without doubt torn to pieces.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo abono siniui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo le ani sajlani’-no; mungsa swk bolongni manwirok bono buthar khna; Ani sajla le katswi phondrik-phandrak khlaijakla hwna.”

English: Then Jacob rent his garments, and put sackcloth upon his loins, and mourned for his son many days.
Kokborok: Jakob bwkha báijagwi bini rí chir kha tei rí kichikrok kan kha, bwslani bagwi sal kwbángma bo bwkha khamwi tong kha.

English: All his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted, and said, “No, I shall go down to Sheol to my son, mourning.” Thus his father wept for him.
Kokborok: Bini bebak bwslasong bwsajwksong bono bwkha kwchang rwna bagwi phai lai kha, tamo khe bo bwkha kwchangjak li ya wngwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi, ang ani sajlani bagwi bwkha kham te kham te hakhoro se tháng sinai.” Omo hai hwnwi saui bupha bwsajlani bagwi bwkha sajagwi kap kha.

English: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold him in Egypt to Poti-phar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard.
Kokborok: A jora-o ha Ijibo Midiansasongrok Josepno Potipharni thani phalwi rw kha, bo wng kha habugrani khoroksa tẃiphang, bo noyung mwrwknairokni bokhorok.

English: It happened at that time that Judah went down from his brothers, and turned in to a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.
Kokborok: A jora-o Juda bini takhuksong bai kagwi thángwi Hira hwnwi Adulamni borok khoroksa bai tongnani bagwi tháng kha.

English: There Judah saw the daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua; he married her and went in to her,
Kokborok: Aro Juda Kananjwk khoroksa bai malai kha, a bwrwini mung wng kha Sua; bo a bwrwino káijak kha tei bo bai thu kha-chá kha

English: and she conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Er.
Kokborok: hakhe bo ogo bwsa wngwi bwsa chwla khoroksa man kha, bo a bwsano Er hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Again she conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Onan.
Kokborok: Haino hai bo ogo bwsa wngwi tei khoroksa bwsa chwla man phi kha, abono khe Onam hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Yet again she bore a son, and she called his name Shelah. She was in Chezib when she bore him.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo tei khoroksa chwla bwsa man phi kha, bo bono Sela hwnwi mung phar kha. Chejibo tong phuru bo a bwsano mano.

English: And Judah took a wife for Er his first-born, and her name was Tamar.
Kokborok: Bwsla okra Erni bagwi bo hamjwk khoroksa tubu kha. Bini mung khe Tamar.

English: But Er, Judah's first-born, was wicked in the sight of the Lord; and the Lord slew him.
Kokborok: Phiya Subraiyungni mokolo Judani bwsla okra Erni tongmung khe hamya; hakhe Subraiyung bono buthar kha.

English: Then Judah said to Onan, “Go in to your brother's wife, and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her, and raise up offspring for your brother.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Juda Onamno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng nwbachwini aro tháng di, nwng buprang ba nini khlaithai tongmano khlai si di, nwtani bagwi bwsa wng rw di.”

English: But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his; so when he went in to his brother's wife he spilled the semen on the ground, lest he should give offspring to his brother.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Onam saui mano a bwsa le bini wng glak, hakhe bo bubachwini aro thángwi bo bai pal thu lai phuru khe bwtani bwsa wngwi man ya tẃi khe ha-o se sákolom bwtwi khiklai rw si-o.

English: And what he did was displeasing in the sight of the Lord, and he slew him also.
Kokborok: Hakhe Subraiyung bini a samungno chajak li ya tei bo bonobo buthar kha.

English: Then Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, “Remain a widow in your father's house, till Shelah my son grows up”—for he feared that he would die, like his brothers. So Tamar went and dwelt in her father's house.
Kokborok: Hakhe Juda bahamjwk Tamarno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng randi wngwi’-no nwphani nogo thángwi tongwi gra di, ani sajla Sela tor gra si thung”—tamni hwn ba bo bono bini takhuksong hai khe thwina kirijak kha. Hakhe Tamar buphani nogo thángwi tongwi kha.

English: In course of time the wife of Judah, Shua's daughter, died; and when Judah was comforted, he went up to Timnah to his sheepshearers, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
Kokborok: Salsa khe Suani bwsajwk Judani bihik thwi kha; Juda bihik thwimani kókno kisa pogwi bwkha kwchang phuru khe bo bini merarokni buikhumu ranairokni thani Timna-o tháng kha, bikiching Adulamsa Hirabo bo bai baksa tháng kha.

English: And when Tamar was told, “Your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep,”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Tamarno, “nwkra bini merarokni buikhumu rana bagwi Timna-o thángwi tongo” hwnwi sajak khe,

English: she put off her widow's garments, and put on a veil, wrapping herself up, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah was grown up, and she had not been given to him in marriage.
Kokborok: bo bini randijwkrokni kanmungrokno khibiui, kubun rí kanwi rí hungklaiwi Timna-o thángma lamani Enaimo hapma lampra-o tong kha; tamni hwn ba Sela torwi suda se bwkra bono bo bai káiwi rw li ya abono bo romwi man kha.

English: When Judah saw her, he thought her to be a harlot, for she had covered her face.
Kokborok: Juda bono nuk kheno sak phalnaijwk hwnwi chong kha, tamni hwn khe bini mwkhang rí hungjak.

English: He went over to her at the road side, and said, “Come, let me come in to you,” for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said, “What will you give me, that you may come in to me?”
Kokborok:
Bo lama gandaro bini thani tháng kha tei hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nono sak tangnai,” tamni hwn khe bo bono bahamjwk hwnwi saui man ya.
Tamar hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng ani sak tang khe nwng ano tamo rwnai ba?”

English: He answered, “I will send you a kid from the flock.” And she said, “Will you give me a pledge, till you send it?”
Kokborok:
Hakhe Juda hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nono mera bwsa masa rwui rohornai.”
Tamar hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng abo rohor ya sini swkang sakni bagwi ano manwi mungsa swk klang grana diba?”

English: He said, “What pledge shall I give you?” She replied, “Your signet and your cord, and your staff that is in your hand.” So he gave them to her, and went in to her, and she conceived by him.
Kokborok:
Juda hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nono tamo manwi klangnai ba?”
Tamar hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini mari dwkhwi tei nini yakni raidangno klang di.” Hakhe bo a manwirokno rwui Tamarni sak tang kha, hakhe bo, bo bai khe ogo bwsa wng kha.

English: Then she arose and went away, and taking off her veil she put on the garments of her widowhood.
Kokborok: Hakhe Tamar aroni bachaui tháng kha tei bini rí chummarokno, hungmarok khibiui randijwkni rírokno kan phi kha.

English: When Judah sent the kid by his friend the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman's hand, he could not find her.
Kokborok: Juda bikiching Adulamsani yak tẃi khe mera bwsa a bwrwini thani rwui rohorwi a kók rw phuru kabuma manwirokno na rw horna hwnwi rohor khe a bwrwino aro manẃi li ya.

English: And he asked the men of the place, “Where is the harlot who was at Enaim by the wayside?” And they said, “No harlot has been here.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo aroni borokrokno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “O Enaimni lama gandaro tongnai a sak phalnaijwk le biyang tháng kha sla?” hakhe bórok, “Oro khoroksa phano sak phalnaijwk kwrwi bla,” hwnwi sa kha.

English: So he returned to Judah, and said, “I have not found her; and also the men of the place said, ‘No harlot has been here.’ ”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo Judani thani kiphilwi thángwi hai hwnwi saui kha, “Ang a bwrwino malai li ya; tei aroni borokrok, ‘oro khoroksa phano sak phalnai kwrwi’ ” hwnwi sa-o.

English: And Judah replied, “Let her keep the things as her own, lest we be laughed at; you see, I sent this kid, and you could not find her.”
Kokborok: Juda hai hwnwi sa kha, “A bwrwi a manwirokno bini’-no khlai si thung, wng ya khe chwng mwnwijaknai; nai di ang o manwirokno nono rwui rohor kha, nwng bono ma se manẃi li ya.”

English: About three months later Judah was told, “Tamar your daughter-in-law has played the harlot; and moreover she is with child by harlotry.” And Judah said, “Bring her out, and let her be burned.”
Kokborok:
Taltham lái twtwi hwn kheno Juda hai hwnwi sajak kha, “Nahamjwk Tamar sak phalnai wng kha; tei abo hai khe tabuk ogo bwsa chu kha.”
Hakhe Juda hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bono phataro tikhlai tubu-ui hór bai khe sógwi pái di.”

English: As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law, “By the man to whom these belong, I am with child.” And she said, “Mark, I pray you, whose these are, the signet and the cord and the staff.”
Kokborok: Bono phataro tikhlai tubu khe, bo bwkrano hai hwnwi kók kwchárwi rohor kha, “O manwirokni bugra bai se ang sak kupulung wngo.” Tei bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “O buduk, raidang tei marino nai di, omo saboni?”

English: Then Judah acknowledged them and said, “She is more righteous than I, inasmuch as I did not give her to my son Shelah.” And he did not lie with her again.
Kokborok: Hakhe Juda abórokno siniui gosiui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bo ani slái se borok sói kuk, ang bono angsajla Selano rwjak li ya.” Phiya bo tei bini sak tang phi li ya.

English: When the time of her delivery came, there were twins in her womb.
Kokborok: Bini sak kengna jora wng kheno, apher hwnwi sijak kha.

English: And when she was in labor, one put out a hand; and the midwife took and bound on his hand a scarlet thread, saying, “This came out first.”
Kokborok: Bo sanchwgwi tong phuru, yak kongsa ongkhor kha; hakhe lomojwk a yago khutwng kwchak khaui rwui hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omono swkang phai kha.”

English: But as he drew back his hand, behold, his brother came out; and she said, “What a breach you have made for yourself!” Therefore his name was called Perez.
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo yak bisingo tlangwi tháng phi kha; phiya bini takhuk nongkhorwi phai kha; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng bahai khe sakni bagwi lama phiyogwi phai man kha!” Aboni bagwi bono Peres hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Afterward his brother came out with the scarlet thread upon his hand; and his name was called Zerah.
Kokborok: Kisa ulo khe bini takhuk yago khutwng kwchak khajak khe nongkhorwi phai kha; tei bono Jera hwnwi mung phar kha.

English: Now Joseph was taken down to Egypt, and Poti-phar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
Kokborok: Tabuk khe Josepno ha Ijibo tlangwi thángjak kha tei Potiphar hwnwi khoroksa Pharaoni tangphang tei noyung mwrwknairokni bokhorokno bono aro tubunai Ismaelsarokni aroni paiwi na kha.

English: The Lord was with Joseph, and he became a successful man; and he was in the house of his master the Egyptian,
Kokborok: Subraiyung Josep bai pal tongo, hakhe bo bini samungrokno cha rw kha; bo bini nokphang Ijipsani nogono tong phuru,

English: and his master saw that the Lord was with him, and that the Lord caused all that he did to prosper in his hands.
Kokborok: bo nuk kha Subraiyung Josep bai baksa tongo tei bo tangma bebak samungrokno cha rw-o.

English: So Joseph found favor in his sight and attended him, and he made him overseer of his house and put him in charge of all that he had.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi Potiphar bono bwkha phurjagwi bini baithangni nokni naikolphang snamwi bono bini nokni tongma bebak manwirokni barja yapharwi rw kha.

English: From the time that he made him overseer in his house and over all that he had the Lord blessed the Egyptian's house for Joseph's sake; the blessing of the Lord was upon all that he had, in house and field.
Kokborok: Bono nini baithangni nokni naikolphang snamwi nokni bebakni barja yaphar phuruni simi khe Josepni jarwi Subraiyung a Ijipsani núkhungni bebakno tei nokni tongma tei hapharrogo tongma manwirokno hamari rw kha.

English:
So he left all that he had in Joseph's charge; and having him he had no concern for anything but the food which he ate.
Now Joseph was handsome and good-looking.
Kokborok:
Hakhe bo Josepni yago bebakni barja yapharwi rw bai kha; bini chánanino karwi bebakno bo tei mungsa phano si li ya.
Yang Josep khe sakbo kaham, chwlabo naithok.

English: And after a time his master's wife cast her eyes upon Joseph, and said, “Lie with me.”
Kokborok: Sal kisa lái khe nokphangni bihik Josepno mokol tháijak kha, hakhe, “Ang bai thu di,” hwnwi sa kha.

English: But he refused and said to his master's wife, “Lo, having me my master has no concern about anything in the house, and he has put everything that he has in my hand;
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo soi li ya phiya nokphangni bihikno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ani nokphang bo nokni mungsano phano si li ya, ani yago bebakno yapharwi rw bai kha;

English: he is not greater in this house than I am; nor has he kept back anything from me except yourself, because you are his wife; how then can I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?”
Kokborok: bo o nogo ani slái kotor ya; tei nono karwi bo ano mungsa phano yaphar ya kwrwi kha, tamni hwnma le nwng le bini bihik ba; hakhe ang bahai khe aswk samung hamya khlaiwi mannai tei Kaitorni thani bahai khe sumsok khlaiwi mannai?”

English: And although she spoke to Joseph day after day, he would not listen to her, to lie with her or to be with her.
Kokborok: Bo Josepno sal brúmno abo hai khe sa phano bo bini kókrokno khunju kan li ya tei bo baibo pal thu li ya tei tong li ya.

English: But one day, when he went into the house to do his work and none of the men of the house was there in the house,
Kokborok: Tamo khe salsa bo bini samung tangna bagwi nok bisingo tháng kha, aphuru aro tei kubun borokrokbo kwrwi,

English: she caught him by his garment, saying, “Lie with me.” But he left his garment in her hand, and fled and got out of the house.
Kokborok: bo bini ríno romwi “Ang bai pal thu di.” hwnwi sa kha. Tamo khe bo bini ríno bini thani khibiui klangwi nokni nongkhorwi khár kha.

English: And when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and had fled out of the house,
Kokborok: Bo bini ríno khibiui klangwi nokni ongkhorwi khárma nuk khe,

English: she called to the men of her household and said to them, “See, he has brought among us a Hebrew to insult us; he came in to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice;
Kokborok: bini nogo tongnai chwlarokno ringwi bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ani angsai a Hibruno chwngno lachi rwna bagwi se tubu si-o; bo ani nogo ano romphrena bagwi hap khe ang sotonwi chirik khe;

English: and when he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried, he left his garment with me, and fled and got out of the house.”
Kokborok: bo ani chirikmano khna khe bini rírokno ani thani khibiui klang kha tei nokni nongkhorwi khárwi tháng kha.”

English: Then she laid up his garment by her until his master came home,
Kokborok: Bini nokphang phai ya sak bo Josepni ríno narwgwi ton kha,

English: and she told him the same story, saying, “The Hebrew servant, whom you have brought among us, came in to me to insult me;
Kokborok: tei bo bono a bini a snamwi samani kókrokno hai hwnwi saui khna rw kha, “Nwng chini thani a Hibrusa selengno tubumani, bo ani thani phaiwi ano romphrena naiwi lachi rw kha;

English: but as soon as I lifted up my voice and cried, he left his garment with me, and fled out of the house.”
Kokborok: hakhe ang khorang sotonwi chirik khe tei kap khe, bo bini ríno ani oro khibiui klangwi nokni nongkhorwi khár kha.”

English: When his master heard the words which his wife spoke to him, “This is the way your servant treated me,” his anger was kindled.
Kokborok: Josepni nokphang a kókrokno bihikni khúgo, “Nai di nini seleng ano omo hai khe khlai kha,” hwnwi khna khe bo belai jolijak kha.

English: And Joseph's master took him and put him into the prison, the place where the king's prisoners were confined, and he was there in prison.
Kokborok: Tei Josepni nokphang Josepno romwi alongo chobwi rw kha, abo wng kha habugra bini borokrokno chopmani along, bo’-bo aro’-no tlangwi tonjak kha.

English: But the Lord was with Joseph and showed him steadfast love, and gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Subraiyung Josep bai baksa tongo tei bono kaham khe khairok kha, alongni barja bokhorok bono hamjak kha.

English: And the keeper of the prison committed to Joseph's care all the prisoners who were in the prison; and whatever was done there, he was the doer of it;
Kokborok: Hakhe alongni barja bokhorok Josepni yago bebak alongo tongnairokno nainani barja rwui rw kha tei aro khlaijakma samungrok bono abono khlai-o.

English: the keeper of the prison paid no heed to anything that was in Joseph's care, because the Lord was with him; and whatever he did, the Lord made it prosper.
Kokborok: Josepni yago rwma barjarokno a alongni barja bokhorok mungsa phano sakthar ya, tamni hwn ba Subraiyung Josep bai baksa tongo; tei bo tangma bebakno’-no samung cha rw-o.

English: Some time after this, the butler of the king of Egypt and his baker offended their lord the king of Egypt.
Kokborok: Aboni sal kisa ulo khe Ijipni habugrani khuri tẃinaisa tei auang snamnaisa habugrani thani samung hamya khlai kha.

English: And Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief butler and the chief baker,
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao o khuri tẃinaisa bai auang snamnaisarokni bokhorokno khoroknwirokno jolijak kha,

English: and he put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, in the prison where Joseph was confined.
Kokborok: tei bórokno along mwrwknai bokhorokni aro ton kha, arono Josep bo chopjak.

English: The captain of the guard charged Joseph with them, and he waited on them; and they continued for some time in custody.
Kokborok: Alongni barja bokhorok Josepno o borokroknobo naikolnani barja ber kha, hakhe bórok sal kolokmano alongo baksa khe tong lai kha.

English: And one night they both dreamed—the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison—each his own dream, and each dream with its own meaning.
Kokborok: Horsa khe habugrani khuri tẃinaisa tei auang snamnaisa alongo chopjak a khoroknwirok imang nuk lai kha; bórokni imang nukmanibo janija tei aboni gujokbo juda juda.

English: When Joseph came to them in the morning and saw them, they were troubled.
Kokborok: Josep phung ai khe bórokni thani phai khe bórok khoroknwino mok laijak nuk kha.

English: So he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him in custody in his master's house, “Why are your faces downcast today?”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo, bo bai baksa bini bugrani alongo chopjak Pharaoni a tẃiphangrokno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Tini tamni norokni bumkhang aswk mok laijak sla?”

English: They said to him, “We have had dreams, and there is no one to interpret them.” And Joseph said to them, “Do not interpretations belong to God? Tell them to me, I pray you.”
Kokborok:
Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng khoroknwi’-no imang kaisa kaisa nuk lai kha tei oro khoroksa phano a imangni gujokno saui mannai kwrwi.” Hakhe Josep bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha,
“Kaitor se gujokno le saui man rw-o ahai ya diba? Norokni imangni kókno ano sa ja bla.”

English: So the chief butler told his dream to Joseph, and said to him, “In my dream there was a vine before me,
Kokborok: Hakhe a khuri tẃinaisa bokhorok ahai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani a imango ang ani bwskango angur buphang nuk kha,

English: and on the vine there were three branches; as soon as it budded, its blossoms shot forth, and the clusters ripened into grapes.
Kokborok: aboni bedek dektham tongo; aro bwlái rotomsa kheno, bwkhrwi rwsaui bar kha tei bochop bochop khe angurrok mun kha.

English: Pharaoh's cup was in my hand; and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup, and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.”
Kokborok: Ang habugrani khurino tẃijak; hakhe ang angur naui sebwi a khuri-o rw kha tei abono bono yapharwi rw kha.”

English: Then Joseph said to him, “This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days;
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Aboni gujok khe omo hai: O kwtham bedek wng kha abo saltham hwnmani se;

English: within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your office; and you shall place Pharaoh's cup in his hand as formerly, when you were his butler.
Kokborok: salthamni salo habugra nono khocha phinai tei nono nini samungno rw phinai; hakhe nwng swkango khlaima hai nwng bono arakni khuri ma yaphar phinai.

English: But remember me, when it is well with you, and do me the kindness, I pray you, to make mention of me to Pharaoh, and so get me out of this house.
Kokborok: Tamo khe nini jora kaham phuru ano muitu khlai ja di, ano khairogwi habugrani thani ani kók kisa sa ja di tei ano o alongni yókna bagwi chuba ja di.

English: For I was indeed stolen out of the land of the Hebrews; and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.”
Kokborok: Nai di, ang Hibrurokni hani romwi tubujakma se tei o Ijip ha-obo ang mungsa phano omo hai khe alongo chopjaknai samung tangma kwrwi.”

English: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, “I also had a dream: there were three cake baskets on my head,
Kokborok: A auang snamnaisani bokhorok nai ba a khuri tẃinaini imangni kók swbai samani abo kaham’-no, hakhe bo Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Angbo imang kaisa nukma tongo: Ani bokhorogo khe, auangni ura kaitham rujujak,

English: and in the uppermost basket there were all sorts of baked food for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating it out of the basket on my head.”
Kokborok: tei sakani a ura-o khe Pharaoni banta podrepot auangrok tongo, tamo khe toksarok abono chá laiwi tongo.”

English: And Joseph answered, “This is its interpretation: the three baskets are three days;
Kokborok: Josep hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omoni gujok khe omo hai: A uraktham abo khe saltham hwnmani se;

English: within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head—from you!—and hang you on a tree; and the birds will eat the flesh from you.”
Kokborok: salthamni salo nini bokotok tansu-ui páinai!—hakhe bo nini mangno buphango khachiui rwnai tei tokrok nini bahan chánai.”

English: On the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, he made a feast for all his servants, and lifted up the head of the chief butler and the head of the chief baker among his servants.
Kokborok: Salni salthamni salo khe Pharaoni achaima sal, bo bini selengrokni bagwi chámung panda kaisa khlai kha; bo bini a khuri tẃinaino tei a auang snamnaini bokhorokrokno phiyógwi a tẃiphangrokni bwskango tubu kha.

English: He restored the chief butler to his butlership, and he placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand;
Kokborok: Bo a khuri tẃinaino khe bini samungno rw phi kha;

English: but he hanged the chief baker, as Joseph had interpreted to them.
Kokborok: tamo khe Josep sama hai bo a auang snamnai bokhorokno khe kotok soro rwui buthar kha.

English: Yet the chief butler did not remember Joseph, but forgot him.
Kokborok: Tamo khe a khuri tẃinaisa bokhorok Josepni kókno muitu wngjak li ya, bo bebak pogwi tháng kha.

English: After two whole years, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing by the Nile,
Kokborok: Bisiknwini ulo khe Pharao imang nuk kha, a imang Pharao bo Nil twima rukungo bachaui tongo,

English: and behold, there came up out of the Nile seven cows sleek and fat, and they fed in the reed grass.
Kokborok: nai ba mak sni musuk kuphung tei naithok twimani kasaui phaiwi jabra chá lai tongjak.

English: And behold, seven other cows, gaunt and thin, came up out of the Nile after them, and stood by the other cows on the bank of the Nile.
Kokborok: Hakhe tei musuk mak sni twimani kasaui phai khua; abórok khe keram tei bekreng sijak máng. Bórok phaiwi tei kubunrok bai baksa Nil twima rukungo bacha phai kha.

English: And the gaunt and thin cows ate up the seven sleek and fat cows. And Pharaoh awoke.
Kokborok: Tei a keram musuk mak snirok a musuk kuphung mak snirokno cháui pái kha. Hakhe Pharao sicha kha.

English: And he fell asleep and dreamed a second time; and behold, seven ears of grain, plump and good, were growing on one stalk.
Kokborok: Bo haino hai muktrwi tháng kha hakhe tei kaisa imang nuk kha; maitang tángsni, abo kupulung tei kumun, abórok kwrẃngsa-o’-no torwi tongo.

English: And behold, after them sprouted seven ears, thin and blighted by the east wind.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo nuk kha tei maitang tángsni nongkhorjak abo salkabaini nokbar bujak bukcha bwkrai kwrwi.

English: And the thin ears swallowed up the seven plump and full ears. And Pharaoh awoke, and behold, it was a dream.
Kokborok: Tei maitang bukcha a bwtang kupulungrokno monogwi pái kha. Hakhe Pharao thuma sicha kha tei bo imang nugwi tongmano saui man kha.

English: So in the morning his spirit was troubled; and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men; and Pharaoh told them his dream, but there was none who could interpret it to Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Hakhe phung ai khe bo uanajak kha; hakhe bo ha Ijipni bebak elem kwrwngrokno tei kók mwtai kwrwngrokno na rwhorwi bo bini imangni kókno bórokno saui khna rw kha, tamo khe khoroksa phano Pharaoni imangni kókno swbaiwi saui man li ya.

English: Then the chief butler said to Pharaoh, “I remember my faults today.
Kokborok: Hakhe a khuri tẃinaisa bokhorok Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang cha ya khlaimano ang tini muitu man kha.

English: When Pharaoh was angry with his servants, and put me and the chief baker in custody in the house of the captain of the guard,
Kokborok: Nwng nini seleng auang snamnai bokhorok tei ano jolijagwi chwngno along mwrwknai bokhorokni nogo ton phuru,

English: we dreamed on the same night, he and I, each having a dream with its own meaning.
Kokborok: horsa khe chwng khoroknwino imang nuk lai kha tei a imangni gujok juda juda.

English: A young Hebrew was there with us, a servant of the captain of the guard; and when we told him, he interpreted our dreams to us, giving an interpretation to each man according to his dream.
Kokborok: Aro chwng bai baksa khoroksa Hibru chwla sikla tongmani, bo wng kha a along mwrwknai bokhorokni seleng, chwng bono chini imangni kókrokno sa kha tei chwngno bo abono swbaiwi saui khna rw kha.

English: And as he interpreted to us, so it came to pass; I was restored to my office, and the baker was hanged.”
Kokborok: Bo sama hai’-no chini bebakno wng kha; nwng ano ani samungno rw phi kha, tamo khe a auang snamnaini bokhorokno khe kotok soro rwui buthar kha.”

English: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon; and when he had shaved himself and changed his clothes, he came in before Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Pharao Josepno ring rwui na rw hor kha, hakhe aphuru’-no bono alongni doui tubujak kha; bini khwnairok ra bai khe tei rí kwtalrok kan bai khe Pharaoni bwskango tháng kha.

English: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I have had a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it; and I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang imang kaisa nukmani, abono khoroksa phano swbaiwi saui man li ya; nwng imangni kókno swbaiwi saui mano hwnwi ang khna kha.”

English: Joseph answered Pharaoh, “It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh a favorable answer.”
Kokborok: Josep Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang le man ja ya; Kaitor se sói nangmani gujokno buchi rwnai.”

English: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Behold, in my dream I was standing on the banks of the Nile;
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “A imango ang Nil twimani rukungo bachaui tongmani;

English: and seven cows, fat and sleek, came up out of the Nile and fed in the reed grass;
Kokborok: aphuru musuk mak sni kuphung tei milik twini kasaui phaiwi jabra chá laiwi tong lai kha;

English: and seven other cows came up after them, poor and very gaunt and thin, such as I had never seen in all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: hakhe tei musuk mak sni kasaui phai kha abórok khe keram tei bekreng máng, ang abórok hai ha Ijibo nu se nuk ya kho.

English: And the thin and gaunt cows ate up the first seven fat cows,
Kokborok: A musuk keramrok swkangni a musuk kuphungrokno cháui pái kha,

English: but when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them, for they were still as gaunt as at the beginning. Then I awoke.
Kokborok: tamo khe bórok abono chá phano khoroksa phano abono saui man li ya, tamni hwn khe abórok swkangni hai’-no keram wngwi tong kha. Hakhe ang sicha kha.

English: I also saw in my dream seven ears growing on one stalk, full and good;
Kokborok: Ang omo hai imangbo nuk kha; maikong kongsa-o maitang tángsni abo bokol kupulung tei kaham kaham;

English: and seven ears, withered, thin, and blighted by the east wind, sprouted after them,
Kokborok: hakhe aboni yagulo khe tei maitang tángsni nongkhor kha, abórok khe kohoi tei abo salkabaini nokbar nangwi ramprajak tei bukubuk,

English: and the thin ears swallowed up the seven good ears. And I told it to the magicians, but there was no one who could explain it to me.”
Kokborok: a maitang kohoirok a kaham maitang bwtangno monogwi pái kha. Hakhe ang danda-baji phunuknairokno ani imangni kókno sa kha, tamo khe khoroksa phano ano abono sukhrubwi saui man li ya.”

English: Then Joseph said to Pharaoh, “The dream of Pharaoh is one; God has revealed to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Josep Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Pharaoni a imang kainwini gujok le kaisa se; Kaitor Pharaono bo tamo khlaina naiwi tong abono sa kha.

English: The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years; the dream is one.
Kokborok: A musuk kuphung mak sni wng kha abo nokha bisi sni tei a bokol kupulung maitang abobo nokha bisi sni’-no; abórokni gujokbo kaisa’-no.

English: The seven lean and gaunt cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears blighted by the east wind are also seven years of famine.
Kokborok: Tei ulni a mak sni musuk keram kasaui phaima bai salkabaini nokbar bujak a maitang kohoi abo wng kha nokha bisi sni asarini kók se sa-o.

English: It is as I told Pharaoh, God has shown to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
Kokborok: Omo ang Pharaono sama hai Kaitor Pharaono bo tamo khlaina naiwi tong abono phunukma se.

English: There will come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of Egypt,
Kokborok: Nai di, nokha bisi sni ha Ijibo belai’-no mai-khul tháinai,

English: but after them there will arise seven years of famine, and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt; the famine will consume the land,
Kokborok: aboni ulo khe nokha bisi sni belai kheno asari nangnai tei ha Ijibo aswkni mai-khul hammani kókrokno pogwi tháng anw; asari hano khrang-khrang khe ransa rwnai,

English: and the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of that famine which will follow, for it will be very grievous.
Kokborok: aro aswk kirimasingcha asari nangma bai a ha-o chámung-nungmung asuda tong langma kókrokno poknai, tamni hwn ba a asari belai khe belai kotor wngnai.

English: And the doubling of Pharaoh's dream means that the thing is fixed by God, and God will shortly bring it to pass.
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao a imangno uáinwi nukmani gujok wng kha; abo hai khe Kaitor khlai súknai hwnwi samani kók se tei Kaitor abono dakti’-no khlainai hwnma se.

English: Now therefore let Pharaoh select a man discreet and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi Pharao ha Ijipni tangphangni bagwi sinai nuknaini naikolphang khoroksa chong thung.

English: Let Pharaoh proceed to appoint overseers over the land, and take the fifth part of the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven plenteous years.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi nwng Pharao, ha gwnangwi naikolnai borokrokno chong di, hakhe ha Ijibo a nokha bisi sni kwbáng khe mai-khul tháima jorani bebakni khakbano narwk di.

English: And let them gather all the food of these good years that are coming, and lay up grain under the authority of Pharaoh for food in the cities, and let them keep it.
Kokborok: Tei bórokno a phainai bisi kaham jorani bebak chámungrok thumwi narwk thung tei bebak kamiyungo bórokni chámungni bagwi abórokno rúngnani suar rwui mwrwkna dagiui rw di.

English: That food shall be a reserve for the land against the seven years of famine which are to befall the land of Egypt, so that the land may not perish through the famine.”
Kokborok: A chámung khe ha Ijibo nokha bisi sni asari nangna hwnma jorani chámung wngnai, hakhe hani borokrok ma chá yaui komor glak.”

English: This proposal seemed good to Pharaoh and to all his servants.
Kokborok: O kókbitino Pharao tei bini tangphangrok chajak kha.

English: And Pharaoh said to his servants, “Can we find such a man as this, in whom is the Spirit of God?”
Kokborok: Tei Pharao bini selengrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “O borokni slái chúkjak Kaitorni Phola bai kupulung borok chwng tei buro mannai?”

English: So Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Since God has shown you all this, there is none so discreet and wise as you are;
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor nono omorok bebakno phunuk kha, aboni bagwi nini tei buini slái simung tei nukmung kwbáng le tei kwrwi;

English: you shall be over my house, and all my people shall order themselves as you command; only as regards the throne will I be greater than you.”
Kokborok: nwng ani hani tei ani bebak borokrok nini dagima kókrokno khnanai; achukthayungo achukna simi se ani suar kwbáng wngnai.”

English: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Behold, I have set you over all the land of Egypt.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di ang nono bebak ha Ijipni saka-o tang Dagiphang kotor chongo.”

English: Then Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's hand, and arrayed him in garments of fine linen, and put a gold chain about his neck;
Kokborok: Pharao bini yasini bugrani mari bujak yasitemno sokok kha tei abono Josepni yasi-o kan rwui rw kha, bono lilonni rí kaham chum rwui rw kha tei kotogo khe rangchakni bwtang soraui rw kha;

English: and he made him to ride in his second chariot; and they cried before him, “Bow the knee!” Thus he set him over all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: bono kasa rwna bagwi bugrani uldrop kaisa korai malkhungno kasana bagwi rw kha; tei bini bwskang bwskang khe bono bórom khlainai bodolrok bono tolwi tẃi lai kha tei, “Yáskú sung bai di!” Hwnwi chirik lai kha. Hakhe bo bono bebak ha Ijipni barja yaphar kha.

English: Moreover Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no man shall lift up hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.”
Kokborok: Pharao Josepno hai hwnwibo sa kha, “Ang Pharao, o ha Ijibo nono si rw yaui khoroksa phano mungsa swk yapribo séui man glak tei yakbo tisaui man glak.”

English: And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him in marriage Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On. So Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno Japhenat-panea hwnwi mung kwtal phar kha; tei On kamiyungni ochai Potipherani bwsajwk Asenat bai kái kha. Aboni ulo khe Josep bebak ha Ijibo kitingwi phaina bagwi nongkhor kha.

English: Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went through all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Josep Ijipni habugra Pharao bai samung berjak phuru bini umor nokha bisi thamchi wng kha. Bo ha Pharaoni aroni nongkhorwi thángwi bebak ha Ijip gwnangwi kitingwi nai kha.

English: During the seven plenteous years the earth brought forth abundantly,
Kokborok: A nokha bisi sni hamchama jora-o ha-o belaino kwbáng khe mai-khul thái kha,

English: and he gathered up all the food of the seven years when there was plenty in the land of Egypt, and stored up food in the cities; he stored up in every city the food from the fields around it.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo a bisini tháimani chámungrokno Ijip hani kamiyungrokni mayamnokrogo rúng bai kha; bo kamiyung gana-ginini huk-habarokni chámungrokno kamiyungrogo rúng bai kha.

English: And Joseph stored up grain in great abundance, like the sand of the sea, until he ceased to measure it, for it could not be measured.
Kokborok: Omo hai kheno Josep aro kwbángma mairok rúng kha, abo twijlangni hasing báng tẃi wng kha, bo rwtaui man ya sak jora rúng kha, tamni hwn ba abono rwtaui manna kók kwrwi kha.

English: Before the year of famine came, Joseph had two sons, whom Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, bore to him.
Kokborok: Asari nangnani swkang Josep Onni ochai Potipherani bwsajwk Asenat bai bwsa khoroknwi man kha.

English: Joseph called the name of the first-born Manasseh, “For,” he said, “God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house.”
Kokborok: Bo okrano khe Manase hwnwi mung phar kha, tamni hwnma le bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ano aswkni lengpromarokno tei aphani nokni bebakno pok rw kha”

English: The name of the second he called Ephraim, “For God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.”
Kokborok: Aboni yakorai károno khe Ephraim hwnwi mung phar kha, tamni hwn ba, “Kaitor ano ang birman manmani ha-o bwthái thái rw kha.”

English: The seven years of plenty that prevailed in the land of Egypt came to an end;
Kokborok: Ha Ijibo kwbángma khe manmani a nokha bisi snini jora páima bwkhak sók phai kha;

English: and the seven years of famine began to come, as Joseph had said. There was famine in all lands; but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
Kokborok: tei Josep samani hai’-no a bisisnini asari cheng kha. Tei bebak kubun ha-obo asari nang kha; tamo khe ha Ijip gwnangwi khe chámung kupulung’-no tong kha.

English: When all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread; and Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, “Go to Joseph; what he says to you, do.”
Kokborok: Ha Ijipni borokrok ma chá ya wngna cheng kheno, bórok Pharaoni thani chámungni bagwi chirik lai kha; hakhe Pharao bebak Ijipsarokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Josepni thani tháng di; tei bo samani tẃi’-no norok khlai di.”

English: So when the famine had spread over all the land, Joseph opened all the storehouses, and sold to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe in the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Asari belai khe nang kha tei abo ha gwnangwi’-no pírjak kha, hakhe Josep bebak mayam nokrokno phiyogwi rw kha tei Ijipsarokni thani mai phal kha.

English: Moreover, all the earth came to Egypt to Joseph to buy grain, because the famine was severe over all the earth.
Kokborok: Hayung gwnangwini borokrok Josepni thani chámung paina bagwi phai lai kha, tamni hwn khe ha gwnangwi’-no belai khe asari nang kha.

English: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why do you look at one another?”
Kokborok: Jakob ha Ijibo chámung tongo hwnwi saui man kheno bo bwsarokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tamo norok khoroksa tei khoroksani bumkhang simi nai lai tong?”

English: And he said, “Behold, I have heard that there is grain in Egypt; go down and buy grain for us there, that we may live, and not die.”
Kokborok: Bo hai hwnwibo sa kha, “Nai di ang ha Ijibo chámung tongo hwnwi khna-o; chwngno ma chá yaui thwinani yakni phiyókna bagwi kisa aro thángwi chámung paiwi naharwi phai di.”

English: So ten of Joseph's brothers went down to buy grain in Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josepni takhuk khorokchirok ha Ijibo chámung paina tháng lai kha.

English: But Jacob did not send Benjamin, Joseph's brother, with his brothers, for he feared that harm might befall him.
Kokborok: Tamo Jakob, Josepni achaisong buphayung Benjaminno khe bórok bai baksa rohor li ya, tamni hwn khe bini mungsa swk da wngno hwnwi bo kirijago.

English: Thus the sons of Israel came to buy among the others who came, for the famine was in the land of Canaan.
Kokborok: Ha Kanano asari nangma bagwi Israilni bwsarokbo tei bui bai baksa chámung paina bagwi tháng lai kha.

English: Now Joseph was governor over the land; he it was who sold to all the people of the land. And Joseph's brothers came, and bowed themselves before him with their faces to the ground.
Kokborok: Aphuru Josep khe hani tang dagiphang; bo ha gwnangwini borokrokni thani chámung phalwi tongo. Hakhe Josepni takhuksong phai lai kha tei bini bwskango phaiwi ha-o bolokhógwi khulum lai kha.

English: Joseph saw his brothers, and knew them, but he treated them like strangers and spoke roughly to them. “Where do you come from?” he said. They said, “From the land of Canaan, to buy food.”
Kokborok:
Josep bini takhuksongno nuk kheno bórok bebakno’-no siniui man kha, tamo khe bo bórokno sini ya hai khe tong kha tei “Norok buroni phai?” hwnwi khorang sokromwi sẃng kha. Aphuru khe bórok hai hwnwi sa kha:
“Chwng ha Kananni se chámung paina hwnwi phai-o.”

English: Thus Joseph knew his brothers, but they did not know him.
Kokborok: Josep bini takhuksongno siniui man phano, bórok bono siniui man li ya.

English: And Joseph remembered the dreams which he had dreamed of them; and he said to them, “You are spies, you have come to see the weakness of the land.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Josep bórokno tẃi imang nukmani kókno muitu manjak kha; tei bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok naichomnairok se, norok chini hani buro tamo kebel tong naina phaimani se.”

English: They said to him, “No, my lord, but to buy food have your servants come.
Kokborok: Bórok bono hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “I’nhi bugrasa nini o selengrok le chámung paina hwnwi phaijamani se.

English: We are all sons of one man, we are honest men, your servants are not spies.”
Kokborok: Chwng bebakno ok kaisani takhukrok se, chwng naichomnairok ya, chwng nini o selengrok borok kosong gwja se.”

English: He said to them, “No, it is the weakness of the land that you have come to see.”
Kokborok: Bo bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “I’nhi, norok chini hani buro tamo kebel tong naina phaima se.”

English: And they said, “We, your servants, are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is no more.”
Kokborok: Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chwng nini selengrok bebagwi takhuk chinwi ká-o, Kanan ha-o tongnai borok khoroksani’-no bwsa. Takhuk khoroksa khe thwi kha tei kusu kuk khe tabuk aphani aro.”

English: But Joseph said to them, “It is as I said to you, you are spies.
Kokborok: Tamo khe Josep bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok ang sama hai’-no naichomnairok se.

English: By this you shall be tested: by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go from this place unless your youngest brother comes here.
Kokborok: Norokno omo hai khe se potalinai: ang Pharaoni rangmani mungwi swmai tangwi sa-o, norokni takhuk kusu oro phai ya sak norok oroni kutulwi thángwi man glak.

English: Send one of you, and let him bring your brother, while you remain in prison, that your words may be tested, whether there is truth in you; or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies.”
Kokborok: Norokni bere khoroksa thángwi bono tubu-ui ma phainai, teirok khe bebakno oro chopjagwi ma tongnai, omo tẃi khe se norok samani kók kubui de ereng ma phunuknai; wng ya khe Pharaoni rangmani mungwi swmai tangwi sa-o kubu kubui’-no norok naichomnairok se.”

English: And he put them all together in prison for three days.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo bórokno saltham alongo chobwi ton kha.

English: On the third day Joseph said to them, “Do this and you will live, for I fear God:
Kokborok: Salthamni salo khe Josep bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Kaitorno kirinai borok, ang norokno omo hai khe le phiyógwi mano:

English: if you are honest men, let one of your brothers remain confined in your prison, and let the rest go and carry grain for the famine of your households,
Kokborok: tumung norok borok kaham’-no hwnwi sijakna hwn khe, norokni bere khoroksano alongo klangwi tei kubunrok khe norokni ma chá yaui tongnai núkhungni borokrokni thani a chámung tlangwi tháng di

English: and bring your youngest brother to me; so your words will be verified, and you shall not die.” And they did so.
Kokborok: tei norokni bere kusu kuk takhukno ani thani tubu-ui phai di; ahai khe se norok kók kubui sa-o hwnwi saui mannai tei norokno butharjak glak.” Hakhe bórokbo abo hai’-no khlai lai kha.

English: Then they said to one another, “In truth we are guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he besought us and we would not listen; therefore is this distress come upon us.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórok khoroksa tei khoroksano hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Kubui’-no tabuk chwng manjakmani le chini takhukni bati nangma se; bo tekto nangjak phuru bwswk se chwngno chubana bagwi koi kha phiya ba chwng aphuru khna lai li ya, aboni bagwino chini saka-o omo tẃi birman klai si-o.”

English: And Reuben answered them, “Did I not tell you not to sin against the lad? But you would not listen. So now there comes a reckoning for his blood.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Ruben bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang norokno a chwraino ta dera rw di hwnwi sa ya da? Hai phano norok khunju kan ya gwja. Hakhe tabuk chwng bini thẃini yagul se sujagwi tongo.”

English: They did not know that Joseph understood them, for there was an interpreter between them.
Kokborok: Bórok Josepno bórokni kók buchi-o hwnwi si li ya, tamni hwn ba khoroksa kók slái sanai bai khe se bo bórok bai kók sa-o.

English: Then he turned away from them and wept; and he returned to them and spoke to them. And he took Simeon from them and bound him before their eyes.
Kokborok: Josep bórokni aroni kutulwi thángwi kabwi tongwi kha; ulo khe bo bórokni aro kiphil phi kha tei bórok bai kók sa lai kha, hakhe bo Simionno bórokni bisingni khagwi bórokni mokol bwskango kha ka.

English: And Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, and to replace every man's money in his sack, and to give them provisions for the journey. This was done for them.
Kokborok: Josep bini takhuksongni kholjango chámung supungwi da di hwnwi dagi kha tei bórokni a kholjango bórokni a rangrokni solanobo daui rw di hwnwi dagi kha, phiya lamani bagwi bórokni chámungbo rohor di hwnwi sa kha. Hakhe sama hai kheno khlaijak kha.

English: Then they loaded their asses with their grain, and departed.
