Kokborok: Hakhe bórok chámungrokno korai chirka saka-o boksa laiwi lam rw lai kha.

English: And as one of them opened his sack to give his ass provender at the lodging place, he saw his money in the mouth of his sack;
Kokborok: Bórok kwrẃngsa bathakthai-o bathagwi kha tei bórokni bere khoroksa kholjang khengwi korai chirkano chá rwna hwnwi nai khe bini a rang búchúno kholjangni búkhúgo khe nuk kha;

English: and he said to his brothers, “My money has been put back; here it is in the mouth of my sack!” At this their hearts failed them, and they turned trembling to one another, saying, “What is this that God has done to us?”
Kokborok: aphuru khe bo bini takhuksongno hai hwnwi sa kha “Ani rang ano kiphilwi rohorjak kha; iko oro ani o rang búchú!” Aphuru khe bórokni bwkha báijak kha, hakhe khoroksa tei khoroksa kwlwibrajak khe hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Kaitorba omo chwngno tamo khlai lang?”

English: When they came to Jacob their father in the land of Canaan, they told him all that had befallen them, saying,
Kokborok: Bórok ha Kanano bupha Jakobni thani phai sók phai khe, bórok bono aro wngmani bebak kókrok hai hwnwi saui khna rw kha,

English: “The man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly to us, and took us to be spies of the land.
Kokborok: “ha Ijipni tangdagiphang kotor chwng bai sokromwi se kók sa-o tei chwngno a hano naichomna bagwi thángo hwnwi majra ber kha.

English: But we said to him, ‘We are honest men, we are not spies;
Kokborok: Chwng naichomnai ya hwnwi bono sa phi kha, ‘chwng borok kaham se;

English: we are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is no more, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan.’
Kokborok: chwng bebagwi takhuk chiknwi tei bupha kaisani’-no bwsa, takhuk khoroksa khe thwi kha tei kusu kuk khe tabuk ha Kanano apha bai.’

English: Then the man, the lord of the land, said to us, ‘By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers with me, and take grain for the famine of your households, and go your way.
Kokborok: Hakhe a hani tang dagiphang hai hwnwi sa phi kha, ‘Omo hai khe se ang norokno borok kosong hwnwi hwnnai: norokni bere khoroksano oro ang bai klang di, teirok khe norokni núkhungni ma chá ya tongnairokni bagwi chámung tlangwi tháng di.

English: Bring your youngest brother to me; then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver to you your brother, and you shall trade in the land.’ ”
Kokborok: Hakhe norokni takhuk kusuno ani thani tubu-ui phai di; ahai khe se ang norokno kubui’-no naichomnairok ya tamo khe borok kaham hwnwi ang hwnnai tei ang norokni takhukno phiyóknai tei norokbo omo hai khe oro pai-phalrok khlaiwi mannai.’ ”

English: As they emptied their sacks, behold, every man's bundle of money was in his sack; and when they and their father saw their bundles of money, they were dismayed.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok bórokni sak sakni kholjang kheng khe bebakno kholjango khe bórokni rang búchu man lai kha; tei a rangno nuk lai khe bórok tei bupha abono nuk khe kiri lai kha.

English: And Jacob their father said to them, “You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin; all this has come upon me.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórokni bupha Jakob bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok ani bwsarokno kwma rw bai kha: Josep kwrwi kha; Simionbo kwrwi kha; hakhe norok tabuk Benjaminnobo tlangna nai tong-u kha; ang’-no manjaknai le wng si-o!”

English: Then Reuben said to his father, “Slay my two sons if I do not bring him back to you; put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Ruben buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang bono nini oro kiphilwi tubu ya khe ani sajla khoroknwino butharwi pái di; tabuk bono se ani yago yapharwi rohor di ang bono nini thani kiphilwi tubu anw.”

English: But he said, “My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he only is left. If harm should befall him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani sajla nwng bai thángwi man glak; bwtabo thwi kha, tabuk bo simi se khoroksa tong si-o. Lamarogo bini mungsa swk wng khe ani bwkhnai phurwi thwina jora-o nwng ano bwkha-o teibo nang rwui se ano mangkhoro hapson rw sinai.”

English: Now the famine was severe in the land.
Kokborok: Aphuru a ha-o belai khe asari nang kha.

English: And when they had eaten the grain which they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go again, buy us a little food.”
Kokborok: Tei bórok ha Ijipni tubuma bebak chámungrokno cháui pái khe bórokni bupha hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di chini bagwi tei kisa chámung paiwi tubu di.”

English: But Judah said to him, “The man solemnly warned us, saying, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’
Kokborok: Tamo khe Juda bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “A borok chwngno kwrak khe ragdama khe sa ma tongo, ‘Norok norokni takhukno logi-o tubu ya khe ani mwkhang nuk glak.’

English: If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food;
Kokborok: Tumung nwng nwslano chwng bai baksa rohor khe chwng thángwi nini bagwi chámung paiwi tubunai;

English: but if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’ ”
Kokborok:
tamo khe nwng chini takhukno rohor ya khe chwng aro thángwi man glak, tamni hwn khe a borok ‘norokni takhuk norok bai kwrwi khe ani mwkhang nuk glak’ hwnwi sama tongo.’ ”
Israil hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tamo norok chini tei

English: Israel said, “Why did you treat me so ill as to tell the man that you had another brother?”
Kokborok: khoroksa takhuk tongo hwnwi a borokno saui ano tabuk aswk tekto nang rw lang?”

English: They replied, “The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred, saying, ‘Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?’ What we told him was in answer to these questions; could we in any way know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother down’?”
Kokborok: Bórok bono kók phirogwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “A borok chini kókno tei chini núkhungni kókno tẃi ahai hwnwi sẃng kha, ‘Norokni nwpha kwthang da tong kho? Norokni tei achaisong takhuk da tong kho?’ omorokno sẃngma bagwi se omorokno chwng ma sa-o; chwng bahai khe bono ‘Norokni takhukno oro tubu di’ hwnwi sinai ba.”

English: And Judah said to Israel his father, “Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die, both we and you and also our little ones.
Kokborok: Hakhe Juda bupha Israilno hai hwnwi sa kha, “O chwraino ang bai khe rohor di tei hakhe chwng lam rwnai; hakhe se nwng tei chwng tei chini chwrairok thwinani ma yóknai.

English: I will be surety for him; of my hand you shall require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame for ever;
Kokborok: Ang bini barja gosi-o; bono nwng ani yago mannai. Ang bono nini bwskango kaham kwrwng khe tubu-ui man ya khe nwng ano kusung kusung aboni majra ber phlai máng tong di;

English: for if we had not delayed, we would now have returned twice.”
Kokborok: chwng omo hai khe lerwi tong ya khe tabuk jora le uáinwi se thángwi phai man kha mun.”

English: Then their father Israel said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry down to the man a present, a little balm and a little honey, gum, myrrh, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórokni bupha Israil bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Omo hai hwn khe, ahai se khlai di, kholjangrogo khe norok a borokni banta chini ha-o wngma kaham kuk bwtháirok, resin kisa, piya bwtwi, motom mosola, thok motom, badam tei almondrok tlangwi thángwi hili yapharwi di.

English: Take double the money with you; carry back with you the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks; perhaps it was an oversight.
Kokborok: Tei norok rangbo duna khe tlangwi tháng di; kholjang búkhúgo khe rang dajak khe kiphilwi manma rangroknobo tlangwi tháng di; abo nukslap yaui phano rohorjak khna.

English: Take also your brother, and arise, go again to the man;
Kokborok: Norokni takhuknobo tlangwi a borokni aro tháng phi di,

English: may God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, that he may send back your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.”
Kokborok: Kwplaisa Kaitor a borokno norokno khairokna bwkha rw thung, omo hai khe Benjaminno tei norokni takhukno kiphilwi rohor thung. Ang ani angsarokno kwmana nang khe kwphano kwma si thung.”

English: So the men took the present, and they took double the money with them, and Benjamin; and they arose and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok hilirok na lai kha, rangbo duna kha tei Benjaminno tẃiwi ha Ijip bwkhak lam rw lai kha, hakhe bórok ha Ijibo thángwi Josepni bwskango bachaui lai kha.

English: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, “Bring the men into the house, and slaughter an animal and make ready, for the men are to dine with me at noon.”
Kokborok: Josep Benjaminno bórok bai baksa nuk khe bini nokni samung tangnaino ahai hwnwi sa kha, “O borokrokno ani nok bisingo tlang di tei mui ratharwi chámung tiyar khlai di, o borokrok diborni chámung ang bai baksa chánai.”

English: The man did as Joseph bade him, and brought the men to Joseph's house.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe a seleng Josep dagima hai kheno bórokno Josepni nogo tlangwi tháng kha.

English: And the men were afraid because they were brought to Joseph's house, and they said, “It is because of the money, which was replaced in our sacks the first time, that we are brought in, so that he may seek occasion against us and fall upon us, to make slaves of us and seize our asses.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Josepni nogo tubujak khe kirijak lai kha tei hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Chwng omo khe swkango a chini kholjango daui kiphilwi rohormani rangni bagwi se wng khna, hakhe bo tabuk chwngno achomsa khe swpragwi chini korai chirkarokno segwi naui páiwi chwngno bini seleng snamnai khna.”

English: So they went up to the steward of Joseph's house, and spoke with him at the door of the house,
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Josepni a selengni nok duglamo phai lai kha,

English: and said, “Oh, my lord, we came down the first time to buy food;
Kokborok: tei hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “O bubagrasa, chwng swkango oro uáisa chámung paina bagwi phai kha;

English: and when we came to the lodging place we opened our sacks, and there was every man's money in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; so we have brought it again with us,
Kokborok: tei chwng bathak thai-o sógwi khe chini kholjangno kheng phuru, bebakni kholjang búkhúgo khe rangno man kha, hakhe chwng abórokno bebakno tubu-ui phai phi kha,

English: and we have brought other money down in our hand to buy food. We do not know who put our money in our sacks.”
Kokborok: tei chwng teibo chámung paina bagwibo alaida rangbo tubu kha. Sabo ba chini kholjango a rangrokno tonwi rw kha chwng saui man li ya.”

English: He replied, “Rest assured, do not be afraid; your God and the God of your father must have put treasure in your sacks for you; I received your money.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
Kokborok: A seleng hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok ta uana di, ta kiri di; norokni Kaitor tei norokni nwphani Kaitor se norokni kholjango a rangrokno ton khna; ang norokni rangno man kha.” Hakhe bo Simionno bórokni thani tubu-ui phai kha.

English: And when the man had brought the men into Joseph's house, and given them water, and they had washed their feet, and when he had given their asses provender,
Kokborok: A borok a borokrokno Josepni nogo tubu-ui phai khe bórokno twirok rwjak kha tei yakungrok sú lai kha tei bórokni korai chirkarokno chámungrok rwjak kha,

English: they made ready the present for Joseph's coming at noon, for they heard that they should eat bread there.
Kokborok: bórok Josep diboro sók phai kheno bórokni hili rwna bagwi tiyar khe ton lai kha, tamni hwn khe bórok bo bai baksa chánani bagwi sajak.

English: When Joseph came home, they brought into the house to him the present which they had with them, and bowed down to him to the ground.
Kokborok: Josep nogo phai khe, bórok nok bisingo bórokni hilirok tlangwi thángwi bono bomwi ha-o klai khulum lai kha.

English: And he inquired about their welfare, and said, “Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?”
Kokborok: Bo bórok kaham kwrwng da tong bai hwnwi sẃngwi, “Norok nwpha chakrasani kók samani bo le kaham kwrwng da tong? Bo kwthang tong khona bla?” hwnwi sẃng kha.

English: They said, “Your servant our father is well, he is still alive.” And they bowed their heads and made obeisance.
Kokborok: Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Chini apha nini selengsa kwthang’-no tongo tei kaham kwrwngno tong.” Hakhe bórok bono khorok bomwi bórom rwui khulum lai kha.

English: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother's son, and said, “Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me? God be gracious to you, my son!”
Kokborok: Josep bini bumani bwsa tei achaisong takhuk Benjaminno nuk khe hai hwnwi sa kha, “O, omo da norokni takhuk kusu hwnwi sama a borok? Kaitor nono hamari rw thung sajla!”

English: Then Joseph made haste, for his heart yearned for his brother, and he sought a place to weep. And he entered his chamber and wept there.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep aroni doui kutulwi tháng kha, tamni hwn khe bo buphayungno belaino khátangjak kha. Hakhe bo kapna jaga rutuk kha. Bo bini khotolo habwi kabwi kha.

English: Then he washed his face and came out; and controlling himself he said, “Let food be served.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bo bini mwkhang sú-ui nongkhorwi phai kha; tei sakno methebwi “Khalai khlai si di” hwnwi dagi kha.

English: They served him by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians might not eat bread with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.
Kokborok: Bo bini bagwi tei bini takhuksongni bagwibo juda khe, phiya bo bai chánai Ijipsarokni bagwi tebul juda-o khe chámung rw kha, tamni hwn ba Ijipsarok Hibrurok bai thansa khe chá lai ya, abo bórokni bagwi selengmasingcha samung wngjago.

English: And they sat before him, the first-born according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth; and the men looked at one another in amazement.
Kokborok: Bórok Josepni bwkhak bumkhang khlaiwi okrani simi kusu jora mareui achuk lai kha; tei khoroksa tei khoroksano mwlang chaui naisik laiwi tong lai kha.

English: Portions were taken to them from Joseph's table, but Benjamin's portion was five times as much as any of theirs. So they drank and were merry with him.
Kokborok: Bórokno Josepni tebulni mai-muirok naui bokjak kha, tamo khe Benjaminno khe buini slái teibo bakba kwbáng khe bokjak kha. Hakhe bórok bo bai baksa tongthogwi chá lai kha-nung lai kha.

English: Then he commanded the steward of his house, “Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man's money in the mouth of his sack,
Kokborok: Josep bini nokni seleng bokhorokno hai hwnwi dagiui sa kha, “O borokrokni kholjangrokno chakma swkno tei bórok páisakno supungwi da di tei bórokni rangrokno khe kholjangni búkhúgo khe daui rohor di,

English: and put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, with his money for the grain.” And he did as Joseph told him.
Kokborok: ani rangchakni khurino khe rang bai baksa khe a kusu kukni kholjango daui rohor di.” Hakhe bo dagima hai kheno khlai kha.

English: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away with their asses.
Kokborok: Phung aidrobo khe takhukrokno bórokni korai chirka bai khe lam rw rwui rohor kha.

English: When they had gone but a short distance from the city, Joseph said to his steward, “Up, follow after the men; and when you overtake them, say to them, ‘Why have you returned evil for good? Why have you stolen my silver cup?
Kokborok: Bórok kamiyungni kisa hakchalo sógwi khe, Josep bini nokni seleng bokhorokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “A borokrokni uklogo do lang gra di; nwng bórokno malai kheno ahai hwnwi hwn di, ‘norok tamni bagwi samung kahamni ba omo hai khe samung hamya khlaiwi su kha de? norok tamni bagwi rangchakni khurino ma khok lang?

English: Is it not from this that my lord drinks, and by this that he divines? You have done wrong in so doing.’ ”
Kokborok: Abo bai ani nokphangsa nubo nungo tei abo bono samung kwtharobo thepamani khuri ya diba? Norok omo tẃi samung tangwi samung hamya khlai kha.’ ”

English: When he overtook them, he spoke to them these words.
Kokborok: A seleng a borokrokno swlabwi malai khe, bo a kókno risaui sa kha.

English: They said to him, “Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants that they should do such a thing!
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórok bono uphilwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nokphangsa tamni omo tẃi kókrok sa trwng? Nini o selengrok ba bahai khe omo tẃi samungrokno ba khlainai!

English: Behold, the money which we found in the mouth of our sacks, we brought back to you from the land of Canaan; how then should we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?
Kokborok: Nai di, chwng chini kholjango rang manmano Kanan hani se nini thani kiphilwi rw phai kha; hakhe chwng bahai khe nini nokphangni rangchak eba rukphaino khoknai?

English: With whomever of your servants it be found, let him die, and we also will be my lord's slaves.”
Kokborok: Nini o selengrokni bisingo khoroksa swkni aro abo manjak khe bo ma thwinai tei chwngbo bugrasani seleng wngwi pái si thung.”

English: He said, “Let it be as you say: he with whom it is found shall be my slave, and the rest of you shall be blameless.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ao, nwng sama tẃi’-no wng thung: ang khuri nanaino ang ani seleng snamnai tei kubunrokno khe majra kwrwi hwnwi phiyógwi rwjaknai.”

English: Then every man quickly lowered his sack to the ground, and every man opened his sack.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok bebakno doui bórokni kholjangrokno ha-o tikhlai lai kha tei abono kheng lai kha.

English: And he searched, beginning with the eldest and ending with the youngest; and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
Kokborok: A borok okrani simi mareui kusuni kholjango khe abono naituk kha; tei a khurino Benjaminni kholjango khe man kha.

English: Then they rent their clothes, and every man loaded his ass, and they returned to the city.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórok sak sakni rírok chir lai kha tei korai chirka saka-o manwi boksaui kamiyung bwkhak kiphilwi phai lai kha.

English: When Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, he was still there; and they fell before him to the ground.
Kokborok: Juda tei bini takhuksong Josepni nogo kiphilwi phai khe nuk kha, aphurubo bo aro’-no tong kho; hakhe bórok bono ha-o botok khe klaiwi khulum lai kha.

English: Joseph said to them, “What deed is this that you have done? Do you not know that such a man as I can indeed divine?”
Kokborok: Josep bórokno hai hwn kha, “Norok omo tamo khlai kha? Norok tamo saui man ya da ang tẃi borok mwtaini suar bai sema naiwi omorokno bebakno tikhlai mano?”

English: And Judah said, “What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we clear ourselves? God has found out the guilt of your servants; behold, we are my lord's slaves, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found.”
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Juda hai hwnwi sa kha “Chwng ba chini bugrano tei tamo sa sinai? Eba chwng bahai khe chini majra kwrwi hwnwi phunugui man sinai? Kaitor nini o selengrokni majrano phunugui rw kha; nai di, a khuri manjakma borok tei chwng bebakno tabuk nini seleng wng kha.”

English: But he said, “Far be it from me that I should do so! Only the man in whose hand the cup was found shall be my slave; but as for you, go up in peace to your father.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang omo hai samungrok ta ma khlai thung! A khuri manjakmani borok simi se ani seleng wngnai; phiya norok khe bebakno kaham kwrwng khe norokni nwphani thani kiphilwi tháng di.”

English: Then Judah went up to him and said, “O my lord, let your servant, I pray you, speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not your anger burn against your servant; for you are like Pharaoh himself.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Juda bini samo thángwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “O bubagrasa nwng le Pharao bai baksa, nini o selengno nini thani kók kisa sanani rw ja di, ano ta joli ja di.

English: My lord asked his servants, saying, ‘Have you a father, or a brother?’
Kokborok: Hodasa, nini selengrokno ‘Norokni nwpha tei takhuk da tong kho?’ Hwnwi sẃng kha.

English: And we said to my lord, ‘We have a father, an old man, and a young brother, the child of his old age; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children; and his father loves him.’
Kokborok: Hakhe chwng bugrasano ‘chini apha chakrajak tongo tei bo bini a chakra umoro bini bwsa kusuno manmani chini phayung kusu tongo; o chwraini takhuk khe thwi kha tei bo simi’-no tabuk bini bumani bwsa tongsi-o; bupha bono belai kheno hamjago’ hwnwi ma sa kha.

English: Then you said to your servants, ‘Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes upon him.’
Kokborok: Aphuru khe nwng nini o selengno sa kha, ‘Bono oro tubu di ang bono mokol baithang nai grana.’

English: We said to my lord, ‘The lad cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.’
Kokborok: Chwng bugrasano sa kha, ‘A chwrai buphano yakarwi man glak, tumungno bo buphano yakarwi kabu khe bupha thwiwi páinai.’

English: Then you said to your servants, ‘Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall see my face no more.’
Kokborok: Aphuru khe nwng nini o selengrokno ahai hwnwi sa-u kha, ‘Norokni takhukno norok bai baksa tubu ya khe ani bwskango ta nukjak phai di.’

English: When we went back to your servant my father we told him the words of my lord.
Kokborok: Chwng nini seleng chini aphani thani kiphilwi tháng khe bugrasa samani kókrokno chwng bono sa kha.

English: And when our father said, ‘Go again, buy us a little food,’
Kokborok: Hakhe bo chwngno ‘Tháng di chámung kisa paiwi nai phi di’ hwnwi hwn khe

English: we said, ‘We cannot go down. If our youngest brother goes with us, then we will go down; for we cannot see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us.’
Kokborok: chwng bono hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘Chwng thángwi man glak, chini takhuk kusuno chwng bai baksa tháng khe se chwng thángwi mannai; tamni hwn ba chini takhuk kusu chwng bai tháng ya khe chwng bo bai malai man glak.’

English: Then your servant my father said to us, ‘You know that my wife bore me two sons;
Kokborok: Aphuru khe nini seleng chini apha chwngno hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘Norok saui mano ani hik ani bagwi bwsa chwla khoroknwi se bá-o;

English: one left me, and I said, Surely he has been torn to pieces; and I have never seen him since.
Kokborok: aboni bere khoroksa swkang’-no ano khibiui klang kha. Bo bolongni manwirok bai bahan swkagwi phondrik-phandrak khlaijak khna hwnwi ang man hwno; aphuruni simi bono ang tei nuk li ya.

English: If you take this one also from me, and harm befalls him, you will bring down my gray hairs in sorrow to Sheol.’
Kokborok: Tabuk norok bonobo ani oroni tlangwi tháng khe tei bini saka-o mungsa swk hamya pati khe, hakhe norok ani khwnai phurwi chakraui tongma jora-o se teibo bwkha tekto rwui norok ano thwima-o se khiklai sinai.’

English: Now therefore, when I come to your servant my father, and the lad is not with us, then, as his life is bound up in the lad's life,
Kokborok: “Aboni bagwi tabuk, ang nini seleng aphani thani kiphilwi tháng phuru tumungno o chwrai chwng bai kwrwi hwn khe, chini aphani langma khe o chwrai bai se kwrwngjakba,

English: when he sees that the lad is not with us, he will die; and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.
Kokborok: o chwraino chwng bai nuk ya khe le, bo thwiwi páinai; hakhe nini o seleng chini khwnai kuphur aphano bwkha swbaiwi mangkhoro sógwi rw sinai.

English: For your servant became surety for the lad to my father, saying, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame in the sight of my father all my life.’
Kokborok: Tamni hwn ba nini o seleng o chwraino kaham kwrwng kheno tubunai hwnwi aphano ahai hwnwi saui kók rwjak, ‘Bono nini thani kiphilwi tubu-ui man ya hwn khe, ang nini thani aboni majrano thwi yasakni bagwi ruju-ui nanai.’

English: Now therefore, let your servant, I pray you, remain instead of the lad as a slave to my lord; and let the lad go back with his brothers.
Kokborok: Hakhe tabuk, o bugrasa, ang nini selengno o chwraini sak slái-o nini thani seleng snamwi narwgwi o chwraino bini takhuksong bai baksa khe kiphilwi rohor ja di hwnwi ang nini selengsa nono koi ja-o.

English: For how can I go back to my father if the lad is not with me? I fear to see the evil that would come upon my father.”
Kokborok: O chwrai ang bai baksa kwrwi hwn khe ang bahai khe ani aphani thani kiphilwi thángnai? Ang aphani saka-o hamya patima nukna kiri-o.”

English: Then Joseph could not control himself before all those who stood by him; and he cried, “Make every one go out from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Josep bini bwkhano tei bini gana-o khe bachaui tongnairokni bwskango soiwi tongwi man li ya; hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani bwskangni kutul bai di.” Hakhe Josep bini takhuksongno bo sabo sa phuru aro tei bui khoroksabo kwrwi kha.

English: And he wept aloud, so that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.
Kokborok: Bo sotonwi chirigwi kap kha, hakhe Ijipsarok abono khna kha tei a kók Pharaoni nokni borokrokbo a kókno khna kha.

English: And Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph; is my father still alive?” But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed at his presence.
Kokborok: Josep bini takhuksongno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Josep se; ani apha tabukbo kwthang tong kho hai da?” Tamo khe bini takhuksong bini bwskango kók tháisa phano saui man li ya kwlwibraui se tong si-o.

English: So Joseph said to his brothers, “Come near to me, I pray you.” And they came near. And he said, “I am your brother, Joseph, whom you sold into Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bini takhuksongno hai hwnwi sa kha, “ani samo phai ja di.” aphuru khe bórok bini samo tháng lai kha. Bo hai hwnwibo sa kha, “Norok ha Ijibo phalwi rohor laimani ang norokni a takhuk Josep se.

English: And now do not be distressed, or angry with yourselves, because you sold me here; for God sent me before you to preserve life.
Kokborok: “Tabuk norok bwkha hamya ta wngjak di, eba norok ano oro phalwi rohoro hwnwi sak sakno majra ta ber di; omo Kaitor se ano norokni slái swkang borokrokni langmano mwthangna bagwi rwi rohoro.

English: For the famine has been in the land these two years; and there are yet five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvest.
Kokborok: O ha-o asari nangmani bisik nwi se wng kho; tei bisi kaiba asari tong kho aphuru tei al baimabo tong glak tei ma ramabo tong glak.

English: And God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors.
Kokborok: Kaitor norokno tei kwbángmano o ha saka-o mwthangwi narwkna bagwi ano norokni slái swkang rwui rohor kha.

English: So it was not you who sent me here, but God; and he has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi oro norok ano rohor ya, tamo khe Kaitor se ano oro rohoro; bo oro ano Pharaoni bupha hai snam kha tei bini nokni, bini Ijip hani tangdagiphang snam kha.

English: Make haste and go up to my father and say to him, ‘Thus says your son Joseph, God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not tarry;
Kokborok: Aphani thani do lang si di tei bono ahai hwnwi saui di, ‘Nwsla Josep hai hwnwi sa-o; Kaitor ano bebak ha Ijipni tẃiphang snam kha; ani thani dwk yaui phai si di;

English: you shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children's children, and your flocks, your herds, and all that you have;
Kokborok: nwng Goseno khe tong phai di, omo hai khe nwng ani samo tongwi mannai, nwng bai norokni bwsarok, norokni bwsani bwsarok, norokni merarok, norokni punrok, norokni musuk tok-nokrok;

English: and there I will provide for you, for there are yet five years of famine to come; lest you and your household, and all that you have, come to poverty.’
Kokborok: aro ang chámungrok rwnai, tamni hwn ba teibo nokha bisik ba asari tongnai kho; wng ya khe nwng tei norokni núkhungni borokrok tei nini mal-matarok ma chá yaui ma tongnai se.’

English: And now your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see, that it is my mouth that speaks to you.
Kokborok: Tabuk ang samano norok bebakno mokol baithang nuk kha tei angphayung Benjaminbo nuk súk kha.

English: You must tell my father of all my splendor in Egypt, and of all that you have seen. Make haste and bring my father down here.”
Kokborok: Ani aphano thángwi saui di, ang ha Ijibo bwswk se suar tẃi tong tei norok bebak nugwi thángmani kókrokno saui di.”

English: Then he fell upon his brother Benjamin's neck and wept; and Benjamin wept upon his neck.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bo bini takhuk Benjaminno gwdna-o kwbagwi kap kha; bo bai kwbakjak kheno Benjaminbo kap kha.

English: And he kissed all his brothers and wept upon them; and after that his brothers talked with him.
Kokborok: Bo tei bini takhukroknobo kwbagwi motom su kha tei kap kha; aboni ulo khe bini takhuksongbo bo bai kók sanani cheng kha.

English: When the report was heard in Pharaoh's house, “Joseph's brothers have come,” it pleased Pharaoh and his servants well.
Kokborok: Pharao tei bini nokni borokrok, “Josepni takhuksong phai-o” hwnwi khna khe bo tei bini selengrok bwkha tongthokjak bai kha.

English: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Say to your brothers, ‘Do this: load your beasts and go back to the land of Canaan;
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini takhuksongno sa di, ‘Korai chirka saka-o chámung kupulung khe boksaui ha Kanan bwkhak tháng lai thung;

English: and take your father and your households, and come to me, and I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you shall eat the fat of the land.’
Kokborok: hakhe nwphano tei bórokni núkhungni borokrokno tubu-ui oro kiphilwi phai thung, ang o ha Ijipni kaham kuk hano bórokno rwnai, omo hai khe bórok o hani kaham kuk chámungno cháui mannai.’

English: Command them also, ‘Do this: take wagons from the land of Egypt for your little ones and for your wives, and bring your father, and come.
Kokborok: Bórokno ahai hwnwibo dagi di, ‘Norokni núkhungni bihik bwsarokni bagwi ha Ijipni malkhungo khe cháthai tlang thung tei bórok bai baksa nwphano tubu-ui phai thung.

English: Give no thought to your goods, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.’ ”
Kokborok: Bórokni manwi-khwnwirokni bagwi khá chingching ta ma tongjak thung, tamni hwn ba ha Ijipni kaham kuk hano bórokni wngnai.’ ”

English: The sons of Israel did so; and Joseph gave them wagons, according to the command of Pharaoh, and gave them provisions for the journey.
Kokborok: Israilni bwsarokbo abo hai’-no khlai lai kha; Pharao dagima hai khe Josep bórokno malkhungrogo khe boksaui cháthai rwui rohor kha tei tháng phuruni lamani bagwibo chámung rwui rohor kha.

English: To each and all of them he gave festal garments; but to Benjamin he gave three hundred shekels of silver and five festal garments.
Kokborok: Bo bórokno jani jani kan-sláina chum-sláina bagwibo rí jorsa khe rwui rohor kha; tamo khe bo Benjaminno khe rangchakni rang thamra tei kan-swláijaknai chum-sláijaknai khe jor ba rw kha.

English: To his father he sent as follows: ten asses loaded with the good things of Egypt, and ten she-asses loaded with grain, bread, and provision for his father on the journey.
Kokborok: Bo buphani bagwi khe korai chirka mak chi-o khlaiwi ha Ijipni kaham kuk manwirok boksaui tei bwrwi korai chirka mak chi-o khe lamani bagwi auang tei kubun chámungrok rwui rohor kha.

English: Then he sent his brothers away, and as they departed, he said to them, “Do not quarrel on the way.”
Kokborok: Abo hai khe bo bini takhuksongno rwui rohor kha tei bórok tháng phuru khe bórokno “Lamarogo ta uálai lai di” hwnwi saui rohor kha.

English: So they went up out of Egypt, and came to the land of Canaan to their father Jacob.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok ha Ijip yakarwi tháng lai kha tei Kanan ha-o bórokni bupha Jakobni thani kiphilwi phai kha.

English: And they told him, “Joseph is still alive, and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt.” And his heart fainted, for he did not believe them.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe bórok bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Josep tabukbo kwthang’-no tong kho tei bo bebak ha Ijipni tang dagiphang wngwi tong kha.” O kók khna khe bo uanajak kha tei bórokno poito khlaiwi man li ya.

English: But when they told him all the words of Joseph, which he had said to them, and when he saw the wagons which Joseph had sent to carry him, the spirit of their father Jacob revived;
Kokborok: Tamo bórok bono Josep samani bebak kókrokno sa khe tei Josep bono ha Ijibo tlangna bagwi rohormani a malkhungrokno nuk khe bórok bupha Jakob mwlang chamani sra kha;

English: and Israel said, “It is enough; Joseph my son is still alive; I will go and see him before I die.”
Kokborok: aphuru khe Israil hai hwnwi sa kha, “Angsla Josep kwthang tong kho; aswk khe se wng kha: ang thwi ya sini bono thángwi nai anw.”

English: So Israel took his journey with all that he had, and came to Beer-sheba, and offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
Kokborok: Hakhe Israil bini tong kwrwi bebak tẃiwi lam rw kha tei Birseba-o thángwi aro bupha Isakni Kaitorni thani ratharwi yaphar kha.

English: And God spoke to Israel in visions of the night, and said, “Jacob, Jacob.” And he said, “Here am I.”
Kokborok:
Kaitor bono horo imang-mukthang khe ringwi “Jakob, Jakob” hwnwi sa kha.
Aphuru khe bo, “I’n ang orono tongo” hwnwi sa kha.

English: Then he said, “I am God, the God of your father; do not be afraid to go down to Egypt; for I will there make of you a great nation.
Kokborok: Bono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Kaitor, nini nwphani Kaitor; Ijibo thángna bagwi ta kiri di; tamni hwn ba ang aro nono bosong kotor khlainai.

English: I will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also bring you up again; and Joseph's hand shall close your eyes.”
Kokborok: Ang nwng bai baksa ha Ijibo thángnai tei ang nono aroni kiphilwi’-bo tubu phinai; tei nwng thwi phuru Josep se nini mokolno kholopnai.”

English: Then Jacob set out from Beer-sheba; and the sons of Israel carried Jacob their father, their little ones, and their wives, in the wagons which Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Birsebani lam rw kha; tei Israilni bwsarok bupha Jakobno tei chwrai-khonairokno tei bihiksongno Pharao malkhung rohorma-o kasa rw bai kha.

English: They also took their cattle and their goods, which they had gained in the land of Canaan, and came into Egypt, Jacob and all his offspring with him,
Kokborok: Bórok ha Kananno ajimani bórokni mal-matarok, manwi-khwnwirok tẃiwi ha Ijibo tháng lai kha, Jakobni logi-o bini bosongni borokrok,

English: his sons, and his sons' sons with him, his daughters, and his sons' daughters; all his offspring he brought with him into Egypt.
Kokborok: bini bwsarok tei bini bwsarokni bwsarok, bwsajwksong, bwslasongni bwsajwksong bebak bini bosongni borokrokno tẃiwi Ha Ijibo tháng lai kha.

English: Now these are the names of the descendants of Israel, who came into Egypt, Jacob and his sons. Reuben, Jacob's first-born,
Kokborok: Jakob bai baksa ha Ijibo thángnai bini bosongni borokrok wng kha; Jakob tei bwsarok. Bwsla okra Ruben,

English: and the sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
Kokborok: tei Rubenni bwsarok; Hanok, Palu, Hesron tei Karmi.

English: The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanitish woman.
Kokborok: Simion tei bini bwsarok; Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jakhin, Johar tei Saul, bórok khe khoroksa Kananjwkmani bwsarok.

English: The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
Kokborok: Lebi tei bini bwsarok; Gerson, Kohat tei Merari.

English: The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah (but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan); and the sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
Kokborok: Juda tei bini bwsarok; Er, Onan, Sela, Peres tei Jera. (Phiya ba Er tei Onan, ha Kanano thwi kha); Peresni bwsarok wng kha, Hesron tei Hamul.

English: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puvah, Iob, and Shimron.
Kokborok: Isakar tei bwsarok; Tola, Puba, Jasub tei Simron.

English: The sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel
Kokborok: Jebulun tei bini bwsarok; Sered, Elon tei Jalil

English: (these are the sons of Leah, whom she bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram, together with his daughter Dinah; altogether his sons and his daughters numbered thirty-three).
Kokborok: o bwsarokno tei bwsajwk Dinano khe Lea Jakobni bagwi Padan-aramo tong phuru bámani; bórok bebagwi sajwk-sajlasong khorokthamchitham ká-o.

English: The sons of Gad: Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
Kokborok: Gad tei bini bwsarok; Jiphion, Hagi, Suni, Ejbon, Eri, Arodi tei Areli.

English: The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, with Serah their sister. And the sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel
Kokborok: Aser tei bini bwsarok khe; Imna, Isba, Isbi, Beria tei bórokni bukhuk Sera. Beriani bwsarok khe; Heber tei Malkiel

English: (these are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter; and these she bore to Jacob—sixteen persons).
Kokborok: (o khorokchi-dokrok wng kha Jakob Jilpa bai manmani bosong, bo wng kha Laban bwsajwk Leano rohormani a selengjwk).

English: The sons of Rachel, Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.
Kokborok: Jakobni bihik Rahel bini bagwi bwsa chwla khoroknwi bá kha; bórok wng kha Josep tei Benjamin.

English: And to Joseph in the land of Egypt were born Manasseh and Ephraim, whom Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera the priest of On, bore to him.
Kokborok: Ha Ijibo Josep bwsa chwla khoroknwi man kha; bórok wng kha Manase tei Ephraim, bórok Onni ochai Potipherani bwsajwk Asenatni ogo achainairok.

English: And the sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard
Kokborok: Benjaminni bwsarok khe; Bela, Becher, Asbel, Gera, Naman, Ehi, Ros, Mupim, Hupim tei Ard

English: (these are the sons of Rachel, who were born to Jacob—fourteen persons in all).
Kokborok: (O khorok-chibrwirok wng kha Jakob Rahel bai manmani).

English: The sons of Dan: Hushim.
Kokborok: Danni bwsla Husim.

English: The sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem
Kokborok: Naptali tei bini bwslasong; Jajil, Guni, Jejer tei Silem

English: (these are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter, and these she bore to Jacob—seven persons in all).
Kokborok: bórok khe Laban bwsajwk Rahelno rwmani selengjwk Bilha Jakob bai manmarok, o khoroksni bosong wng kha; Jakob Bilhani bai manmani bo wng kha selengjwk abo Laban; bórok khoroksni ká-o.

English: All the persons belonging to Jacob who came into Egypt, who were his own offspring, not including Jacob's sons' wives, were sixty-six persons in all;
Kokborok: Jakobni sakni bwsarok ha Ijibo thángnairok bebagwi khorok dokchidok ká-o, abo bini bahamjwksongno karwi se;

English: and the sons of Joseph, who were born to him in Egypt, were two; all the persons of the house of Jacob, that came into Egypt, were seventy.
Kokborok: ha Ijibo Josepni bwsa chwla khoroknwi wngmabo tongo; ha Ijibo phainai Jakobni bosongrok bebagwi khoroksnichi ká-o.

English: He sent Judah before him to Joseph, to appear before him in Goshen; and they came into the land of Goshen.
Kokborok: Bo Judano swkang Josep bai malaina bagwi Goseno phai malai phaina bagwi rwui rohor kha; tei bórok Goseno phaiwi sók phai kha.

English: Then Joseph made ready his chariot and went up to meet Israel his father in Goshen; and he presented himself to him, and fell on his neck, and wept on his neck a good while.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Josep bini korai malkhungo kasaui bini bupha Israil bai malaina bagwi Goseno tháng kha; tei bórok malai lai khe Josep bono gwdna-o kwbagwi tei jora kolokma kap kha.

English: Israel said to Joseph, “Now let me die, since I have seen your face and know that you are still alive.”
Kokborok: Israil Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang tabuk le thwiwi man kha, tamni hwn ba ang nini mwkhang nuk kha tei nwng tabukbo kwthang tong kho hwnwi saui man kha.”

English: Joseph said to his brothers and to his father's household, “I will go up and tell Pharaoh, and will say to him, ‘My brothers and my father's household, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me;
Kokborok: Josep bini takhuksongno tei bini nokni borokrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang Pharaono ahai hwnwi sanai, ‘Ha Kanano tongnai ani aphani tei ani takhuksongni nokni borokrok ani thani phai kha;

English: and the men are shepherds, for they have been keepers of cattle; and they have brought their flocks, and their herds, and all that they have.’
Kokborok: bórok khe mera mwrwknairok; tei tabuk jora bórok merarokno mwrwgwi tong bai-o; hakhe bórok bórokni tongma musukrok-merarok tubu-ui phai lai kha.’

English: When Pharaoh calls you, and says, ‘What is your occupation?’
Kokborok: Pharao norokno ringwi ‘Norokni samung tamo?’ Hwnwi sẃng khe

English: you shall say, ‘Your servants have been keepers of cattle from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,’ in order that you may dwell in the land of Goshen; for every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians.”
Kokborok: norok ahai hwnwi sa di, ‘Nini o selengrok khe chwrai phuruni simi tabuk jora musuk-merarokno riui mwrwgwi phai lai ja-o, chwng tei chini aphasong omo tẃi samung’-no khlai-o,’ omo hai khe bo norokno Gusen amchoi-o tongna bagwi rwnai; tamni hwn ba musuk-mera mwrwknairok khe Ijipsarok selengjago.”

English: So Joseph went in and told Pharaoh, “My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they possess, have come from the land of Canaan; they are now in the land of Goshen.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep Pharaoni thani thángwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani apha tei ani takhuksong ha Kananni bórokni mal-matarok tei bórokni tong-kwrwi bebak manwirok tubu-ui phai lai kha; bórok tabuk Gusen amchoi-o.”

English: And from among his brothers he took five men and presented them to Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Bo bini takhuk khorokbano Pharaoni thani tlang kha.

English: Pharaoh said to his brothers, “What is your occupation?” And they said to Pharaoh, “Your servants are shepherds, as our fathers were.”
Kokborok:
Pharao bórokno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Norok tamo samungrok khlai?”
“Nini o selengrok khe chini chuchusong hai’-no mera mwrwknai se bugra” hwnwi bórok sa kha.

English: They said to Pharaoh, “We have come to sojourn in the land; for there is no pasture for your servants' flocks, for the famine is severe in the land of Canaan; and now, we pray you, let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen.”
Kokborok: Bórok Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ha Kanano belai khe asari nang kha tei chini mal-matarokno chá rwna bagwi jabrarok kwrwi kha; hakhe o ha o tongnani hwnwi chwng ma phai kha, chwngno Gosen amchoi-o tongna bagwi kisa rw ja di.”

English: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Your father and your brothers have come to you.
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nini nwpha tei nini takhuksongrok nini thani phai kha.

English: The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land; let them dwell in the land of Goshen; and if you know any able men among them, put them in charge of my cattle.”
Kokborok: Ha Ijip ba nini bwskango’-no, nini takhuksongno tei nwphano o hani kaham kuk amchoino’-no rw di; bórokno Gosen amchoi-o tong thung; bórokni bisingo chúknai borokrok tong khe bono ani mal-matani naikolna barja rw di.”

English: Then Joseph brought in Jacob his father, and set him before Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bupha Jakobno Pharaoni bwskango tubu-ui phai kha tei Jakob Pharaono hamari rw kha.

English: And Pharaoh said to Jacob, “How many are the days of the years of your life?”
Kokborok: Hakhe Pharao Jakobno hai hwnwi sẃng kha, “Nini umor bisi bwswk wng kha?”

English: And Jacob said to Pharaoh, “The days of the years of my sojourning are a hundred and thirty years; few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not attained to the days of the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their sojourning.”
Kokborok: Jakob Pharaono hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang beraiwi kitingwi tongmani khe tabuk nokha bisi sarathamchi ká kha. A lái thángma bisirok khe báng ya tamo khe tong kesep se báng kugo. Ani tei chuchusongrokni bórokni beraiwi kitingwi thangsungo tong langma swk báng ya.”

English: And Jacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from the presence of Pharaoh.
Kokborok: Jakob Pharaono hamari rwui bini aroni ongkhorwi tháng kha.

English: Then Joseph settled his father and his brothers, and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep Pharao dagima hai khe’-no buphano tei bini takhukrokno ha Ijipni kaham kuk hano rw kha, bórokno ha Ijipni kaham kuk ha-o ton kha, abo wng kha Ramase ha-o.

English: And Joseph provided his father, his brothers, and all his father's household with food, according to the number of their dependents.
Kokborok: Josep buphano tei bini takhuksongno tei bini bebak buphani núkhungni borokrokno tei chwrai khonairokno bórok bángma swk tei nangma swk chámung rw kha.

English: Now there was no food in all the land; for the famine was very severe, so that the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished by reason of the famine.
Kokborok: Asari aswk khe wng kha, kwrẃngsa-o phano chámung kwrwi kha; ha Ijipni borokrok tei Kananni borokrok ma chá ya wngwi belai khe lengpro bai kha.

English: And Joseph gathered up all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, for the grain which they bought; and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh's house.
Kokborok: Ijip tei Kanan hani bebak rangrokno bórok chámung páina bagwi ma tubu kha tei Josep bebak rangno thumwi naui Pharaoni noyungo tubu-ui phai kha.

English: And when the money was all spent in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came to Joseph, and said, “Give us food; why should we die before your eyes? For our money is gone.”
Kokborok: Ulo ha Ijipni tei ha Kananni rang pái bai khe, bebak Ijipsarok Josepni thani phaiwi hai hwnwi sa phai kha, “Chwngno chámung rw di; nini mokol bwskango chwng tamni thwinai? Chini rangrok thung pái bai kha.”

English: And Joseph answered, “Give your cattle, and I will give you food in exchange for your cattle, if your money is gone.”
Kokborok: Josep hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tumung norokni rangrok pái kheba norokni mal-matarokno tubu di, abórokni sak slái-o ang norokno chámung rw anw.”

English: So they brought their cattle to Joseph; and Joseph gave them food in exchange for the horses, the flocks, the herds, and the asses: and he supplied them with food in exchange for all their cattle that year.
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Josepni thani bórokni mal-matarok tubu-ui phai kha; tei Josep bórokni bebak korairok, merarok, punrok tei musuk-toknokrok tei korai chirkarokni sak slái-o bórokno a bisini chámung rw kha.

English: And when that year was ended, they came to him the following year, and said to him, “We will not hide from my lord that our money is all spent; and the herds of cattle are my lord's; there is nothing left in the sight of my lord but our bodies and our lands.
Kokborok: A bisini ulo bórok bini thani phaiwi hai hwnwi sa lai phai kha, “Chwng nini thani huiwi sa yaui ton li ya, Hodasa chini bebak rangrokbo pái kha tei chini bebak mal-matarokbo nini’-no wngwi pái kha; tabuk chini bwsak tei chini harok simi se tong si-o.

English: Why should we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land for food, and we with our land will be slaves to Pharaoh; and give us seed, that we may live, and not die, and that the land may not be desolate.”
Kokborok: Chwng tamni chini harokno tẃiwi nini mokol bwskango thwinai? Chwngno chámung rwui chini harok bai baksa khe páiwi pái si di hakhe chwng Pharaoni paijak seleng wngwi pái sinai; chwngno bwchlwibo kisa rw di hakhe chwngbo thwi glak tei harok bo chúk ya khibijak wng glak.”

English: So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh; for all the Egyptians sold their fields, because the famine was severe upon them. The land became Pharaoh's;
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep ha Ijipni bebak hano Pharaoni banta paiwi na kha; tamni hwn ba Ijipsarok tong kwrwi harokno asari nangma bagwi ma phal kha. Hakhe bebak ha’-no Pharaoni wng kha;

English: and as for the people, he made slaves of them from one end of Egypt to the other.
Kokborok: tei ha Ijipni yaphangni simi buchuk jorani bebak borokrok sak sakno seleng ma snam bai kha.

English: Only the land of the priests he did not buy; for the priests had a fixed allowance from Pharaoh, and lived on the allowance which Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their land.
Kokborok: Bo ochairokni ha simi se pai li ya; bórok Pharaoni thani rang manma bai cháui tongo; aboni bagwi bórok bórokni harokno phalna nang li ya.

English: Then Joseph said to the people, “Behold, I have this day bought you and your land for Pharaoh. Now here is seed for you, and you shall sow the land.
Kokborok: Josep borokrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang tabuk norokno tei norokni hano Pharaoni bagwi pai kha. Iko norokni bagwi norokni kheto-o sarnani bwchlwi.

English: And at the harvests you shall give a fifth to Pharaoh, and four fifths shall be your own, as seed for the field and as food for yourselves and your households, and as food for your little ones.”
Kokborok: Norok ra bai pái khe banta kaibani kaisa Pharaono ma rwnai, bakbani bakbrwi khe nini wngnai, abo bwchlwini bagwi tei nini núkhungni chwrai-khonairokni bagwi chámung wngnai.”

English: And they said, “You have saved our lives; may it please my lord, we will be slaves to Pharaoh.”
Kokborok: Bórok hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwng chini langmarokno mwthang kha; nwng naima tẃi’-no chwng Pharaoni seleng wng sinai.”

English: So Joseph made it a statute concerning the land of Egypt, and it stands to this day, that Pharaoh should have the fifth; the land of the priests alone did not become Pharaoh's.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep ha Ijipni bagwi raida kaisa snam kha, abo tabukbo rákhokjago, ochairokni harok simi Pharaoni wng li ya. Jakobni koimung

English: Thus Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen; and they gained possessions in it, and were fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.
Kokborok: Omo tẃi khe Israil ha Ijipni Gosen amchoi-o tong kha; bórok aro gwnang kha ka tei kwbángma bwsa-bẃtẃirok man kha.

English: And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; so the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were a hundred and forty-seven years.
Kokborok: Jakob ha Ijibo nokha bisi chisni tong kha; bo umor nokha bisi sara tei brwichisni ká kha.

English: And when the time drew near that Israel must die, he called his son Joseph and said to him, “If now I have found favor in your sight, put your hand under my thigh, and promise to deal loyally and truly with me. Do not bury me in Egypt,
Kokborok: Bini thwinani jora samphaui phai khe, bo bini bwsla Josepno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tumung nwng ano hamjak khe, nini yakno ani yaphungo beraui kubui khe swmai tang di. Nwng ano ha Ijibo ta phob di,

English: but let me lie with my fathers; carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burying place.” He answered, “I will do as you have said.”
Kokborok: tamo khe o ha Ijipni kariui tlangwi aphasong bai pal khe bórok phobjakthani se ano phobwi di.” Bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nwng sama tẃi’-no khlainai.”

English: And he said, “Swear to me”; and he swore to him. Then Israel bowed himself upon the head of his bed.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ani thani swmai tang di”; hakhe bo swmai tang kha. Abo pái khe Israil bini thuyamo bokhorok bomwi tong kha.

English: After this Joseph was told, “Behold, your father is ill”; so he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
Kokborok: Aboni ulo khe Josep hai hwnwi sajak kha “Nai di nwpha lumui tong kha” hakhe bo bwslasong khoroknwi Manase tei Ephraimno logi-o tlangwi tháng kha.

English: And it was told to Jacob, “Your son Joseph has come to you”; then Israel summoned his strength, and sat up in bed.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakobno hai hwnwi sajak kha, “Nwsla Josep nini thani phai-o”; aphuru khe bo bini bebak phan sochaui bini thuyamo achuksa kha.

English: And Jacob said to Joseph, “God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me,
Kokborok: Hakhe Jakob Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kwplaisa Kaitor Kanan hani Lujo ang bai nukjak kha tei hamari rwui

English: and said to me, ‘Behold, I will make you fruitful, and multiply you, and I will make of you a company of peoples, and will give this land to your descendants after you for an everlasting possession.’
Kokborok: ano hai hwnwi sa kha, ‘Nai di ang nono kwbángma bwsa rwnai tei nini bosongrokno kwbángma dopha khlainai; ang nini bosongrokno nini yagulo kusung kusungni bagwi o hano rwnai.’

English: And now your two sons, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are mine; Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, as Reuben and Simeon are.
Kokborok: Hakhe tabuk ang ha Ijibo phaina swkang achaimani nwsla khoroknwirok ani se; Ruben tei Simion ani wngma hai Ephraim tei Manasebo ani’-no.

English: And the offspring born to you after them shall be yours; they shall be called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.
Kokborok: Tei bórokni ulo achainai nini bwsarok nini bwsa’-no wngnai; tei bórokni bosong bwtasongni bosongni mung bai se sinijaknai.

English: For when I came from Paddan, Rachel to my sorrow died in the land of Canaan on the way, when there was still some distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem).”
Kokborok: Tamni hwn ba ang Padanni phai phuru Ephrato sógwinani kisa swkang Rahel ano bwkha swbaiwi thwi kha, abo Ephrato sógwina kisa chal kho; hakhe ang bono Ephratni lama’-ono phob kha. (abo’-no Betlehem.)”

English: When Israel saw Joseph's sons, he said, “Who are these?”
Kokborok: Israil Josepni bwsarokno nuk khe, “Bórok le sabo?” hwnwi sẃng kha.

English: Joseph said to his father, “They are my sons, whom God has given me here.” And he said, “Bring them to me, I pray you, that I may bless them.”
Kokborok:
Josep buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Kaitor ano oro rwmani bórok khe ani sajlasong se.”
Hakhe Jakob hai hwnwi sa kha, “Bórokno ang hamari rwui manna tẃi khe ani thani tubu ja di.”

English: Now the eyes of Israel were dim with age, so that he could not see. So Joseph brought them near him; and he kissed them and embraced them.
Kokborok: Umor bángmani bagwi aphuru Jakobni mokol nukma mwnaksese wngjagwi tong kha, hakhe bo kaham khe nuktha li ya. Ahaini bagwi Josep bwsarokno bini thani tubu-ui phai kha; hakhe bo bórokno kwbak kha tei motom su kha.

English: And Israel said to Joseph, “I had not thought to see your face; and lo, God has let me see your children also.”
Kokborok: Israil Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang nini mwkhangno nuk phir anw hwnwi chongmani ya; tamo khe nai di Kaitor ano nwslasongno suda phunuk kha.”

English: Then Joseph removed them from his knees, and he bowed himself with his face to the earth.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bórokno bini khurini na kha tei bo bono ha-o bomwi khulum kha.

English: And Joseph took them both, Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel's left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel's right hand, and brought them near him.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bini bupha Israilni thani Ephraimno bini yagra tei Israilni yaksi-o khe tei Manaseno khe bini yaksi, phiya Israilni yagra-o tubu-ui phai kha.

English: And Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it upon the head of Ephraim, who was the younger, and his left hand upon the head of Manasseh, crossing his hands, for Manasseh was the first-born.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakob bini yagra yakno phologwi bini yaksi babaini bwsa kusu Ephraimni khorogo tei yak kebengwi bwsa okra Manaseno khe tei yasa yak bai bokhorogo yak bera kha.

English:
And he blessed Joseph, and said,
“The God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked,
the God who has led me all my life long to this day,
Kokborok:
Hakhe bo Josepno hai hwnwi saui hamari rw kha,
“Ani apha, Abraham tei Jakob kók khnaui tongmani a Kaitor,
ani langmano tabuksak jora
tẃitherwi phainai Kaitor,

ano bebak hamyani kati rwnai, a Suari o chwrairokno hamari rw thung;
bórokni bising tẃi ani mung tei ani aphasong Abraham
tei Jakobni mung sinijak thung; tei bórok o ha saka-o bwsa
kwbángma manẃi er thung.”

English: When Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand upon the head of Ephraim, it displeased him; and he took his father's hand, to remove it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.
Kokborok: Josep buphano Ephraimni bokhorogo bini yagra yak rwma nuk khe bwkha hamjak li ya; hakhe bo buphani yakno Ephraimni bokhorokni norwi Manaseni khorogo tlangna nai kha.

English: And Joseph said to his father, “Not so, my father; for this one is the first-born; put your right hand upon his head.”
Kokborok: Josep buphano hai hwnwi sa kha, “Apha ahai ya kho; omo se bwsa okra; bini khorogo se nini yagra yak bera di.”

English: But his father refused, and said, “I know, my son, I know; he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great; nevertheless his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his descendants shall become a multitude of nations.”
Kokborok: Tamo khe bupha lon li ya tei hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang si-o angsajla, ang si-o, bo’-bo kaisa bosong wngnai tei kotor wngnai; tamo khe buphayung bini slái kotor wngnai tei bini bosongrok kwbángma wngwi ernai.”

English:
So he blessed them that day, saying,
“By you Israel will pronounce blessings, saying,
‘God make you as Ephraim and as Manasseh’ ”;
and thus he put Ephraim before Manasseh.
Kokborok: Hakhe bo a salo bórokno hai hwnwi hamari rw kha, “Israilrok hamari sakhlai phuru nini mungno hai hwnwi khu-ui sanai, ‘Kaitor nono Ephraim hai tei Manase hai khlai thung.’ ”

English: Then Israel said to Joseph, “Behold, I am about to die, but God will be with you, and will bring you again to the land of your fathers.
Kokborok: Hakhe Israil Josepno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nai di, ang thwina sampha laha, tamo khe Kaitor nwng bai pal tongnai tei nono tei uáisa nuchusongni ha-o kiphilwi tlangnai.

English: Moreover I have given to you rather than to your brothers one mountain slope which I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow.”
Kokborok: Ang nwtasongno rw ya karwi nono se ha Sekemno rw-o, ang abono Amorsarokni yakni ani seng bai tei ani chakhla bai khe segwi namani.”

English: Then Jacob called his sons, and said, “Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you what shall befall you in days to come.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Jakob bwslasongno ringwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Norok kuthum phai bai di, thinango norokni tamo wngnai ang norokno abono sa anw:

English:
“Assemble and hear, O sons of Jacob,
and hearken to Israel your father.
Kokborok:
“O Jakobni bwsarok, kuthum phai bai di
tei nwpha Israilni kókrok khna di.

English:
Reuben, you are my first-born,
my might, and the first fruits of my strength,
pre-eminent in pride and pre-eminent in power.
Kokborok:
Ruben nwng ani sajla okra, nwng’-se ani phan
tei ani phanni tháikra bwthái,
ani swkangni bórom tei phan.

Nwng twi kochokjak hai,
tamo khe nini swkang wngmani manmungrok tong glak,
tamni hwn ba nwng nwphani thuthai-o
tháng kha; tei abono kwthar ya khlai kha,
ani thuyamo kasa kha!

English:
Simeon and Levi are brothers;
weapons of violence are their swords.
Kokborok:
“Simion bai Lebi bórok takhuknwirok;
tungphlajakni aitalrok khe bórokni sengrok.

English:
Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce;
and their wrath, for it is cruel!
I will divide them in Jacob
and scatter them in Israel.
Kokborok:
Bórokni jolima-o swraimung nang thung,
tamni hwn khe abo panjalwi wngma klai-o;
bórokni a jolimung belai khe khairokma kwrwi!
Jakobni bisingo ang bórokno baknai tei
Israilni bisingo pisa-piring khlainai.

English:
Judah is a lion's whelp;
from the prey, my son, you have gone up.
He stooped down, he couched as a lion,
and as a lioness; who dares rouse him up?
Kokborok:
Juda wng khe Singho bwsa se;
angsla mui romwi kasaui phai kha.
Bo singho hai khe achugo tei singho
bwrwi hai khe thu-o, bono sabo socha raknai?

English:
The scepter shall not depart from Judah,
nor the ruler's staff from between his feet,
until he comes to whom it belongs;
and to him shall be the obedience of the peoples.
Kokborok:
Judani yakni habugrani a raidang kwma glak,
tei bini yakung ká thaini bujni raidang kwma glak,
aboni bubagra (Silo) phai ya sak;
dopharok bini kók khnaui tongnai.

English:
Binding his foal to the vine
and his ass's colt to the choice vine,
he washes his garments in wine
and his vesture in the blood of grapes;
Kokborok:
Bo angur buphango bini koraino khanai,
tei bini hamjakma angur buphango
korai chirka bwsano khanai,
bo bini rírokno angur bwtwi bai khe sú-o,
tei bini kanma-chumma ríno
angurrokni thẃi-o khe sú-o;

English:
his eyes shall be red with wine,
and his teeth white with milk.
Kokborok:
bini mokolrokbo angur bwtwi nungma bai kwchak wngo,
tei bua khe abuktwi nungma bai kuphur wngo.

English:
Issachar is a strong ass,
crouching between the sheepfolds;
Kokborok:
“Isakar khe phan gwnang korai chirka,
bo mera bodolrokni kwcharo thu-o;

English:
he saw that a resting place was good,
and that the land was pleasant;
so he bowed his shoulder to bear,
and became a slave at forced labor.
Kokborok:
bo nuk kha lenglathai khe kaham,
tei a habo kwchang kwchang;
hakhe bo poja hornani bo phairukung bom rw kha,
tei seleng hai khe samung kwrak tang kha.

English:
Dan shall judge his people
as one of the tribes of Israel.
Kokborok:
Dan Israilrokni tei bosongrok hai
bini borokrokni buj thumnai wngnai.

English:
Dan shall be a serpent in the way,
a viper by the path,
that bites the horse's heels
so that his rider falls backward.
Kokborok:
Dan khe lama gandarni chibuk wngnai,
lama gandarni bis gwnang chibuk,
bo koraini yakorai-o sugo,
hakhe a korai kanai uklogo klaiwi thángo.

English: I wait for thy salvation, O Lord.
Kokborok: O Subraiyung ang nini phiyókmano naisingwi tongo

English:
Raiders shall raid Gad,
but he shall raid at their heels.
Kokborok:
“Gadno bodol kaisa swprakjaknai,
tamo khe bo’-bo bórokno uphilwi swprak phinai

English:
Asher's food shall be rich,
and he shall yield royal dainties.
Kokborok:
“Aserni chámungrok kaham wngnai,
tei bo habugrani bagwi nangma chámungrok rwnai.

English:
Naphtali is a hind let loose,
that bears comely fawns.
Kokborok:
“Naptali khe dwkhwi kwrwi
mwswi bwrwi hai,
bini khugo khe kók kwtẃi-kwtẃi máng.

“Josep khe bwthái kupulung bedek,
twisa rukungni bwthái kupulung bedek,
bini bedekrok chok deualno kasaui láiwi thángo.

English:
The archers fiercely attacked him,
shot at him, and harassed him sorely;
Kokborok:
Badukhung chakhla tẃinairok bono belai khe’-no swprak kha,
chakhla kogwi bono dobo khiklai kha;

English:
yet his bow remained unmoved,
his arms were made agile
by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob
(by the name of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel),
Kokborok:
hai phano bini badukhung ninang li ya,
bini yakbo haino srai-srai’-no tongo,
Jakobni a Phan kwraksani yak
(mwrwknaisa tei a Israilni Holongni mung bai),

English:
by the God of your father who will help you,
by God Almighty who will bless you
with blessings of heaven above,
blessings of the deep that couches beneath,
blessings of the breasts and of the womb.
Kokborok:
Nini nwphani Kaitor nono chubanai,
Phankrak Kaitor nono kuchuk sakani hamari rwnai tei ha bisingni twi chersa rwui hamari rwnai,
ogo tei khája-o nono hamari rwnai,

English:
The blessings of your father
are mighty beyond the blessings of the eternal mountains,
the bounties of the everlasting hills;
may they be on the head of Joseph,
and on the brow of him who was separate from his brothers.

Benjamin is a ravenous wolf,
in the morning devouring the prey,
and at even dividing the spoil.”
Kokborok:
“Benjamin akarjak swikur hai,
phungo khe bo mui romwi uásigwi chá-o,
tei sanja-o khe segwi tubuma manwirokno banta khlai-o.”

English: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel; and this is what their father said to them as he blessed them, blessing each with the blessing suitable to him.
Kokborok:
Omorok wng kha Israilni bosong Chiknwirok;
bórokni bupha hamari rw phuru o
kókrokno bosong thwgwini bagwi sa-o.
Bo bebakno’-no bórokni manthai hamarirokno
gothok rwui rw kha.

English: Then he charged them, and said to them, “I am to be gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
Kokborok: Ulo bo bórokno kók kwchárwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang ani borokrokni thani thángnani bagwi ani jora sók phai kha; aphasongno phob thani anobo bórok bai baksa khe Hitisa Ephronni hapharni a hakhoro phob di,

English: in the cave that is in the field at Mach-pelah, to the east of Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite to possess as a burying place.
Kokborok: abo wng kha Makpela-o, Mamreni salkabai-o tei abo ha Kananno, Abraham o hakhor tei hapharno simalwngni bagwi Hitisa Ephronni yakni paimani.

English: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife; and there I buried Leah the field and the cave that is in it were purchased from the Hittites.”
Kokborok: Aro’-no bórok Abrahamno tei Bihik Sarano phobo; Aro’-no bórok Isak tei bihik Rebekanobo phobo; Ang Leanobo arono phobo. A haphar tei hakhor Hitisarokni yakni pai-o.”

English: When Jacob finished charging his sons, he drew up his feet into the bed, and breathed his last, and was gathered to his people.
Kokborok: Jakob bini bwsarokno kók kwchárwi pái kha, aphuru khe bo bini thuyamo yakung kongnwino bochorop khlai kha tei rangma thagwi thwiwi bini borokrok tong thani tháng kha.

English: Then Joseph fell on his father's face, and wept over him, and kissed him.
Kokborok: Aphuru khe Josep buphani mwkhango motom su-ui kap kha.

English: And Joseph commanded his servants the physicians to embalm his father. So the physicians embalmed Israel;
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bini buphani mango molom phulna bagwi bithi-kwrwngrokno thol kha. Bithi kwrwngrok Israilno molom phul lai kha;

English: forty days were required for it, for so many are required for embalming. And the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.
Kokborok: abo khlainani sal brwichi nang kha, Ijipsarok bini bagwi salsnichi bwkha kham lai kha.

English: And when the days of weeping for him were past, Joseph spoke to the household of Pharaoh, saying, “If now I have found favor in your eyes, speak, I pray you, in the ears of Pharaoh, saying,
Kokborok: Bini bwkha khamma sal lái khe, Josep Pharaoni nokni borokrokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tumung ang norokni mokolo bwkha phurjak khe Pharaono kisa hai hwnwi saui ja di hwnwi ma koi-o;

English: My father made me swear, saying, ‘I am about to die: in my tomb which I hewed out for myself in the land of Canaan, there shall you bury me.’ Now therefore let me go up, I pray you, and bury my father; then I will return.”
Kokborok: apha thwinani swkang ano hai hwnwi swmai tang rwui kók kwchár lang kha, ‘Ani thwinani jora wng kha: Ha Kanano ani mangkhor khurjak tongma’-ono nwng ano phob di’ aboni bagwi ano kisa aphano phobnani rwui rohor ja di, hakhe ang bono phobwi kiphilwi phai anw.”

English: And Pharaoh answered, “Go up, and bury your father, as he made you swear.”
Kokborok: Pharao hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tháng di tei nwphano nwng kók rwma tẃi phobwi di.”

English: So Joseph went up to bury his father; and with him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his household, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep buphano phobna bagwi tháng kha; bo bai baksa ha Ijipni habugrani nokni okrarok, tangphangrok, tẃiphangrok, sebukrok tháng lai kha,

English: as well as all the household of Joseph, his brothers, and his father's household; only their children, their flocks, and their herds were left in the land of Goshen.
Kokborok: Josepni nokni borokrok, bini takhuksong tei buphani nokni tei tongnai borokrok bo bai baksa tháng bai kha; chwrai bwsarok tei bórokni merarok, punrok tei musuk-toknokrok simi se Goseno tong rwk si-o.

English: And there went up with him both chariots and horsemen; it was a very great company.
Kokborok: Korai malkhung kanairok tei korai saka kanairokbo bo bai baksa tháng kha; abo belai’-no bodol kotorma wng kha.

English: When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they lamented there with a very great and sorrowful lamentation; and he made a mourning for his father seven days.
Kokborok: Bórok Jordanni ayang nar Atado mai nakma jidolo sók phai kheno, bórok bwkha báiwi chirigwi kap lai kha; tei Josep salsni buphani bagwi kap kha.

English: When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning on the threshing floor of Atad, they said, “This is a grievous mourning to the Egyptians.” Therefore the place was named Abel-mizraim; it is beyond the Jordan.
Kokborok: Kannani borokrok bórokno a mai nakma jidoloni Atado kap laima nuk khe bórok hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Ijipsarok belai khe bwkha báijagwi kap lai kha.” Aboni bagwi abono Abel-Mijraim hwnwi mung pharjak kha; abo khe Jordanni ayang naro.

English: Thus his sons did for him as he had commanded them;
Kokborok: Hakhe bini bwslasongno bo dagima hai khe’-no bórok khlai kha;

English: for his sons carried him to the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field at Mach-pelah, to the east of Mamre, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite, to possess as a burying place.
Kokborok: bwslasong bini mangno ha Kanano tlang lai kha tei Makpelani hagurungo phobwi lai kha, abo khe Mamreni salkabai-o, abono Abraham simalwng khlaina bagwi Hitisa Ephronni aroni paimani.

English: After he had buried his father, Joseph returned to Egypt with his brothers and all who had gone up with him to bury his father.
Kokborok: Josep buphano phobwi pái khe, bo ha Ijibo bini takhuksong bai tei bo bai pal khe buphano phobna bagwi simalwngo thángnairok khe kiphil kha.

English: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, “It may be that Joseph will hate us and pay us back for all the evil which we did to him.”
Kokborok: Bórokni bupha thwimani ulo, Josepni takhuksong hai hwnwi sa kha, “Tabuk khe Josep chwng bini hamya khlaima bagwi chwngno yagul su-ui mano.”

English: So they sent a message to Joseph, saying, “Your father gave this command before he died,
Kokborok: Hakhe bórok Josepni thani borok rohorwi hai hwnwi sa kha, “Nwpha thwi ya sini hai hwnwi dagi lang kha,

English: ‘Say to Joseph, Forgive, I pray you, the transgression of your brothers and their sin, because they did evil to you.’ And now, we pray you, forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father.” Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
Kokborok: ‘Josepno hai hwnwi sa di ang koi ja-o, nini takhukrok nono hamya khlaimano nakar ja di.’ Phiya, tabuk nini o nwphani Kaitorni selengrokno nakar di.” Hakhe Josepno o kók sajak kheno kap kha.

English: His brothers also came and fell down before him, and said, “Behold, we are your servants.”
Kokborok: Hakhe bini takhuksong baithang phaiwi bono botok khe klai khulumui hai hwnwi sa lai kha, “Nai di chwng le nini selengrok se.”

English: But Joseph said to them, “Fear not, for am I in the place of God?
Kokborok: Tamo khe Josep bórokno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ta kiri di, ang ba Kaitor diba?

English: As for you, you meant evil against me; but God meant it for good, to bring it about that many people should be kept alive, as they are today.
Kokborok: Norok khe ani bagwi hamya chapmani; tamo khe Kaitor khe abono kaham khlai kha; kwbángma borokrokni langmano mwthangwi narwkna bagwi, tabuk kwthang tongmarok abo swkango a wngmarokni bising tẃi se wngo.

English: So do not fear; I will provide for you and your little ones.” Thus he reassured them and comforted them.
Kokborok: Aboni bagwi ta kiri di; ang norokni tei norokni bwsarokni nangmano rwnai.” Hakhe bo bórokno bwkha kwchang rwmung kók saui kók rw phi kha.

English: So Joseph dwelt in Egypt, he and his father's house; and Joseph lived a hundred and ten years.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep ha Ijibo bini buphani núkhungni borokrok bai khe tong lai kha; Josep nokha bisi sarachi kwthang tong lang kha.

English: And Joseph saw Ephraim's children of the third generation; the children also of Machir the son of Manasseh were born upon Joseph's knees.
Kokborok: Bo Ephraimni bwsarokni busuksongno nuk lang kha; Manaseni bwsla Makirni bwsaroknobo bo bini khuri-o khe bam lang kha.

English: And Joseph said to his brothers, “I am about to die; but God will visit you, and bring you up out of this land to the land which he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep bini takhuksongno hai hwnwi sa kha, “Ang thwinani kasu laha, tamo khe Kaitor norokno nainai tei bo norokno o hani kariui tlangwi Abraham, Isak tei Jakobno rwna hwnwi swmai tangmani a ha-o tlangwi thángnai.”

English: Then Joseph took an oath of the sons of Israel, saying, “God will visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.”
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep Israilsarokno hai hwnwi swmai tang rw kha “Kaitor norokno naiwi tongnai, norok ani bekrengno oroni tlangwi ma thángnai.”

English: So Joseph died, being a hundred and ten years old; and they embalmed him, and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.
Kokborok: Hakhe Josep ha Ijibo nokha bisi sarachi-o khe thwi kha; tei bórok bini mango molom sil lai kha tei buphangni bokhogo daui ton kha.
